Table flight.scheduled_flight {
  scheduled_flight_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the scheduled flight record. Primary key for the scheduled flight master data.']
  aircraft_type_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to fleet.aircraft_type. Business justification: Schedule planning systems require aircraft type master data (seating capacity, range, performance) to validate route feasibility and publish accurate capacity. aircraft_type_code is denormalized looku']
  foreign_carrier_permit_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.foreign_carrier_permit. Business justification: Scheduled international services must comply with bilateral permits specifying traffic rights, frequencies, capacity limits, and route authorities. Link required for schedule compliance validation, sl']
  route_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to route.route. Business justification: Scheduled flights operate on defined routes. This FK links the flight schedule to the route master for schedule planning, slot coordination, and network analysis.']
  aircraft_configuration_code varchar [note: 'Code identifying the specific cabin configuration and seat layout for the scheduled aircraft (e.g., 3-class, 2-class, all-economy). Links to detailed seat maps and cabin class definitions.']
  arrival_airport_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter IATA airport code for the scheduled arrival airport (e.g., LAX, CDG, SIN).']
  arrival_terminal varchar [note: 'Scheduled arrival terminal identifier at the destination airport (e.g., Terminal 2, T5, Concourse A).']
  block_time_minutes varchar [note: 'Planned block time in minutes from gate departure (Out) to gate arrival (In). This is the scheduled elapsed time for the flight leg.']
  codeshare_indicator boolean [note: 'Flag indicating whether this scheduled flight is part of a codeshare agreement where multiple airlines market the same flight operation. True if codeshare, false if single-carrier operation.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this scheduled flight record was first created in the system. Audit field for data lineage and compliance.']
  day_of_week_pattern varchar [note: 'Seven-character string indicating which days of the week the flight operates (1=operates, 0=does not operate). Position 1=Monday through position 7=Sunday (e.g., 1111100 = Monday-Friday only).']
  departure_airport_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter IATA airport code for the scheduled departure airport (e.g., JFK, LHR, DXB).']
  departure_terminal varchar [note: 'Scheduled departure terminal identifier at the origin airport (e.g., Terminal 1, T3, Concourse B).']
  effective_from_date date [note: 'The first date this scheduled flight is valid and may operate. Start of the schedule validity period.']
  effective_until_date date [note: 'The last date this scheduled flight is valid and may operate. End of the schedule validity period. Nullable for open-ended schedules.']
  etops_approval_indicator boolean [note: 'Flag indicating whether this scheduled flight route requires ETOPS certification for twin-engine aircraft operations over extended distances from diversion airports. True if ETOPS-certified route required.']
  flight_distance_km decimal(10,2) [note: 'Great circle distance between departure and arrival airports in kilometers. Used for RPK and ASK calculations.']
  flight_number varchar [note: 'The published flight number consisting of airline code and numeric identifier (e.g., AA1234, BA456). This is the externally-known identifier for the scheduled flight.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this scheduled flight record was last updated. Audit field for change tracking and data quality monitoring.']
  marketing_carrier_code varchar [note: 'Two-character IATA airline code of the carrier marketing and selling the flight. May differ from operating carrier in codeshare arrangements.']
  meal_service_code varchar [note: 'Single-character IATA code indicating the type of meal service provided: B=Breakfast, L=Lunch, D=Dinner, S=Snack, M=Meal (unspecified), N=No meal service.']
  minimum_connecting_time_minutes varchar [note: 'Minimum time in minutes required for passengers to make a legal connection from this flight to another flight at the arrival airport. Varies by airport, terminal, and connection type (domestic-domestic, domestic-international, international-international).']
  operating_carrier_code varchar [note: 'Two-character IATA airline code of the carrier actually operating the flight (e.g., AA for American Airlines, BA for British Airways).']
  published_date date [note: 'Date when this scheduled flight was first published to Global Distribution Systems (GDS) and made available for booking. Critical for revenue management and distribution.']
  regulatory_approval_status varchar [note: 'Status of regulatory approval from civil aviation authorities for this scheduled flight route and slot allocation: approved (fully authorized), pending (awaiting approval), conditional (approved with restrictions), or denied (not authorized).']
  route_network_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the scheduled flight within the airlines route network strategy: hub_spoke (connecting through a hub), point_to_point (direct city-pair), regional_feeder (short-haul feed to hub), long_haul_international (intercontinental), or domestic_trunk (major domestic route).']
  schedule_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the scheduled flight: active (currently operating), suspended (temporarily not operating), cancelled (permanently removed), seasonal_inactive (out of season), or pending_approval (awaiting regulatory approval).']
  schedule_version_number varchar [note: 'Version number of this schedule record. Incremented each time the scheduled flight details are modified (time changes, equipment changes, etc.). Supports schedule change tracking and audit.']
  scheduled_arrival_time timestamp [note: 'Published scheduled arrival time in local airport time. This is the planned gate arrival time (In time in OOOI).']
  scheduled_departure_time timestamp [note: 'Published scheduled departure time in local airport time. This is the planned gate departure time (Out time in OOOI).']
  season_code varchar [note: 'IATA season identifier for this schedule (e.g., S24 for Summer 2024, W24 for Winter 2024/2025). IATA defines two seasons per year: summer (last Sunday in March to last Saturday in October) and winter (last Sunday in October to last Saturday in March).']
  service_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the flight service: passenger (revenue passenger service), cargo (freight-only), ferry (non-revenue repositioning), charter (non-scheduled charter), or positioning (crew/aircraft repositioning).']
  traffic_restriction_code varchar [note: 'IATA traffic restriction code indicating any limitations on passenger or cargo carriage (e.g., C=Cargo only, G=Technical stop no traffic rights, F=Passenger service only). Empty if no restrictions.']
  wet_lease_indicator boolean [note: 'Flag indicating whether this scheduled flight is operated under a wet lease agreement (aircraft leased with crew, maintenance, and insurance from another carrier). True if wet lease operation.']

  Note: 'Master record for a planned flight operation as published in the airline\'s schedule. Represents the canonical scheduled flight leg with planned route, aircraft type, departure/arrival airports, scheduled times (STD/STA), flight number, codeshare indicators, operating carrier, service type (passenger/cargo/ferry), day-of-week frequency pattern, season validity (IATA summer/winter), and schedule status (active/suspended/cancelled). SSOT for all planned flight operations prior to day-of-operations. Each operated flight_leg references exactly one scheduled_flight as its planned baseline.'
}

Table flight.flight_leg {
  flight_leg_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the flight leg. Primary key for the operational instance of a single flight leg.']
  aircraft_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to fleet.aircraft. Business justification: Flight legs are operated by specific aircraft. While aircraft_registration exists, linking to the aircraft master (aircraft_id) enables access to full aircraft details (ownership, configuration, maint']
  company_code_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.company_code. Business justification: Flight operations must be attributed to legal entity for financial consolidation, regulatory reporting (DOT Form 41, IATA financial statements), intercompany revenue/cost allocation, and tax complianc']
  flight_number_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to route.flight_number. Business justification: Operational flights must validate against flight number registry for codeshare agreement compliance, slot allocation verification, GDS distribution rules, and traffic rights validation. Flight operati']
  occurrence_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to safety.occurrence. Business justification: Flight legs frequently generate safety occurrences (bird strikes, turbulence injuries, system malfunctions). Operations Control Center and Safety Management System require direct flight_leg→occurrence']
  scheduled_flight_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the planned baseline flight schedule for comparison of planned versus actual operations.']
  actual_arrival_time timestamp [note: 'The actual time the aircraft arrived at the gate (In time in OOOI), in local airport timezone.']
  actual_departure_time timestamp [note: 'The actual time the aircraft departed the gate (Out time in OOOI), in local airport timezone.']
  airborne_time_hours decimal(5,2) [note: 'Total time the aircraft was airborne, from wheels-off (Off) to wheels-on (On), measured in hours.']
  aircraft_registration varchar [note: 'The unique aircraft registration number (tail number) assigned to the aircraft operating this flight leg.']
  arrival_terminal varchar [note: 'The terminal identifier at the destination airport where the flight arrived.']
  block_hours decimal(5,2) [note: 'Total elapsed time from gate departure (Out) to gate arrival (In), measured in hours. Key metric for flight operations and crew duty time.']
  cabin_configuration_code varchar [note: 'Code identifying the specific cabin layout and seating configuration of the aircraft operating this flight leg (e.g., number of first, business, premium economy, and economy seats).']
  cancellation_reason_code varchar [note: 'Code indicating the reason for flight cancellation (e.g., weather, mechanical, crew, operational).']
  delay_code varchar [note: 'IATA standard two-digit delay code indicating the primary reason for any departure or arrival delay.']
  delay_minutes varchar [note: 'Total delay time in minutes, calculated as the difference between scheduled and actual departure or arrival time.']
  departure_terminal varchar [note: 'The terminal identifier at the origin airport from which the flight departed.']
  destination_airport_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter IATA airport code for the arrival airport of this flight leg.']
  dispatch_release_number varchar [note: 'Unique identifier for the dispatch release document authorizing this flight leg to operate, issued by flight dispatch.']
  diversion_airport_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter IATA airport code for the alternate airport if the flight was diverted from its original destination.']
  estimated_arrival_time timestamp [note: 'The current estimated arrival time, updated based on operational conditions. ETA is the best current estimate of when the flight will arrive.']
  estimated_departure_time timestamp [note: 'The current estimated departure time, updated based on operational conditions. ETD is the best current estimate of when the flight will depart.']
  flight_number varchar [note: 'The actual flight number operated on this leg, including carrier code and numeric identifier (e.g., AA1234, BA456).']
  flight_status varchar [note: 'Current operational status of the flight leg in its lifecycle. Tracks progression from scheduled through boarding, departure, airborne, landing, arrival, or exceptional states like cancelled or diverted. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: scheduled|boarding|departed|airborne|landed|arrived|cancelled|diverted — 8 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  flight_status_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The timestamp when the current flight status was last updated, in UTC.']
  fuel_uplift_kg decimal(10,2) [note: 'The amount of fuel loaded onto the aircraft for this flight leg, measured in kilograms.']
  in_time timestamp [note: 'The time the aircraft arrived at the gate and parking brake was set. Fourth event in OOOI (Out-Off-On-In) sequence.']
  is_codeshare boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this flight leg is operated as a codeshare with one or more partner airlines.']
  off_time timestamp [note: 'The time the aircraft wheels left the ground at takeoff. Second event in OOOI (Out-Off-On-In) sequence.']
  on_time timestamp [note: 'The time the aircraft wheels touched down on the runway at landing. Third event in OOOI (Out-Off-On-In) sequence.']
  operating_carrier_code varchar [note: 'Two or three-letter IATA airline code of the carrier actually operating the flight (may differ from marketing carrier in codeshare scenarios).']
  operating_date date [note: 'The date on which this flight leg is operated or planned to be operated, in local departure timezone.']
  origin_airport_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter IATA airport code for the departure airport of this flight leg.']
  out_time timestamp [note: 'The time the aircraft pushed back from the gate or began taxi for departure. First event in OOOI (Out-Off-On-In) sequence.']
  public_status_message varchar [note: 'Customer-facing status message displayed on airport FIDS, mobile apps, and passenger notifications describing the current flight status.']
  record_created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The timestamp when this flight leg record was first created in the system, in UTC.']
  record_updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The timestamp when this flight leg record was last modified, in UTC.']
  scheduled_arrival_time timestamp [note: 'The originally scheduled arrival time for this flight leg, in local airport timezone.']
  scheduled_departure_time timestamp [note: 'The originally scheduled departure time for this flight leg, in local airport timezone.']
  status_source varchar [note: 'The source system or feed that provided the current flight status update (ACARS, ATC feed, airport FIDS, manual OCC update, GDS, NetLine/Ops).']

  Note: 'Operational instance of a single flight leg actually operated or planned to be operated on a specific date. The atomic unit of flight execution and the central hub entity of the flight domain. Contains actual flight number, operating date, origin/destination airport pair, aircraft registration (tail number), cabin configuration, full flight status lifecycle with history (scheduled → boarding → departed → airborne → landed → arrived, or cancelled/diverted), status change timestamps with source attribution (ACARS, ATC feed, airport FIDS, manual OCC update), all OOOI timestamps (Out-Off-On-In), computed block hours and airborne time, estimated and actual departure/arrival times (ETD/ETA/ATD/ATA), gate/stand information, and public-facing status message. Links to scheduled_flight for planned baseline comparison. All operational flight records (dispatch, fuel, delays, performance) reference this entity. Supports real-time passenger notifications, airport FIDS integration, and OCC monitoring via NetLine/Ops.'
}

  dangerous_goods_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to cargo.dangerous_goods. Business justification: Dispatchers must review and acknowledge dangerous goods declarations during dispatch release preparation per IATA DGR and regulatory requirements. This link enables verification that DG cargo is prope']
  employee_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the certified flight dispatcher who prepared and authorized this dispatch release.']
  flight_leg_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the specific flight leg that this dispatch release authorizes for departure.']
  flight_plan_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to flight.flight_plan. Business justification: Dispatch release is based on and references a specific flight plan version. The dispatcher reviews the flight plan and issues the release authorizing departure. This is a core operational relationship']
  member_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the pilot-in-command (captain) who accepted and co-signed this dispatch release.']
  operational_approval_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.operational_approval. Business justification: Dispatch releases must reference specific operational approvals (ETOPS, RNP, CAT II/III) authorizing the planned operation. Critical for pre-flight compliance verification, regulatory audit trails, an']
  superseded_by_release_dispatch_release_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the dispatch release that superseded this one, if applicable. Used to track release amendment history.']
  additional_fuel_kg decimal(10,2) [note: 'Extra fuel added at captain or dispatcher discretion beyond regulatory minimums for operational flexibility, measured in kilograms.']
  aircraft_registration varchar [note: 'Tail number (registration) of the aircraft assigned to this flight at time of dispatch release issuance.']
  aircraft_type varchar [note: 'ICAO aircraft type designator for the aircraft assigned to this flight (e.g., B738, A320, B77W).']

  alternate_airport_2_icao varchar [note: 'Four-letter ICAO code of the secondary alternate airport if required by operational conditions or regulations.']
  alternate_fuel_kg decimal(10,2) [note: 'Fuel required to fly from destination to the designated alternate airport, measured in kilograms.']
  captain_name varchar [note: 'Full name of the pilot-in-command who accepted this dispatch release.']
  captain_signoff_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the captain electronically accepted and co-signed the dispatch release, indicating agreement with flight plan and operational conditions.']
  cargo_weight_kg decimal(10,2) [note: 'Total weight of cargo and baggage loaded on this flight, measured in kilograms.']

  dispatcher_name varchar [note: 'Full name of the certified flight dispatcher who issued this dispatch release.']
  dispatcher_signoff_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the dispatcher electronically signed and approved the dispatch release.']
  estimated_block_time_minutes varchar [note: 'Estimated total block time from gate departure (out) to gate arrival (in), measured in minutes.']
  estimated_flight_time_minutes varchar [note: 'Estimated total flight time from takeoff to landing, measured in minutes.']
  etops_authorization_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this flight is authorized for ETOPS operations (extended-range twin-engine flight over water or remote areas).']
  etops_entry_point varchar [note: 'Geographic waypoint or coordinate where the flight enters ETOPS airspace, if applicable.']
  etops_exit_point varchar [note: 'Geographic waypoint or coordinate where the flight exits ETOPS airspace, if applicable.']
  final_reserve_fuel_kg decimal(10,2) [note: 'Minimum fuel that must remain on board upon landing at the alternate airport, typically 30 minutes holding at 1500 feet above alternate, measured in kilograms.']
  hazmat_onboard_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether dangerous goods or hazardous materials are being carried on this flight, requiring special handling and documentation.']
  landing_weight_kg decimal(10,2) [note: 'Planned aircraft landing weight at destination, measured in kilograms.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this dispatch release record was last updated or amended.']
  mel_items_accepted_count varchar [note: 'Number of MEL (Minimum Equipment List) deferred maintenance items accepted and documented in this dispatch release, allowing flight with specific inoperative equipment.']
  mel_items_summary varchar [note: 'Textual summary of all MEL items accepted for this flight, including item numbers, descriptions, and operational limitations.']
  minimum_fuel_kg decimal(10,2) [note: 'Regulatory minimum fuel quantity required for this flight considering all fuel policy components, measured in kilograms.']
  notam_briefing_package varchar [note: 'Comprehensive text package of all relevant NOTAMs (Notices to Air Missions) for departure, en-route, destination, and alternate airports with operational significance flags.']
  passenger_count varchar [note: 'Total number of passengers planned to be carried on this flight at time of dispatch release.']
  planned_cruise_altitude_ft varchar [note: 'Planned cruising altitude for this flight leg, measured in feet above mean sea level.']
  planned_cruise_speed_kts varchar [note: 'Planned true airspeed during cruise phase, measured in knots.']
  planned_fuel_uplift_kg decimal(10,2) [note: 'Total quantity of fuel planned to be loaded onto the aircraft for this flight, measured in kilograms.']
  regulatory_authority varchar [note: 'Primary aviation regulatory authority governing this flight operation (FAA for US, EASA for EU, etc.).']
  release_issued_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the dispatch release was officially issued and authorized for flight departure. Principal business event timestamp for this regulatory document.']
  release_number varchar [note: 'Official externally-known dispatch release document number assigned by flight operations control. Unique business identifier for regulatory traceability.']
  release_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the dispatch release document in the authorization workflow.']
  release_version_number varchar [note: 'Version number of this dispatch release if it has been amended or superseded after initial issuance.']
  route_description varchar [note: 'Detailed flight route including airways, waypoints, and navigation fixes from departure to destination.']
  special_handling_notes varchar [note: 'Free-text field for any special operational considerations, restrictions, or handling instructions for this flight.']
  takeoff_weight_kg decimal(10,2) [note: 'Planned aircraft takeoff weight including fuel, payload, and operating empty weight, measured in kilograms.']
  trip_fuel_kg decimal(10,2) [note: 'Fuel required to fly from departure to destination under planned conditions, measured in kilograms.']
  weather_briefing_package varchar [note: 'Comprehensive weather briefing including METARs (Meteorological Aerodrome Reports), TAFs (Terminal Aerodrome Forecasts), SIGMETs (Significant Meteorological Information), PIREPs (Pilot Reports), and winds-aloft data for all relevant stations.']
  zero_fuel_weight_kg decimal(10,2) [note: 'Aircraft weight excluding all usable fuel, including operating empty weight and payload, measured in kilograms.']

  Note: 'Official dispatch release document authorizing a specific flight leg to depart. Contains dispatcher authorization, MEL (Minimum Equipment List) items accepted, fuel release quantities (planned uplift, minimum fuel, contingency, alternate, final reserve), alternate airports, NOTAM briefing package (relevant NOTAMs for departure/en-route/destination/alternate with operational significance flags), weather briefing package (METARs, TAFs, SIGMETs, PIREPs, winds-aloft for all relevant stations), ETOPS authorization if applicable, and dispatcher/captain sign-off timestamps. Mandatory regulatory document required by FAA 14 CFR Part 121.687 / EASA OPS for each commercial flight departure.'
}

Table flight.flight_plan {
  flight_plan_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the flight plan record. Primary key.']
  aircraft_type_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to fleet.aircraft_type. Business justification: Flight planning systems require aircraft type performance data (fuel capacity, cruise speed, MTOW, range) to calculate fuel requirements, weight limits, and route viability. aircraft_type_icao is deno']
  employee_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the flight dispatcher or flight planning officer who generated this flight plan.']
  flight_leg_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to flight.flight_leg. Business justification: Flight plans are operationally tied to specific flight legs (actual operations on a date), not just the schedule master. A flight plan is prepared for a specific operational instance. This FK links th']
  member_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the pilot-in-command who accepted and signed off on the operational flight plan.']
  scheduled_flight_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the specific flight leg for which this flight plan was generated.']
  additional_fuel_kg varchar [note: 'Extra fuel in kilograms added at the discretion of the pilot-in-command or dispatcher for operational reasons (weather, delays, etc.).']
  aircraft_registration varchar [note: 'Tail number or registration mark of the aircraft assigned to this flight plan.']
  alternate_airport_1_icao varchar [note: 'Four-letter ICAO code of the primary alternate airport in case destination is unavailable.']
  alternate_airport_2_icao varchar [note: 'Four-letter ICAO code of the secondary alternate airport.']
  alternate_fuel_kg varchar [note: 'Fuel in kilograms required to fly from destination to the alternate airport and conduct an approach and landing.']
  arrival_airport_icao varchar [note: 'Four-letter ICAO code of the destination airport.']
  contingency_fuel_kg varchar [note: 'Contingency fuel in kilograms to account for unforeseen factors (typically 5% of trip fuel or 5 minutes holding fuel, whichever is greater).']
  cost_index varchar [note: 'Cost index value used for flight planning optimization, balancing time cost versus fuel cost. Higher values favor speed; lower values favor fuel efficiency.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this flight plan record was first created in the system.']
  cruise_altitude_ft varchar [note: 'Planned cruise altitude in feet above mean sea level (MSL) for the primary cruise segment.']
  cruise_speed_kts varchar [note: 'Planned cruise speed in knots (true airspeed or indicated airspeed as per flight planning convention).']
  departure_airport_icao varchar [note: 'Four-letter ICAO code of the departure airport.']
  etops_entry_point varchar [note: 'Waypoint or geographic coordinate where the flight enters ETOPS airspace.']
  etops_exit_point varchar [note: 'Waypoint or geographic coordinate where the flight exits ETOPS airspace.']
  etops_qualified_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this flight plan is qualified for ETOPS operations (extended-range twin-engine flights over water or remote areas).']
  filed_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this flight plan version was filed with Air Traffic Control (ATC) or ICAO Flight Plan Processing System.']
  final_reserve_fuel_kg varchar [note: 'Final reserve fuel in kilograms: minimum fuel required to fly for 30 minutes at holding speed at 1,500 feet above alternate airport elevation in standard conditions.']
  landing_weight_kg varchar [note: 'Planned landing weight in kilograms at destination: takeoff weight minus trip fuel burn.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this flight plan record was last updated.']
  operational_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this flight plan version was accepted by the crew and loaded into the Flight Management System (FMS) as the operational flight plan (OFP).']
  plan_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the flight plan. Filed: submitted to ATC/ICAO; Amended: re-cleared or updated; Operational: final crew-accepted OFP loaded into FMS for execution; Cancelled: plan withdrawn; Rejected: not accepted by ATC; Superseded: replaced by newer version.']
  planned_block_hours decimal(5,2) [note: 'Estimated total block time in hours from gate departure (Out) to gate arrival (In), including taxi, flight, and approach time.']
  planned_flight_time_hours decimal(5,2) [note: 'Estimated airborne time in hours from wheels-off to wheels-on.']
  planned_taxi_in_minutes varchar [note: 'Estimated taxi time in minutes from landing to gate arrival.']
  planned_taxi_out_minutes varchar [note: 'Estimated taxi time in minutes from gate departure to takeoff.']
  route_of_flight varchar [note: 'Full textual representation of the planned route including RNAV waypoints, airways, Standard Instrument Departures (SIDs), and Standard Terminal Arrival Routes (STARs).']
  step_climb_schedule varchar [note: 'Planned altitude step-climb profile showing waypoints and altitudes where the aircraft will request higher cruise levels to optimize fuel efficiency as weight decreases.']
  takeoff_weight_kg varchar [note: 'Planned takeoff weight in kilograms: zero fuel weight plus total fuel on board at brake release.']
  total_fuel_required_kg varchar [note: 'Total fuel required in kilograms: sum of trip fuel, contingency fuel, alternate fuel, final reserve fuel, taxi fuel, and any additional fuel.']
  trip_fuel_kg varchar [note: 'Planned fuel burn in kilograms from takeoff to landing at destination airport.']
  version_number varchar [note: 'Sequential version number of this flight plan. Increments with each amendment or update to the plan for the same flight.']
  wind_data_source varchar [note: 'Source of wind and temperature forecast data used in flight planning (e.g., NOAA GFS, ECMWF, proprietary meteorological service).']
  zero_fuel_weight_kg varchar [note: 'Planned zero fuel weight in kilograms: aircraft operating empty weight plus payload (passengers, cargo, baggage) but excluding all usable fuel.']

  Note: 'Computerized flight plan for a specific flight leg, managed as a versioned document through its full lifecycle from initial filing to final crew-accepted Operational Flight Plan (OFP) loaded into the FMS. Contains route of flight (RNAV waypoints, airways, SIDs/STARs), planned altitude profile and step-climb schedule, estimated en-route time, fuel burn calculations by segment, alternate airports, wind and temperature data used, cost index, planned block hours, zero fuel weight, takeoff weight, landing weight, and performance data. Version status tracks progression: filed (submitted to ATC/ICAO), amended (re-cleared/updated), operational (final crew-accepted OFP loaded into FMS for execution). The operational version represents the definitive flight plan used by the crew and is the SSOT for planned route, fuel, and performance targets against which actuals are compared. Generated by Jeppesen Flight Planning System or equivalent.'
}

Table flight.oooi_event {
  oooi_event_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for each OOOI event record. Primary key for the OOOI event entity.']
  flight_leg_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the specific flight leg for which this OOOI event was recorded. Links to the operational flight leg in the flight operations domain.']
  acars_message_label varchar [note: 'The ACARS message label identifier associated with this OOOI event transmission (e.g., H1 for OUT, 10 for OFF, 11 for ON, 12 for IN). Null if data source is not ACARS.']
  acars_message_number varchar [note: 'The unique ACARS message sequence number for this OOOI event transmission. Used for message tracking and reconciliation. Null if data source is not ACARS.']
  aircraft_registration varchar [note: 'The tail number or registration identifier of the aircraft that generated this OOOI event. Used for cross-validation and aircraft-specific analysis.']
  airport_code varchar [note: 'The three-letter IATA airport code where the OOOI event occurred (departure airport for OUT/OFF, arrival airport for ON/IN).']
  altitude_feet varchar [note: 'The aircraft altitude in feet above mean sea level at the time of the OOOI event. Primarily populated for OFF and ON events from FMS or ADS-B data. Used for performance analysis.']
  correction_reason varchar [note: 'Free-text description explaining why this OOOI event timestamp was corrected. Null if is_corrected is False. Used for audit trail and data quality improvement.']
  data_quality_flag varchar [note: 'Indicates the quality and reliability of the OOOI event data: VERIFIED (confirmed accurate), ESTIMATED (calculated or interpolated), CORRECTED (manually adjusted after initial capture), DISPUTED (conflicting data from multiple sources), PENDING (awaiting validation).']
  data_source varchar [note: 'The system or method that captured and reported the OOOI event: ACARS (Aircraft Communications Addressing and Reporting System), ADS-B (Automatic Dependent Surveillance-Broadcast), MANUAL (crew or ground staff entry), FMS (Flight Management System), OOOI_SENSOR (dedicated aircraft sensor), GROUND_SYSTEM (airport ground handling system).']
  delay_code varchar [note: 'The IATA standard delay code indicating the reason for delay if the event was delayed (e.g., 11-Aircraft defects, 31-Flight deck crew, 81-Weather). Null if no delay occurred.']
  delay_minutes varchar [note: 'The number of minutes this OOOI event occurred after the scheduled timestamp. Positive values indicate delay, negative values indicate early operation. Foundation for OTP and DOT delay reporting.']
  event_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The actual date and time when the OOOI event occurred, recorded in UTC. This is the authoritative timestamp for the event used in OTP reporting and block hours calculation.']
  event_timestamp_local timestamp [note: 'The OOOI event timestamp converted to the local time zone of the airport where the event occurred. Used for operational reporting and crew scheduling.']
  event_type varchar [note: 'The type of OOOI event: OUT (pushback/gate departure), OFF (wheels-off/takeoff), ON (wheels-on/landing), IN (gate arrival/chocks-in). Foundation for block hours and OTP calculation.']
  flight_number varchar [note: 'The commercial flight number associated with this OOOI event (e.g., AA100, DL2345). Used for operational tracking and passenger communication.']
  gate_stand varchar [note: 'The gate or stand identifier where the OUT (pushback) or IN (arrival) event occurred. Null for OFF and ON events. Used for ground operations coordination.']
  ground_speed_knots varchar [note: 'The aircraft ground speed in knots at the time of the OOOI event. Primarily populated for OFF and ON events. Used for performance and safety analysis.']
  is_corrected boolean [note: 'Boolean flag indicating whether this OOOI event timestamp was manually corrected after initial capture due to data quality issues or system errors. True if corrected, False if original.']
  is_estimated boolean [note: 'Boolean flag indicating whether this OOOI event timestamp was estimated or interpolated rather than directly captured from aircraft or ground systems. True if estimated, False if actual.']
  latitude decimal(9,6) [note: 'The geographic latitude coordinate where the OOOI event occurred, captured from ADS-B or FMS. Primarily populated for OFF and ON events. Used for flight path analysis and safety investigations.']
  longitude decimal(9,6) [note: 'The geographic longitude coordinate where the OOOI event occurred, captured from ADS-B or FMS. Primarily populated for OFF and ON events. Used for flight path analysis and safety investigations.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text field for operational notes, comments, or additional context about this OOOI event. Used by flight operations staff to document unusual circumstances or data quality issues.']
  original_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The original OOOI event timestamp before any corrections were applied. Null if is_corrected is False. Preserved for audit trail and data lineage.']
  otp_eligible boolean [note: 'Boolean flag indicating whether this OOOI event is eligible for inclusion in OTP calculations per IATA or DOT standards. False if flight was cancelled, diverted, or otherwise excluded from OTP metrics.']
  processed_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The timestamp when the OOOI event data was validated, quality-checked, and integrated into the operational database. Used for data pipeline monitoring.']
  received_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The timestamp when the OOOI event data was received and recorded by the ground-based flight operations system. Used for data latency analysis and system performance monitoring.']
  reporting_status varchar [note: 'Indicates whether this OOOI event has been included in regulatory reporting submissions to DOT, IATA, or other authorities: REPORTED (included in submission), UNREPORTED (not yet submitted), EXCLUDED (intentionally omitted per reporting rules), AMENDED (corrected in a subsequent filing).']
  runway_identifier varchar [note: 'The runway designation used for the OFF (takeoff) or ON (landing) event. Format follows ICAO runway naming conventions (e.g., 27L, 09R). Null for OUT and IN events.']
  scheduled_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The originally scheduled timestamp for this OOOI event type (scheduled departure for OUT, scheduled takeoff for OFF, scheduled landing for ON, scheduled arrival for IN). Used to calculate delay and OTP metrics.']

  Note: 'Captures the four key gate/runway timestamps for each flight leg: Out (pushback/gate departure), Off (wheels-off/takeoff), On (wheels-on/landing), and In (gate arrival/chocks-in). Each event record includes the event type, actual timestamp, source system (ACARS, manual entry, ADS-B), airport and runway/gate reference, and data quality flag. Foundation for block hours calculation, OTP (On-Time Performance) reporting, and DOT/IATA punctuality statistics.'
}

Table flight.fuel_uplift {
  fuel_uplift_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the fuel uplift transaction record.']
  accounts_payable_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.accounts_payable. Business justification: Fuel supplier invoices flow through AP for payment processing and three-way matching (uplift record, invoice, payment). Links operational fuel transaction to financial settlement for cash management, ']
  flight_leg_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the specific flight leg for which fuel was uplifted.']
  fueling_vendor_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to procurement.vendor. Business justification: Fuel uplifts are performed by contracted into-plane agents (vendors). Airlines track vendor performance, invoicing, and SLA compliance per uplift. Current fueling_company_code is denormalized; proper ']
  station_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to airport.station. Business justification: Fuel uplift occurs at specific stations; linking enables fuel cost allocation by station, vendor performance tracking, price variance analysis, and station-level fuel consumption reporting. Core finan']
  aircraft_registration varchar [note: 'The tail number or registration code of the aircraft that received the fuel uplift.']
  carbon_emission_kg decimal(12,2) [note: 'Estimated carbon dioxide emissions in kilograms resulting from the combustion of the uplifted fuel, used for CORSIA reporting.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this fuel uplift record was first created in the system.']
  fuel_batch_number varchar [note: 'Batch or lot number of the fuel supplied, used for quality traceability.']
  fuel_density decimal(6,4) [note: 'Density of the fuel at the time of uplift, measured in kilograms per litre, used for mass-volume conversions.']
  fuel_price_currency varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for the fuel price.']
  fuel_price_per_unit decimal(10,4) [note: 'Price per unit (per kilogram or per litre) of fuel charged by the supplier.']
  fuel_quality_certificate_number varchar [note: 'Certificate number issued by the fuel supplier confirming fuel quality and compliance with aviation standards.']
  fuel_quantity_kg decimal(12,2) [note: 'Quantity of fuel uplifted measured in kilograms.']
  fuel_quantity_lbs decimal(12,2) [note: 'Quantity of fuel uplifted measured in pounds.']
  fuel_quantity_litres decimal(12,2) [note: 'Quantity of fuel uplifted measured in litres.']
  fuel_surcharge_amount decimal(12,2) [note: 'Additional surcharge or fee applied to the fuel uplift transaction.']
  fuel_temperature_celsius decimal(5,2) [note: 'Temperature of the fuel at the time of uplift, measured in degrees Celsius, used for density correction.']
  fuel_ticket_number varchar [note: 'Unique ticket or document number issued by the fueling company for this uplift transaction.']
  fuel_type varchar [note: 'Type of aviation fuel uplifted (e.g., Jet-A, Jet-A1, JP-8).']
  fuel_uplift_status varchar [note: 'Current status of the fuel uplift transaction (e.g., completed, partial, cancelled, disputed).']
  fueling_duration_minutes varchar [note: 'Duration of the fueling operation in minutes.']
  fueling_start_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the fuel uplift operation began.']
  fueling_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the fuel uplift operation was completed, in ISO 8601 format (yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX).']
  into_plane_agent varchar [note: 'Name or identifier of the into-plane agent or ground handler responsible for the physical fueling operation.']
  invoice_number varchar [note: 'Invoice number issued by the fuel supplier for this uplift transaction.']
  payment_method varchar [note: 'Method of payment used for the fuel uplift (e.g., credit account, cash, card, prepaid).']
  post_fueling_quantity_kg decimal(12,2) [note: 'Total quantity of fuel on board the aircraft after the uplift, measured in kilograms.']
  pre_fueling_quantity_kg decimal(12,2) [note: 'Quantity of fuel already on board the aircraft before the uplift, measured in kilograms.']
  remarks varchar [note: 'Free-text field for additional notes or comments related to the fuel uplift transaction.']
  tax_amount decimal(12,2) [note: 'Tax amount applied to the fuel uplift transaction, including excise or environmental taxes.']
  total_fuel_cost decimal(14,2) [note: 'Total cost of the fuel uplift transaction in the specified currency.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this fuel uplift record was last updated in the system.']

  Note: 'Records actual fuel loaded onto an aircraft for a specific flight leg at a specific station. Contains fuel quantity uplift (kg/lbs and litres), fuel density, fuel type (Jet-A, Jet-A1, JP-8), fuelling company, into-plane agent, fuel ticket number, fuel price per unit, total fuel cost, pre-fuelling quantity on board, post-fuelling quantity on board, and fuelling timestamp. Critical for fuel cost accounting, fuel efficiency analysis, and CORSIA carbon reporting.'
}

Table flight.acars_message {
  acars_message_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the ACARS message record. Primary key.']
  flight_leg_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the flight leg during which this ACARS message was transmitted or received.']
  oooi_event_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to flight.oooi_event. Business justification: ACARS messages with oooi_event_type populated are specifically OOOI event reports (Out-Off-On-In gate/runway timestamps). When an ACARS message IS an OOOI event report, it should FK to the oooi_event ']
  acknowledgement_required_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the message requires an acknowledgement response from the receiving party.']
  acknowledgement_timestamp timestamp [note: 'UTC timestamp when the acknowledgement was received for this message. Null if no acknowledgement required or not yet received.']
  aircraft_registration varchar [note: 'Tail number or registration identifier of the aircraft that transmitted or received the message.']
  altitude_feet varchar [note: 'Aircraft altitude in feet above mean sea level at the time of message transmission. Populated for position report and engine data messages.']
  arrival_airport_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter IATA code of the arrival airport for the flight leg associated with this message.']
  block_number varchar [note: 'Block identifier used in ACARS protocol for message segmentation and reassembly when messages exceed single transmission size.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'UTC timestamp when this ACARS message record was first created in the data system.']
  data_link_service_provider varchar [note: 'Communication service provider or medium used for ACARS message transmission (SITA network, ARINC network, satellite communication, VHF radio, or HF radio).']
  departure_airport_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter IATA code of the departure airport for the flight leg associated with this message.']
  error_code varchar [note: 'Error code indicating transmission or processing failure, if applicable. Null for successful messages.']
  flight_phase varchar [note: 'Phase of flight during which the ACARS message was transmitted, derived from OOOI events and flight profile data. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: pre_departure|taxi_out|takeoff|climb|cruise|descent|approach|landing|taxi_in|post_arrival — 10 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  ground_speed_knots varchar [note: 'Aircraft ground speed in knots at the time of message transmission. Populated for position report messages.']
  message_content varchar [note: 'Full text payload of the ACARS message containing the transmitted data, parameters, or free-text communication.']
  message_direction varchar [note: 'Direction of message transmission: uplink (ground to aircraft) or downlink (aircraft to ground).']
  message_label varchar [note: 'Two-character ARINC 618 message label code that identifies the message category and routing (e.g., H1 for OOOI, 44 for free text, B6 for position report).']
  message_sequence_number varchar [note: 'Sequential number assigned to the message within the flight leg or transmission session for ordering and duplicate detection.']
  message_size_bytes varchar [note: 'Size of the ACARS message payload in bytes.']
  message_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the ACARS message indicating its purpose: OOOI (Out-Off-On-In gate events), engine data, position report, crew free-text, CPDLC (Controller-Pilot Data Link Communications), ATIS (Automated Terminal Information Service), weather uplink, PIREP (Pilot Report), maintenance alert, or fuel report. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: OOOI|engine_data|position_report|free_text|cpdlc|atis|weather_uplink|pirep|maintenance_alert|fuel_report — 10 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  oooi_event_type varchar [note: 'Specific OOOI gate event captured in this message: OUT (pushback from gate), OFF (wheels up/takeoff), ON (wheels down/landing), IN (arrival at gate). Populated only for OOOI message types.']
  position_latitude decimal(9,6) [note: 'Latitude coordinate of the aircraft position at the time of message transmission, in decimal degrees. Populated for position report messages.']
  position_longitude decimal(9,6) [note: 'Longitude coordinate of the aircraft position at the time of message transmission, in decimal degrees. Populated for position report messages.']
  priority_level varchar [note: 'Priority classification of the message indicating urgency of processing and delivery (urgent for safety-critical, high for operational, normal for routine, low for informational).']
  processing_status varchar [note: 'Current processing state of the ACARS message in the ground system workflow.']
  receipt_timestamp timestamp [note: 'UTC timestamp when the ACARS message was received by the ground station or aircraft system.']
  retry_count varchar [note: 'Number of transmission retry attempts for this message due to delivery failures or acknowledgement timeouts.']
  transmission_timestamp timestamp [note: 'UTC timestamp when the ACARS message was transmitted from the aircraft or ground station.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'UTC timestamp when this ACARS message record was last updated in the data system.']

  Note: 'ACARS (Aircraft Communications Addressing and Reporting System) messages transmitted between aircraft and ground systems during flight operations. Stores message type (OOOI, engine data, position report, crew free-text, ATC CPDLC datalink, ATIS, weather uplink, pilot report/PIREP), message content/payload, aircraft registration, flight leg reference, ground station identifier, transmission timestamp, message direction (uplink/downlink), priority level, and processing status. Sourced from SITA/ARINC ACARS network. Enables real-time flight monitoring, automated OOOI capture, crew communications, and ATC datalink clearance documentation.'
}

Table flight.delay_record {
  delay_record_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the delay record. Primary key.']
  consumer_protection_case_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.consumer_protection_case. Business justification: Delays exceeding regulatory thresholds (3+ hours EU261, tarmac delay rules) trigger consumer protection obligations. Link enables compensation eligibility determination, regulatory reporting, and trac']
  cost_centre_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.cost_centre. Business justification: Delay costs (crew overtime, passenger compensation, missed slot fees, reactionary delays) are allocated to responsible cost centres for operational cost analysis, CASK calculation, and departmental pe']
  flight_leg_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the specific flight leg that experienced this delay.']
  occurrence_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to safety.occurrence. Business justification: Safety occurrences (bird strikes, medical emergencies, security events) directly cause operational delays. Delay analysis and regulatory reporting (DOT 14 CFR Part 234) require linking delay records t']
  primary_delay_verified_by_user_employee_id varchar [note: 'User identifier of the supervisor or quality assurance staff member who verified and approved this delay record. Used for data quality control and compliance auditing.']
  responsible_station_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to airport.station. Business justification: Delay accountability requires linking to responsible station entity for performance tracking, cost allocation of controllable delays, station manager accountability, and regulatory DOT reporting by st']
  affected_passenger_count varchar [note: 'Number of passengers (Pax) affected by this delay event. Used for customer impact assessment and compensation calculations.']
  compensation_issued_flag boolean [note: 'Boolean flag indicating whether passenger compensation (monetary or vouchers) was issued as a result of this delay under EU261, DOT, or airline policy. True if compensation issued, False if not.']
  corrective_action_taken varchar [note: 'Description of corrective or preventive actions taken to resolve the delay or prevent recurrence. Used for continuous improvement and safety management system (SMS) tracking.']
  data_source_system varchar [note: 'Identifier of the source system from which this delay record originated (e.g., Lufthansa Systems NetLine/Ops, Jeppesen Flight Planning, manual entry by operations staff, ACARS messaging, or other systems).']
  delay_category varchar [note: 'High-level classification of the delay cause: airline operational issues, ATC (Air Traffic Control) restrictions, airport facility problems, weather conditions, passenger-related issues, reactionary delays from late inbound aircraft, security events, or other causes. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: airline_operational|air_traffic_control|airport_facilities|weather|passenger_related|reactionary|security|other — 8 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  delay_cost_estimate_usd decimal(15,2) [note: 'Estimated financial cost of the delay in US dollars, including crew overtime, passenger compensation, fuel burn, missed connections, and operational disruption. Used for cost-benefit analysis and delay reduction initiatives.']
  delay_description varchar [note: 'Free-text narrative description providing detailed context and explanation of the delay event, root cause, and any corrective actions taken.']
  delay_duration_minutes varchar [note: 'Total duration of the delay event measured in minutes. Used for OTP (On-Time Performance) calculations and DOT reporting.']
  delay_end_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp marking the resolution or end of the delay event in UTC. Nullable if delay is ongoing or not yet resolved.']
  delay_notification_sent_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when delay notification was sent to passengers via SMS, email, or mobile app. Used to measure compliance with customer communication standards.']
  delay_sequence_number varchar [note: 'Sequential ordering of multiple delay events for the same flight leg. Allows tracking of multiple delay causes on a single flight.']
  delay_start_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp marking the beginning of the delay event in UTC. Used to calculate delay duration and analyze delay patterns by time of day.']
  delay_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the delay record: open (delay ongoing), resolved (delay ended), under investigation (root cause analysis in progress), closed (finalized and archived), or disputed (under review or challenge).']
  delay_verification_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the delay record was verified and approved by a supervisor. Nullable if verification is pending.']
  is_controllable_delay boolean [note: 'Boolean flag indicating whether the delay was within the airline\'s control (e.g., maintenance, crew scheduling) versus external factors (e.g., weather, ATC). Used for internal performance accountability and root cause analysis.']
  is_reactionary_delay boolean [note: 'Boolean flag indicating whether this delay was reactionary (caused by a late inbound aircraft from a previous flight leg). True if reactionary, False if not.']
  is_reportable_to_dot boolean [note: 'Boolean flag indicating whether this delay must be reported to the US Department of Transportation under 14 CFR Part 234 (applies to delays of 15 minutes or more for covered carriers). True if reportable, False if not.']
  missed_connection_count varchar [note: 'Number of passengers who missed their connecting flights as a result of this delay. Used for IROP (Irregular Operations) management and customer service planning.']
  primary_delay_code varchar [note: 'Two-digit IATA delay code representing the primary cause of the delay per AHM 730 standard (e.g., 11=Aircraft defects, 31=Flight deck crew, 81=Weather).']
  record_created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this delay record was first created in the system. Used for audit trail and data lineage tracking.']
  record_last_updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this delay record was last modified. Used for audit trail and change tracking.']
  responsible_department_code varchar [note: 'Internal department code identifying the organizational unit responsible for or associated with the delay (e.g., MX=Maintenance, OPS=Operations, CREW=Crew Scheduling, GND=Ground Handling).']
  secondary_delay_code varchar [note: 'Two-digit IATA delay code representing a secondary contributing cause of the delay per AHM 730 standard. Nullable if only one cause exists.']

  Note: 'Captures delay events associated with a flight leg including primary and secondary IATA delay codes (AHM 730 standard), delay duration in minutes, responsible department/station, delay description narrative, delay category (airline, ATC, airport, weather, pax-related), and whether the delay was reactionary (caused by late inbound aircraft). Supports OTP reporting, DOT delay reporting, root cause analysis, and operational performance management.'
}

Table flight.diversion {
  diversion_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the flight diversion event. Primary key.']
  cost_centre_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.cost_centre. Business justification: Diversions incur incremental costs (extra fuel, ground handling, passenger accommodation, crew expenses) that must be allocated to specific cost centres for operational variance analysis, budget contr']
  decision_authority_employee_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to workforce.employee. Business justification: Diversion decisions require named authority for regulatory accountability (FAA/EASA reporting). Operations Control Center duty managers or dispatchers make diversion calls; tracking the specific emplo']
  flight_leg_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the flight that was diverted. Links to the flight operations record.']
  occurrence_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to safety.occurrence. Business justification: Diversions are significant safety events requiring occurrence reporting under ICAO Annex 13 and national regulations. Direct linkage enables Safety Management System to track diversion→occurrence rela']
  regulatory_filing_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.regulatory_filing. Business justification: Diversions often require mandatory regulatory filings (safety reports, incident notifications to aviation authorities). Link tracks filing completeness, ensures timely submission, and maintains audit ']
  station_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to airport.station. Business justification: Diversion events require station entity link for ground handler coordination, passenger care resource allocation, diversion cost tracking by station, hotel/meal vendor management, and station capabili']
  actual_gate_arrival_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the aircraft arrived at the gate at the diversion airport. Completes the In event of OOOI tracking. Format: yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX.']
  actual_landing_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the aircraft touched down at the diversion airport. Part of OOOI (Out-Off-On-In) event tracking. Format: yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX.']
  aircraft_registration varchar [note: 'Tail number or registration mark of the aircraft that was diverted. Used for aircraft-specific tracking and maintenance correlation.']
  airport_icao_code varchar [note: 'Four-letter ICAO airport code of the diversion airport. Provides international standard identifier for flight planning and ATC coordination.']
  atc_coordination_reference varchar [note: 'Reference number or identifier provided by Air Traffic Control (ATC) for the diversion coordination. Used for operational tracking and incident investigation.']
  compensation_eligibility_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether passengers are eligible for monetary compensation under applicable passenger rights regulations due to the diversion. True if eligible; False otherwise.']
  continuation_departure_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the continuation flight departed from the diversion airport toward the original destination. Null if no continuation occurred. Format: yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX.']
  continuation_flight_number varchar [note: 'Flight number assigned to the continuation flight if the aircraft or passengers continued to the original destination after the diversion. Null if flight was cancelled or returned to origin.']
  cost_impact_currency_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for the estimated cost impact amount.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this diversion record was first created in the system. Format: yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX.']
  crew_duty_time_impact_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the diversion caused crew duty time or flight time limitations to be exceeded or approached, requiring crew replacement or schedule adjustments. True if impacted; False otherwise.']
  decision_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the decision to divert was made by the pilot-in-command or operational control. Format: yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX.']
  dispatch_release_updated_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether a new or amended dispatch release was issued for the continuation flight from the diversion airport. True if updated; False otherwise.']
  disposition_action varchar [note: 'Subsequent operational action taken after landing at the diversion airport. Continued_to_destination indicates flight resumed to original destination; returned_to_origin indicates flight returned to departure airport; cancelled indicates flight was terminated; aircraft_swap indicates passengers transferred to another aircraft; overnight_recovery indicates passengers accommodated overnight.']
  diversion_number varchar [note: 'Business identifier for the diversion event, used for operational tracking and reporting. Format: DIV followed by numeric sequence.']
  diversion_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the diversion event. Tracks progression from initial declaration through resolution.']
  estimated_cost_impact_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'Estimated total financial cost impact of the diversion event, including fuel, landing fees, passenger care, crew expenses, and operational disruption costs. Expressed in the airline\'s reporting currency.']
  fuel_uplift_at_diversion_kg decimal(10,2) [note: 'Amount of fuel uplifted at the diversion airport in kilograms. Required if the aircraft continued to the original destination or another airport after the diversion.']
  ground_time_duration_minutes varchar [note: 'Total time in minutes that the aircraft spent on the ground at the diversion airport, from gate arrival to departure (if continuation occurred) or final shutdown.']
  hotel_accommodation_provided_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether hotel accommodation was provided to passengers due to the diversion. True if accommodation was arranged; False otherwise.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this diversion record was last updated in the system. Format: yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX.']
  meal_voucher_provided_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether meal vouchers or meals were provided to passengers due to the diversion. True if meals were provided; False otherwise.']
  notam_reference varchar [note: 'Reference to any relevant NOTAM (Notice to Air Missions) that contributed to or was issued in relation to the diversion event.']
  passenger_care_action_taken varchar [note: 'Description of passenger care actions provided at the diversion airport, including meals, accommodation, rebooking, ground transportation, and communication. Documents compliance with passenger rights regulations.']
  passenger_count_diverted varchar [note: 'Total number of passengers (Pax) on board the aircraft at the time of diversion. Used for passenger care planning and regulatory reporting.']
  planned_destination_airport_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter IATA airport code of the original planned destination airport before diversion was declared.']
  reason_code varchar [note: 'Primary reason category for the diversion. Weather includes wx conditions at destination; medical includes passenger or crew medical emergencies; mechanical includes aircraft technical issues; fuel includes fuel shortage or fuel planning issues; ATC includes air traffic control restrictions; security includes security threats or incidents; airport_closure includes runway closures or airport operational issues. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: weather|medical|mechanical|fuel|atc|security|airport_closure — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  reason_description varchar [note: 'Detailed narrative explanation of the specific circumstances that led to the diversion decision. Provides operational context beyond the reason code.']
  regulatory_report_required_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the diversion event requires formal reporting to aviation regulatory authorities (FAA, EASA, national CAA). True if reporting is required; False otherwise.']
  regulatory_report_submitted_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the required regulatory report was submitted to the aviation authority. Null if no report was required or not yet submitted. Format: yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX.']
  safety_report_reference varchar [note: 'Reference number of any safety report filed in the airline\'s Safety Management System (SMS) related to the diversion event. Used for safety trend analysis and continuous improvement.']
  weather_condition_description varchar [note: 'Description of the weather (wx) conditions at the planned destination or en route that contributed to the diversion decision, if weather was a factor. Includes visibility, ceiling, wind, precipitation, and other meteorological phenomena.']

  Note: 'Records flight diversions where an aircraft lands at an airport other than the planned destination. Contains diversion reason (weather, medical, mechanical, ATC, security), diversion airport, time of diversion decision, actual landing time at diversion airport, subsequent disposition (continued to destination, returned to origin, cancelled), passenger handling actions taken, and estimated cost impact. Supports IROP management, regulatory reporting, and passenger care obligations.'
}

Table flight.flight_irop_event {
  flight_irop_event_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the irregular operations event record. Primary key for the flight IROP event entity.']
  affected_station_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to airport.station. Business justification: IROP events impact specific stations; linking enables station-level impact analysis, resource allocation during irregular operations, recovery plan coordination with station managers, and financial im']
  event_coordinator_employee_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to workforce.employee. Business justification: IROP events (irregular operations) have designated coordinators managing recovery. Current event_coordinator_name is denormalized. Airlines track coordinator accountability for performance reviews, re']
  general_ledger_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.general_ledger. Business justification: IROP events trigger significant financial impacts (recovery operations, passenger compensation, rebooking costs, crew expenses) that must be posted to GL for financial reporting, variance analysis, an']
  regulatory_filing_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.regulatory_filing. Business justification: Major IROP events (weather disruptions, system failures affecting multiple flights) trigger regulatory reporting obligations to aviation authorities. Link required for filing management, demonstrating']
  actual_passengers_affected varchar [note: 'Actual total number of passengers impacted by the irregular operations event across all affected flights. Calculated after event resolution.']
  actual_total_delay_minutes varchar [note: 'Actual cumulative delay minutes across all affected flights resulting from this irregular operations event. Calculated after event resolution.']
  additional_affected_stations varchar [note: 'Comma-separated list of additional IATA airport codes for stations impacted by the irregular operations event beyond the primary affected station.']
  atc_coordination_notes varchar [note: 'Notes documenting coordination activities and communications with Air Traffic Control during the irregular operations event.']
  cdm_session_reference varchar [note: 'Reference identifier linking to the Collaborative Decision Making session coordinating the operational response to this irregular operations event.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this irregular operations event record was first created in the system. Used for audit trail and data lineage.']
  estimated_financial_impact_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'Estimated total financial impact of the irregular operations event including operational costs, passenger compensation, and revenue loss. Expressed in airline base currency.']
  estimated_passengers_affected varchar [note: 'Estimated total number of passengers impacted by the irregular operations event across all affected flights. Used for customer service resource planning.']
  estimated_total_delay_minutes varchar [note: 'Estimated cumulative delay minutes across all affected flights resulting from this irregular operations event. Used for impact assessment and reporting.']
  event_code varchar [note: 'Standardized code identifying the type of irregular operations event. Used for operational reporting and analysis.']
  event_description varchar [note: 'Detailed narrative description of the irregular operations event including circumstances, operational impact, and contributing factors.']
  event_end_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the irregular operations event was resolved and normal operations resumed. Nullable for ongoing events.']
  event_start_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the irregular operations event was first detected or began affecting operations. Critical for timeline analysis and delay attribution.']
  event_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the irregular operations event. Tracks progression from initial detection through resolution and closure.']
  event_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the root cause driving the irregular operations event. Determines recovery strategy and operational response.']
  flights_cancelled_count varchar [note: 'Total number of flights cancelled as a direct result of this irregular operations event. Key metric for operational impact assessment.']
  flights_delayed_count varchar [note: 'Total number of flights delayed as a direct result of this irregular operations event. Key metric for operational impact assessment.']
  flights_diverted_count varchar [note: 'Total number of flights diverted to alternate airports as a direct result of this irregular operations event.']
  last_updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this irregular operations event record was most recently modified. Used for audit trail and change tracking.']
  notam_reference varchar [note: 'Reference identifier for any associated Notice to Air Missions issued in connection with this irregular operations event.']
  passenger_compensation_triggered_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this irregular operations event triggered passenger compensation obligations under applicable regulations such as EU261 or DOT rules.']
  recovery_plan_description varchar [note: 'Detailed description of the operational recovery plan including actions, resource allocation, and timeline for restoring normal operations.']
  recovery_plan_status varchar [note: 'Current state of the operational recovery plan developed to address the irregular operations event and restore normal operations.']
  regulatory_notification_required_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this irregular operations event requires notification to regulatory authorities such as FAA, EASA, or national civil aviation authorities.']
  regulatory_notification_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when regulatory authorities were notified of this irregular operations event. Nullable if notification not required or not yet completed.']
  responsible_department varchar [note: 'Name of the operational department or division responsible for managing the response to this irregular operations event.']
  root_cause_description varchar [note: 'Detailed explanation of the underlying root cause that triggered the irregular operations event. Used for post-event analysis and prevention planning.']
  severity_level varchar [note: 'Operational impact severity classification of the irregular operations event. Determines escalation procedures and resource allocation priority.']
  weather_condition_summary varchar [note: 'Summary of weather conditions contributing to or associated with the irregular operations event. Applicable for weather-related IROP events.']

  Note: 'Irregular Operations (IROP) event master record capturing disruptions that affect multiple flights or require operational recovery actions. Covers event type (weather, ATC ground stop, airport closure, aircraft AOG, crew shortage, security), affected station(s), event start/end times, severity level, CDM (Collaborative Decision Making) session reference, recovery plan status, and estimated total delay minutes and passengers affected. Distinct from individual delay_record or diversion — represents the root cause event driving multiple disruptions.'
}

Table flight.cancellation {
  cancellation_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the flight cancellation record. Primary key.']
  authority_employee_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to workforce.employee. Business justification: Flight cancellations require named decision authority for financial accountability (revenue impact tracking), regulatory reporting (DOT on-time performance), and union grievance defense. Current cance']
  consumer_protection_case_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.consumer_protection_case. Business justification: Flight cancellations triggering passenger compensation thresholds (EU261, DOT rules) generate consumer protection cases. Link required for regulatory reporting, compensation tracking, and demonstratin']
  flight_irop_event_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the broader irregular operations event that triggered this cancellation, if part of a multi-flight disruption scenario.']
  flight_leg_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the cancelled flight leg in the flight operations system.']
  general_ledger_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.general_ledger. Business justification: Cancellations trigger GL postings for revenue adjustments, passenger compensation expense, and operational cost write-offs. Financial reporting and variance analysis require linking cancellation event']
  occurrence_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to safety.occurrence. Business justification: Safety-related cancellations (maintenance defects, crew fatigue, hazardous weather, security threats) require occurrence reporting and SMS tracking. Direct cancellation→occurrence linkage supports reg']
  scheduled_flight_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the scheduled flight record that was cancelled.']
  advance_notice_hours decimal(5,2) [note: 'Number of hours between cancellation decision and scheduled departure time. Critical for determining passenger compensation eligibility under EU261 (14-day, 7-day thresholds).']
  affected_passenger_count varchar [note: 'Total number of passengers with confirmed bookings on the cancelled flight who require rebooking or refund.']
  aircraft_registration varchar [note: 'Tail number of the aircraft that was assigned to the cancelled flight at the time of cancellation.']
  atc_restriction_type varchar [note: 'Type of air traffic control restriction that contributed to the cancellation (SLOT=slot restriction, GDP=ground delay program, GS=ground stop, CLOSURE=airport closure, CAPACITY=capacity reduction, FLOW=flow control).']
  cancellation_type varchar [note: 'Whether the cancellation was proactive (planned in advance to minimize disruption) or reactive (last-minute response to an operational event).']
  compensation_amount_per_passenger decimal(10,2) [note: 'Standard compensation amount per passenger under applicable regulations (e.g., EUR 250/400/600 under EU261 based on distance and delay).']
  compensation_eligibility_status varchar [note: 'Status of passenger compensation eligibility determination under applicable regulations (EU261, DOT, local regulations).']
  crew_legality_issue varchar [note: 'Type of crew legality or availability issue that caused the cancellation (DUTY_TIME=exceeded duty limits, REST=insufficient rest, QUALIFICATION=crew not qualified, AVAILABILITY=crew unavailable, POSITIONING=crew positioning failure).']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'ISO 4217 three-letter currency code for all monetary amounts in this record.']
  decision_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the decision to cancel the flight was made by the authorized authority.']
  effective_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the cancellation became effective in operational systems and passenger notifications were triggered.']
  estimated_revenue_impact_amount decimal(12,2) [note: 'Estimated revenue loss from the cancelled flight including ticket revenue, ancillary revenue, and potential future booking impact.']
  first_notification_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the first passenger notification was sent regarding the cancellation.']
  is_controllable boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the cancellation was within the airline\'s control (maintenance, crew, operational decisions) versus uncontrollable (weather, ATC, security, force majeure). Used for passenger compensation eligibility under EU261 and DOT regulations.']
  is_dot_reportable boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this cancellation must be reported to the US Department of Transportation under 14 CFR Part 234 (applies to US domestic flights and international flights to/from US by US carriers).']
  maintenance_defect_code varchar [note: 'ATA chapter or MEL (Minimum Equipment List) code for the maintenance defect that caused the cancellation, if maintenance-related.']
  network_optimization_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the cancellation was a strategic network optimization decision (e.g., consolidating low-load flights, repositioning aircraft) rather than an operational failure.']
  notification_method varchar [note: 'Primary method(s) used to notify affected passengers of the cancellation (e.g., SMS, email, mobile app push, airport announcement, GDS message). [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: SMS|EMAIL|PUSH|AIRPORT_ANNOUNCEMENT|GDS|PHONE|WEBSITE — promote to reference product]']
  occ_remarks varchar [note: 'Free-text operational remarks and notes from the Operations Control Center regarding the cancellation decision and handling.']
  passenger_care_cost_amount decimal(12,2) [note: 'Total cost incurred for passenger care obligations including meals, accommodation, transportation, and compensation payments under EU261 or DOT regulations.']
  reason_code varchar [note: 'IATA AHM 730 standard cancellation reason code (e.g., WX for weather, MX for maintenance, CR for crew, OA for operational, etc.).']
  reason_description varchar [note: 'Detailed narrative explanation of the cancellation reason for operational and customer service purposes.']
  rebooked_passenger_count varchar [note: 'Number of affected passengers who were successfully rebooked on alternative flights.']
  record_created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this cancellation record was first created in the system.']
  record_updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this cancellation record was last updated in the system.']
  refunded_passenger_count varchar [note: 'Number of affected passengers who requested and received full refunds instead of rebooking.']
  regulatory_report_submitted_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the required regulatory report (DOT, EASA, local CAA) has been submitted for this cancellation.']
  regulatory_report_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the regulatory report for this cancellation was submitted to the governing authority.']
  substitute_departure_time timestamp [note: 'Scheduled departure time of the substitute flight, if one was operated to replace the cancelled service.']
  substitute_flight_number varchar [note: 'Flight number of the substitute or replacement flight operated to cover the cancelled service, if applicable.']
  weather_condition_code varchar [note: 'METAR/TAF weather phenomenon code if weather was a contributing factor to the cancellation (e.g., TS for thunderstorm, FG for fog, SN for snow).']
