  Note: 'Records flight cancellations including cancellation reason code (IATA AHM 730), cancellation decision timestamp, cancellation authority (OCC, station, crew), whether cancellation was proactive or reactive, passenger rebooking status, estimated revenue impact, and whether the cancellation was DOT-reportable. Distinct from delay_record (flight operated late) and diversion (flight landed elsewhere). Supports DOT 14 CFR Part 234 reporting and passenger care compliance.'
}

Table flight.operational_flight_plan {
  operational_flight_plan_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the operational flight plan record. Primary key.']
  dispatch_release_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to flight.dispatch_release. Business justification: The OFP is generated from and linked to a specific dispatch release. Currently has dispatch_release_number as STRING, which should be replaced with proper FK. The dispatch release number can be retrie']
  flight_leg_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the flight leg this operational flight plan supports.']
  flight_plan_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to flight.flight_plan. Business justification: The OFP (operational flight plan loaded into FMS) is derived from the filed flight_plan. This links the operational version to the planning version, enabling tracking of how the filed plan was adapted']
  operational_approval_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.operational_approval. Business justification: Operational flight plans must reference applicable operational approvals (ETOPS authorization, special airport approvals, RNP specifications) to validate flight legality. Critical for dispatch complia']
  operational_standard_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to route.operational_standard. Business justification: OFP generation requires direct reference to route-specific turnaround standards for accurate crew duty time calculations, minimum ground time validation, and fuel planning. Dispatchers validate planne']
  primary_operational_employee_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the flight dispatcher who prepared and released this operational flight plan.']
  additional_fuel_kg decimal(10,2) [note: 'Additional fuel carried at the discretion of the pilot in command or dispatcher beyond regulatory minimums in kilograms.']
  alternate_airport_1_code varchar [note: 'IATA or ICAO code of the primary alternate airport designated in the operational flight plan.']
  alternate_airport_2_code varchar [note: 'IATA or ICAO code of the secondary alternate airport designated in the operational flight plan.']
  alternate_fuel_kg decimal(10,2) [note: 'Fuel required to fly from destination to the designated alternate airport in kilograms.']
  contingency_fuel_kg decimal(10,2) [note: 'Contingency fuel carried to account for unforeseen factors such as wind, routing changes, or ATC delays in kilograms.']
  cost_index varchar [note: 'Cost index value used by the FMS to optimize speed and fuel burn trade-offs. Higher values favor speed over fuel economy.']
  crew_accepted_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the flight crew formally accepted the operational flight plan. Represents the final crew-accepted version distinct from the initial computerized flight plan filed with ATC.']
  estimated_block_time_minutes varchar [note: 'Estimated block time from gate departure to gate arrival in minutes.']
  estimated_time_enroute_minutes varchar [note: 'Estimated time enroute from takeoff to landing at destination in minutes.']
  etops_alternate_code varchar [note: 'IATA or ICAO code of the ETOPS alternate airport for extended-range twin-engine operations.']
  etops_approval_indicator boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this flight operates under ETOPS approval for extended-range twin-engine operations.']
  etops_rule_time_minutes varchar [note: 'ETOPS rule time in minutes (e.g., 120, 180, 207) indicating maximum diversion time to an alternate airport.']
  final_reserve_fuel_kg decimal(10,2) [note: 'Final reserve fuel required for 30 minutes holding at 1500 feet above alternate airport in kilograms.']
  flight_planning_system_code varchar [note: 'Code identifying the flight planning system that generated this operational flight plan (e.g., Jeppesen, Lido, NavBlue).']
  minimum_equipment_list_items varchar [note: 'List of MEL items or deferred maintenance items affecting this flight. Comma-separated MEL reference numbers.']
  notam_summary varchar [note: 'Summary of relevant NOTAMs affecting the route, departure, destination, and alternate airports.']
  ofp_version_number varchar [note: 'Version number of the operational flight plan. Increments when the plan is revised after initial dispatch.']
  plan_generated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the operational flight plan was generated by the flight planning system.']
  plan_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the operational flight plan. Tracks progression from draft through crew acceptance to completion. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: draft|released|accepted|active|completed|cancelled|superseded — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  planned_cruise_altitude_ft varchar [note: 'Initial planned cruise altitude in feet above mean sea level.']
  planned_cruise_speed_mach decimal(4,3) [note: 'Planned cruise speed expressed as Mach number.']
  planned_landing_weight_kg decimal(10,2) [note: 'Planned landing weight of the aircraft in kilograms at destination.']
  planned_takeoff_weight_kg decimal(10,2) [note: 'Planned takeoff weight of the aircraft in kilograms including fuel, payload, and crew.']
  record_created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this operational flight plan record was first created in the system.']
  record_updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this operational flight plan record was last updated in the system.']
  route_description varchar [note: 'Full textual description of the flight route including waypoints, airways, and navigation fixes as filed with ATC and loaded into the FMS.']
  step_climb_profile varchar [note: 'Planned step-climb altitude profile showing waypoints and altitudes for fuel-efficient cruise climbs.']
  taxi_fuel_kg decimal(10,2) [note: 'Fuel expected to be consumed during taxi operations before takeoff in kilograms.']
  total_fuel_required_kg decimal(10,2) [note: 'Total fuel required for the flight including trip, contingency, alternate, reserve, additional, and taxi fuel in kilograms.']
  trip_fuel_kg decimal(10,2) [note: 'Planned fuel required from takeoff to landing at destination in kilograms.']
  weather_deviation_indicator boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the operational flight plan includes route deviations due to weather conditions.']
  zero_fuel_weight_kg decimal(10,2) [note: 'Zero fuel weight of the aircraft in kilograms. Total weight excluding usable fuel.']

  Note: 'The operational flight plan (OFP) as loaded into the FMS and used by the crew in flight. Contains the final agreed route, step-climb profile, fuel burn by segment, estimated time en-route, waypoint ETAs, alternate fuel requirements, zero fuel weight, takeoff weight, landing weight, and performance data. Represents the final crew-accepted version distinct from the initial computerized flight_plan filed with ATC. Sourced from Jeppesen Flight Planning System.'
}

Table flight.weight_balance {
  weight_balance_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the weight and balance loadsheet record. Primary key.']
  dispatch_release_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to flight.dispatch_release. Business justification: Weight & balance loadsheet is part of the dispatch release package in aviation operations. The W&B must be coordinated with and approved as part of the dispatch release. This is a standard operational']
  flight_leg_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the flight leg for which this weight and balance loadsheet was prepared.']
  operational_flight_plan_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to flight.operational_flight_plan. Business justification: W&B loadsheet and OFP are coordinated operational documents. The OFP uses W&B data (ZFW, TOW, CG) for performance calculations and FMS loading. This FK links the loadsheet to the operational plan it s']
  aircraft_registration varchar [note: 'Tail number or registration code of the aircraft for which this weight and balance was calculated.']
  aircraft_type_code varchar [note: 'ICAO or IATA aircraft type designator (e.g., B738, A320, B77W) identifying the aircraft model.']
  baggage_weight_kg decimal(10,2) [note: 'Total weight of checked passenger baggage loaded in cargo holds.']
  captain_acceptance_signature varchar [note: 'Digital or physical signature of the pilot in command accepting the weight and balance loadsheet and authorizing departure.']
  captain_acceptance_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the pilot in command accepted and signed the weight and balance loadsheet.']
  cargo_weight_kg decimal(10,2) [note: 'Total weight of revenue cargo (freight and mail) loaded in cargo holds, excluding passenger baggage.']
  cg_aft_limit_percent_mac decimal(5,2) [note: 'Aft limit of the certified CG envelope for this weight and configuration. CG must not be behind this limit.']
  cg_forward_limit_percent_mac decimal(5,2) [note: 'Forward limit of the certified CG envelope for this weight and configuration. CG must not be ahead of this limit.']
  cg_landing_percent_mac decimal(5,2) [note: 'Estimated longitudinal centre of gravity position at landing expressed as percentage of mean aerodynamic chord. Must remain within certified CG envelope.']
  cg_takeoff_percent_mac decimal(5,2) [note: 'Longitudinal centre of gravity position at takeoff expressed as percentage of mean aerodynamic chord. Must be within certified CG envelope.']
  dispatcher_license_number varchar [note: 'FAA or EASA dispatcher license number of the dispatcher who signed off on the loadsheet.']
  dispatcher_name varchar [note: 'Name of the flight dispatcher who reviewed and signed off on the weight and balance loadsheet.']
  dry_operating_weight_kg decimal(10,2) [note: 'The weight of the aircraft including crew, crew baggage, catering, and all operational equipment but excluding fuel, passengers, cargo, and baggage. Also known as basic operating weight.']
  finalized_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the loadsheet was finalized and signed off, authorizing the flight for departure.']
  hold_aft_weight_kg decimal(10,2) [note: 'Total weight loaded in the aft cargo hold compartment.']
  hold_bulk_weight_kg decimal(10,2) [note: 'Total weight loaded in the bulk cargo hold compartment, typically used for loose baggage and small cargo items.']
  hold_forward_weight_kg decimal(10,2) [note: 'Total weight loaded in the forward cargo hold compartment.']
  landing_weight_kg decimal(10,2) [note: 'Estimated weight of the aircraft at landing. Calculated as takeoff weight minus trip fuel. Must not exceed maximum landing weight.']
  load_distribution_compliant boolean [note: 'Flag indicating whether the load distribution meets all structural and CG envelope requirements for safe flight.']
  loadmaster_license_number varchar [note: 'Professional license or certification number of the loadmaster who prepared the loadsheet.']
  loadmaster_name varchar [note: 'Name of the loadmaster or load control agent who prepared the weight and balance loadsheet.']
  loadsheet_number varchar [note: 'Unique externally-known identifier for the weight and balance loadsheet document, typically assigned by the load control system or dispatcher.']
  loadsheet_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the weight and balance loadsheet. Final status indicates dispatcher sign-off and regulatory compliance approval.']
  loadsheet_version varchar [note: 'Version number of the loadsheet. Incremented when corrections or updates are made prior to departure.']
  mail_weight_kg decimal(10,2) [note: 'Weight of mail cargo loaded in cargo holds.']
  max_landing_weight_kg decimal(10,2) [note: 'Maximum permissible landing weight for this aircraft as specified in the aircraft flight manual. Structural limit.']
  max_takeoff_weight_kg decimal(10,2) [note: 'Maximum permissible takeoff weight for this aircraft under current conditions (runway length, temperature, altitude, obstacles). May be limited below certified MTOW.']
  max_zero_fuel_weight_kg decimal(10,2) [note: 'Maximum permissible zero fuel weight for this aircraft as specified in the aircraft flight manual. Structural limit.']
  passenger_count_business varchar [note: 'Number of passengers seated in business class cabin.']
  passenger_count_economy varchar [note: 'Number of passengers seated in economy cabin.']
  passenger_count_first varchar [note: 'Number of passengers seated in first class cabin.']
  passenger_count_premium_economy varchar [note: 'Number of passengers seated in premium economy cabin.']
  passenger_count_total varchar [note: 'Total number of passengers on board across all cabin classes (first, business, premium economy, economy).']
  passenger_weight_kg decimal(10,2) [note: 'Total weight of all passengers calculated using standard or actual passenger weights per regulatory requirements.']
  prepared_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the weight and balance loadsheet was initially prepared by the loadmaster or dispatcher.']
  record_created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'System timestamp when this weight and balance record was first created in the database.']
  record_updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'System timestamp when this weight and balance record was last modified in the database.']
  remarks varchar [note: 'Free-text field for additional notes, special instructions, or clarifications related to the weight and balance calculation.']
  special_load_indicator boolean [note: 'Flag indicating whether the flight carries special loads requiring additional handling or documentation (dangerous goods, live animals, human remains, etc.).']
  takeoff_fuel_kg decimal(10,2) [note: 'Total usable fuel on board at takeoff, including trip fuel, contingency, alternate, final reserve, and additional fuel.']
  takeoff_weight_kg decimal(10,2) [note: 'Total weight of the aircraft at brake release for takeoff. Calculated as zero fuel weight plus takeoff fuel. Must not exceed maximum takeoff weight.']
  total_payload_kg decimal(10,2) [note: 'Total revenue payload weight including passengers, baggage, cargo, and mail.']
  trim_setting varchar [note: 'Calculated horizontal stabilizer trim setting required for takeoff based on CG position and weight. Entered into flight management system or set manually.']
  trip_fuel_kg decimal(10,2) [note: 'Fuel required to fly from departure to destination airport under expected conditions.']
  underfloor_cargo_uld_count varchar [note: 'Number of unit load devices (containers and pallets) loaded in underfloor cargo holds.']
  zero_fuel_weight_kg decimal(10,2) [note: 'Total weight of the aircraft excluding usable fuel. Calculated as dry operating weight plus payload (passengers, baggage, cargo). Critical structural limit.']

  Note: 'Weight and balance (W&B) loadsheet for each flight leg. Contains zero fuel weight (ZFW), takeoff weight (TOW), landing weight (LW), maximum structural weights, centre of gravity (CG) position at takeoff and landing, trim setting, load distribution by zone, passenger count by cabin class, cargo/baggage weight by hold, and loadmaster/dispatcher sign-off. Mandatory regulatory document for every commercial flight per FAA/EASA airworthiness requirements.'
}

Table flight.status {
  status_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the flight status record. Primary key.']
  flight_leg_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the flight leg this status record describes. Links to the flight_leg product.']
  aircraft_registration varchar [note: 'Tail number or registration of the aircraft currently assigned to this flight leg. May change due to aircraft swaps or operational adjustments.']
  altitude_feet varchar [note: 'Current altitude of the aircraft in feet above mean sea level. Updated from ACARS or ADS-B feeds during flight.']
  arrival_gate varchar [note: 'Current gate assignment for arrival. May be updated during flight based on airport operations and gate availability.']
  atc_slot_time timestamp [note: 'Allocated takeoff or landing slot time assigned by ATC. Critical for slot-controlled airports and ATFM (Air Traffic Flow Management) compliance.']
  boarding_start_time timestamp [note: 'Actual time when passenger boarding commenced at the departure gate. Used for operational metrics and passenger communication.']
  boarding_status varchar [note: 'Current boarding process status. Indicates the stage of passenger boarding operations at the departure gate.']
  cancellation_reason_code varchar [note: 'Standardized code indicating the reason for flight cancellation if status is cancelled. Used for operational analysis and passenger compensation processing.']
  delay_minutes varchar [note: 'Total delay in minutes compared to scheduled departure or arrival time. Used for OTP (On-Time Performance) reporting and DOT compliance.']
  delay_reason_code varchar [note: 'Standardized code indicating the primary cause of delay if the flight is delayed. Used for operational analysis and regulatory reporting.']
  departure_gate varchar [note: 'Current gate assignment for departure. May change during operational disruptions or gate reassignments.']
  diversion_airport_code varchar [note: 'IATA three-letter airport code of the diversion airport if the flight was diverted. Null if no diversion occurred.']
  estimated_arrival_time timestamp [note: 'Current best estimate of when the flight will arrive at the destination gate. Updated dynamically based on flight progress and conditions. Used for passenger notifications and ground operations planning.']
  estimated_departure_time timestamp [note: 'Current best estimate of when the flight will depart from the gate. Updated dynamically based on operational conditions. Used for passenger notifications and airport FIDS displays.']
  fids_published_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this status has been published to airport FIDS displays. Used for display synchronization and audit purposes.']
  flight_phase varchar [note: 'Current phase of flight operations. Provides granular operational state for real-time tracking and operational control center monitoring. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: pre_departure|taxi_out|takeoff|climb|cruise|descent|approach|landing|taxi_in|arrived — 10 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  gate_closure_time timestamp [note: 'Actual time when the departure gate was closed and no further passengers were accepted for boarding. Critical for on-time departure metrics.']
  gds_distribution_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this status was distributed to GDS systems (Amadeus, Sabre, Travelport). Used for external partner data synchronization tracking.']
  ground_speed_knots varchar [note: 'Current ground speed of the aircraft in knots. Updated from ACARS or ADS-B feeds during flight for ETA calculation.']
  in_time timestamp [note: 'Actual time when aircraft arrived at the arrival gate and parking brake was set. Final event in OOOI sequence. Used for block time and turnaround planning.']
  is_irop boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this flight is currently under irregular operations status requiring special handling, rebooking, or recovery procedures.']
  notification_sent_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether passenger notifications (SMS, email, push) have been sent for this status update. Used to prevent duplicate notifications.']
  occ_remarks varchar [note: 'Internal operational notes or remarks entered by OCC staff regarding this flight status. Used for operational coordination and handover communication.']
  off_time timestamp [note: 'Actual time when aircraft wheels left the ground at departure. Second event in OOOI sequence. Used for airborne time calculation.']
  on_time timestamp [note: 'Actual time when aircraft wheels touched down at destination. Third event in OOOI sequence. Used for flight time and arrival metrics.']
  out_time timestamp [note: 'Actual time when aircraft pushed back from the gate or began taxi. First event in OOOI (Out-Off-On-In) sequence. Used for block time calculation.']
  position_latitude decimal(9,6) [note: 'Current latitude coordinate of the aircraft in decimal degrees. Updated from ACARS or ADS-B feeds during flight for real-time tracking.']
  position_longitude decimal(9,6) [note: 'Current longitude coordinate of the aircraft in decimal degrees. Updated from ACARS or ADS-B feeds during flight for real-time tracking.']
  public_status_message varchar [note: 'Customer-facing status message displayed on airport FIDS, mobile apps, and passenger notifications. Provides human-readable explanation of current flight status.']
  record_created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this flight status record was first created in the system. Used for audit trail and data lineage.']
  record_updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this flight status record was last modified. Used for audit trail and change tracking.']
  status_code varchar [note: 'Current operational state of the flight leg. Represents the lifecycle stage from scheduled through completion or cancellation. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: scheduled|boarding|departed|airborne|landed|arrived|cancelled|diverted|delayed|returned — 10 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  status_source varchar [note: 'System or channel that provided this status update. Indicates the origin of the status information for data quality and reconciliation purposes.']
  status_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this status was recorded or became effective. Represents the business event time for this status change.']
  weather_condition_code varchar [note: 'Current weather condition code at departure or arrival airport affecting flight status. Used for delay attribution and operational decision-making.']

  Note: 'Real-time and historical flight status records providing the current operational state of a flight leg at any point in time. Contains status code (scheduled, boarding, departed, airborne, landed, arrived, cancelled, diverted, delayed), status timestamp, source system (ACARS, ATC feed, airport FIDS, manual OCC update), estimated departure/arrival times (ETD/ETA), gate information, and public-facing status message. Supports real-time passenger notifications, airport FIDS, and OCC monitoring via NetLine/Ops.'
}

Table flight.flight_performance {
  flight_performance_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the flight performance record. Primary key.']
  aircraft_type_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to fleet.aircraft_type. Business justification: Performance analysis requires aircraft type baseline data (typical fuel burn, cruise performance) to calculate variance indices and identify anomalies. Fleet performance benchmarking compares actual f']
  emissions_report_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.emissions_report. Business justification: Flight-level fuel burn and CO2 emissions aggregate into regulatory emissions reports for CORSIA, EU ETS, and carbon schemes. Link essential for emissions accounting, verification audits, and demonstra']
  flight_leg_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the completed flight leg for which performance data was captured.']
  flight_plan_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to flight.flight_plan. Business justification: Post-flight performance data should reference the flight plan that was filed/used for the flight. This allows comparing planned vs actual performance metrics (fuel burn, block time, cruise altitude, e']
  operational_flight_plan_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to flight.operational_flight_plan. Business justification: Flight_performance captures post-flight data from FDR/FMS/ACARS. It should reference the specific OFP (operational flight plan loaded into FMS) that was executed. This is more specific than the filed ']
  actual_block_time_minutes varchar [note: 'The actual block time in minutes from Out to In (OOOI) as recorded during flight operations.']
  actual_fuel_burn_kg decimal(12,2) [note: 'The actual fuel consumed during the flight in kilograms, captured from FDR/FMS/ACARS data.']
  aircraft_registration varchar [note: 'Tail number or registration identifier of the aircraft that operated this flight.']
  anomaly_description varchar [note: 'Textual description of any performance anomalies flagged during this flight, including threshold violations and engineering notes.']
  anomaly_flag boolean [note: 'Boolean flag indicating whether any performance anomalies were detected during this flight (e.g., excessive fuel burn, engine parameter deviations).']
  atc_delay_minutes varchar [note: 'Total delay in minutes attributed to ATC restrictions, holding patterns, or routing changes during this flight.']
  average_cruise_speed_mach decimal(5,3) [note: 'The average cruise speed expressed as a Mach number during the cruise phase of flight.']
  average_ground_speed_knots varchar [note: 'The average ground speed in knots during the flight, accounting for wind effects.']
  block_time_variance_minutes varchar [note: 'The difference between planned and actual block time in minutes. Positive indicates longer than planned, negative indicates shorter.']
  co2_emissions_kg decimal(12,2) [note: 'Calculated CO2 emissions in kilograms for this flight based on actual fuel burn and fuel type emission factors.']
  corsia_eligible_flag boolean [note: 'Boolean flag indicating whether this flight is eligible for CORSIA carbon reporting based on route and regulatory criteria.']
  cruise_altitude_feet varchar [note: 'The actual cruise altitude achieved during flight in feet above mean sea level, captured from FMS data.']
  data_quality_score decimal(3,2) [note: 'A quality score from 0.00 to 1.00 indicating the completeness and reliability of the performance data captured for this flight.']
  data_source varchar [note: 'The primary source system from which performance data was captured: FDR (Flight Data Recorder), FMS (Flight Management System), ACARS (Aircraft Communications Addressing and Reporting System), or MANUAL entry.']
  destination_airport_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter IATA code of the arrival airport for this flight leg.']
  engine_1_avg_egt_celsius decimal(6,2) [note: 'Average Exhaust Gas Temperature for engine 1 during cruise phase in degrees Celsius, captured from FDR.']
  engine_1_avg_fuel_flow_kg_per_hour decimal(10,2) [note: 'Average fuel flow rate for engine 1 in kilograms per hour during cruise phase.']
  engine_1_avg_n1_percent decimal(5,2) [note: 'Average N1 (low-pressure spool speed) for engine 1 during cruise phase as a percentage of maximum RPM.']
  engine_2_avg_egt_celsius decimal(6,2) [note: 'Average Exhaust Gas Temperature for engine 2 during cruise phase in degrees Celsius, captured from FDR.']
  engine_2_avg_fuel_flow_kg_per_hour decimal(10,2) [note: 'Average fuel flow rate for engine 2 in kilograms per hour during cruise phase.']
  engine_2_avg_n1_percent decimal(5,2) [note: 'Average N1 (low-pressure spool speed) for engine 2 during cruise phase as a percentage of maximum RPM.']
  flight_number varchar [note: 'The flight number for this performance record, typically carrier code plus numeric identifier.']
  flight_phase_coverage varchar [note: 'Comma-separated list of flight phases for which performance data was successfully captured (e.g., TAXI_OUT, TAKEOFF, CLIMB, CRUISE, DESCENT, LANDING, TAXI_IN).']
  fuel_efficiency_index decimal(8,4) [note: 'Normalized fuel efficiency metric comparing actual fuel burn to planned fuel burn. Values near 1.0 indicate on-plan performance.']
  fuel_variance_kg decimal(12,2) [note: 'The difference between planned and actual fuel burn in kilograms. Positive indicates over-burn, negative indicates under-burn.']
  operating_date date [note: 'The date on which the flight operated, used for performance tracking and reporting.']
  origin_airport_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter IATA code of the departure airport for this flight leg.']
  performance_category varchar [note: 'Overall performance classification for this flight based on fuel efficiency, block time adherence, and engine performance metrics.']
  planned_block_time_minutes varchar [note: 'The scheduled block time in minutes from gate departure to gate arrival as planned during scheduling.']
  planned_cruise_altitude_feet varchar [note: 'The planned cruise altitude in feet as filed in the flight plan.']
  planned_fuel_kg decimal(12,2) [note: 'The planned fuel load in kilograms as calculated during flight planning and dispatch release.']
  record_created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this flight performance record was first created in the system.']
  record_updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this flight performance record was last updated or modified.']
  remarks varchar [note: 'Free-text remarks or notes from flight operations, engineering, or performance analysts regarding this flight performance record.']
  weather_impact_flag boolean [note: 'Boolean flag indicating whether significant weather conditions (turbulence, icing, convective activity) impacted flight performance.']
  wind_component_knots varchar [note: 'The average wind component in knots experienced during cruise. Positive values indicate tailwind, negative values indicate headwind.']

  Note: 'Post-flight performance data captured from FDR/FMS/ACARS for each completed flight leg. Contains actual vs. planned fuel burn, actual vs. planned block time, cruise altitude achieved, average cruise speed (Mach), wind component (headwind/tailwind), fuel efficiency index, CO2 emissions calculated, engine performance parameters (EGT, N1, fuel flow), and any performance anomalies flagged. Supports fuel efficiency programs, CORSIA carbon reporting, and engineering performance monitoring.'
}

Table flight.booking_segment {
  booking_segment_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key uniquely identifying this passenger-flight booking segment']
  flight_leg_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to the specific flight leg on which this passenger is booked']
  pax_profile_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to the passenger profile for the traveler on this segment']
  pnr_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the Passenger Name Record (PNR) that contains this booking segment. Multiple segments may belong to the same PNR for multi-leg journeys.']
  baggage_allowance varchar [note: 'The checked baggage allowance for this passenger on this flight segment, expressed as piece concept (e.g., 2PC) or weight concept (e.g., 23KG).']
  boarding_sequence varchar [note: 'Numeric boarding priority assigned to this passenger for this flight, determining boarding group and order. Lower numbers board first.']
  boarding_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the passenger\'s boarding pass was scanned at the gate and they boarded the aircraft for this flight leg. UTC timestamp.']
  booking_class varchar [note: 'Single-letter booking class (RBD - Revenue Booking Designator) for this passenger on this flight segment. Determines fare level, upgrade eligibility, and mileage accrual.']
  cabin_class varchar [note: 'The physical cabin class in which the passenger is seated for this flight segment.']
  check_in_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the passenger completed check-in for this flight segment, either online, mobile, kiosk, or airport counter. UTC timestamp.']
  fare_basis varchar [note: 'The fare basis code indicating the specific fare rules, restrictions, and conditions applied to this passenger\'s ticket for this segment.']
  seat_assignment varchar [note: 'The specific seat assigned to the passenger on this flight leg (e.g., 12A, 34F). May be pre-assigned at booking, selected during check-in, or assigned at gate.']
  segment_created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this booking segment was created in the reservation system. UTC timestamp.']
  segment_status varchar [note: 'Current status of this booking segment in its lifecycle. Tracks progression from booking through travel completion.']
  segment_updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this booking segment was last modified. Updated on any change to segment details. UTC timestamp.']
  special_service_requests varchar [note: 'Comma-separated list of IATA SSR codes for special services requested for this passenger on this flight (e.g., WCHR, VGML, PETC).']
  standby_priority varchar [note: 'Priority ranking for standby passengers on this flight segment. Lower numbers have higher priority for available seats. Null if passenger is confirmed.']
  ticket_number varchar [note: 'The 13-digit IATA ticket number issued for this passenger on this flight segment. Links to ticketing and revenue accounting systems.']
  upgrade_status varchar [note: 'Status of any cabin upgrade request or confirmation for this passenger on this flight segment. NONE=no upgrade, REQUESTED=waitlisted, CONFIRMED=upgrade confirmed, CLEARED=upgrade processed at gate, DENIED=upgrade denied.']

  Note: 'This association product represents the operational booking/reservation segment linking a passenger to a specific flight leg. It captures the passenger\'s journey on a single flight, including seat assignment, boarding details, check-in status, fare information, and service entitlements. Each record represents one passenger\'s travel on one flight leg. This is the core operational entity referenced by check-in systems, boarding gate systems, departure control, and in-flight service systems. Aligns with Amadeus Altéa PNR segment concept.. Existence Justification: In airline operations, the relationship between flight legs and passenger profiles is a canonical many-to-many: each flight leg carries many passengers (typically 100-400 depending on aircraft), and each passenger travels on many flight legs over their lifetime and even within a single journey (multi-leg itineraries). This relationship is operationally managed as booking segments or PNR segments in reservation systems like Amadeus Altéa, with rich attributes including seat assignments, check-in/boarding timestamps, fare details, and service entitlements that belong to neither the flight nor the passenger alone.'
}

Table flight.flight_allotment {
  flight_allotment_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for flight_allotment']
  allotment_id varchar [note: 'Unique identifier for this flight allotment record. Primary key.']
  freight_forwarder_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to the freight forwarder holding the allotment. References the cargo agent master record.']
  scheduled_flight_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to the scheduled flight on which capacity is allocated. References the master scheduled flight record.']
  allocated_volume_cbm decimal(10,2) [note: 'The contracted cargo volume capacity in cubic meters that the freight forwarder has secured on this scheduled flight. Volume constraint applies in addition to weight.']
  allocated_weight_kg decimal(10,2) [note: 'The contracted cargo weight capacity in kilograms that the freight forwarder has secured on this scheduled flight. This is the maximum weight the forwarder can book against this allotment.']
  allotment_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the allotment: active (currently in effect), suspended (temporarily inactive), expired (validity period ended), cancelled (terminated before expiry).']
  created_date timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this allotment record was created in the system.']
  last_modified_date timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this allotment record was last updated.']
  minimum_utilization_threshold_pct decimal(5,2) [note: 'The minimum percentage of allocated capacity that the freight forwarder must utilize to maintain the allotment. Failure to meet this threshold may result in capacity reduction or allotment cancellation.']
  priority_level varchar [note: 'The booking priority level assigned to this allotment, determining precedence during capacity allocation and overbooking scenarios: platinum (highest priority), gold, silver, standard.']
  rate_class varchar [note: 'The pricing tier or rate class applicable to this allotment: contract (negotiated contract rate), published (standard published tariff), spot (market-driven spot rate), promotional (temporary promotional rate).']
  validity_end_date date [note: 'The last date on which this allotment contract is valid. After this date, the allotment expires and capacity reverts to general sale.']
  validity_start_date date [note: 'The first date on which this allotment contract becomes effective. The forwarder can book cargo against this allotment starting from this date.']

  Note: 'This association product represents the contractual capacity allocation between a scheduled flight and a freight forwarder. It captures the pre-booked cargo space (weight and volume) that a forwarder has secured on a specific scheduled flight, along with commercial terms, validity periods, and utilization thresholds. Each record links one scheduled_flight to one freight_forwarder with attributes that exist only in the context of this capacity contract.. Existence Justification: In airline cargo operations, freight forwarders negotiate capacity allotments on multiple scheduled flights to secure space for their shipments (e.g., a forwarder holds allotments on LAX-JFK, LAX-ORD, SFO-JFK). Simultaneously, each scheduled flight has capacity allocated to multiple forwarders to maximize load factor and revenue. The allotment itself is a contractual business entity with specific attributes (allocated weight/volume, validity periods, rate class, utilization thresholds) that belong to neither the flight nor the forwarder alone, but to the relationship between them.'
}

Table flight.cargo_segment {
  cargo_segment_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for this cargo segment record. Primary key.']
  awb_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to the air waybill being transported on this segment']
  flight_leg_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to the specific flight leg on which this cargo segment was transported']
  compartment_code varchar [note: 'Code identifying the specific cargo compartment or hold position where this AWB was loaded on this flight leg (e.g., FWD, AFT, BULK). Used for weight and balance calculations.']
  handling_station_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter IATA airport code for the station where this cargo was handled for this segment (loading, transfer, or offloading). May differ from flight origin/destination for transfer cargo.']
  loaded_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this AWB was physically loaded onto the aircraft for this flight leg. Used for operational performance tracking and load reconciliation.']
  loading_priority varchar [note: 'Priority level for loading this AWB on this specific flight leg, which may differ from overall AWB priority based on connection times, special handling requirements, or operational constraints.']
  manifested_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this AWB was added to the cargo manifest for this specific flight leg. Used for operational cutoff tracking and regulatory compliance.']
  offloaded_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this AWB was physically offloaded from the aircraft after this flight leg. Used for transfer coordination and delivery tracking.']
  proration_factor decimal(5,4) [note: 'Revenue allocation factor for this segment, used to distribute total AWB revenue across multiple flight legs for interline and codeshare accounting. Typically based on segment distance or weight-distance.']
  segment_chargeable_weight_kg decimal(10,2) [note: 'Chargeable weight for this specific segment, used for proration calculations and revenue allocation to this flight leg.']
  segment_sequence varchar [note: 'Sequential order of this flight leg within the AWB\'s complete routing (1 for first leg, 2 for second leg, etc.). Critical for multi-leg shipment tracking and transfer coordination.']
  segment_status varchar [note: 'Current operational status of this cargo segment within its lifecycle: booked (reserved on flight), manifested (included in flight manifest), loaded (physically loaded on aircraft), departed (flight departed), arrived (flight arrived), offloaded (cargo removed from aircraft), transferred (handed over to next segment).']
  segment_weight_kg decimal(10,2) [note: 'Actual weight of this AWB carried on this specific flight leg, in kilograms. May differ from total AWB weight if shipment is split or consolidated during transit.']
  transfer_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this AWB was transferred between flight legs at an intermediate station. Null for origin and final destination segments. Critical for tracking transfer performance and connection integrity.']
  uld_number varchar [note: 'Identifier of the Unit Load Device (container or pallet) on which this AWB was loaded for this flight leg. Null for bulk cargo. Used for load planning and tracking.']

  Note: 'This association product represents the operational segment-level tracking of air waybill shipments on specific flight legs. It captures the core operational reality of air cargo routing: multi-leg shipments travel on multiple flight_legs, and each flight_leg carries multiple AWBs. Each record links one flight_leg to one AWB with segment-specific attributes including weight carried per leg, proration factors for revenue sharing, transfer handling details, and segment sequencing. This is the atomic unit of cargo operations and revenue accounting.. Existence Justification: In airline cargo operations, multi-leg shipments routinely travel on multiple flight_legs (e.g., LAX→LHR via JFK requires two flight_legs), and each flight_leg carries multiple AWBs. This is the fundamental operational reality of air cargo routing. Airlines actively manage segment-level data including weight carried per leg, proration factors for revenue sharing among carriers, transfer handling at intermediate stations, and segment sequencing for operational coordination.'
}

Table flight.dispatch_exemption_invocation {
  dispatch_exemption_invocation_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for this specific invocation of an exemption within a dispatch release. Primary key.']
  dispatch_release_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to the dispatch release document that invokes this exemption']
  exemption_waiver_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to the regulatory exemption or waiver being invoked']
  applicability_justification varchar [note: 'Dispatcher\'s explanation of why this specific exemption is applicable to this flight (e.g., \'ETOPS diversion time waiver applies due to North Atlantic routing with limited alternate airports\'). Required for regulatory audit defense.']
  application_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the dispatcher applied this exemption to the dispatch release during the authorization workflow. Captures the operational moment of invocation.']
  approval_status varchar [note: 'Status of the exemption invocation approval within the dispatch release workflow (approved, conditionally_approved, rejected, pending_review). Tracks whether the dispatcher\'s use of this exemption was validated.']
  condition_compliance_notes varchar [note: 'Detailed notes documenting how the flight meets all conditions attached to the exemption (e.g., \'Aircraft equipped with required ETOPS equipment per exemption condition 3a; crew ETOPS-qualified per condition 2b\').']
  conditions_accepted_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the dispatcher explicitly acknowledged and accepted all conditions attached to this exemption as applicable to this specific flight. Required for regulatory compliance attestation.']
  dispatcher_acknowledgment varchar [note: 'Textual acknowledgment or attestation statement by the dispatcher confirming understanding of the exemption\'s conditions and applicability to this flight. May include dispatcher comments on specific condition compliance.']
  expiry_verification_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the dispatcher verified that the exemption was still valid (not expired) at the time of dispatch release issuance. Critical for audit trail demonstrating due diligence.']
  invocation_sequence_number varchar [note: 'Sequential order in which this exemption was applied within the dispatch release workflow. Useful for understanding dispatcher decision-making sequence during release preparation.']

  Note: 'This association product represents the operational invocation of regulatory exemptions and waivers within specific dispatch release documents. Each record captures when a dispatcher explicitly invokes a regulatory relief instrument to authorize a flight departure under non-standard conditions, including the dispatcher\'s acknowledgment of conditions, verification of applicability, and compliance attestation required for FAA/EASA audit trails per 14 CFR Part 121.. Existence Justification: In airline dispatch operations, a single dispatch release can invoke multiple regulatory exemptions simultaneously (e.g., ETOPS diversion time relief + MEL extension + special airport procedure waiver), and each exemption applies to many dispatch releases over its validity period. Dispatchers actively manage which exemptions are invoked per release, documenting acknowledgment of conditions, verification of applicability, and compliance attestation to create an auditable regulatory trail required by FAA 14 CFR Part 121.687.'
}

Table flight.codeshare_flight_execution {
  codeshare_flight_execution_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for this codeshare flight execution record. Primary key.']
  flight_leg_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to the specific operational flight leg instance on which this codeshare agreement is executed.']
  partnership_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to the route partnership agreement that governs this codeshare execution.']
  codeshare_flight_number_used varchar [note: 'The specific marketing carrier flight number assigned and used for this codeshare execution, including carrier code and numeric identifier. This is the flight number passengers see on their tickets.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'UTC timestamp when this codeshare flight execution record was created in the system, typically during flight planning or schedule publication.']
  execution_status varchar [note: 'Operational status of this codeshare execution instance. Tracks whether the codeshare arrangement is active for this specific flight or has been cancelled/suspended due to operational or commercial reasons.']
  last_updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'UTC timestamp when this codeshare flight execution record was last modified, tracking changes to settlement status, passenger counts, or operational status.']
  partner_notification_timestamp timestamp [note: 'UTC timestamp when the partner carrier was notified of operational details for this codeshare flight execution (schedule changes, delays, cancellations). Critical for partner carrier customer service operations.']
  passenger_count varchar [note: 'Number of passengers booked on this specific codeshare flight number for this flight leg execution. Used for revenue allocation and capacity management.']
  prorate_calculation_basis varchar [note: 'The method or formula used to calculate revenue allocation for this specific flight execution (e.g., IATA standard prorate, special prorate agreement percentage, block-space fixed amount). Documents how revenue_share_amount was derived.']
  revenue_share_amount decimal(12,2) [note: 'The calculated revenue amount allocated to the partner carrier for this specific flight leg execution under the partnership agreement terms. Used for settlement reconciliation.']
  seat_allocation_count varchar [note: 'Number of seats allocated to the marketing carrier for this specific flight execution under block-space agreements. Null for free-flow codeshare arrangements.']
  settlement_status varchar [note: 'Current status of revenue settlement processing for this codeshare flight execution. Tracks lifecycle from initial calculation through final settlement between carriers.']

  Note: 'This association product represents the operational execution of a codeshare agreement on a specific flight leg. It captures the commercial partnership arrangement applied to each operated flight, including the marketing carrier\'s flight number used, revenue allocation calculations, settlement tracking, and partner notification status. Each record links one flight_leg to one route_partnership agreement with attributes that exist only in the context of this specific codeshare execution instance.. Existence Justification: In airline codeshare operations, a single flight leg can be marketed under multiple partner carrier flight numbers simultaneously (e.g., UA123 operated by United is also sold as LH7500 by Lufthansa and AC9876 by Air Canada under different partnership agreements). Conversely, each route partnership agreement covers hundreds or thousands of flight legs over time. The business actively manages each codeshare execution instance with specific revenue calculations, settlement tracking, passenger counts, and partner notifications that belong to neither the flight leg nor the partnership agreement alone.'
}

Table passenger.profile {
  profile_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for profile']
  ffp_member_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the loyalty program membership record for this passenger. Links passenger profile to their FFP account for tier status and mileage accrual.']
  preferred_city_pair_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to route.city_pair. Business justification: Route development and network planning teams analyze passenger home/preferred city pairs to identify underserved markets and optimize route networks. Marketing uses this for targeted route launch camp']
  accessibility_requirements varchar [note: 'Special assistance or accessibility needs for passengers with disabilities or reduced mobility. Free-text field capturing wheelchair requirements, visual/hearing impairments, service animal needs, medical equipment, etc. Detailed SSR codes stored in separate SSR product.']
  completeness_score decimal(5,2) [note: 'Calculated percentage score (0.00 to 100.00) indicating the completeness of the passenger profile based on presence of key fields (contact details, travel documents, preferences, loyalty linkage). Used for data quality monitoring and personalization readiness.']
  country_of_residence varchar [note: 'Country where the passenger permanently resides, represented as ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 country code. Used for customs declarations and passenger segmentation analytics.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the passenger profile was first created in the system. Immutable audit field for profile lifecycle tracking.']
  creation_source varchar [note: 'System or channel through which the passenger profile was originally created. Tracks profile origin for data quality and channel analytics. GDS=Global Distribution System (Amadeus, Sabre, Travelport).']
  customer_segment varchar [note: 'Marketing segmentation classification for the passenger based on travel behavior, spend patterns, and loyalty tier. Examples: premium_frequent_flyer, leisure_traveler, business_traveler, occasional_flyer, vip_corporate. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: premium_frequent_flyer|leisure_traveler|business_traveler|occasional_flyer|vip_corporate|group_traveler|family_traveler — promote to reference product]']
  data_sharing_consent boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the passenger has consented to sharing their personal data with airline partners, codeshare airlines, and third-party service providers. True=consented, False=not consented.']
  date_of_birth date [note: 'Date of birth of the passenger. Required for age verification, infant/child classification, unaccompanied minor processing, and compliance with TSA Secure Flight and API (Advanced Passenger Information) requirements.']
  emergency_contact_name varchar [note: 'Full name of the passenger\'s designated emergency contact person to be notified in case of travel disruptions, medical emergencies, or incidents.']
  emergency_contact_phone varchar [note: 'Phone number of the passenger\'s designated emergency contact person. E.164 international format recommended.']
  emergency_contact_relationship varchar [note: 'Relationship of the emergency contact person to the passenger (e.g., spouse, parent, child, sibling, friend).']
  first_name varchar [note: 'Legal first name (given name) of the passenger as it appears on government-issued identification documents. Must match travel documents for international travel.']
  gender varchar [note: 'Gender of the passenger as recorded in travel documents. M=Male, F=Female, X=Unspecified/Non-binary, U=Undisclosed. Required for API and TSA Secure Flight compliance.']
  known_traveler_number varchar [note: 'TSA PreCheck Known Traveler Number or equivalent trusted traveler program identifier (e.g., Global Entry, NEXUS, SENTRI). Enables expedited security screening at participating airports.']
  last_name varchar [note: 'Legal last name (surname/family name) of the passenger as it appears on government-issued identification documents. Must match travel documents for international travel.']
  marketing_opt_in boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the passenger has consented to receive marketing communications, promotional offers, and newsletters from the airline. True=opted in, False=opted out.']
  meal_preference varchar [note: 'Passenger\'s preferred meal type for in-flight catering. Standard IATA SSR meal codes (e.g., VGML=Vegetarian, KSML=Kosher, DBML=Diabetic, MOML=Muslim, HNML=Hindu). [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: VGML|KSML|DBML|MOML|HNML|AVML|BBML|BLML|CHML|FPML|GFML|LCML|LFML|LSML|NLML|ORML|PRML|SFML|SPML|VJML|VOML — promote to reference product]']
  middle_name varchar [note: 'Middle name or initial of the passenger. Optional field that may be required for certain international destinations or to match travel documents.']
  nationality varchar [note: 'Nationality of the passenger represented as ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 country code. Required for visa validation, customs processing, and API submission to destination countries.']
  passenger_type varchar [note: 'Classification of passenger by age and travel status. ADT=Adult (12+ years), CHD=Child (2-11 years), INF=Infant (under 2 years), UMN=Unaccompanied Minor. Determines fare rules, seating requirements, and service protocols.']
  preferred_language varchar [note: 'Preferred language for passenger communications, notifications, and in-flight services. ISO 639-1 two-letter language code (e.g., en, es, fr, de, zh).']
  primary_email varchar [note: 'Primary email address for passenger communications, booking confirmations, flight notifications, and digital receipts. Display-only summary field; authoritative multi-valued contact store is the contact_detail product.']
  primary_phone varchar [note: 'Primary contact phone number for passenger communications, flight alerts, and operational notifications. E.164 international format recommended. Display-only summary field; authoritative multi-valued contact store is the contact_detail product.']
  profile_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the passenger profile. Active=profile in use, Inactive=dormant profile, Suspended=temporarily blocked, Merged=consolidated into another profile, Deceased=passenger deceased per notification.']
  redress_number varchar [note: 'DHS Traveler Redress Inquiry Program (TRIP) control number issued to passengers who have been misidentified or delayed during security screening. Prevents future watchlist false positives.']
  seat_preference varchar [note: 'Passenger\'s preferred seat location on the aircraft. Used for automated seat assignment and upgrade processing.']
  suffix varchar [note: 'Name suffix such as Jr, Sr, III, etc. Used to distinguish passengers with identical names.']
  title varchar [note: 'Courtesy title or honorific for the passenger used in communications and boarding documents.']
  unaccompanied_minor_guardian_name varchar [note: 'Full name of the parent or legal guardian responsible for an unaccompanied minor passenger. Required for UMN bookings to coordinate drop-off and pick-up procedures.']
  unaccompanied_minor_guardian_phone varchar [note: 'Contact phone number for the parent or legal guardian of an unaccompanied minor passenger. Used for coordination during travel and emergency contact.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the passenger profile was last modified. Updated on any change to profile attributes, contact details, or preferences.']
  vip_indicator boolean [note: 'Flag indicating whether the passenger is designated as a VIP requiring special handling, premium services, or executive attention. True=VIP status, False=standard passenger.']

  Note: 'Core master record for every individual passenger served by the airline — the authoritative identity hub (SSOT) for the passenger domain. Stores full legal name, date of birth, gender, nationality, preferred language, passenger type (adult, child, infant, unaccompanied minor), profile creation source (Altéa PSS, web, mobile, GDS), profile status, and data consent flags. Holds a summary primary contact reference (single email, single phone) for display purposes only — the authoritative multi-valued contact store is the contact_detail product. Referenced by reservation, loyalty, and ancillary domains via passenger_id. Aligns with Amadeus Altéa passenger profile entity.'
}

Table passenger.travel_identity_document {
  travel_identity_document_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the travel identity document record. Primary key.']
  pax_profile_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the passenger who holds this travel document. Links to the passenger master record.']
  regulatory_requirement_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.regulatory_requirement. Business justification: Travel documents must comply with APIS, Secure Flight, and border authority regulations (19 CFR 122.49b, EU PNR Directive). Airlines verify document validity against regulatory mandates for passenger ']
  apis_response_code varchar [note: 'Response code received from border control authority after APIS transmission. OK=cleared to board; INHIBITED=passenger denied boarding; CONDITIONAL=manual review required. Codes vary by country.']
  apis_transmission_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the document data was transmitted to border control authorities. Used to prove compliance with advance notification requirements (typically 72 hours before departure for USA).']
  apis_transmitted_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this documents data has been successfully transmitted to destination country border control authorities via APIS. Required for flights to USA, Canada, UK, EU, and many other jurisdictions.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this document record was first created in the system. Part of audit trail for regulatory compliance and data lineage.']
  date_of_birth date [note: 'Date of birth of the document holder as printed on the travel document. Required for APIS reporting, secure flight screening (TSA), and age-based service eligibility (unaccompanied minor, senior fares).']
  document_image_url varchar [note: 'Secure storage location (URI) of the scanned or photographed document image. Used for manual verification, fraud investigation, and regulatory compliance. Access restricted to authorized personnel only.']
  document_number varchar [note: 'Unique alphanumeric identifier printed on the travel document. This is the primary identifier used for APIS (Advance Passenger Information System) reporting and border control verification.']
  document_source varchar [note: 'Method by which the document data was captured. Manual_entry=agent typed data; OCR_scan=automated optical character recognition from image; Mobile_app=passenger self-service via mobile; GDS=received from Global Distribution System; API_integration=received from partner system.']
  document_type varchar [note: 'Type of travel identity document. Passport is the most common for international travel; national ID cards are accepted within certain regions (e.g., EU, Schengen); visas grant entry permission; residence permits prove legal residency; refugee travel documents and stateless person documents are issued under international conventions.']
  expiry_date date [note: 'Date the travel document expires. Most countries require passport validity of 6 months beyond the intended stay. System must alert if document expires before return travel date.']
  gender varchar [note: 'Gender marker as printed on the travel document. M=Male, F=Female, X=Unspecified/Non-binary (per ICAO 2018 guidelines). Required for APIS and secure flight reporting.']
  given_names varchar [note: 'First and middle names as printed on the travel document. Must match PNR for secure flight and APIS compliance.']
  is_primary_document boolean [note: 'Flag indicating whether this is the passengers primary travel document for the current booking. A passenger may hold multiple documents (e.g., passport + national ID), but only one is designated primary for APIS reporting.']
  issue_date date [note: 'Date the travel document was issued by the authority. Used to calculate document age and assess fraud risk (newly issued documents before travel may trigger additional screening).']
  issuing_authority varchar [note: 'Name of the government agency or authority that issued the document (e.g., \'U.S. Department of State\', \'UK Home Office\'). Used for document verification and fraud detection.']
  issuing_country_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 code of the country or authority that issued the document. Critical for APIS reporting and visa waiver program eligibility determination.']
  known_traveller_number varchar [note: 'Trusted traveller program membership number (e.g., TSA PreCheck, Global Entry, NEXUS, SENTRI, APEC Business Travel Card). Enables expedited security screening and border clearance. Stored for automatic inclusion in secure flight data.']
  known_traveller_program varchar [note: 'Name of the trusted traveller program associated with the known traveller number. Determines which expedited lanes and services the passenger is eligible for.']
  last_used_flight_date date [note: 'Date of the most recent flight on which this document was used for travel. Helps identify stale or outdated document records and supports passenger profile analytics.']
  mrz_line1 varchar [note: 'First line of the machine-readable zone at the bottom of the document. Contains document type, issuing country, and surname/given names in standardized format. Used for automated document scanning and APIS transmission.']
  mrz_line2 varchar [note: 'Second line of the machine-readable zone. Contains document number, nationality, date of birth, gender, expiry date, and check digits. Enables rapid data capture via optical character recognition (OCR) at check-in kiosks and border control.']
  mrz_line3 varchar [note: 'Third line of the machine-readable zone (present on some document types such as ID cards). Contains additional personal data and check digits.']
  nationality_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 code representing the nationality of the document holder. May differ from issuing country for dual nationals or residents. Required for APIS and visa requirement checks.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text field for agent or system notes regarding the document. May include verification issues, special handling instructions, or discrepancies requiring follow-up.']
  place_of_birth varchar [note: 'City and country of birth as printed on the travel document. Required for certain APIS destinations (e.g., USA, Canada) and visa applications.']
  redress_number varchar [note: 'DHS Traveler Redress Inquiry Program (TRIP) control number issued to passengers who have been misidentified as a security threat. Prevents false positive watchlist matches during secure flight screening.']
  secure_flight_status varchar [note: 'TSA Secure Flight screening result for USA-bound flights. Not_submitted=data not yet sent; Cleared=passenger approved for boarding; Inhibited=passenger denied boarding (no-fly list match); Conditional=requires additional screening or documentation.']
  surname varchar [note: 'Family name or surname as printed on the travel document. Must match exactly with booking PNR (Passenger Name Record) for boarding pass issuance and border clearance.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this document record was last modified. Tracks changes to document data, verification status, or APIS transmission results.']
  verification_status varchar [note: 'Current verification state of the document. Not_verified=newly entered, pending check; Verified=passed authenticity and expiry checks; Failed=document rejected (invalid format, fraudulent); Expired=document past expiry date; Flagged=requires manual review by security or compliance team.']
  verification_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the document verification status was last updated. Used for audit trail and compliance reporting.']
  verified_by varchar [note: 'User ID or system identifier of the agent or automated process that performed the verification. Supports audit trail and accountability for document acceptance decisions.']
  visa_required_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether a visa is required for the passengers itinerary based on nationality, destination, and transit countries. Calculated using IATA Timatic rules. If true, a separate visa document record must exist.']

  Note: 'Stores all identity and travel documents associated with a passenger — passports, national ID cards, visas, residence permits, and known traveller numbers (e.g., TSA PreCheck, Global Entry, NEXUS). Captures document number, issuing country (ICAO alpha-2), document type, issue date, expiry date, machine-readable zone (MRZ) data, and document verification status. Supports APIS (Advance Passenger Information System) regulatory reporting and border control compliance. A passenger may hold multiple documents across multiple nationalities.'
}

Table passenger.contact_detail {
  contact_detail_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the contact detail record. Primary key.']
  pax_profile_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the passenger who owns this contact detail. Links to the passenger profile in the passenger domain.']
  address_line_1 varchar [note: 'The primary street address line (street number, street name, building). Part of the postal address for the passenger.']
  address_line_2 varchar [note: 'Secondary address line for apartment, suite, unit, or building details. Optional component of postal address.']
  city varchar [note: 'The city or municipality name for the postal address.']
  contact_channel varchar [note: 'The communication channel or medium through which this contact method operates (voice, SMS, email, postal, WhatsApp, app notification). Distinct from contact_type which defines the category.']
  contact_status varchar [note: 'The current lifecycle status of this contact detail: active (in use), inactive (not in use), bounced (delivery failed), invalid (format error), pending_verification (awaiting confirmation).']
  contact_type varchar [note: 'The category of contact channel: email, mobile phone, home phone, work phone, postal address, or emergency contact.']
  country_code varchar [note: 'The 3-letter ISO country code for the contact address or phone number (e.g., USA, GBR, CAN). Aligns with IATA and ICAO standards.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The timestamp when this contact detail record was first created in the system. Supports audit trail and data lineage.']
  effective_from_date date [note: 'The date from which this contact detail becomes active and valid for use. Supports temporal contact management.']
  effective_to_date date [note: 'The date until which this contact detail remains active and valid. Null indicates no expiration. Supports temporal contact management and historical tracking.']
  email_address varchar [note: 'The email address for this contact record. Used for e-ticket delivery, booking confirmations, IROP notifications, and marketing communications per DOT consumer communication requirements.']
  emergency_contact_name varchar [note: 'The full name of the emergency contact person. Populated only when contact_type is emergency. Used for incident response and passenger welfare.']
  emergency_contact_relationship varchar [note: 'The relationship of the emergency contact to the passenger (spouse, parent, child, sibling, friend, other). Populated only when contact_type is emergency.']
  is_preferred boolean [note: 'Flag indicating whether this is the passengers preferred contact method for operational and marketing communications. Only one contact per type should be marked preferred.']
  is_verified boolean [note: 'Flag indicating whether this contact detail has been verified (e.g., email confirmed via link, phone verified via OTP). Supports data quality and fraud prevention.']
  last_used_date date [note: 'The date when this contact detail was last used for communication (email sent, SMS delivered, call made). Supports contact data quality and hygiene.']
  opt_in_date date [note: 'The date when the passenger provided consent for communications via this contact channel. Required for audit and compliance purposes.']
  opt_in_marketing boolean [note: 'Flag indicating whether the passenger has consented to receive marketing communications via this contact channel. Required for GDPR and CAN-SPAM compliance.']
  opt_in_operational boolean [note: 'Flag indicating whether the passenger has consented to receive operational communications (flight status, IROP notifications, gate changes) via this contact channel. Typically defaults to true for service delivery.']
  opt_out_date date [note: 'The date when the passenger withdrew consent for communications via this contact channel. Supports unsubscribe tracking and compliance.']
  phone_country_code varchar [note: 'The international dialing code for the phone number (e.g., +1 for USA, +44 for UK). Supports global passenger contact management.']
  phone_number varchar [note: 'The telephone number in E.164 international format. Includes mobile, home, and work phone numbers. Used for operational notifications, IROP proactive communication, and SSR coordination.']
  postal_code varchar [note: 'The postal or ZIP code for the address. Format varies by country (e.g., 5-digit ZIP in USA, alphanumeric in Canada/UK).']
  source_system varchar [note: 'The system or channel through which this contact detail was originally captured (Altéa PSS, mobile app, web booking, call center, airport kiosk, GDS, partner API). Supports data lineage and quality assessment. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: altea_pss|mobile_app|web_booking|call_center|airport_kiosk|gds|partner_api — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  state_province varchar [note: 'The state, province, or region for the postal address. Supports jurisdiction-specific compliance and tax reporting.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The timestamp when this contact detail record was last modified. Supports audit trail and change tracking.']
  verification_date date [note: 'The date when this contact detail was last verified by the passenger. Supports contact data quality management.']

  Note: 'All contact channels for a passenger — email addresses, phone numbers (mobile, home, work), postal addresses, and emergency contacts. Captures contact type, channel, preferred contact flag, opt-in/opt-out status for marketing and operational communications, verification status, and effective date range. Supports IROP proactive notification and DOT consumer communication requirements. A passenger may have multiple active contact records across channels. Distinct from pax_profile (which holds a summary contact reference) — this is the detailed, multi-valued contact store.'
}

Table passenger.travel_preference {
  travel_preference_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the travel preference record. Primary key.']
  pax_profile_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the passenger who owns this travel preference profile. Links to the passenger master record in Amadeus Altéa PSS.']
  preferred_departure_airport_station_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to airport.station. Business justification: Travel preferences drive booking search personalization, corporate travel policy enforcement, and loyalty program targeting by home airport. Airlines use preferred departure station to optimize route ']
  preferred_route_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to route.route. Business justification: Revenue management and personalization engines use preferred route data to customize fare offers and upgrade opportunities. Loyalty programs track route preferences for targeted promotions. Critical f']
  accessibility_requirement_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the passenger has accessibility or special assistance requirements that should be considered during booking and service delivery. When true, detailed SSR records (WCHR, WCHS, WCHC, DEAF, BLIND, etc.) should be reviewed.']
  active_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this preference is currently active and should be applied to future bookings. Inactive preferences are retained for historical analysis but not used in operational systems.']
  alliance_preference varchar [note: 'Passenger\'s preferred airline alliance for travel. Influences interline and codeshare booking recommendations to maximize FFP mileage accrual and benefits.']
  ancillary_preference varchar [note: 'Passenger\'s preferred ancillary services for automatic offer targeting: extra baggage allowance, lounge access, priority boarding, fast-track security, in-flight wifi. Supports personalized EMD (Electronic Miscellaneous Document) generation.']
  boarding_pass_format_preference varchar [note: 'Passenger\'s preferred format for receiving boarding passes: mobile wallet, email PDF, print-at-home, or no preference. Supports digital transformation and sustainability initiatives.']
  booking_channel_preference varchar [note: 'Passenger\'s preferred channel for making flight reservations. Used for channel-specific marketing and user experience optimization.']
  cabin_class_preference varchar [note: 'Passenger\'s preferred cabin class for travel. Used by revenue management for personalized offer construction and upsell targeting.']
  check_in_preference varchar [note: 'Passenger\'s preferred method for flight check-in. Used to send targeted reminders and optimize ground handling resource allocation.']
  communication_channel_preference varchar [note: 'Passenger\'s preferred channel for receiving airline communications (booking confirmations, flight status updates, promotional offers). Supports GDPR consent management.']
  connection_time_preference varchar [note: 'Passenger\'s preferred connection time for multi-leg journeys: minimum (MCT only), short (MCT + 30 min), medium (1-2 hours), long (2+ hours for lounge time). Influences itinerary construction and IROP reprotection.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this travel preference record was first created in the system. Used for audit trail and data lineage tracking.']
  data_source_system varchar [note: 'Source system that created or last updated this preference record. Used for data lineage tracking and system-of-record resolution. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: altea_pss|loyalty_system|mobile_app|website|gds|call_center|import — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  direct_flight_preference_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the passenger strongly prefers direct (non-stop) flights over connecting itineraries. Used for search result ranking and offer personalization.']
  effective_from_date date [note: 'Date from which this preference becomes active and applicable to bookings. Supports time-bound preference management (e.g., seasonal preferences, temporary dietary restrictions).']
  effective_to_date date [note: 'Date until which this preference remains active. Nullable for open-ended preferences. Used for automatic preference expiration and profile hygiene.']
  frequent_flyer_tier_preference varchar [note: 'Passenger\'s target or preferred FFP tier level. Used for personalized upgrade offers and tier-based service customization. Links to loyalty tier status for benefit entitlement.']
  infant_travel_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the passenger frequently travels with an infant (under 2 years). When true, bulkhead seating with bassinet availability is prioritized, and infant-specific SSRs are auto-generated.']
  language_preference_code varchar [note: 'ISO 639-1 two-letter language code for the passenger\'s preferred communication language. Used for personalized communications, in-flight service, and customer service interactions.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this travel preference record was last updated. Used for change tracking and data synchronization with Amadeus Altéa PSS.']
  last_used_date date [note: 'Date when this preference was last applied to a booking or SSR generation. Used for preference relevance scoring and profile hygiene.']
  meal_preference_code varchar [note: 'IATA standard 4-letter code for passenger\'s standing dietary meal preference (e.g., VGML=Vegetarian Vegan, KSML=Kosher, DBML=Diabetic, MOML=Muslim, AVML=Asian Vegetarian, HNML=Hindu, LCML=Low Calorie, NLML=Low Lactose, GFML=Gluten Free, BLML=Bland). This standing preference is used to auto-generate per-flight SSR meal requests.']
  notification_preference varchar [note: 'Passenger\'s preference for the types of notifications to receive: all (flight status, booking confirmations, promotions), flight_status_only (operational updates), booking_only (transactional), none (opt-out).']
  pet_travel_preference_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the passenger frequently travels with pets (in-cabin or cargo). Used to auto-generate PETC (pet in cabin) or AVIH (animal in hold) SSRs and ensure compliance with pet travel regulations.']
  preference_confidence_score decimal(5,2) [note: 'Confidence score (0.00 to 100.00) indicating the reliability of inferred preferences based on historical booking patterns and ML model predictions. Higher scores indicate stronger preference signals.']
  preference_priority varchar [note: 'Priority ranking (1=highest) for this preference when multiple preferences of the same type exist. Used to resolve conflicts during automated SSR generation and seat assignment.']
  preference_source varchar [note: 'Origin of the preference data: stated (explicitly provided by passenger), inferred (derived from booking history and behavior), imported (from partner FFP or GDS profile), default (system-assigned).']
  preference_type varchar [note: 'Category of travel preference: seat (seating position), cabin (class of service), meal (dietary), airport (departure preference), airline (carrier preference), communication (channel preference), language (communication language). [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: seat|cabin|meal|airport|airline|communication|language — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
