  preferred_airline_code varchar [note: 'IATA 2-character airline designator code for the passenger\'s preferred carrier. Used for codeshare and interline booking prioritization.']
  seat_location_preference varchar [note: 'Passenger\'s preferred location within the cabin (front, middle, rear sections, exit row for extra legroom, bulkhead for bassinet or legroom). Influences automated seat assignment logic.']
  seat_position_preference varchar [note: 'Passenger\'s preferred seat position within the aircraft cabin. Used by Altéa PSS for automated seat assignment and SSR generation.']
  time_of_day_preference varchar [note: 'Passenger\'s preferred departure time of day. Used for personalized flight search results and schedule optimization.']
  travel_companion_preference varchar [note: 'Passenger\'s typical travel companion profile (solo traveler, family with children, business colleagues, group travel). Used for personalized service delivery and seating arrangements.']
  upgrade_preference_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the passenger prefers to be automatically considered for cabin upgrades when available (using miles, revenue, or operational upgrades). Used by revenue management for upgrade inventory allocation.']

  Note: 'Captures a passenger\'s stated and inferred travel preferences stored against their profile — seat preferences (window, aisle, exit row, bulkhead), cabin class preference (economy, premium economy, business, first), preferred departure airports, preferred airlines and alliance partners, language preference, and communication channel preferences. Meal preference codes (SPML: VGML, KSML, DBML, MOML, etc.) are stored here as the passenger\'s standing dietary preference; per-flight meal SSR instances are generated from this record into ssr_record. Preferences are used by Altéa PSS for automated SSR generation and by revenue management for personalised offer construction.'
}

Table passenger.ssr_record {
  ssr_record_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the SSR record instance. Primary key.']
  consumer_protection_case_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.consumer_protection_case. Business justification: SSR failures (wheelchair, medical oxygen, service animal) trigger consumer protection cases under DOT Part 382 and EC 1107/2006. Airlines track which accessibility SSRs resulted in regulatory complain']
  employee_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the booking agent, customer service representative, or system user who created this SSR record.']
  ffp_member_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to loyalty.ffp_member. Business justification: Links special service requests to loyalty status for benefit fulfillment validation. Airlines must verify tier-based entitlements (complimentary lounge access, priority boarding, extra baggage) when p']
  flight_inventory_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to inventory.flight_inventory. Business justification: SSRs (wheelchairs, meals, oxygen) are fulfilled on specific flight inventory instances. Operations must track which inventory leg delivers each service for catering, ground handling, and compliance. C']
  flight_leg_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the specific flight leg for which this SSR applies. Nullable if SSR applies to entire journey.']
  handling_crew_member_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to crew.crew_member. Business justification: Airlines track which cabin crew member fulfilled special service requests (wheelchair assistance, medical needs, special meals) for quality assurance, training feedback, and incident investigation. Op']
  pax_profile_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the passenger profile to whom this SSR is linked.']
  pnr_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the PNR booking under which this SSR was requested.']
  revenue_emd_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to revenue.emd. Business justification: SSRs (special service requests like meals, wheelchairs, extra baggage) are sold as ancillary services and documented via EMDs. Links service delivery records to revenue recognition for ancillary billi']
  route_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to route.route. Business justification: SSR fulfillment planning requires route-level context (wheelchair assistance varies by airport infrastructure, meal catering by route length/type). Operations teams pre-position resources by route. Co']
  service_provider_vendor_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to procurement.vendor. Business justification: Special service requests (wheelchairs, medical oxygen, special meals, unaccompanied minor escort) are fulfilled by contracted vendors tracked in procurement. Airlines must link SSR fulfillment to vend']
  advance_notice_hours varchar [note: 'Minimum number of hours before departure that this SSR must be requested. Nullable if no advance notice required.']
  booking_channel varchar [note: 'Channel through which this SSR was requested: web, mobile_app, call_center, airport_counter, travel_agent, GDS, API. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: web|mobile_app|call_center|airport_counter|travel_agent|gds|api — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  cancellation_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the SSR was cancelled. Nullable if not cancelled.']
  charge_amount decimal(10,2) [note: 'Monetary amount charged for this SSR service. Nullable if not chargeable or charge not yet determined.']
  charge_currency_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for the SSR charge amount (e.g., USD, EUR, GBP).']
  confirmation_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the SSR was confirmed by the airline or service provider. Nullable if not yet confirmed.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'System timestamp when this SSR record was first created in the database.']
  equipment_required varchar [note: 'Description of special equipment needed to fulfill this SSR (e.g., onboard wheelchair, oxygen concentrator, stretcher, incubator).']
  fulfillment_notes varchar [note: 'Free-text notes recorded by ground staff or cabin crew documenting how the SSR was fulfilled, any issues encountered, or passenger feedback.']
  handling_instructions varchar [note: 'Operational instructions for ground staff, cabin crew, or service providers on how to fulfill this SSR, including equipment needs, timing, and coordination requirements.']
  is_chargeable boolean [note: 'Boolean flag indicating whether this SSR incurs a charge to the passenger (true) or is provided as a complimentary service (false).']
  is_interline boolean [note: 'Boolean flag indicating whether this SSR applies to an interline segment operated by a partner carrier (true) or an own-metal flight (false).']
  is_standing_preference boolean [note: 'Boolean flag indicating whether this SSR was auto-populated from the passenger\'s standing travel preferences (true) or requested ad-hoc for this booking (false).']
  meal_code varchar [note: 'Four-letter IATA meal code for dietary SSRs (e.g., VGML for vegetarian, KSML for kosher, DBML for diabetic). Applicable only when ssr_code is SPML.']
  medical_clearance_status varchar [note: 'Status of medical clearance for SSRs requiring medical approval. Values: not_required, pending, approved, denied.']
  operating_carrier_code varchar [note: 'Two-character IATA airline code of the carrier operating the flight for which this SSR applies. Relevant for interline SSRs.']
  request_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the SSR was initially requested by the passenger or booking agent.']
  requires_advance_notice boolean [note: 'Boolean flag indicating whether this SSR type requires advance notice to the airline (true) or can be requested at check-in or boarding (false).']
  requires_medical_clearance boolean [note: 'Boolean flag indicating whether this SSR requires medical clearance or MEDA form approval before confirmation (true for MEDA, OXYG, STCR SSRs).']
  service_delivery_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the SSR service was actually delivered or completed. Nullable if not yet delivered.']
  ssr_category varchar [note: 'High-level categorization of the SSR type for reporting and operational grouping (accessibility, medical, dietary, unaccompanied minor, pet transport, special baggage, other). [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: accessibility|medical|dietary|unaccompanied_minor|pet_transport|special_baggage|other — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  ssr_code varchar [note: 'Four-letter IATA SSR code identifying the type of service requested (e.g., WCHR, WCHC, BLND, DEAF, MEDA, OXYG, SPML, UMNR, PETC, DPNA).']
  ssr_description varchar [note: 'Free-text description providing additional details about the SSR, including handling instructions, meal preferences, medical conditions, or special equipment requirements.']
  ssr_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the SSR indicating whether the service has been requested, confirmed by the airline, waitlisted, cancelled, denied, or completed.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'System timestamp when this SSR record was last modified.']
  wheelchair_type varchar [note: 'Specific wheelchair assistance level for accessibility SSRs: WCHR (can walk stairs), WCHS (cannot walk stairs), WCHC (immobile, requires onboard wheelchair). Applicable only for wheelchair SSR codes.']

  Note: 'Special Service Request (SSR) records linked to a passenger profile — capturing per-flight or per-booking service instances for accessibility needs (WCHR, WCHC, BLND, DEAF), medical requirements (MEDA, OXYG), dietary meal requests (SPML codes instantiated from travel_preference standing preferences or ad-hoc), unaccompanied minor (UMNR) service, and other service codes defined by IATA SSR taxonomy. Each SSR record carries the IATA SSR code, free-text description, service status (requested, confirmed, cancelled), applicable flight scope, and handling instructions for ground and cabin crew. Distinct from travel_preference (standing preferences) and accessibility_profile (persistent PRM master) — this is the per-flight service instance.'
}

Table passenger.loyalty_linkage {
  loyalty_linkage_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the loyalty program linkage record. Primary key.']
  ffp_member_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to loyalty.ffp_member. Business justification: Enables real-time loyalty benefit validation during check-in, boarding, and service delivery. Airlines must resolve member number to full loyalty profile for tier recognition, lounge access, baggage a']
  pax_profile_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the passenger profile to whom this loyalty program membership is linked.']
  alliance_code varchar [note: 'The global airline alliance to which the FFP program belongs (Star Alliance, oneworld, SkyTeam). None indicates the program is not part of a major alliance or is the airline\'s own non-alliance program.']
  auto_recognition_enabled_flag boolean [note: 'Boolean flag indicating whether automatic tier recognition and benefit application is enabled for this linkage at check-in, boarding, and service touchpoints. True enables automatic recognition; False requires manual override.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The timestamp when this loyalty linkage record was first created in the system. Immutable audit field.']
  data_quality_score decimal(5,2) [note: 'Numeric score (0.00 to 100.00) representing the completeness and accuracy of the loyalty linkage data. Higher scores indicate higher confidence in the linkage validity. Used for data stewardship and cleansing prioritization.']
  external_reference_code varchar [note: 'The unique identifier or key used by the external loyalty system or partner FFP to reference this linkage. Used for API calls, reconciliation, and cross-system traceability.']
  ffp_program_code varchar [note: 'IATA two-letter or three-character airline code identifying the loyalty program (e.g., UA for United MileagePlus, BA for British Airways Executive Club, LH for Lufthansa Miles & More). Includes own airline and partner programs within alliances (Star Alliance, oneworld, SkyTeam).']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The timestamp when this loyalty linkage record was last modified. Updated on every change to the record.']
  last_sync_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The timestamp when this loyalty linkage record was last synchronized with the source loyalty system (e.g., Oracle Loyalty Cloud, partner FFP API). Used for data freshness monitoring and incremental load orchestration.']
  last_verified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The timestamp when the linkage and tier status were last verified or synchronized with the authoritative loyalty system. Used to determine data freshness and trigger re-verification workflows.']
  linkage_source_system varchar [note: 'The operational system or channel from which this loyalty linkage was created or last updated (e.g., Amadeus Altéa PSS, Oracle Loyalty Cloud, Mobile App, Web Portal, Customer Service, GDS).']
  linkage_status varchar [note: 'Current status of the loyalty program linkage. Active indicates the linkage is operational and tier benefits apply. Suspended indicates temporary hold (e.g., fraud investigation). Unlinked indicates the passenger has removed the association. Pending verification indicates awaiting confirmation from the FFP. Expired indicates the membership has lapsed. Merged indicates the membership was consolidated into another record.']
  linkage_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the loyalty linkage. Primary indicates the passenger\'s main FFP membership with the airline. Secondary indicates an additional membership. Partner indicates a linked membership with a codeshare or alliance partner. Reciprocal indicates a mutual recognition agreement membership.']
  linked_date date [note: 'The date on which this loyalty program membership was first linked to the passenger profile.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text field for customer service agents or data stewards to record additional context, exceptions, or special handling instructions related to this loyalty linkage (e.g., VIP handling, disputed tier status, manual override reasons).']
  reciprocal_benefit_eligible_flag boolean [note: 'Boolean flag indicating whether this linkage qualifies for reciprocal benefits under alliance or partnership agreements (e.g., lounge access, priority boarding on partner airlines). True indicates eligibility; False indicates ineligible or restricted.']
  recognition_priority varchar [note: 'Numeric priority order for tier recognition when a passenger has multiple linked FFP memberships. Lower numbers indicate higher priority (1 = highest). Used at check-in and boarding to determine which tier benefits to apply.']
  sync_status varchar [note: 'Status of the most recent synchronization attempt with the source loyalty system. Success indicates data is current. Failed indicates synchronization error. Pending indicates sync in progress. Skipped indicates sync was bypassed. Partial indicates incomplete data received.']
  tier_effective_date date [note: 'The date on which the current tier status became effective for this membership.']
  tier_expiry_date date [note: 'The date on which the current tier status expires. Null if the tier does not expire or is lifetime status.']
  unlinked_date date [note: 'The date on which this loyalty program membership was unlinked from the passenger profile. Null if the linkage is still active or has never been unlinked.']
  unlinked_reason_code varchar [note: 'The reason code explaining why the loyalty linkage was unlinked. Null if the linkage has never been unlinked.']

  Note: 'Association entity linking a passenger profile to one or more Frequent Flyer Program (FFP) memberships — both the airlines own programme and partner airline programmes (Star Alliance, oneworld, SkyTeam). Stores FFP number, programme code (IATA carrier code), tier status at linkage time, tier expiry, linkage status (active, suspended, unlinked), and source system. This is a reference pointer only — loyalty master data (accrual, redemption, tier calculation) lives in the loyalty domain. Enables tier recognition at check-in and boarding without SSOT duplication.'
}

Table passenger.traveller_segment {
  traveller_segment_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for traveller_segment']
  ffp_member_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to loyalty.ffp_member. Business justification: Enables loyalty-driven segmentation strategy and targeted promotions. Airlines use passenger behavioral segments to design tier qualification campaigns, bonus mile offers, and retention programs—requi']
  pax_profile_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the passenger being segmented. Links to the passenger master record in the passenger domain.']
  preferred_city_pair_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to route.city_pair. Business justification: Segmentation models analyze route preferences by customer type (business travelers prefer hub-to-hub, leisure prefer resort destinations). Marketing campaigns target segments with city-pair-specific o']
  ancillary_propensity_score decimal(5,4) [note: 'Propensity score (0.0000 to 1.0000) indicating likelihood of purchasing ancillary services (seat selection, baggage, meals, lounge access). Used for upsell targeting.']
  assignment_date date [note: 'Date when the passenger was assigned to this segment. Used for segment tenure analysis and lifecycle tracking.']
  booking_channel_preference varchar [note: 'Preferred or most frequently used booking channel: direct web, mobile app, GDS (Global Distribution System), travel agent, call center, or mixed.']
  cabin_preference varchar [note: 'Predominant cabin class preference observed or predicted for this passenger segment: first, business, premium economy, economy, or mixed (no clear preference).']
  churn_risk_score decimal(5,4) [note: 'Predictive score (0.0000 to 1.0000) indicating the likelihood of passenger churn (switching to competitor or ceasing travel). Higher score indicates higher risk.']
  confidence_score decimal(5,4) [note: 'Statistical confidence or probability score (0.0000 to 1.0000) indicating the model\'s certainty in this segment assignment. Applicable for ML-derived segments; null for rule-based assignments.']
  corporate_affiliation_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the passenger is affiliated with a corporate travel program or business account (true) or is a leisure/individual traveller (false).']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this segment assignment record was first created in the system. Used for audit trail and data lineage.']
  effective_from_date date [note: 'Date from which this segment assignment becomes active and should be used for targeting, pricing, and personalization decisions.']
  effective_to_date date [note: 'Date until which this segment assignment remains valid. Nullable for open-ended assignments. Used for segment expiry and refresh scheduling.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this segment assignment record was last updated. Used for change tracking and audit purposes.']
  last_review_date date [note: 'Date when this segment assignment was last reviewed or refreshed by the segmentation process. Used for data quality monitoring and refresh scheduling.']
  lifetime_value_tier varchar [note: 'Lifetime value tier classification derived from historical and predicted revenue: platinum (highest value), gold, silver, bronze, or standard. Distinct from loyalty program tier.']
  loyalty_engagement_level varchar [note: 'Level of engagement with the airline\'s FFP (Frequent Flyer Program): highly engaged (active accrual and redemption), engaged, moderately engaged, low engagement, or not enrolled.']
  next_review_date date [note: 'Scheduled date for the next review or refresh of this segment assignment. Ensures segments remain current and accurate.']
  price_sensitivity_indicator varchar [note: 'Assessed price sensitivity level of the passenger segment: very high (highly price-sensitive, seeks lowest fares), high, medium, low, or very low (price-insensitive, values convenience/service).']
  revenue_contribution_score decimal(15,2) [note: 'Estimated or actual revenue contribution value associated with this passenger segment, used for value-based segmentation and prioritization. Currency in airline\'s base currency.']
  route_preference_type varchar [note: 'Predominant route type preference: domestic, short-haul international, long-haul international, or mixed. Used for network planning and targeted offers.']
  segment_category varchar [note: 'High-level classification of the segment type: value-based, behavior-based, lifecycle stage, propensity model, demographic, or geographic segmentation.']
  segment_code varchar [note: 'Unique code identifying the segment type (e.g., HVT for high-value traveller, CFF for corporate frequent flyer, LSR for leisure traveller, PSN for price-sensitive, PRM for premium loyalist, LPS for lapsed traveller).']
  segment_description varchar [note: 'Detailed textual description of the segment characteristics, targeting criteria, and business rationale. Used for segment documentation and stakeholder communication.']
  segment_model_version varchar [note: 'Version identifier of the segmentation model or rule set used to assign this segment. Enables tracking of model evolution and reprocessing.']
  segment_name varchar [note: 'Human-readable name of the passenger segment (e.g., High-Value Traveller, Corporate Frequent Flyer, Leisure Traveller, Price-Sensitive, Premium Loyalist, Lapsed Traveller).']
  segment_notes varchar [note: 'Free-text field for additional notes, comments, or context about this segment assignment. Used for manual overrides, special cases, or analyst annotations.']
  segment_priority varchar [note: 'Numeric priority ranking (1=highest) used when a passenger belongs to multiple segments simultaneously. Determines which segment takes precedence for targeting and personalization.']
  segment_source varchar [note: 'Origin of the segment assignment: rules engine (business rule-based), ML model (machine learning algorithm), manual (analyst-assigned), third-party (external data provider), or hybrid (combination of methods).']
  segment_source_system varchar [note: 'Name or identifier of the system or model that generated the segment assignment (e.g., Sabre AirVision Revenue Management, internal propensity model, CRM segmentation engine).']
  segment_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the segment assignment: active (in use), inactive (not currently applied), expired (past effective date), suspended (temporarily disabled), or pending (awaiting activation).']
  targeting_eligible_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this segment assignment makes the passenger eligible for targeted marketing campaigns and personalized offers (true) or should be excluded (false) due to opt-out or regulatory restrictions.']
  travel_frequency_band varchar [note: 'Classification of passenger travel frequency based on flight count over a defined period: very high (frequent business traveller), high, medium, low, very low, or inactive (lapsed).']

  Note: 'Passenger segmentation and marketing classification records — assigns passengers to analytically derived or rule-based segments such as high-value traveller, corporate frequent flyer, leisure traveller, price-sensitive, premium loyalist, or lapsed traveller. Captures segment code, segment name, segment source (rules engine, ML model, manual), assignment date, expiry date, and confidence score where applicable. Used by revenue management for personalised pricing, by loyalty for targeted offers, and by ancillary for upsell propensity. Distinct from loyalty tier — this is a commercial segmentation layer.'
}

Table passenger.consent {
  consent_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for consent']
  data_subject_request_id varchar [note: 'Reference to a data subject access request (DSAR), right-to-erasure request, or data portability request that triggered this consent record update. Null if the consent was not updated as part of a formal data subject request.']
  pax_profile_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the passenger profile to whom this consent record belongs. Links to the authoritative passenger identity in the passenger domain.']
  regulatory_requirement_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.regulatory_requirement. Business justification: Consent management must comply with GDPR Article 7, CCPA Section 1798.120, and other privacy regulations. Airlines link each consent record to the governing regulatory requirement to demonstrate lawfu']
  audit_trail varchar [note: 'A structured log or reference to detailed audit records capturing all consent lifecycle events (granted, modified, withdrawn, expired). Supports regulatory audit and dispute resolution.']
  channel varchar [note: 'The channel or touchpoint through which the passenger provided or updated their consent. Critical for audit trail and consent validity verification.']
  consent_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the consent. Indicates whether the passenger has granted, withdrawn, or is pending decision on the consent request. Expired indicates consent has passed its validity period.']
  consent_type varchar [note: 'The category of consent granted or withheld by the passenger. Defines the specific data processing activity for which consent is required under GDPR, CCPA, PDPA, or other applicable data protection regulations.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when this consent record was first created in the system. Recorded in ISO 8601 format (yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX). Distinct from consent_granted_timestamp, which reflects the passenger\'s action.']
  device_type varchar [note: 'The type of device used by the passenger to provide consent. Supports user experience analysis and consent validity verification.']
  dpo_review_flag boolean [note: 'Boolean indicator of whether this consent record has been reviewed or flagged for review by the airline\'s Data Protection Officer (DPO). Used for high-risk or disputed consent cases.']
  expiry_date date [note: 'The date on which the consent expires and must be re-requested from the passenger. Null for consents with no expiration. Recorded in ISO 8601 date format (yyyy-MM-dd). Required for jurisdictions with time-bound consent validity.']
  granted_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when the passenger granted consent. Recorded in ISO 8601 format (yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX). Required for GDPR and CCPA audit trails.']
  ip_address varchar [note: 'The IP address from which the passenger submitted their consent. Used for fraud detection, audit trail, and jurisdiction determination. May be considered PII in some jurisdictions.']
  jurisdiction_code varchar [note: 'The three-letter ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 country code representing the legal jurisdiction under which this consent was collected. Determines applicable data protection regulation (GDPR for EU, CCPA for California, PDPA for Singapore, etc.).']
  language_code varchar [note: 'The two-letter ISO 639-1 language code in which the consent request was presented to the passenger. Required for demonstrating informed consent in the passenger\'s preferred language.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when this consent record was last modified. Recorded in ISO 8601 format (yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX). Required for audit trail and data lineage.']
  legal_basis varchar [note: 'The lawful basis under GDPR Article 6 for processing the passenger\'s personal data. While this product focuses on consent, other legal bases may apply for specific processing activities.']
  minor_consent_flag boolean [note: 'Boolean indicator of whether this consent record pertains to a minor (passenger under the age of consent in the applicable jurisdiction). Triggers additional parental consent and verification requirements under GDPR Article 8 and COPPA.']
  modified_by_user varchar [note: 'The identifier of the system user or automated process that last modified this consent record. Supports audit trail and accountability.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text field for capturing additional context, exceptions, or special handling instructions related to this consent record. Used by customer service and compliance teams.']
  opt_in_flag boolean [note: 'Boolean indicator of whether the consent was obtained via opt-in (true) or opt-out (false) mechanism. GDPR requires opt-in for most marketing and profiling activities.']
  parental_consent_verified_flag boolean [note: 'Boolean indicator of whether parental or guardian consent has been verified for a minor passenger. Null if minor_consent_flag is false. Required for GDPR Article 8 and COPPA compliance.']
  processing_purpose varchar [note: 'The specific purpose for which the passenger\'s data will be processed under this consent. Examples include marketing communications, service personalization, loyalty program analytics, or partner offers. Required for GDPR Article 13 transparency.']
  renewal_due_date date [note: 'The date by which the airline should proactively request consent renewal from the passenger. Used for consent lifecycle management and regulatory compliance in jurisdictions requiring periodic re-consent.']
  source_system varchar [note: 'The name or identifier of the source system or application that captured the consent record. Examples include Amadeus Altéa PSS, airline mobile app, loyalty portal, or third-party booking platform.']
  text_snapshot varchar [note: 'A snapshot or reference to the exact consent text presented to the passenger at the time of consent. Supports audit and dispute resolution by preserving the wording the passenger agreed to.']
  third_party_sharing_scope varchar [note: 'Description of the third-party entities or categories of entities with whom passenger data may be shared under this consent. Required for transparency under GDPR Article 13 and CCPA Section 1798.100(b).']
  user_agent varchar [note: 'The user agent string of the browser or application used by the passenger to provide consent. Supports technical audit and fraud detection.']
  version varchar [note: 'The version identifier of the privacy policy or consent terms that were in effect at the time the passenger granted consent. Enables tracking of consent under different policy versions and supports re-consent workflows when policies change.']
  withdrawn_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when the passenger withdrew their consent. Null if consent has not been withdrawn. Recorded in ISO 8601 format (yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX). Required for right-to-erasure compliance.']

  Note: 'Manages passenger data privacy and consent records in compliance with GDPR, CCPA, PDPA, and other applicable data protection regulations. Captures consent type (marketing email, SMS, data sharing with partners, profiling, personalisation), consent status (granted, withdrawn, pending), consent channel (web, mobile app, call centre, airport kiosk), consent timestamp, consent version (policy version at time of grant), and jurisdiction. Provides the legal basis for data processing activities and supports right-to-erasure and data portability requests. Mandatory for DOT and EASA consumer protection compliance.'
}

Table passenger.profile_history {
  profile_history_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for profile_history']
  employee_id varchar [note: 'Unique identifier of the airline agent, call center representative, or customer service employee who performed the profile change. Null for passenger self-service or automated system changes. Supports accountability and training quality analysis.']
  pax_profile_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key reference to the master pax_profile record that this history entry tracks. Links each audit event to the subject passenger profile.']
  reversed_history_profile_history_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key reference to the pax_profile_history_id of the original change record that this entry reverses. Null for non-reversal changes. Creates an audit chain linking corrections to their original errors.']
  agent_location_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter IATA airport or city code representing the geographic location where the agent performed the change. Null for automated or self-service changes. Supports regional compliance and operational analysis.']
  audit_retention_category varchar [note: 'Classification determining the retention period for this audit record. STANDARD follows default policy, EXTENDED for high-value passengers or complex cases, LEGAL_HOLD for litigation or investigation, PERMANENT for regulatory or historical preservation.']
  automated_process_code varchar [note: 'Identifier of the automated system process, batch job, or integration workflow that triggered the profile change. Null for human-initiated changes. Examples: NIGHTLY_DATA_QUALITY_JOB, GDS_SYNC_PROCESS, LOYALTY_TIER_RECALC.']
  change_batch_reference varchar [note: 'Identifier linking this change to a batch processing run or bulk update operation. Null for individual real-time changes. Enables rollback of entire batch operations and supports bulk change audit reporting.']
  change_reason_code varchar [note: 'Standardized code indicating the business reason for the profile change. Examples: PASSENGER_REQUEST, DATA_QUALITY_FIX, REGULATORY_COMPLIANCE, FRAUD_PREVENTION, SYSTEM_MIGRATION, DUPLICATE_MERGE. Supports root-cause analysis and compliance reporting. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: promote to reference product if more than 6 distinct codes emerge]']
  change_reason_description varchar [note: 'Free-text explanation providing additional context for the profile change. Captures agent notes, passenger comments, or automated process descriptions that supplement the reason code.']
  change_source_system varchar [note: 'The system or channel that originated the profile change. Identifies whether the mutation came from the core PSS, passenger self-service channels, agent-assisted channels, or external GDS platforms. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: ALTEA_PSS|WEB_SELF_SERVICE|MOBILE_APP|CALL_CENTRE|GDS_AMADEUS|GDS_SABRE|GDS_TRAVELPORT — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  change_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Precise date and time when the profile change was committed to the system. Represents the business event time of the mutation. Format: yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX.']
  change_type varchar [note: 'The nature of the data mutation: INSERT for new profile creation, UPDATE for field modifications, DELETE for logical deletion, MERGE for profile consolidation, GDPR_ERASURE for right-to-be-forgotten requests, CORRECTION for data quality remediation.']
  change_validation_status varchar [note: 'The outcome of automated or manual validation checks applied to the profile change. VALIDATED indicates the change passed all business rules, PENDING_REVIEW requires human review, REJECTED indicates the change was blocked, AUTO_APPROVED for low-risk changes, MANUAL_OVERRIDE for supervisor-approved exceptions.']
  changed_field_name varchar [note: 'The specific attribute name in the pax_profile master record that was modified. Enables field-level audit granularity for compliance and data quality investigations.']
  compliance_flag boolean [note: 'Boolean indicator of whether this change was made to satisfy a regulatory compliance requirement (e.g., GDPR data correction request, DOT consumer protection mandate). True for compliance-driven changes, False otherwise.']
  data_quality_score decimal(5,2) [note: 'Numeric quality score (0.00 to 100.00) representing the completeness, accuracy, and consistency of the profile record after this change. Calculated by automated data quality rules. Supports data stewardship and remediation prioritization.']
  data_subject_consent_flag boolean [note: 'Boolean indicator of whether the passenger provided explicit consent for this specific data processing activity at the time of the change. True if consent was obtained, False otherwise. Supports GDPR consent management and audit requirements.']
  fraud_risk_score decimal(5,2) [note: 'Numeric risk score (0.00 to 100.00) assigned by the fraud detection system at the time of the profile change. Higher scores indicate greater likelihood of fraudulent activity. Supports security investigations and pattern analysis.']
  gdpr_lawful_basis varchar [note: 'The legal basis under GDPR Article 6 that justifies the processing and retention of this profile change record. Required for EU passenger data. Supports data subject access requests and regulatory audits.']
  ip_address varchar [note: 'The IP address of the client device or system from which the profile change was initiated. Captured for security, fraud detection, and forensic analysis. Null for backend batch processes.']
  new_value decimal(18,2) [note: 'The value of the changed field after the mutation. Stored as string representation to accommodate all data types. Null for DELETE operations. Enables forward reconstruction of profile state evolution.']
  pnr_locator varchar [note: 'Six-character alphanumeric PNR record locator associated with the transaction that triggered the profile change. Null if the change was not initiated within the context of a specific reservation. Supports cross-domain audit trail linking.']
  previous_value decimal(18,2) [note: 'The value of the changed field immediately before the mutation. Stored as string representation to accommodate all data types. Null for INSERT operations. Supports point-in-time reconstruction and rollback scenarios.']
  record_hash varchar [note: 'Cryptographic hash (SHA-256) of the complete change record payload. Ensures immutability and tamper detection for audit integrity. Supports forensic verification and regulatory compliance with audit trail non-repudiation requirements.']
  retention_expiry_date date [note: 'The date on which this audit record is eligible for deletion or archival per the airlines data retention policy and applicable regulatory requirements. Null for records with indefinite retention. Format: yyyy-MM-dd.']
  reversal_flag boolean [note: 'Boolean indicator of whether this history record represents a reversal or rollback of a previous change. True if this is a corrective reversal, False for normal forward changes. Supports error correction audit trails.']
  session_reference varchar [note: 'Unique identifier of the user session or API session during which the profile change occurred. Enables correlation of multiple changes within a single interaction and supports session replay for troubleshooting.']
  transaction_reference varchar [note: 'Unique identifier linking this profile change to the broader business transaction or session that triggered it. Enables correlation of profile mutations with reservation bookings, check-in events, or loyalty transactions.']
  user_agent_string varchar [note: 'The HTTP user agent string identifying the browser, mobile app version, or API client that submitted the profile change. Supports device analytics, compatibility troubleshooting, and fraud pattern detection.']

  Note: 'Immutable audit history of changes to the pax_profile master record — capturing every field-level change with before/after values, change timestamp, change source system (Altéa PSS, web self-service, mobile app, call centre, GDS), agent ID or automated process identifier, and change reason code. Supports GDPR data subject access requests, regulatory audit trails, fraud investigation, and data quality root-cause analysis. Enables point-in-time reconstruction of a passenger\'s profile state for any historical date.'
}

Table passenger.minor_guardian {
  minor_guardian_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the minor-guardian relationship record. Primary key.']
  accepting_crew_member_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to crew.crew_member. Business justification: Unaccompanied minor programs require airlines to document which cabin crew member accepted custody of the child at departure and handover. Legal liability and duty-of-care mandate. Crew member signs h']
  employee_id varchar [note: 'Employee or agent identifier of the staff member who verified the guardian authorization. Required for audit trail and accountability.']
  pax_profile_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the unaccompanied minor (UMNR) passenger. Links to the passenger master record.']
  authorization_document_expiry_date date [note: 'Expiration date of the authorization document, if applicable. Ensures authorization is valid for the travel date.']
  authorization_document_reference varchar [note: 'Reference number or identifier of the authorization document. Used for audit trail and compliance verification.']
  authorization_document_type varchar [note: 'Type of legal authorization document provided to permit the minor to travel unaccompanied. Required for regulatory compliance and liability protection.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this guardian record was first created in the system. Part of the audit trail.']
  dropoff_authorization_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this guardian is authorized to drop off the minor at the origin airport. True if authorized, False otherwise. Used for origin handover verification.']
  effective_from_date date [note: 'Start date from which this guardian authorization is valid. Supports time-bound guardianship arrangements.']
  effective_to_date date [note: 'End date until which this guardian authorization is valid. Null indicates open-ended authorization. Supports time-bound guardianship arrangements.']
  guardian_address_line_1 varchar [note: 'Primary street address line for the guardian. Required for emergency contact and legal documentation.']
  guardian_address_line_2 varchar [note: 'Secondary address line for the guardian (apartment, suite, unit number). Optional address component.']
  guardian_alternate_phone_number varchar [note: 'Secondary contact phone number for the guardian. Used as backup contact channel during UMNR service delivery.']
  guardian_city varchar [note: 'City or municipality of the guardian residence. Part of the complete contact address.']
  guardian_country_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO country code of the guardian residence country (e.g., USA, CAN, GBR). Required for international UMNR services.']
  guardian_email_address varchar [note: 'Email address of the guardian. Used for pre-flight notifications, service confirmations, and post-flight communication.']
  guardian_first_name varchar [note: 'First name or given name of the guardian. Used for identity verification during minor handover.']
  guardian_full_name varchar [note: 'Full legal name of the authorized guardian or escort responsible for the unaccompanied minor. Required for handover procedures at origin and destination.']
  guardian_government_id_number varchar [note: 'Identification number from the guardian government-issued document. Recorded for liability and handover audit trail.']
  guardian_government_id_type varchar [note: 'Type of government-issued identification document presented by the guardian for identity verification during minor handover.']
  guardian_id_issuing_country varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO country code of the country that issued the guardian identification document (e.g., USA, GBR, CAN).']
  guardian_last_name varchar [note: 'Last name or family name of the guardian. Used for identity verification during minor handover.']
  guardian_phone_country_code varchar [note: 'International dialing code for the guardian phone number (e.g., +1, +44, +971). Ensures correct routing for international UMNR services.']
  guardian_phone_number varchar [note: 'Primary contact phone number for the guardian. Used for operational communication during UMNR service delivery and irregular operations (IROP).']
  guardian_postal_code varchar [note: 'Postal or ZIP code of the guardian residence. Used for address validation and emergency contact.']
  guardian_role varchar [note: 'Operational role of the guardian in the UMNR service workflow. Determines which airport handover procedures apply.']
  guardian_state_province varchar [note: 'State, province, or administrative region of the guardian residence. Required for certain jurisdictions.']
  guardianship_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the guardian authorization record. Determines whether the guardian can be used for active UMNR bookings.']
  pickup_authorization_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this guardian is authorized to pick up the minor at the destination airport. True if authorized, False otherwise. Critical for ground handling handover procedures.']
  pnr_locator varchar [note: 'Six-character alphanumeric PNR locator code associated with the unaccompanied minor booking. Used for operational handover and service fulfillment tracking.']
  relationship_to_minor varchar [note: 'Nature of the relationship between the guardian and the unaccompanied minor. Determines authorization level and liability scope.']
  source_system varchar [note: 'Name of the operational system that created or last updated this guardian record (e.g., Amadeus Altéa, SITA Airport Management). Used for data lineage and troubleshooting.']
  special_instructions varchar [note: 'Free-text field for special handling instructions related to the guardian handover (e.g., custody arrangements, security concerns, language preferences). Used by ground handling staff.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this guardian record was last modified. Supports change tracking and audit compliance.']
  verification_date date [note: 'Date when the guardian identity and authorization documents were verified by airline staff. Part of the compliance audit trail.']
  verification_status varchar [note: 'Status of identity and authorization document verification for the guardian. Critical for compliance and liability management.']

  Note: 'Manages the relationship between unaccompanied minor (UMNR) passengers and their authorised guardians or escorts. Captures guardian full name, relationship to minor (parent, legal guardian, authorised adult), contact phone numbers, government-issued ID reference, authorisation document reference, pickup authorisation at destination, and the effective date range of the guardianship record. Required for UMNR service fulfilment, ground handling handover procedures, and liability compliance. Distinct from contact_detail — this entity carries legal authorisation and handover workflow data specific to minors.'
}

Table passenger.watchlist_check {
  watchlist_check_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for watchlist_check']
  employee_id varchar [note: 'Employee or agent identifier of the security officer who resolved the watchlist match.']
  pax_profile_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the passenger profile being screened.']
  regulatory_requirement_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.regulatory_requirement. Business justification: Watchlist screening is mandated by TSA Secure Flight (49 CFR 1560), EU-PNR Directive, and national security regulations. Airlines link each screening event to the governing requirement to demonstrate ']
  regulatory_violation_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.regulatory_violation. Business justification: Screening failures (false negatives, missed checks, system outages) can result in regulatory violations and enforcement actions from TSA or border authorities. Airlines track which watchlist check fai']
  api_pnr_indicator varchar [note: 'Indicates whether the screening was performed using API data, PNR data, or both.']
  boarding_pass_issued boolean [note: 'Indicates whether a boarding pass was issued following the watchlist check clearance.']
  check_status varchar [note: 'Current status of the watchlist check record in its lifecycle.']
  check_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the watchlist screening check was performed.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this watchlist check record was first created in the system.']
  data_retention_date date [note: 'Date when this watchlist check record must be purged per regulatory data retention requirements.']
  destination_airport_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter IATA airport code for the flight destination.']
  expiry_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the watchlist check result expires and a new check must be performed.']
  flight_number varchar [note: 'Flight number for which the watchlist check was performed, following IATA flight number format.']
  known_traveler_number varchar [note: 'TSA PreCheck or other trusted traveler program number that may expedite screening clearance.']
  match_reason varchar [note: 'Description of the reason for the match, including which passenger attributes triggered the screening alert.']
  match_score decimal(5,2) [note: 'Numerical confidence score (0.00 to 100.00) indicating the likelihood of a true match when a potential match is detected.']
  match_status varchar [note: 'Result of the watchlist screening check indicating whether the passenger matched any watchlist entries.']
  origin_airport_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter IATA airport code for the flight origin.']
  pnr_locator varchar [note: 'Six-character alphanumeric PNR locator code associated with the booking being screened.']
  redress_number varchar [note: 'DHS Traveler Redress Inquiry Program (TRIP) redress number provided by the passenger to prevent false matches.']
  regulatory_submission_reference varchar [note: 'Reference number or identifier for the regulatory submission of screening results to TSA, EU authorities, or other governing bodies.']
  resolution_action varchar [note: 'Action taken to resolve a potential or confirmed match, including clearance, escalation, or denial.']
  resolution_notes varchar [note: 'Free-text notes documenting the resolution process, verification steps, and outcome.']
  resolution_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the watchlist match was resolved or cleared.']
  scheduled_departure_date date [note: 'Scheduled departure date of the flight for which the passenger was screened.']
  screening_jurisdiction varchar [note: 'Two or three-letter country or region code indicating the regulatory jurisdiction under which screening was performed.']
  screening_program varchar [note: 'The regulatory or security program under which the watchlist check was performed.']
  screening_system varchar [note: 'Name or identifier of the automated screening system that performed the watchlist check.']
  screening_version varchar [note: 'Version number of the screening algorithm or watchlist data used for the check.']
  selectee_indicator boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the passenger was designated for enhanced secondary screening (SSSS - Secondary Security Screening Selection).']
  submission_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when screening results were submitted to the regulatory authority.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this watchlist check record was last modified.']
  watchlist_name varchar [note: 'Name or identifier of the specific watchlist that triggered a match (e.g., No Fly List, Selectee List, OFAC SDN List).']

  Note: 'Records the results of security watchlist screening checks performed against a passenger profile — covering TSA Secure Flight, OFAC sanctions screening, Interpol notices, and national no-fly list checks. Captures check timestamp, screening programme (Secure Flight, OFAC, EU PNR Directive, etc.), match status (clear, potential match, confirmed match, resolved), resolution action, resolving officer ID, and regulatory submission reference. Mandatory for TSA Secure Flight compliance on US-bound flights and EU PNR Directive compliance on EU-bound flights. Distinct from redress_record — this captures the screening event, not the resolution credential.'
}

Table passenger.corporate_traveller {
  corporate_traveller_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the corporate traveller relationship record. Primary key.']
  company_code_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.company_code. Business justification: Corporate accounts may be subsidiary-specific for intercompany revenue allocation and legal entity-specific corporate agreements. Airlines with multiple operating entities need to track which legal en']
  corporate_contract_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to revenue.corporate_contract. Business justification: Corporate travelers are governed by negotiated contracts specifying discounts, fare rules, and billing terms. Essential for fare validation at booking, discount application, contract compliance report']
  cost_centre_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.cost_centre. Business justification: Corporate travel expense allocation requires linking travelers to employer cost centers for expense reporting, budget tracking, and travel policy compliance. Airlines track which cost center to bill f']
  employee_id varchar [note: 'Employee identifier or personnel number within the corporate entity\'s HR system. Links the passenger to their corporate employment record.']
  ffp_member_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to loyalty.ffp_member. Business justification: Links corporate travel bookings to personal loyalty accounts for mile accrual. Airlines must credit corporate-paid flights to individual frequent flyer accounts per negotiated agreements—enables autom']
  pax_profile_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the passenger profile linked to this corporate travel account. Links to the passenger master entity.']
  preferred_cabin_class_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to inventory.cabin_class. Business justification: Corporate travel policies enforce cabin class restrictions (e.g., economy only, business for long-haul). Booking systems validate reservations against corporate policy rules. Policy enforcement and co']
  preferred_route_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to route.route. Business justification: Corporate travel policies often restrict or prefer specific routes based on negotiated corporate agreements and duty-of-care requirements. Corporate booking tools enforce policy compliance by validati']
  affiliation_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the corporate traveller affiliation. Active = currently valid; suspended = temporarily inactive; terminated = ended; pending = awaiting activation.']
  approver_email varchar [note: 'Email address of the travel approver for notification and approval workflow purposes.']
  approver_name varchar [note: 'Name of the manager or approver responsible for authorizing travel bookings for this corporate traveller.']
  company_name varchar [note: 'Legal or trading name of the corporate entity to which this traveller is affiliated.']
  corporate_account_code varchar [note: 'Unique alphanumeric code identifying the corporate travel account or negotiated corporate agreement. This is the business identifier used in booking systems and invoicing.']
  corporate_billing_address_line_1 varchar [note: 'First line of the corporate billing address for invoicing and financial reconciliation.']
  corporate_billing_address_line_2 varchar [note: 'Second line of the corporate billing address (suite, floor, building name).']
  corporate_billing_city varchar [note: 'City of the corporate billing address.']
  corporate_billing_country_code varchar [note: 'ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 country code of the corporate billing address.']
  corporate_billing_postal_code varchar [note: 'Postal or ZIP code of the corporate billing address.']
  corporate_billing_state_province varchar [note: 'State or province of the corporate billing address.']
  corporate_contact_email varchar [note: 'Email address of the corporate travel coordinator for account management and communication.']
  corporate_contact_name varchar [note: 'Name of the primary corporate travel coordinator or contact person at the corporate entity for this account.']
  corporate_contact_phone varchar [note: 'Phone number of the corporate travel coordinator.']
  corporate_tier varchar [note: 'Classification of the corporate account based on volume, revenue, or strategic importance. SME = Small/Medium Enterprise; preferred = mid-tier negotiated rates; strategic = top-tier partnership; global/regional/local = geographic scope of agreement.']
  corporate_travel_policy_reference varchar [note: 'Reference code or identifier for the specific corporate travel policy governing this traveller\'s bookings, including fare class restrictions, advance purchase rules, and approval workflows.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this corporate traveller record was first created in the system.']
  department_name varchar [note: 'Name of the department or business unit within the corporate entity to which the employee belongs.']
  discount_percentage decimal(5,2) [note: 'Percentage discount off published fares applicable to this corporate traveller under the negotiated agreement.']
  duty_of_care_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this corporate traveller is enrolled in duty-of-care tracking and emergency notification services. True = enrolled; False = not enrolled.']
  effective_from_date date [note: 'Date from which the corporate traveller affiliation becomes active and the negotiated terms apply.']
  effective_to_date date [note: 'Date on which the corporate traveller affiliation expires or is terminated. Null indicates an open-ended affiliation.']
  job_title varchar [note: 'Job title or role of the employee within the corporate entity. Used for travel policy enforcement and duty-of-care segmentation.']
  last_booking_date date [note: 'Date of the most recent booking made under this corporate traveller affiliation. Used for activity tracking and account review.']
  negotiated_fare_code varchar [note: 'Fare code or corporate discount code applicable to this traveller under the corporate agreement. Used in booking and pricing systems to apply negotiated rates.']
  source_system varchar [note: 'Name of the source system or application from which this corporate traveller record originated (e.g., Amadeus Altéa, SAP S/4HANA, TMC portal).']
  termination_reason varchar [note: 'Reason for termination of the corporate traveller affiliation (e.g., employee departure, contract expiry, policy violation).']
  total_bookings_count varchar [note: 'Cumulative count of bookings made under this corporate traveller affiliation since inception. Used for volume tracking and tier qualification.']
  travel_arranger_iata_code varchar [note: 'Eight-digit IATA numeric code identifying the travel management company or travel agency arranging bookings for this corporate traveller.']
  travel_arranger_name varchar [note: 'Name of the travel management company (TMC) or travel arranger responsible for booking travel for this corporate traveller.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this corporate traveller record was last modified.']
  vip_status_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this corporate traveller has VIP status requiring special handling, priority service, or executive-level support. True = VIP; False = standard.']

  Note: 'Links a passenger profile to a corporate travel account or negotiated corporate agreement — capturing the corporate account code, company name, corporate travel policy reference, cost centre, employee ID within the corporate structure, corporate tier (SME, preferred, strategic), and the effective date range of the corporate affiliation. Supports B2B commercial relationships where the airline has negotiated fares or service agreements with corporate clients. Enables corporate travel reporting, duty-of-care tracking, and corporate billing reconciliation. Distinct from pax_segment (which is marketing segmentation) — this is a formal commercial affiliation.'
}

Table passenger.apis_submission {
  apis_submission_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the APIS submission record. Primary key.']
  flight_inventory_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to inventory.flight_inventory. Business justification: APIS submissions are regulatory filings tied to specific flight inventory instances for border control. Customs authorities require passenger manifests linked to exact operating flights. Mandatory com']
  flight_leg_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the specific flight leg for which APIS data was submitted to border control authorities.']
  original_submission_apis_submission_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the original APIS submission record if this is a resubmission or correction. Null for original submissions.']
  pax_profile_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the passenger whose travel document data was submitted to border control authorities.']
  regulatory_requirement_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.regulatory_requirement. Business justification: APIS submissions are mandated by specific border authority regulations (TSA Secure Flight, CBP APIS, EU-PNR Directive). Airlines link each submission to the governing regulatory requirement to demonst']
  regulatory_violation_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.regulatory_violation. Business justification: Failed, late, or incomplete APIS submissions result in regulatory violations and penalties from border authorities (CBP fines up to $10,000 per violation). Airlines track which APIS failures triggered']
  arrival_airport_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter IATA airport code of the arrival airport for the flight leg associated with this APIS submission.']
  authority_response_code varchar [note: 'Response code returned by the border control authority indicating acceptance, rejection reason, or error condition. Codes vary by authority (e.g., CBP response codes, UKBF codes).']
  authority_response_message varchar [note: 'Human-readable message returned by the border control authority providing additional detail on the submission outcome or rejection reason.']
  authority_response_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the border control authority returned a response to the APIS submission.']
  border_authority_code varchar [note: 'Code identifying the specific border control authority that received the submission (e.g., CBP for US Customs and Border Protection, UKBF for UK Border Force, EES for EU Entry/Exit System).']
  compliance_mandate varchar [note: 'The specific regulatory mandate or legal requirement under which this APIS submission was made (e.g., US CBP APIS Final Rule, EU Entry/Exit System Regulation 2017/2226, ICAO Annex 9 Standard 3.47).']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this APIS submission record was first created in the data platform.']
  departure_airport_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter IATA airport code of the departure airport for the flight leg associated with this APIS submission.']
  destination_country_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 code of the destination country whose border control authority received the APIS submission.']
  document_expiry_date date [note: 'Expiration date of the travel document as submitted to border control authorities. Snapshot at time of submission.']
  document_issuing_country_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 code of the country that issued the travel document submitted in the APIS data.']
  document_number varchar [note: 'The unique document number of the travel document (passport number, national ID number, etc.) as submitted to border control authorities. Snapshot at time of submission.']
  document_type varchar [note: 'Type of travel document submitted in the APIS data (passport, national ID card, visa, travel permit, refugee travel document, or other recognized travel document).']
  message_reference_number varchar [note: 'Unique reference number assigned to the APIS transmission message by the sending system or border control authority for tracking and reconciliation purposes.']
  passenger_date_of_birth date [note: 'Date of birth of the passenger as submitted to border control authorities. Snapshot at time of submission.']
  passenger_gender varchar [note: 'Gender of the passenger as recorded on the travel document submitted to border control authorities (M=Male, F=Female, X=Unspecified/Other). Snapshot at time of submission.']
  passenger_given_name varchar [note: 'Given name(s) (first and middle names) of the passenger as they appear on the travel document submitted to border control authorities. Snapshot at time of submission.']
  passenger_nationality_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 code representing the nationality of the passenger as submitted to border control authorities. Snapshot at time of submission.']
  passenger_surname varchar [note: 'Surname (family name) of the passenger as it appears on the travel document submitted to border control authorities. Snapshot at time of submission.']
  pnr varchar [note: 'Six-character alphanumeric Passenger Name Record locator associated with the booking for which APIS data was submitted.']
  resubmission_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this submission is a resubmission of previously rejected or corrected APIS data (True) or an original submission (False).']
  scheduled_arrival_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Scheduled arrival date and time of the flight leg for which APIS data was submitted.']
  scheduled_departure_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Scheduled departure date and time of the flight leg for which APIS data was submitted.']
  submission_channel varchar [note: 'Technical channel or interface used to transmit the APIS data (e.g., SITA Type B message, XML web service, EDI, proprietary API).']
  submission_method varchar [note: 'Method by which the APIS data was submitted to border control authorities: automated (system-triggered), manual (agent-initiated), batch (scheduled bulk transmission), real_time (interactive submission).']
  submission_source_system varchar [note: 'Name or code of the operational system that generated and transmitted the APIS submission (e.g., Amadeus Altéa DCS, Sabre AirCentre, SITA Horizon).']
  submission_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the APIS submission: pending (awaiting authority response), accepted (approved by border control), rejected (denied by border control), error (technical transmission failure), cancelled (submission withdrawn).']
  submission_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the APIS data was transmitted to the destination country border control authority. This is the principal business event timestamp for the submission.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this APIS submission record was last modified in the data platform.']

  Note: 'Transactional records of Advance Passenger Information System (APIS) data submissions to border control authorities — capturing the submission timestamp, destination country authority (CBP, UK Border Force, EU Entry/Exit System, etc.), submission status (pending, accepted, rejected, error), passenger travel document data snapshot at time of submission, flight reference, and authority response code. Required for regulatory compliance on international routes under ICAO Annex 9, US CBP APIS, and EU Entry/Exit System mandates. Distinct from travel_document (the master record) — this captures the regulatory submission event.'
}

Table passenger.accessibility_profile {
  accessibility_profile_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the accessibility profile record. Primary key.']
  cabin_class_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to inventory.cabin_class. Business justification: Accessibility requirements (stretcher, wheelchair type) determine cabin class eligibility and seating restrictions. Operations must validate PRM requests against cabin configurations for safety compli']
  consumer_protection_case_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.consumer_protection_case. Business justification: Accessibility service failures (denied boarding, damaged wheelchairs, inadequate assistance) trigger consumer protection cases under DOT Part 382 and EC 1107/2006. Airlines link accessibility profiles']
  mobility_equipment_vendor_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to procurement.vendor. Business justification: Passengers with mobility aids requiring specialized handling (battery-powered wheelchairs, medical equipment) often need vendor-provided equipment or services at stations. Airlines track which ground ']
  pax_profile_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the passenger who owns this accessibility profile. Links to the passenger master record.']
  revenue_emd_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to revenue.emd. Business justification: Accessibility services (wheelchairs, oxygen, medical equipment, stretchers) are often chargeable ancillaries documented via EMDs. Links PRM service profiles to revenue records for billing, EMD reconci']
  ambulatory_status varchar [note: 'Passengers ability to walk and move independently. Determines boarding method, seating restrictions, and emergency evacuation capability.']
  assistance_at_arrival_required_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the passenger requires ground handling assistance at arrival airport (deplaning, baggage claim, customs, onward connection). Triggers PRM service request to ground handler.']
  assistance_at_departure_required_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the passenger requires ground handling assistance at departure airport (check-in, security, gate, boarding). Triggers PRM service request to ground handler.']
  assistance_at_transit_required_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the passenger requires ground handling assistance during transit or connection. Ensures continuity of care between flights.']
  boarding_method_preference varchar [note: 'Preferred method for boarding the aircraft. Pre-board allows extra time; ambulift is a hydraulic lift vehicle; aisle chair is a narrow wheelchair for aircraft aisle; carry-onboard means passenger is carried by assistance personnel.']
  cognitive_assistance_required_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the passenger requires cognitive or developmental disability assistance. May require simplified instructions, extra time, or companion support.']
  companion_traveler_required_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the passenger is required to travel with a companion or safety assistant due to the severity of their disability. May be mandated by airline medical policy.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this accessibility profile record was first created in the system. Immutable audit field.']
  hearing_impairment_level varchar [note: 'Degree of hearing impairment. Determines need for visual safety briefings, written communication, and assistive listening devices.']
  last_verified_date date [note: 'Date when the accessibility profile information was last verified or updated by the passenger or airline staff. Used for data quality and compliance auditing.']
  medical_clearance_expiry_date date [note: 'Date until which the medical clearance (MEDIF) is valid. Passenger cannot travel after this date without renewed clearance.']
  medical_clearance_required_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the passenger requires medical clearance (MEDIF form) from airline medical services before travel. Triggered by certain medical conditions or equipment.']
  medical_equipment_carried varchar [note: 'Description of medical equipment the passenger carries onboard (CPAP, portable oxygen concentrator, nebulizer, insulin pump). Free-text field for crew awareness and stowage planning.']
  mobility_aid_battery_type varchar [note: 'Type of battery powering the mobility aid device. Critical for dangerous goods compliance and cargo hold safety. Lithium-ion batteries have specific IATA DGR restrictions.']
  mobility_aid_battery_watt_hours decimal(8,2) [note: 'Energy capacity of the mobility aid battery in watt-hours. Required for lithium battery compliance checks per IATA DGR (batteries over 300Wh require special handling).']
  mobility_aid_foldable_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the mobility aid device can be folded or disassembled for stowage. Affects cargo hold space allocation and handling procedures.']
  mobility_aid_height_cm decimal(6,2) [note: 'Height dimension of the mobility aid device in centimeters. Used to determine cargo hold compatibility and stowage location.']
  mobility_aid_length_cm decimal(6,2) [note: 'Length dimension of the mobility aid device in centimeters. Used to determine cargo hold compatibility and stowage location.']
  mobility_aid_type varchar [note: 'Type of mobility aid device used by the passenger. Determines handling and stowage requirements. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: manual_wheelchair|electric_wheelchair|mobility_scooter|walker|cane|crutches|prosthetic|none — 8 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  mobility_aid_weight_kg decimal(6,2) [note: 'Weight of the mobility aid device in kilograms. Required for cargo hold planning and aircraft weight and balance calculations.']
  mobility_aid_width_cm decimal(6,2) [note: 'Width dimension of the mobility aid device in centimeters. Used to determine cargo hold compatibility and stowage location.']
  oxygen_flow_rate_lpm decimal(5,2) [note: 'Prescribed oxygen flow rate in liters per minute. Required for aircraft oxygen system configuration and medical clearance.']
  oxygen_required_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the passenger requires supplemental oxygen during flight. Requires advance coordination with medical services and aircraft equipment availability.']
  preferred_seating_location varchar [note: 'Passengers preferred seating location based on accessibility needs. Bulkhead seats provide extra space for mobility aids; aisle seats facilitate lavatory access.']
  prm_category varchar [note: 'IATA standard PRM category code. WCHR=wheelchair for ramp, WCHS=wheelchair for stairs, WCHC=wheelchair for cabin seat, WCBD=dry cell battery wheelchair, WCBW=wet cell battery wheelchair, WCMP=manual wheelchair, BLND=blind, DEAF=deaf, DPNA=disabled passenger needing assistance, MAAS=meet and assist. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: WCHR|WCHS|WCHC|WCBD|WCBW|WCMP|BLND|DEAF|DPNA|MAAS — 10 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  profile_effective_from_date date [note: 'Date from which this accessibility profile becomes effective and can be used for booking and SSR generation.']
  profile_effective_to_date date [note: 'Date until which this accessibility profile remains valid. Nullable for indefinite profiles; populated for time-limited medical clearances.']
  profile_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the accessibility profile. Active profiles are used for SSR generation; expired profiles require renewal of medical documentation.']
  seating_restriction_notes varchar [note: 'Free-text notes on seating restrictions or requirements (e.g., cannot sit in exit row, requires adjacent seat for companion, needs armrest removal). Used by inventory and departure control systems.']
  service_animal_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the passenger travels with a trained service animal (guide dog, hearing dog, psychiatric service animal). Requires cabin accommodation and documentation.']
  service_animal_type varchar [note: 'Type of trained service animal accompanying the passenger. Determines seating location and cabin crew briefing requirements.']
  source_system varchar [note: 'Name of the operational system that created or last updated this accessibility profile record (e.g., Amadeus Altéa PSS, airport kiosk, mobile app, call center CRM).']
  special_instructions varchar [note: 'Free-text field for additional accessibility instructions, preferences, or notes that do not fit structured fields. Visible to ground handlers and cabin crew.']
  stretcher_required_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the passenger requires stretcher accommodation. Requires removal of multiple seat rows and advance aircraft configuration.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this accessibility profile record was last modified. Updated on every change for audit trail and data freshness tracking.']
  visual_impairment_level varchar [note: 'Degree of visual impairment. Determines need for sighted guide, Braille materials, audio announcements, and service animal accommodation.']

  Note: 'Dedicated master record for passengers with disabilities or reduced mobility (PRM — Passengers with Reduced Mobility) — capturing mobility aid type (manual wheelchair, electric wheelchair, mobility scooter), dimensions and weight of mobility aid, battery type (lithium-ion, dry cell) for cargo hold compliance, ambulatory status, visual impairment level, hearing impairment level, cognitive assistance requirements, medical equipment carried (CPAP, oxygen concentrator), and preferred assistance workflow at each journey touchpoint (check-in, security, gate, boarding, deplaning). Aligns with IATA PRM Handling Guidelines and EU Regulation 1107/2006. Distinct from ssr_record — this is the persistent profile, not the per-flight SSR instance.'
}

Table passenger.pnr_link {
  pnr_link_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for pnr_link']
  booking_agent_employee_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to workforce.employee. Business justification: Booking agents create/modify PNRs; essential for commission calculation, quality audits, fraud investigation, regulatory accountability (IATA agent tracking), and performance management. Standard airl']
  ffp_member_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to loyalty.ffp_member. Business justification: Resolves PNR-level frequent flyer number to loyalty account for automated mile accrual and upgrade processing. Airlines must link each PNR passenger to loyalty profile for post-flight accrual batch jo']
  flight_inventory_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to inventory.flight_inventory. Business justification: PNR passenger associations must link to specific flight inventory for seat control, load management, and overbooking decisions. Revenue management and departure control systems require this for real-t']
  pnr_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to reservation.pnr. Business justification: pnr_link currently stores pnr_locator as string; adding FK to reservation.pnr enables customer service agents to reconcile passenger profile history with booking records during dispute resolution, ref']
  primary_pnr_pax_profile_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key reference to the passenger profile in the passenger domain. Identifies the individual traveler whose identity is linked to the PNR.']
  route_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to route.route. Business justification: IROP (irregular operations) systems use PNR-route linkage for automated rebooking and disruption management. Route-level tracking enables journey analytics and end-to-end customer experience measureme']
  api_data_submitted_flag boolean [note: 'Boolean flag indicating whether Advance Passenger Information (passport, visa, destination address) has been submitted to authorities for this passenger on this PNR. True if submitted; false if not yet submitted. Required for international travel compliance.']
  apis_submission_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when API data was transmitted to border control authorities for this passenger. Null if not yet submitted. Format: yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX.']
  boarding_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this passenger boarded the aircraft for the flight(s) on this PNR. Null if not yet boarded. Format: yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX.']
