  booking_creation_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this passenger was first linked to the PNR in the reservation system. Represents the initial association event. Format: yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX.']
  booking_role varchar [note: 'Defines the passenger\'s role within the PNR booking. Lead passenger is the primary contact and decision-maker; accompanying passenger travels with the lead; infant is a child under 2; group leader manages a group booking; group member is part of a group.']
  cancellation_reason_code varchar [note: 'Standardized code indicating the reason for cancellation of this passenger-PNR link. Passenger_request indicates voluntary cancellation; schedule_change indicates airline-initiated change; involuntary indicates denied boarding or operational reasons; no_show indicates passenger did not appear; duplicate indicates duplicate booking; fraud indicates suspected fraudulent booking. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: passenger_request|schedule_change|involuntary|no_show|duplicate|fraud|other — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  cancellation_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this passenger-PNR link was cancelled. Null if link is still active or completed. Format: yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX.']
  check_in_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this passenger checked in for the flight(s) on this PNR. Null if not yet checked in. Format: yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX.']
  contact_email varchar [note: 'Email address provided for this passenger on this PNR for booking-specific communications. May differ from the email stored in the passenger profile if the passenger provided a different contact for this booking.']
  contact_phone varchar [note: 'Phone number provided for this passenger on this PNR for booking-specific communications. May differ from the phone stored in the passenger profile if the passenger provided a different contact for this booking.']
  emergency_contact_name varchar [note: 'Name of the emergency contact person for this passenger on this PNR. Used in case of travel disruption or incident.']
  emergency_contact_phone varchar [note: 'Phone number of the emergency contact person for this passenger on this PNR. Used in case of travel disruption or incident.']
  group_booking_indicator boolean [note: 'Boolean flag indicating whether this passenger-PNR link is part of a group booking (typically 10 or more passengers traveling together). True if group booking; false if individual or small party.']
  group_name varchar [note: 'Name of the group if this is a group booking. Examples: corporate event name, tour group name, sports team name. Null for non-group bookings.']
  infant_indicator boolean [note: 'Boolean flag indicating whether this passenger is an infant (under 2 years old) traveling on this PNR. True if infant; false otherwise. Affects pricing, seating, and service requirements.']
  infant_on_lap_indicator boolean [note: 'Boolean flag indicating whether the infant is traveling on an adult\'s lap without a separate seat. True if on lap; false if infant has own seat. Impacts seat assignment and safety procedures.']
  known_traveler_number varchar [note: 'TSA PreCheck, Global Entry, or other trusted traveler program number. Enables expedited security screening. Applicable primarily for US travel.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time of the most recent modification to this passenger-PNR link record. Includes changes to name, SSRs, status, or any other attribute. Format: yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX.']
  link_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the passenger-PNR association. Active indicates valid link; cancelled indicates booking was cancelled; no_show indicates passenger did not appear for travel; checked_in indicates passenger has checked in; boarded indicates passenger has boarded; completed indicates travel is finished; suspended indicates temporary hold. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: active|cancelled|no_show|checked_in|boarded|completed|suspended — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  meal_preference varchar [note: 'Passenger\'s meal preference for this PNR, typically expressed as a four-letter SSR code (e.g., VGML for vegetarian, KSML for kosher, MOML for Muslim meal). Used for catering planning.']
  name_association_type varchar [note: 'Categorizes the type of passenger association within the PNR. Primary indicates the main traveler; infant_on_lap indicates a child under 2 traveling on an adult\'s lap without a separate seat; group_member indicates part of a group booking; accompanying indicates a passenger traveling with another; unaccompanied_minor indicates a child traveling alone; crew_deadhead indicates crew repositioning without duty.']
  name_mismatch_flag boolean [note: 'Boolean flag indicating whether the PNR passenger name differs from the legal name on the passenger profile. True if mismatch detected; false if names match. Used for data quality monitoring and potential identity verification issues.']
  passenger_sequence_number varchar [note: 'Sequential position of this passenger within the PNR. Used to maintain passenger order in multi-passenger bookings and for referencing specific passengers in PNR transactions.']
  pnr_locator varchar [note: 'Six-character alphanumeric booking reference code that uniquely identifies the PNR in the reservation system. This is the primary business identifier used by agents, passengers, and partner systems to retrieve the booking.']
  pnr_passenger_name varchar [note: 'Passenger name exactly as it appears on the PNR booking. May differ from the legal name stored in the passenger profile due to name changes, spelling variations, or data entry differences. Used for ticket issuance and travel document verification.']
  redress_number varchar [note: 'Department of Homeland Security Traveler Redress Inquiry Program (DHS TRIP) number for passengers who have been misidentified in the past. Used to prevent false matches with watch lists. Applicable primarily for US travel.']
  seat_preference varchar [note: 'Passenger\'s preferred seat location for this PNR. Window, aisle, or middle seat preference. Used for seat assignment optimization.']
  source_gds varchar [note: 'Identifies the Global Distribution System or booking channel that created the PNR association. Amadeus, Sabre, and Travelport are major GDS platforms; direct indicates airline\'s own PSS; other values indicate distribution channels. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: amadeus|sabre|travelport|direct|travel_agent|online|mobile|call_center|airport — 9 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  source_pss varchar [note: 'Name of the Passenger Service System that originated this PNR link record. Examples include Amadeus Altéa, Sabre SabreSonic, or proprietary airline PSS platforms. Enables traceability to the system of record.']
  special_service_requests varchar [note: 'Comma-separated list of four-letter SSR codes requested for this passenger on this PNR. Examples: WCHR (wheelchair), VGML (vegetarian meal), PETC (pet in cabin), UMNR (unaccompanied minor). Captures service requirements specific to this booking.']
  ticket_number varchar [note: 'Thirteen-digit electronic ticket number issued for this passenger on this PNR. Format: 3-digit airline code + 10-digit serial number. Links the passenger-PNR association to the ticketing domain.']
  travel_document_expiry_date date [note: 'Expiration date of the travel document provided for this passenger on this PNR. Used to validate document validity for travel dates. Format: yyyy-MM-dd.']
  travel_document_issuing_country varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO country code of the country that issued the travel document. Used for border control and visa requirement determination.']
  travel_document_number varchar [note: 'Passport or other travel document number provided for this passenger on this PNR. Used for international travel verification and API submission. May differ from the document stored in the passenger profile if the passenger used a different document for this booking.']
  travel_document_type varchar [note: 'Type of travel document provided for this passenger on this PNR. Passport is most common for international travel; national_id may be accepted for regional travel; visa indicates entry authorization; refugee_travel_document for refugees.']

  Note: 'Association entity linking a passenger profile to one or more PNR (Passenger Name Record) bookings managed in the reservation domain. Captures the PNR locator, passenger name as it appears on the PNR (which may differ from legal name on profile), name association type (primary, infant-on-lap, group member), booking role (lead passenger, accompanying passenger), and the source GDS or PSS that created the association. This is the authoritative cross-domain join key between the passenger identity domain and the reservation domain, enabling profile enrichment of PNR data without duplicating reservation master data.'
}

Table passenger.passenger_booking_passenger {
  passenger_booking_passenger_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for passenger_booking_passenger']
  booking_passenger_id varchar [note: 'Unique identifier for this booking-passenger link record. Primary key.']
  pax_profile_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to the passenger profile participating in this booking']
  pnr_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to the PNR that contains this passenger']
  boarding_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this passenger boarded the aircraft for this booking. Null if not yet boarded. Identified in detection phase.']
  booking_creation_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this passenger was added to the PNR. May differ from PNR creation timestamp if passengers added incrementally. Identified in detection phase.']
  booking_role varchar [note: 'Role of this passenger within the booking context. PRIMARY=main contact/booker, COMPANION=traveling with primary, INFANT=lap infant associated with adult, UNACCOMPANIED_MINOR=child traveling alone with special handling. Identified in detection phase.']
  cancellation_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this passenger was removed or cancelled from this PNR. Null if passenger remains active in booking. Identified in detection phase.']
  check_in_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this passenger checked in for this specific booking. Null if not yet checked in. Identified in detection phase.']
  meal_preference varchar [note: 'Meal type preference for this passenger in this specific booking using IATA SSR meal codes. May override profile-level preference. Identified in detection phase.']
  name_association_type varchar [note: 'Indicates how the passenger name in this booking relates to the profile name. EXACT_MATCH=identical, VARIANT=minor spelling differences, ALIAS=known alternate name. Used for profile matching and fraud detection. Identified in detection phase.']
  passenger_sequence_number varchar [note: 'Position of this passenger within the PNR passenger list. Used for ordering passengers in booking displays and documents. Identified in detection phase.']
  seat_preference varchar [note: 'Seat location preference for this passenger in this specific booking. May override profile-level preference. Identified in detection phase.']

  Note: 'This association product represents the participation relationship between passenger profiles and PNRs in airline reservation operations. It captures the many-to-many reality that one passenger can have multiple bookings over time (frequent flyers, business travelers) and one PNR can contain multiple passengers (family bookings, group travel). Each record links one passenger profile to one PNR with attributes that exist only in the context of this specific booking participation: passenger sequence number, booking role, check-in timestamp, boarding timestamp, and booking-specific preferences.. Existence Justification: Airlines operations exhibit true bidirectional many-to-many cardinality: individual passengers create multiple PNRs over their travel lifetime (frequent flyers, business travelers, leisure trips), and individual PNRs contain multiple passengers (family bookings, group travel, corporate delegations). The relationship itself carries operational data that belongs to neither entity alone: passenger sequence within the booking, booking role (primary/companion/infant), check-in and boarding timestamps, and booking-specific preferences that may override profile defaults.'
}

Table passenger.passenger_booking {
  passenger_booking_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for passenger_booking']
  booking_pnr_id varchar [note: 'Unique identifier for this passenger-flight booking record. Primary key.']
  flight_inventory_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to the specific flight-date inventory record for this booking']
  pax_profile_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to the passenger profile who holds this booking']
  pnr_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the PNR that contains this booking. Multiple bookings (multi-leg journeys) can belong to the same PNR.']
  baggage_allowance_pieces varchar [note: 'Number of checked baggage pieces allowed for this booking based on fare class and route.']
  baggage_allowance_weight_kg varchar [note: 'Total checked baggage weight allowance in kilograms for this booking.']
  boarding_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the passenger\'s boarding pass was scanned at the gate and the passenger boarded the aircraft. Null if not yet boarded.']
  booking_class varchar [note: 'Single-letter fare class (booking class) code for this reservation (e.g., Y, B, M for economy; J, C for business; F, A for first). Determines fare rules, upgrade eligibility, and mileage accrual.']
  booking_source varchar [note: 'Channel through which this booking was created (direct web, mobile app, GDS, call center, airport counter, travel agent, corporate booking tool).']
  booking_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of this booking. CONFIRMED=seat confirmed, WAITLISTED=on waitlist, CANCELLED=booking cancelled, NO_SHOW=passenger did not board, CHECKED_IN=passenger checked in, BOARDED=passenger boarded aircraft.']
  cabin_class varchar [note: 'Physical cabin class for this booking (FIRST, BUSINESS, PREMIUM_ECONOMY, ECONOMY). Determines service level and seat location.']
  check_in_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the passenger checked in for this flight (online, mobile, kiosk, or airport counter). Null if not yet checked in.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this booking was originally created in the reservation system.']
  fare_amount decimal(10,2) [note: 'Base fare amount paid for this booking in the transaction currency, excluding taxes and fees.']
  fare_basis varchar [note: 'Detailed fare basis code that encodes fare rules, restrictions, seasonality, and pricing logic for this booking (e.g., YRT14, BAPX21).']
  fare_currency varchar [note: 'ISO 4217 three-letter currency code for the fare amount (e.g., USD, EUR, GBP).']
  frequent_flyer_number varchar [note: 'Frequent flyer program number provided for this booking to accrue miles and status credits. May differ from passenger profile FFP if using partner program.']
  seat_number varchar [note: 'Assigned seat number for this passenger on this flight (e.g., 12A, 34F). Null if seat not yet assigned.']
  special_service_requests varchar [note: 'Comma-separated list of IATA SSR codes for this booking (e.g., WCHR for wheelchair, VGML for vegetarian meal, PETC for pet in cabin).']
  ticket_number varchar [note: '13-digit IATA e-ticket number issued for this booking (airline code + ticket number). Unique identifier for the ticketed reservation.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this booking was last modified (seat change, status change, upgrade, etc.).']
  upgrade_indicator boolean [note: 'Flag indicating whether this booking was upgraded from the original booking class (e.g., operational upgrade, loyalty upgrade, paid upgrade).']

  Note: 'This association product represents the booking event between a passenger profile and a specific flight inventory. It captures the core reservation relationship in airline operations. Each record links one passenger to one flight-date combination with attributes that exist only in the context of this booking: seat assignment, check-in status, boarding timestamps, ticket details, and fare class. This is the authoritative record of passenger-flight associations managed by Amadeus Altéa PSS and consumed by departure control, revenue management, and customer service systems.. Existence Justification: In airline operations, passengers book multiple flights throughout their travel history (multi-leg journeys, round-trips, frequent travelers), and each flight carries multiple passengers. The booking is the core operational entity managed by reservation systems (Amadeus Altéa PSS), departure control, and customer service. Each booking has its own lifecycle with seat assignment, check-in status, boarding timestamps, ticket number, and fare class that belong to neither the passenger profile nor the flight inventory alone.'
}

Table passenger.travel_history {
  travel_history_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for this passenger-route travel history record. Primary key.']
  pax_profile_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to the passenger profile master record']
  route_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to the route master record']
  average_fare_paid decimal(10,2) [note: 'Average fare amount paid by this passenger for this route across all historical bookings. Used for price sensitivity analysis and personalized pricing strategies.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this travel history record was first created in the system.']
  first_travel_date date [note: 'Date of the passenger\'s first travel on this route. Used for customer tenure analysis and lifecycle marketing.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this travel history record was last updated.']
  last_travel_date date [note: 'Most recent date when this passenger traveled on this route. Used for recency analysis in customer segmentation and reactivation campaigns.']
  preferred_cabin_class varchar [note: 'Most frequently booked cabin class by this passenger on this route. Values: economy, premium_economy, business, first. Used for targeted upgrade offers and cabin-specific promotions.']
  route_affinity_score decimal(5,2) [note: 'Calculated score (0.00 to 100.00) representing the strength of this passenger\'s preference for this route based on frequency, recency, fare paid, and booking behavior. Used for route-specific marketing and personalization.']
  total_bookings_count varchar [note: 'Total number of bookings made by this passenger for this route, including no-shows and cancellations. Differs from travel_frequency which counts completed trips only.']
  travel_frequency_on_route varchar [note: 'Total number of times this passenger has traveled on this specific route. Used for route affinity scoring and frequent traveler identification.']

  Note: 'This association product represents the historical record of passenger travel on specific routes. It captures the travel behavior, preferences, and commercial relationship between a passenger and a route over time. Each record links one passenger profile to one route with attributes that track frequency, recency, fare history, and route-specific preferences used for personalization, revenue optimization, and customer lifetime value analysis.. Existence Justification: In airline operations, passengers travel on multiple routes throughout their customer lifetime, and each route serves thousands of different passengers. The business actively manages and analyzes the passenger-route relationship to drive personalization, revenue optimization, targeted offers, and customer lifetime value analysis. This relationship carries meaningful operational and analytical data (frequency, recency, fare history, preferences) that belongs to neither the passenger profile nor the route master alone.'
}

Table passenger.passenger_promotion_enrollment {
  passenger_promotion_enrollment_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for passenger_promotion_enrollment']
  loyalty_promotion_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key to loyalty.loyalty_promotion.loyalty_promotion_id']
  pax_profile_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to the passenger profile who enrolled in the promotion']
  promotion_enrollment_id varchar [note: 'Unique identifier for this promotion enrollment record. Primary key.']
  promotion_loyalty_promotion_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to the loyalty promotion campaign']
  bonus_miles_earned varchar [note: 'Total bonus miles earned by this passenger under this specific promotion. Accumulated as qualifying activities occur. Explicitly identified in detection phase relationship data.']
  enrollment_channel varchar [note: 'Channel through which the passenger enrolled in the promotion: web, mobile_app, email_link, auto_enrolled (system-initiated for eligible members), call_center, partner_site. Explicitly identified in detection phase relationship data.']
  enrollment_created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this enrollment record was first created in the system. Immutable audit field.']
  enrollment_date date [note: 'Date when the passenger enrolled in or was automatically registered for this promotion. Explicitly identified in detection phase relationship data.']
  enrollment_status varchar [note: 'Current status of the enrollment: enrolled (active, tracking progress), qualified (met all requirements), disqualified (violated terms), expired (promotion ended before qualification), withdrawn (member opted out). Explicitly identified in detection phase relationship data.']
  enrollment_updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this enrollment record was last modified. Updated when status changes or bonus miles are awarded.']
  qualification_progress decimal(5,2) [note: 'Percentage progress (0.00 to 100.00) toward meeting the promotion qualification requirements. Calculated based on flights taken, miles earned, or other promotion-specific criteria. Explicitly identified in detection phase relationship data.']

  Note: 'This association product represents the enrollment event between a passenger profile and a loyalty promotion campaign. It captures the opt-in decision, tracks progress toward qualification targets, and records bonus miles earned per member-promotion pair. Each record links one passenger to one promotion with attributes that exist only in the context of this enrollment relationship.. Existence Justification: In airline loyalty operations, passengers routinely enroll in multiple concurrent promotions (e.g., double miles on transatlantic routes, bonus for credit card spend, tier challenge), and each promotion targets thousands of members. The business actively manages these enrollments as operational entities, tracking enrollment status, progress toward qualification targets, and bonus miles earned per member-promotion pair. This is a genuine M:N relationship with rich relationship semantics that loyalty operations staff query and manage daily.'
}

Table passenger.data_subject_request {
  data_subject_request_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for data_subject_request']
  employee_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the data protection officer or staff member assigned to process this data subject request.']
  profile_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the passenger who submitted the data subject request. Links to the passenger master entity.']
  related_data_subject_request_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing FK on data_subject_request (related_data_subject_request_id)']
  appeal_filed boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the data subject filed an appeal or complaint with a supervisory authority regarding the handling of their request.']
  appeal_reference_number varchar [note: 'Reference number assigned by the supervisory authority or internal appeals process for tracking the appeal.']
  applicable_regulation varchar [note: 'Primary data protection regulation governing this request: GDPR (EU General Data Protection Regulation), CCPA (California Consumer Privacy Act), LGPD (Brazilian General Data Protection Law), PIPEDA (Canadian Personal Information Protection), POPIA (South African Protection of Personal Information Act), PDPA (Singapore Personal Data Protection Act).']
  assigned_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the request was assigned to a data protection officer or staff member for processing.']
  completed_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the data subject request was fully processed and the response was delivered to the requestor.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'System timestamp when this data subject request record was first created in the database.']
  data_systems_searched varchar [note: 'Comma-separated list of internal systems and databases searched to fulfill the data subject request (e.g., Amadeus Altéa PSS, loyalty system, CRM, ancillary booking system).']
  due_date date [note: 'Regulatory deadline by which the data subject request must be fulfilled. Typically 30 days from submission under GDPR, 45 days under CCPA.']
  extended_due_date date [note: 'New deadline if an extension was granted. GDPR allows up to 2 additional months; CCPA allows up to 45 additional days.']
  extension_granted boolean [note: 'Indicates whether an extension to the response deadline was granted due to complexity or volume of the request.']
  extension_reason varchar [note: 'Justification provided to the data subject for granting an extension to the response deadline.']
  identity_verification_method varchar [note: 'Method used to verify the identity of the requestor: document_upload (ID document scan), knowledge_based (security questions), biometric (fingerprint/facial recognition), email_confirmation, phone_verification, or in_person verification.']
  identity_verification_status varchar [note: 'Status of identity verification process for the requestor: pending (awaiting verification), verified (identity confirmed), failed (verification unsuccessful), not_required (verification waived).']
  identity_verified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the requestor\'s identity was successfully verified.']
  internal_notes varchar [note: 'Internal working notes and comments recorded by data protection officers during the processing of the request. Not shared with the data subject.']
  is_authorized_representative boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the request was submitted by an authorized representative on behalf of the data subject (true) or by the data subject themselves (false).']
  jurisdiction varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO country code representing the legal jurisdiction under which the data subject request is being processed (e.g., USA for CCPA, GBR for UK GDPR, DEU for German GDPR).']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'System timestamp when this data subject request record was last updated or modified.']
  priority_level varchar [note: 'Priority classification assigned to the data subject request based on urgency, complexity, or regulatory deadline considerations.']
  records_found_count varchar [note: 'Total number of records or data items identified across all systems that pertain to the data subject.']
  rejection_legal_basis varchar [note: 'Specific legal provision or regulation cited as the basis for rejecting the data subject request (e.g., GDPR Article 17(3) exception).']
  rejection_reason varchar [note: 'Detailed explanation provided to the data subject if the request was rejected, including legal basis for refusal.']
  representative_authorization_document varchar [note: 'Reference to the document or proof of authorization provided by the representative to act on behalf of the data subject.']
  request_description varchar [note: 'Detailed description or notes provided by the requestor explaining the nature and scope of their data subject request.']
  request_reference_number varchar [note: 'Externally-visible unique reference number assigned to the data subject request for tracking and communication purposes.']
  request_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the data subject request: submitted (initial receipt), under_review (being assessed), identity_verification (verifying requestor identity), in_progress (actively being fulfilled), completed (fulfilled and closed), rejected (denied with justification), withdrawn (cancelled by requestor).']
  request_type varchar [note: 'Type of data subject request: access (right to access personal data), rectification (right to correct inaccurate data), erasure (right to be forgotten), restriction (right to restrict processing), portability (right to data portability), objection (right to object to processing).']
  requestor_email varchar [note: 'Email address of the individual submitting the data subject request for correspondence and notification purposes.']
  requestor_name varchar [note: 'Full name of the individual submitting the data subject request. May differ from passenger name if submitted by authorized representative.']
  requestor_phone varchar [note: 'Contact phone number of the individual submitting the data subject request.']
  response_format varchar [note: 'Format in which the data was provided to the data subject for access or portability requests: PDF, CSV, JSON, XML, or paper.']
  response_method varchar [note: 'Method used to deliver the response to the data subject: email, postal_mail, secure_portal, in_person, or phone.']
  submission_channel varchar [note: 'Channel through which the data subject request was submitted: web_portal, mobile_app, email, phone, postal_mail, or airport_counter.']
  submission_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the data subject request was originally submitted by the passenger or their authorized representative.']

  Note: 'Master reference table for data_subject_request. Referenced by data_subject_request_id.'
}

Table reservation.pnr {
  pnr_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique system identifier for the PNR. Primary key. Surrogate key for internal database operations.']
  campaign_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to marketing.campaign. Business justification: Airlines track which marketing campaigns drove bookings for ROI measurement and attribution modeling. Marketing teams require PNR-to-campaign linkage for performance reporting and budget allocation de']
  cdm_message_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to airport.cdm_message. Business justification: PNRs drive CDM messaging for collaborative decision-making. Flight coordination, delay management, and airport slot optimization require this link for operational efficiency and regulatory compliance.']
  company_code_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.company_code. Business justification: Every PNR is booked under a specific airline legal entity (company code) for revenue recognition, regulatory reporting (IATA, DOT), and interline settlement. Aviation domain experts expect this link f']
  ffp_member_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to loyalty.ffp_member. Business justification: PNRs for loyalty members drive mileage accrual, tier qualification, upgrade eligibility, benefit application. Core operational link for FFP program integration with reservations.']
  group_booking_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to reservation.group_booking. Business justification: A PNR can be part of a group booking master record (10+ passengers travelling together). This is the correct direction (many PNRs → one group booking). Column group_name can be retrieved via JOIN from']
  occurrence_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to safety.occurrence. Business justification: Reservation-level safety incidents (booking system failures causing operational disruption, fraud cases escalated to security/safety, group booking coordination failures affecting passenger safety, sp']
  org_unit_id varchar [note: 'Office ID of the airline or agency office responsible for this PNR. Used for accountability, reporting, and revenue allocation. Format varies by PSS (Passenger Service System).']
  agency_iata_number varchar [note: 'Eight-digit IATA numeric code identifying the travel agency that created the booking. Null for direct bookings. Used for commission calculation and BSP settlement.']
  base_fare_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'Base fare component of the total fare, excluding taxes, fees, and surcharges. Used for revenue accounting and interline proration.']
  booking_source varchar [note: 'Origin system or channel through which the PNR was created. Distinguishes between GDS (Global Distribution System), direct airline channels, NDC (New Distribution Capability), call center, mobile app, web, or airport kiosk. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: gds|direct|ndc|call_center|mobile_app|web|kiosk — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  booking_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the PNR. Indicates whether the reservation is active, on waitlist, cancelled, or archived after travel completion or expiry.']
  booking_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the booking based on passenger type and commercial arrangement. Distinguishes individual, group, corporate contract, interline, and codeshare bookings.']
  codeshare_indicator boolean [note: 'Flag indicating whether the PNR includes codeshare segments (flights marketed by one airline but operated by another). True if codeshare present, false otherwise.']
  contact_email varchar [note: 'Primary email address for PNR-level communication. Used for booking confirmations, itinerary changes, and operational notifications. May differ from individual passenger emails.']
  contact_phone varchar [note: 'Primary phone number for PNR-level communication. Used for operational notifications, IROP (Irregular Operations) alerts, and customer service contact.']
  corporate_account_number varchar [note: 'Corporate discount or contract account number associated with the booking. Used for negotiated fare application and corporate reporting.']
  creating_agent_sign varchar [note: 'Agent sign or user ID of the individual who created the PNR. Used for audit trail and quality control.']
  creation_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the PNR was first created in the reservation system. Represents the initial booking event. Format: yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for all monetary amounts in this PNR. Determines the currency for total fare, taxes, and fees.']
  departure_date date [note: 'Date of the first flight segment departure in the itinerary. Used for reporting and operational planning. Format: yyyy-MM-dd.']
  destination_city_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter IATA city code for the final arrival point in the itinerary. Used for origin-destination (O&D) analysis.']
  distribution_channel varchar [note: 'Specific distribution channel or sub-channel identifier. For GDS bookings, includes the GDS name (Amadeus, Sabre, Travelport). For direct bookings, includes the specific platform or partner.']
  form_of_payment_summary varchar [note: 'Summary description of the payment method(s) used for this PNR. May include credit card type, cash, voucher, or multiple forms of payment. Detailed payment data stored separately for PCI compliance.']
  interline_indicator boolean [note: 'Flag indicating whether the PNR includes segments operated by multiple airlines under interline agreements. True if interline, false if single-carrier.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time of the most recent modification to the PNR. Updated whenever any element of the PNR is changed (segments, passengers, services, remarks). Format: yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX.']
  last_modifying_agent_sign varchar [note: 'Agent sign or user ID of the individual who last modified the PNR. Updated with each change for audit trail.']
  origin_city_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter IATA city code for the first departure point in the itinerary. Used for origin-destination (O&D) analysis.']
  passenger_count varchar [note: 'Total number of passengers included in this PNR. Supports group bookings and family travel.']
  point_of_sale_city varchar [note: 'Three-letter IATA city code representing the city where the booking was made. Used for market analysis and revenue management.']
  point_of_sale_country varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO country code representing the country where the booking was made. Critical for revenue accounting, tax calculation, and regulatory reporting.']
  purge_date date [note: 'Date when the PNR is scheduled to be purged or archived from the active reservation system. Typically set based on last travel date plus retention period. Format: yyyy-MM-dd.']
  queue_number varchar [note: 'PSS queue number where the PNR is currently placed for agent action. Used for workflow management and ticketing time limit monitoring.']
  record_locator varchar [note: 'Six-character alphanumeric code uniquely identifying the PNR across systems. The externally-known business identifier used by agents, passengers, and partner systems. Also known as booking reference or confirmation code.']
  return_date date [note: 'Date of the return flight segment departure for round-trip itineraries. Null for one-way or multi-city trips. Format: yyyy-MM-dd.']
  segment_count varchar [note: 'Total number of flight segments (legs) included in this PNR itinerary. Each flight leg is counted separately.']
  special_service_request_codes varchar [note: 'Comma-separated list of four-letter SSR codes applicable to the entire PNR (e.g., WCHR for wheelchair, PETC for pet in cabin). Individual passenger SSRs stored separately.']
  tax_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'Total tax amount for the PNR, including government taxes, airport taxes, and other mandatory levies. Expressed in the currency specified by currency_code.']
  ticketed_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the PNR was ticketed (e-ticket issued). Null if not yet ticketed. Format: yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX.']
  ticketing_time_limit timestamp [note: 'Deadline by which the PNR must be ticketed or the reservation will be automatically cancelled. Critical for revenue management and inventory control. Format: yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX.']
  total_fare_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'Total fare amount for all passengers and segments in the PNR, including base fare, taxes, fees, and surcharges. Expressed in the currency specified by currency_code.']
  tour_code varchar [note: 'Tour operator or bulk fare contract code associated with the PNR. Used for special fare validation and commission calculation.']
  trip_type varchar [note: 'Type of itinerary structure. One-way (single segment), round-trip (outbound and return), multi-city (multiple destinations), or open-jaw (different origin/destination for outbound and return).']

  Note: 'Passenger Name Record (PNR) — the master booking record and SSOT for all reservation data within the airline. Captures the full PNR lifecycle from creation through ticketing, modification, and archival/purge. Stores PNR record locator (6-character alphanumeric), booking source (GDS/direct/NDC), distribution channel, booking date, ticketing time limit (TTL), booking status (confirmed/waitlisted/cancelled/archived), currency, total fare amount, form of payment summary, agency IATA number, responsible office ID, creating agent sign, creation timestamp, last modification timestamp, number of passengers, number of segments, and PNR purge date. Central hub entity to which all other reservation products relate. Aligns with Amadeus Altéa Reservation and IATA RP 1745 PNR standards.'
}

Table reservation.itinerary_segment {
  itinerary_segment_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the itinerary segment. Primary key for this entity representing a single booked flight leg within a PNR.']
  aircraft_type_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to fleet.aircraft_type. Business justification: Flight planning, schedule optimization, passenger notifications, and operational assignment all require knowing which aircraft type operates each segment. Equipment_type_code is denormalized ICAO/IATA']
  award_inventory_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to loyalty.award_inventory. Business justification: Award bookings consume specific award inventory allocations per segment. Business process: award seat allocation tracking, inventory management, waitlist clearance, dynamic award pricing.']
  cabin_class_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to inventory.cabin_class. Business justification: Segments book into specific cabin classes (First, Business, Premium Economy, Economy). This link is essential for cabin-level load factor reporting, service delivery (meal type, baggage allowance, lou']
  cabin_configuration_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to fleet.cabin_configuration. Business justification: Revenue management, seat inventory control, ancillary pricing (preferred seats, extra legroom), and passenger service delivery depend on knowing exact cabin layout for each segment. Cabin configuratio']
  campaign_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to marketing.campaign. Business justification: Revenue management analyzes which campaigns drive bookings on specific routes and flights for pricing strategy optimization. Segment-level campaign attribution enables route-specific promotional effec']
  cdm_message_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to airport.cdm_message. Business justification: Flight segments generate CDM milestone messages (TOBT, EOBT, TSAT). Airport coordination, slot management, and turnaround optimization require this link for collaborative decision-making processes.']
  cost_centre_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.cost_centre. Business justification: Flight segments are attributed to cost centers (route, hub, region) for management accounting, CASK/RASK analysis, and route profitability reporting. Essential for operational finance and network plan']
  departure_station_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to airport.station. Business justification: Every flight segment departs from a station. Flight planning, crew scheduling, slot coordination, ground handling, and operational reporting all require this link. Core operational relationship in air']
  ffp_member_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to loyalty.ffp_member. Business justification: Segment-level FFP tracking required for mileage accrual calculation, tier qualifying segment counting, partner airline crediting. Business process: post-flight accrual posting per segment flown.']
  flight_inventory_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to inventory.flight_inventory. Business justification: Every booked segment must reference the inventory control record for the specific flight being sold. This is the foundational link enabling revenue management to track bookings against authorized capa']
  flight_number_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to route.flight_number. Business justification: Segments are booked on specific flight numbers. Critical for inventory control, codeshare management, schedule change processing, and GDS distribution. Core airline reservation system requirement link']
  occurrence_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to safety.occurrence. Business justification: Flight segment-specific safety occurrences (in-flight turbulence injuries, cabin incidents, medical emergencies, passenger disturbances) require linkage to the itinerary segment for passenger manifest']
  pnr_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the parent PNR containing this segment. Links this flight leg to the overall booking record.']
  route_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to route.route. Business justification: Every booked segment operates on a defined route in the network. Essential for schedule planning, revenue management, network analysis, and operational reporting. Aviation standard practice to link bo']
  slot_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to airport.slot. Business justification: Flight segments at slot-coordinated airports consume allocated slots. Slot compliance monitoring, historic rights protection, and regulatory reporting (IATA slot coordination) require this link for sc']
  turnaround_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to airport.turnaround. Business justification: Flight segments are served by turnarounds. Operational planning (minimum ground time), delay attribution, and ground handling cost allocation require this link for operational efficiency and cost mana']
  action_code varchar [note: 'Transaction action code representing the booking action taken. NN=Need, HN=Have need, HK=Holding confirmed, KK=Confirmed, UC=Unable to confirm, UN=Unable need, NO=No action, HL=Have waitlist, KL=Confirmed from waitlist, TK=Schedule change confirmed, TL=Schedule change waitlist, HX=Have cancel, XX=Cancel confirmed, WK=Waitlist, RQ=On request. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: NN|HN|HK|KK|UC|UN|NO|HL|KL|TK|TL|HX|XX|WK|RQ — 15 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  arrival_airport_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter IATA airport code for the destination airport of this segment.']
  baggage_allowance varchar [note: 'Baggage allowance for this segment expressed in piece concept (e.g., 2PC) or weight concept (e.g., 23KG). Determined by fare class and route.']
  booking_channel_code varchar [note: 'Channel through which this segment was booked. GDS=Global Distribution System, WEB=Website, MOB=Mobile App, CTO=City Ticket Office, RES=Reservations Call Center, API=Direct API.']
  booking_class_code varchar [note: 'Single-letter Reservation Booking Designator (RBD) representing the fare class booked. Determines inventory bucket and fare rules.']
  codeshare_indicator boolean [note: 'Flag indicating whether this segment is a codeshare flight where marketing and operating carriers differ.']
  endorsement_restrictions varchar [note: 'Free-text field containing fare rule endorsements and restrictions applicable to this segment (e.g., NON-REF, CHG FEE APPLIES).']
  fare_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'Base fare amount allocated to this segment for fare construction and revenue accounting purposes. Excludes taxes and fees.']
  fare_basis_code varchar [note: 'Alphanumeric code identifying the specific fare product and associated rules. Encodes booking class, seasonality, restrictions, and fare type.']
  fare_currency_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for the fare amount.']
  flown_indicator boolean [note: 'Flag indicating whether the passenger has completed travel on this segment. Set to true after departure or boarding confirmation.']
  gds_pcc_code varchar [note: 'GDS Pseudo City Code identifying the travel agency or corporate booking location that created this segment reservation.']
  interline_indicator boolean [note: 'Flag indicating whether this segment involves interline ticketing with a different carrier than adjacent segments in the itinerary.']
  marketing_carrier_code varchar [note: 'Two-character IATA airline code of the carrier marketing and selling this segment. May differ from operating carrier in codeshare scenarios.']
  marketing_flight_number varchar [note: 'Flight number as marketed to the passenger. Includes numeric identifier and optional suffix letter.']
  marriage_group_indicator varchar [note: 'Single-letter code grouping segments that must be priced together as a married segment. Segments with the same indicator are married for fare construction.']
  meal_service_code varchar [note: 'IATA meal service code indicating the type of meal service provided. B=Breakfast, K=Continental Breakfast, L=Lunch, D=Dinner, S=Snack, V=Refreshment, M=Meal, R=Refreshment for purchase, G=Food for purchase, H=Hot meal, C=Alcoholic beverages for purchase, F=Food and beverages for purchase, P=Alcoholic beverages complimentary, O=Cold meal, N=No meal service. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: B|K|L|D|S|V|M|R|G|H|C|F|P|O|N — 15 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  number_of_seats varchar [note: 'Quantity of seats booked on this segment for the associated passengers in the PNR.']
  operating_carrier_code varchar [note: 'Two-character IATA airline code of the carrier physically operating this flight. Populated when segment is a codeshare.']
  operating_flight_number varchar [note: 'Actual flight number operated by the operating carrier. Populated when segment is a codeshare and differs from marketing flight number.']
  schedule_change_indicator boolean [note: 'Flag indicating whether this segment has experienced a schedule change since original booking, requiring passenger notification and acceptance.']
  scheduled_arrival_datetime timestamp [note: 'Scheduled arrival date and time in local airport time zone. Represents the planned gate arrival time at booking.']
  scheduled_departure_datetime timestamp [note: 'Scheduled departure date and time in local airport time zone. Represents the planned gate departure time at booking.']
  segment_created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this segment was first added to the PNR in the reservation system.']
  segment_mileage varchar [note: 'Great circle distance in miles between departure and arrival airports. Used for fare construction and frequent flyer accrual.']
  segment_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this segment was last modified (status change, schedule change, or other update).']
  segment_sequence_number varchar [note: 'Sequential ordering of this segment within the PNR itinerary. Determines the travel flow from origin to final destination.']
  segment_status_code varchar [note: 'Current reservation status of the segment. HK=Confirmed, KK=Confirmed by carrier, KL=Confirmed waitlist cleared, HL=Have requested waitlist, UN=Unable to confirm, UC=Unable to accept, NO=No action taken, WK=Waitlisted, RQ=Requested, TK=Schedule change confirmed, TL=Schedule change waitlist. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: HK|KK|KL|HL|UN|UC|NO|WK|RQ|TK|TL — 11 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  stopover_indicator boolean [note: 'Flag indicating whether this segment represents a stopover (stay exceeding MCT) versus a connection in the passenger journey.']
  ticketing_time_limit timestamp [note: 'Deadline by which this segment must be ticketed or the reservation will be automatically cancelled. Set by fare rules or airline policy.']
  tour_code varchar [note: 'Alphanumeric code identifying a tour package or bulk fare contract associated with this segment. Used for negotiated or inclusive tour fares.']

  Note: 'Individual flight segment within a PNR itinerary. Represents a single booked flight leg including operating carrier code, flight number, departure airport IATA code, arrival airport IATA code, scheduled departure date and time, scheduled arrival date and time, booking class (RBD), cabin class, fare basis code, segment status code (HK/KL/UN/NO), number of seats, codeshare indicator, operating carrier flight number, marketing carrier code, equipment type, and segment sequence number. Core operational unit of the itinerary.'
}

Table reservation.e_ticket {
  e_ticket_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for e_ticket']
  accounts_receivable_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.accounts_receivable. Business justification: Tickets generate accounts receivable entries in BSP/ARC settlement cycles. Airlines track AR aging, credit control, and cash collection by ticket. Natural link for revenue accounting and cash flow man']
  campaign_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to marketing.campaign. Business justification: Revenue accounting attributes ticket revenue to marketing campaigns for final ROI calculation after ticketing. Finance reconciles marketing spend against attributed ticket revenue to measure campaign ']
  fare_quote_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to reservation.fare_quote. Business justification: E-ticket is issued based on a stored fare quote within the PNR. Linking to fare_quote provides the authoritative pricing record and fare calculation details. Columns fare_basis_code and fare_calculati']
  org_unit_id varchar [note: 'IATA 9-character office identifier (Office ID) of the location or agency that issued the ticket. Used for settlement and commission tracking.']
  pax_profile_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the passenger for whom this electronic ticket was issued. Links ticket to passenger master data.']
  pnr_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the Passenger Name Record (PNR) associated with this electronic ticket. Links the ticket to the booking record.']
  base_fare_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'Base fare amount for the ticket before taxes and fees. Represents the airline\'s revenue portion of the ticket price.']
  commission_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'Commission amount paid to the issuing agent or travel agency for selling the ticket. Used for BSP/ARC settlement.']
  commission_percentage decimal(5,2) [note: 'Commission rate percentage applied to calculate the commission amount. Null if flat commission was used.']
  conjunction_ticket_indicator boolean [note: 'Flag indicating whether this ticket is part of a conjunction ticket set (multiple tickets issued together for a single journey exceeding 4 flight coupons).']
  conjunction_ticket_numbers varchar [note: 'Comma-separated list of related conjunction ticket numbers if this ticket is part of a multi-ticket set. Empty if not a conjunction ticket.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this e-ticket record was first created in the reservation system. Used for audit trail and data lineage.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'ISO 4217 3-letter currency code for all monetary amounts on this ticket (base fare, tax, total).']
  endorsement_restrictions varchar [note: 'Free-text field containing fare restrictions, endorsements, and special conditions applicable to the ticket (e.g., NON-REFUNDABLE, VALID ON XX ONLY).']
  equivalent_currency_code varchar [note: 'ISO 4217 3-letter currency code for the equivalent fare amount. Null if no currency conversion was applied.']
  equivalent_fare_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'Fare amount converted to the currency of the country of commencement of travel, if different from ticket currency. Used for settlement purposes.']
  exchange_date date [note: 'Date when the ticket was exchanged for a new ticket. Null if ticket has not been exchanged.']
  exchange_ticket_indicator boolean [note: 'Flag indicating whether this ticket was issued as an exchange for a previous ticket. True if this is a reissued ticket.']
  form_of_payment_details varchar [note: 'Detailed payment information including masked card number, approval code, or account reference. Sensitive financial data stored according to PCI-DSS requirements.']
  form_of_payment_type varchar [note: 'Primary payment method used to purchase the ticket. Identifies how the passenger paid for the ticket. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: CASH|CREDIT_CARD|DEBIT_CARD|VOUCHER|MILES|CORPORATE_ACCOUNT|INVOICE — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  gds_pcc varchar [note: 'GDS Pseudo City Code (PCC) of the agency or office that created the booking. Used for GDS reporting and commission tracking.']
  gds_record_locator varchar [note: 'GDS-specific booking reference number (record locator) linking the ticket to the GDS reservation. May differ from airline PNR.']
  in_connection_with_ticket varchar [note: 'Ticket number of a related ticket when this ticket is issued in connection with another (e.g., companion ticket, family booking). Null if standalone.']
  issue_date date [note: 'Date when the electronic ticket was issued to the passenger. Represents the contractual agreement date.']
  issue_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Precise date and time when the electronic ticket was issued, including timezone. Used for audit and settlement purposes.']
  issuing_airline_code varchar [note: 'IATA 2-letter or 3-digit airline code of the carrier that issued the electronic ticket. Identifies the validating carrier responsible for the ticket.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this e-ticket record was last updated. Tracks the most recent change to any field in the record.']
  original_ticket_number varchar [note: '13-digit ticket number of the original ticket if this is an exchange or reissue. Null for original issuances. Used to track ticket exchange history.']
  refund_indicator boolean [note: 'Flag indicating whether a refund has been processed for this ticket. True if ticket has been refunded (fully or partially).']
  tax_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'Total tax amount collected on the ticket. Sum of all applicable government taxes, airport fees, and surcharges.']
  ticket_designator varchar [note: 'Special fare qualifier or corporate discount code applied to the ticket. Used for negotiated corporate rates or promotional fares.']
  ticket_number varchar [note: '13-digit IATA standard ticket number uniquely identifying the electronic ticket document. Format: 3-digit airline code + 10-digit serial number. This is the contractual document of carriage identifier.']
  ticket_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the electronic ticket. OPEN=valid for travel, USED=travel completed, VOID=cancelled before use, REFUNDED=refund processed, EXCHANGED=replaced by new ticket, SUSPENDED=temporarily invalid.']
  total_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'Total amount paid for the ticket including base fare, taxes, and all fees. This is the final price paid by the passenger.']
  tour_code varchar [note: 'Tour operator or bulk fare identifier if the ticket was issued under a tour or group contract. Null for regular published fares.']
  validating_carrier_code varchar [note: 'IATA airline code of the carrier responsible for fare rules, refunds, and exchanges. May differ from issuing airline in interline scenarios.']
  void_date date [note: 'Date when the ticket was voided. Null if ticket has not been voided. Voiding typically occurs within 24 hours of issuance.']

  Note: 'Electronic ticket (e-ticket) record representing the contractual document of carriage issued to a passenger. Stores ticket number (13-digit IATA format), conjunction ticket numbers, issuing airline code, issuing office IATA number, ticket issue date and time, ticket status (OPEN/USED/VOID/REFUNDED/EXCHANGED), fare amount, tax breakdown, total amount, form of payment, fare calculation string, endorsement/restriction text, tour code, commission amount, and original ticket number for exchanges. SSOT for ticketing lifecycle. Aligns with Amadeus Altéa ticketing module.'
}

Table reservation.reservation_booking_passenger {
  reservation_booking_passenger_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the passenger name element (PNE) within the PNR. This is the booking-scoped passenger reference; the canonical passenger identity master is owned by the passenger domain.']
  pax_profile_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key reference to the canonical passenger master record in the passenger domain. Enables cross-booking passenger recognition and loyalty program linkage.']
  pnr_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key reference to the parent PNR booking record. Links this passenger name element to the reservation container.']
  apis_data_complete_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether all required APIS data elements (name, DOB, gender, nationality, passport) have been collected for this passenger. Required for international travel compliance.']
  associated_infant_indicator boolean [note: 'Flag indicating whether this adult passenger has an associated infant (lap child) traveling with them. Used for seat assignment and boarding validation.']
  boarding_pass_issued_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether a boarding pass has been issued to this passenger for at least one segment in the itinerary.']
  booking_status varchar [note: 'Current reservation status of this passenger within the PNR. Reflects confirmation, waitlist, cancellation, or no-show state.']
  check_in_status varchar [note: 'Current check-in and boarding status of the passenger. Tracks progression from reservation through boarding.']
  check_in_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the passenger completed check-in for the first flight segment in the itinerary. Used for boarding priority and operational reporting.']
  contact_email varchar [note: 'Primary email address for this passenger. Used for e-ticket delivery, flight notifications, and IROP communication.']
  contact_phone_number varchar [note: 'Primary contact phone number for this passenger. Used for flight notifications, IROP rebooking, and operational communication.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this passenger name element was first added to the PNR. Audit trail for booking creation.']
  date_of_birth date [note: 'Date of birth of the passenger. Required for infant and child passengers, and for APIS (Advanced Passenger Information System) compliance on international flights.']
  emergency_contact_name varchar [note: 'Full name of the passenger\'s emergency contact person. Collected for safety and incident response purposes.']
  emergency_contact_phone varchar [note: 'Phone number of the passenger\'s emergency contact person. Used in case of flight incidents or medical emergencies.']
  first_name varchar [note: 'Given name of the passenger as it appears on travel documents. Must match government-issued ID for international travel.']
  frequent_flyer_number varchar [note: 'Passenger\'s frequent flyer membership number for the operating carrier or alliance partner. Used for mileage accrual, tier recognition, and SSR processing.']
  frequent_flyer_tier_code varchar [note: 'Current tier or status level of the passenger in the frequent flyer program. Used for priority boarding, lounge access, and upgrade eligibility.']
  gender varchar [note: 'Gender of the passenger as recorded in the booking. M=Male, F=Female, U=Unspecified, X=Non-binary. Required for APIS compliance.']
  known_traveller_number varchar [note: 'TSA PreCheck or other trusted traveler program number. Enables expedited security screening for eligible passengers on US domestic and international flights.']
  last_name varchar [note: 'Family name or surname of the passenger as it appears on travel documents. Must match government-issued ID for international travel.']
  meal_preference_code varchar [note: 'IATA standard meal code representing the passenger\'s dietary preference or restriction. VGML=Vegetarian, HNML=Hindu, KSML=Kosher, MOML=Muslim, AVML=Asian Vegetarian, DBML=Diabetic. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: REGULAR|VGML|HNML|KSML|MOML|AVML|DBML — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  middle_name varchar [note: 'Middle name or initial of the passenger. Optional field used when provided on travel documents.']
  modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this passenger name element was last modified. Tracks name corrections, SSR updates, and status changes.']
  nationality_country_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 country code representing the passenger\'s nationality. Required for APIS and visa validation.']
  passenger_sequence_number varchar [note: 'Sequential ordering of this passenger within the PNR. Used for display, SSR association, and segment assignment. Typically starts at 1.']
  passenger_type_code varchar [note: 'IATA passenger type code indicating age category and fare eligibility. ADT=Adult, CHD=Child, INF=Infant (lap), INS=Infant (seat), YTH=Youth, UNN=Unaccompanied Minor.']
  redress_number varchar [note: 'DHS Traveler Redress Inquiry Program (TRIP) number issued to passengers who have been misidentified during security screening. Prevents false matches on watchlists.']
  seat_preference_code varchar [note: 'Passenger\'s preferred seat location type. Used by seat assignment algorithms and displayed to agents during check-in.']
  source_system_code varchar [note: 'Identifies the PSS or GDS system through which this passenger name element was created. ALTEA=Amadeus Altéa, DIRECT=airline.com or mobile app.']
  special_service_request_count varchar [note: 'Total number of SSR records associated with this passenger in the PNR. Includes wheelchair assistance, unaccompanied minor service, pet in cabin, extra baggage, etc.']
  suffix varchar [note: 'Generational or professional suffix appended to the passenger name (e.g., Jr., Sr., III). Used for name disambiguation.']
  ticket_issue_date date [note: 'Date when the e-ticket was issued to this passenger. Used for fare rule validation and refund calculations.']
  ticket_number varchar [note: '13-digit IATA e-ticket number issued to this passenger. Format: 3-digit airline code + 10-digit serial number. Links to the ticket coupon records.']
  title varchar [note: 'Honorific title of the passenger as recorded in the booking. Used for ticket issuance and customer communication. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: MR|MS|MRS|MISS|DR|PROF|MSTR — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  unaccompanied_minor_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this passenger is traveling as an unaccompanied minor and requires special handling, escort service, and guardian handoff procedures.']
  wheelchair_assistance_code varchar [note: 'IATA SSR code indicating level of wheelchair assistance required. WCHR=Can walk short distances, WCHS=Cannot climb stairs, WCHC=Immobile, requires wheelchair throughout.']

  Note: 'Passenger name element (PNE) within a PNR — the reservation-context association of a traveller to a booking. Stores passenger title, first name, last name, suffix, passenger type code (ADT/CHD/INF/INS/YTH/UNN), date of birth, gender, nationality, associated infant indicator, frequent flyer number and tier (for display/SSR purposes), known traveller number (KTN), redress number, APIS data completeness flag, seat preference (window/aisle/none), meal preference code, SSR count, and passenger sequence number within the PNR. This is the booking-scoped passenger reference; the canonical passenger identity master is owned by the passenger domain.'
}

Table reservation.ssr {
  ssr_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the special service request record. Primary key.']
  employee_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the travel agent, airline staff member, or system user who created or last modified this SSR.']
  itinerary_segment_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the specific flight segment to which this SSR applies. Nullable if SSR applies to all segments.']
  member_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to crew.crew_member. Business justification: SSRs like wheelchair assistance, unaccompanied minor supervision are fulfilled by specific cabin crew members. Airlines track which crew member provided the service for quality assurance, regulatory c']
  occurrence_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to safety.occurrence. Business justification: Special service request failures (wheelchair assistance not provided causing injury, medical oxygen equipment malfunction, unaccompanied minor supervision lapses, service animal incidents) are safety ']
  org_unit_id varchar [note: 'IATA office identifier (9-character alphanumeric) of the agency or airline office that created this SSR.']
  pax_profile_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the specific passenger within the PNR for whom this SSR applies. Nullable if SSR applies to all passengers.']
  pnr_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the PNR to which this SSR is attached.']
  ramp_service_order_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to airport.ramp_service_order. Business justification: Special service requests (wheelchair, stretcher, oxygen) require ramp service coordination. Ground handling service delivery, equipment allocation, and SLA tracking depend on this link for operational']
  regulatory_requirement_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.regulatory_requirement. Business justification: SSRs for wheelchair, medical oxygen, service animals trigger ADA/ACAA/EU accessibility compliance requirements. Special services must satisfy regulatory mandates; operations validate SSR against appli']
  reservation_booking_passenger_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to reservation.booking_passenger. Business justification: SSR (Special Service Request) is often attached to a specific passenger name element within the PNR. While ssr has pax_profile_id, it should also link to the booking-context passenger to maintain the ']
  vendor_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to procurement.vendor. Business justification: Airlines contract third-party vendors (wheelchair assistance, special meal catering, medical oxygen suppliers) to fulfill SSRs at stations. Operations teams track which vendor fulfilled each SSR for i']
  action_required_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether operational action is required by airline staff to fulfill this SSR (e.g., wheelchair provision, meal loading, document verification).']
  apis_required_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this SSR is required for APIS compliance (e.g., DOCS, DOCA, DOCO SSRs for border control and security).']
  cancellation_reason varchar [note: 'Free-text explanation of why this SSR was cancelled (e.g., passenger request, flight change, service unavailable).']
  cancelled_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this SSR was cancelled or removed from the PNR. Null if still active.']
  charge_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'Monetary amount charged for this SSR if charge_applicable_flag is true. Null if complimentary.']
  charge_applicable_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this SSR incurs a charge to the passenger (e.g., paid meal upgrade, pet in cabin fee). False for complimentary services.']
  charge_currency_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for the charge amount (e.g., USD, EUR, GBP).']
  codeshare_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this SSR applies to a codeshare flight segment where the marketing and operating carriers differ.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this SSR record was first created in the reservation system.']
  creation_source varchar [note: 'Channel or system through which the SSR was originally created (e.g., GDS, airline website, mobile app, call center, airport check-in, API integration). [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: gds|web|mobile|call_center|airport|api|amadeus|sabre|galileo|worldspan — 10 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  emd_number varchar [note: '13-digit EMD number issued for this SSR if it is a paid ancillary service. Null if no EMD issued.']
  expiry_date date [note: 'Date after which this SSR is no longer valid or applicable. Relevant for time-sensitive SSRs such as travel documents or temporary medical conditions.']
  free_text varchar [note: 'Free-text field containing additional details or remarks associated with the SSR. May include passenger-specific instructions, medical details, or document numbers.']
  fulfillment_status varchar [note: 'Operational status indicating whether the SSR has been fulfilled by ground or cabin crew (e.g., wheelchair provided, meal loaded, document verified).']
  fulfillment_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the SSR was marked as fulfilled by operational staff. Null if not yet fulfilled.']
  gds_record_locator varchar [note: 'Six-character alphanumeric GDS record locator (PNR locator) in the originating GDS system (Amadeus, Sabre, Galileo, Worldspan).']
  interline_agreement_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this SSR is subject to interline agreement terms between multiple carriers in a multi-carrier itinerary.']
  modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this SSR record was last modified or updated.']
  operating_airline_code varchar [note: 'Two-character IATA airline code of the carrier operating the flight segment to which this SSR applies. May differ from responsible airline in codeshare scenarios.']
  priority_level varchar [note: 'Operational priority assigned to this SSR for fulfillment and crew attention (e.g., critical for medical oxygen, high for wheelchair, medium for meal).']
  quantity varchar [note: 'Number of units or instances of this SSR requested (e.g., 2 wheelchairs, 3 special meals). Defaults to 1 if not specified.']
  regulatory_compliance_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this SSR is mandated by regulatory or security requirements (e.g., TSA, FAA, EASA, national civil aviation authorities).']
  remarks varchar [note: 'Additional internal remarks or notes related to this SSR for operational or customer service reference.']
  responsible_airline_code varchar [note: 'Two-character IATA airline code of the carrier responsible for fulfilling this SSR. Relevant for codeshare and interline bookings.']
  sequence_number varchar [note: 'Sequential ordering number of this SSR within the PNR or segment, used for display and processing order.']
  ssr_category varchar [note: 'Business classification of the SSR for operational routing and fulfillment (e.g., meal service, mobility assistance, travel documentation, frequent flyer program). [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: meal|wheelchair|medical|document|loyalty|security|baggage|pet|infant|unaccompanied_minor|seating|other — 12 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  ssr_code varchar [note: 'Four-letter IATA SSR code identifying the type of special service (e.g., WCHR for wheelchair, VGML for vegetarian meal, UMNR for unaccompanied minor, DOCS for travel document, FQTV for frequent flyer).']
  ssr_status varchar [note: 'Current status of the SSR. HK=confirmed by airline, KK=confirmed by carrier, UN=unable to confirm, NO=not processed, NN=need confirmation, UC=unable to confirm space, UU=unconfirmed, RQ=requested. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: HK|KK|UN|NO|NN|UC|UU|RQ — 8 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']

  Note: 'Special Service Request (SSR) record attached to a PNR or specific segment/passenger combination. Captures SSR code (WCHR/VGML/UMNR/DOCS/DOCA/DOCO/FQTV etc.), SSR status (HK/KK/UN/NO/NN), free-text SSR remark, airline responsible for action, segment applicability, passenger applicability, SSR category (meal/wheelchair/document/loyalty/security), creation source, and action required flag. Supports operational fulfilment of passenger special needs and regulatory APIS/DOCA/DOCO document collection.'
}

Table reservation.boarding_pass {
  boarding_pass_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the boarding pass record. Primary key. Inferred role: TRANSACTION_HEADER (discrete check-in event with lifecycle).']
  aircraft_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to fleet.aircraft. Business justification: Gate agents, operations control, maintenance tracking, and post-flight reconciliation require knowing which specific tail number a passenger boarded. Critical for weight-and-balance verification, IROP']
  consumer_protection_case_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.consumer_protection_case. Business justification: Denied boarding, delay compensation (EU261/DOT) cases reference specific boarding passes. Customer service and legal teams link boarding pass to case for compensation calculation and regulatory report']
  departure_station_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to airport.station. Business justification: Boarding passes are issued at departure stations. Passenger services, gate operations, boarding metrics, and regulatory reporting (e.g., DOT on-time performance) require station linkage for operationa']
  flight_leg_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key reference to the specific flight leg (operating flight) for which this boarding pass is valid.']
  flight_number_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to route.flight_number. Business justification: Boarding passes are issued for specific flight numbers. Required for gate management, boarding control, operational reporting, and passenger flow analytics. Standard airport operations link boarding d']
  gate_assignment_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to airport.gate_assignment. Business justification: Boarding passes are issued for specific gate assignments. Operational coordination between reservation systems and airport operations (gate changes, boarding status) requires this link for real-time p']
  gate_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to airport.gate. Business justification: Boarding passes specify gate assignments for passenger boarding. Gate utilization tracking, passenger flow management, and boarding sequence coordination require this link. Core operational relationsh']
  itinerary_segment_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to reservation.itinerary_segment. Business justification: Boarding pass is issued for a specific booked segment in the PNR itinerary. While boarding_pass has flight_leg_id (operational flight), it should also link to the booked segment to maintain the reserv']
  occurrence_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to safety.occurrence. Business justification: Boarding process incidents (passenger slip/fall at gate, boarding bridge malfunction, gate area injuries) are safety occurrences requiring linkage to the specific boarding pass for passenger identific']
  pax_profile_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key reference to the passenger (party) to whom this boarding pass is issued. Satisfies TRANSACTION_HEADER minimum requirement for PARTY_REFERENCE.']
  pfc_record_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to airport.pfc_record. Business justification: Boarding passes represent flown segments that trigger PFC collection for US domestic flights. Revenue accounting, regulatory reporting (DOT), and airport authority remittance require this link.']
  pnr_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key reference to the PNR (Passenger Name Record) that this boarding pass is issued under. Links to the reservation master record.']
  primary_baggage_item_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to airport.baggage_item. Business justification: Boarding passes are issued to passengers who check baggage. Baggage reconciliation (positive passenger bag match), tracking, and irregularity investigation require this link for operational safety and']
  primary_boarding_employee_id varchar [note: 'Employee identifier of the gate agent who scanned the boarding pass at boarding. Null if self-scanned or not yet boarded.']
  reservation_booking_passenger_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to reservation.booking_passenger. Business justification: Boarding pass is issued to a specific passenger name element (PNE) within the PNR. While boarding_pass has pax_profile_id (master profile), it should also link to the booking-context passenger record ']
  ticket_coupon_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key reference to the specific ticket coupon (flight segment) for which this boarding pass is issued.']
  arrival_airport_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter IATA code of the arrival airport for this flight segment.']
  baggage_tag_numbers varchar [note: 'Comma-separated list of baggage tag numbers (10-digit IATA license plate codes) for checked bags associated with this boarding pass.']
  barcode_data varchar [note: 'Encoded barcode data string conforming to IATA BCBP Type M format. Contains passenger name, flight details, seat, boarding pass number, and security data. Confidential due to embedded passenger information.']
  boarding_group varchar [note: 'Descriptive boarding group name assigned to the passenger (e.g., priority, general, family). Complements boarding_zone with business-friendly label.']
