  boarding_pass_number varchar [note: 'Unique boarding pass sequence number issued by the check-in system. Business identifier for the boarding pass. Satisfies TRANSACTION_HEADER minimum requirement for BUSINESS_IDENTIFIER.']
  boarding_pass_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the boarding pass. Tracks progression from issuance through boarding gate scan. Satisfies TRANSACTION_HEADER minimum requirement for LIFECYCLE_STATUS.']
  boarding_scan_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the boarding pass barcode was scanned at the gate, confirming passenger boarded the aircraft. Null if not yet scanned.']
  boarding_time timestamp [note: 'Scheduled boarding commencement time printed on the boarding pass. Advises passengers when to arrive at the gate.']
  boarding_zone varchar [note: 'Boarding zone or group assigned to the passenger for sequenced boarding. May be numeric (1-9) or alphabetic (A-Z) depending on airline policy.']
  check_in_counter_number varchar [note: 'Counter or kiosk number where the boarding pass was issued. Null for web or mobile check-in.']
  check_in_location_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter IATA airport code where the boarding pass was issued. May differ from departure airport for interline or codeshare itineraries.']
  check_in_method varchar [note: 'Channel through which the passenger completed check-in and received the boarding pass. CUSS = Common Use Self-Service.']
  checked_bag_count varchar [note: 'Number of checked bags associated with this boarding pass at check-in.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this boarding pass record was first created in the system. Satisfies TRANSACTION_HEADER minimum requirement for RECORD_AUDIT_CREATED.']
  document_check_status varchar [note: 'Status of travel document verification (passport, visa, ID) at check-in. Required for international flights.']
  fast_track_eligible_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the passenger is eligible for fast-track security or priority boarding based on fare class, status, or purchase.']
  format varchar [note: 'Physical or digital format of the boarding pass issued to the passenger. BCBP = Bar Coded Boarding Pass (2D barcode).']
  issue_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the boarding pass was first issued by the check-in system. Satisfies TRANSACTION_HEADER minimum requirement for BUSINESS_EVENT_TIMESTAMP.']
  last_reprint_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent boarding pass reprint event. Null if never reprinted.']
  lounge_access_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the passenger has lounge access entitlement printed on the boarding pass.']
  marketing_carrier_code varchar [note: 'Two-character IATA airline code of the marketing carrier (airline selling the ticket). May differ from operating carrier for codeshare flights.']
  marketing_flight_number varchar [note: 'Flight number as marketed to the passenger. May differ from operating flight number for codeshare flights.']
  operating_carrier_code varchar [note: 'Two-character IATA airline code of the operating carrier (airline physically operating the flight).']
  operating_flight_number varchar [note: 'Flight number of the operating carrier. Authoritative flight identifier for operations.']
  reprint_count varchar [note: 'Number of times this boarding pass has been reprinted (e.g., at kiosk, counter, or gate). Zero indicates original issuance.']
  scheduled_departure_time timestamp [note: 'Scheduled departure time (STD) of the flight as printed on the boarding pass. Local time at departure airport.']
  seat_number varchar [note: 'Seat assignment at the time of boarding pass issuance. Format: row number (1-3 digits) followed by seat letter (A-K, excluding I).']
  security_selectee_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the passenger has been selected for additional security screening by TSA or equivalent authority. Confidential security data.']
  sequence_number varchar [note: 'Sequential boarding pass number within the flight leg. Used for boarding order analytics and operational reporting.']
  special_service_codes varchar [note: 'Comma-separated list of IATA SSR codes applicable to this boarding pass (e.g., WCHR for wheelchair, PETC for pet in cabin, UMNR for unaccompanied minor).']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this boarding pass record was last modified. Satisfies TRANSACTION_HEADER minimum requirement for RECORD_AUDIT_UPDATED.']

  Note: 'Boarding pass record issued to a passenger for a specific flight segment at check-in. Stores boarding pass sequence number, check-in method (web/mobile/kiosk/counter/CUSS), boarding pass format (BCBP/paper/mobile), barcode data (IATA BCBP Type M), gate number, boarding time, seat number at issuance, boarding zone/group, baggage tag numbers, security selectee flag, document check status, boarding pass issue timestamp, and boarding pass reprint count. SSOT for check-in and boarding event data.'
}

Table reservation.check_in_event {
  check_in_event_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the check-in event transaction. Primary key for the check-in event record.']
  aircraft_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to fleet.aircraft. Business justification: Check-in systems must record tail number for operational reporting, weight-and-balance calculations, turnaround tracking, and baggage loading. Aircraft assignment is finalized by check-in time; captur']
  booking_pnr_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key reference to the booking record for which this check-in was performed.']
  checkin_session_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to airport.checkin_session. Business justification: Check-in events are recorded within checkin sessions. Counter utilization metrics, agent performance tracking, and operational efficiency analysis require this link for facility and staffing planning.']
  employee_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key reference to the agent or staff member who processed the check-in, applicable for counter or assisted check-in transactions. Null for self-service check-ins.']
  flight_leg_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key reference to the specific flight leg for which the passenger checked in.']
  flight_number_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to route.flight_number. Business justification: Check-in events occur for specific flight numbers. Essential for check-in analytics, station operations, load planning, and passenger flow management. Standard DCS (Departure Control System) requireme']
  gate_assignment_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to airport.gate_assignment. Business justification: Check-in events precede gate assignments. Tracking passenger flow from check-in to gate, connection time analysis, and minimum connect time validation all require this link for operational planning.']
  itinerary_segment_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to reservation.itinerary_segment. Business justification: Check-in is performed for a specific booked segment in the PNR itinerary. While check_in_event has flight_leg_id (operational flight), it should also link to the booked segment to maintain the reserva']
  occurrence_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to safety.occurrence. Business justification: Check-in area safety incidents (baggage handling injuries, counter accidents, system failures causing passenger distress, accessibility equipment failures) must link to the check-in event for root cau']
  pax_profile_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key reference to the passenger who performed the check-in.']
  pfc_record_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to airport.pfc_record. Business justification: Check-in events for US domestic flights trigger PFC collection obligations. Revenue reconciliation, audit trails, and regulatory compliance reporting require this link for financial accuracy.']
  pnr_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key reference to the passenger name record associated with this check-in event.']
  primary_baggage_item_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to airport.baggage_item. Business justification: Baggage is accepted during check-in events. Operational tracking (bag count reconciliation), excess baggage fee validation, and irregularity investigation require this link for revenue and operational']
  reservation_booking_passenger_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to reservation.booking_passenger. Business justification: Check-in event is performed for a specific passenger name element in the PNR. While check_in_event has pax_profile_id, it should also link to the booking-context passenger to maintain the PNR-specific']
  upgrade_request_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to loyalty.upgrade_request. Business justification: Check-in triggers operational upgrade clearance processing. Business process: departure control upgrade confirmation, seat assignment, boarding pass issuance with upgraded cabin, waitlist clearance.']
  apis_submission_status varchar [note: 'Status of the APIS data submission to border control authorities. APIS is required for international flights to transmit passenger data to destination countries.']
  apis_submission_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when APIS data was successfully submitted to border control authorities.']
  baggage_tag_numbers varchar [note: 'Comma-separated list of 10-digit baggage tag numbers issued for checked bags during this check-in event. Used for baggage tracking and reconciliation.']
  boarding_group varchar [note: 'The boarding group or zone assigned to the passenger (e.g., Group 1, Zone A, Priority). Used for organized boarding processes.']
  boarding_pass_number varchar [note: 'Unique boarding pass identifier issued during check-in. Used for boarding gate validation and tracking.']
  boarding_sequence_number varchar [note: 'Sequential boarding number assigned to the passenger, used to determine boarding priority and order.']
  check_in_channel varchar [note: 'The channel or interface through which the passenger performed check-in. CUSS refers to Common Use Self-Service kiosks. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: web|mobile|airport_kiosk|counter|cuss|api|call_center — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  check_in_close_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The scheduled or actual timestamp when check-in closed for this flight. Used to determine if passenger checked in before the cutoff time.']
  check_in_confirmation_code varchar [note: 'Unique confirmation code or reference number generated upon successful check-in completion, provided to the passenger.']
  check_in_counter_number varchar [note: 'The specific counter, kiosk, or terminal identifier where the check-in was performed. Applicable for airport-based check-ins.']
  check_in_remarks varchar [note: 'Free-text remarks or notes recorded by the agent or system during check-in, capturing special circumstances, passenger requests, or operational notes.']
  check_in_sequence_number varchar [note: 'Sequential number indicating the order of check-in within a booking or PNR when multiple passengers or segments are involved.']
  check_in_station_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter IATA airport code or city code where the check-in transaction was performed.']
  check_in_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the check-in event indicating whether the check-in was successfully completed, cancelled, or resulted in a no-show.']
  check_in_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The exact date and time when the passenger completed the check-in transaction. This is the principal business event timestamp for the check-in event.']
  checked_baggage_weight_kg decimal(8,2) [note: 'Total weight in kilograms of all checked baggage for this passenger during check-in.']
  checked_bags_count varchar [note: 'The total number of bags checked by the passenger during this check-in event.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The timestamp when this check-in event record was first created in the system. Used for audit trail and data lineage.']
  document_verification_status varchar [note: 'Status of travel document verification (passport, visa, ID) performed during check-in to ensure compliance with destination country requirements.']
  excess_baggage_fee_amount decimal(10,2) [note: 'The fee charged for excess baggage beyond the allowed free baggage allowance. Zero if no excess baggage fee was applied.']
  excess_baggage_fee_currency_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for the excess baggage fee amount.']
  frequent_flyer_number varchar [note: 'The passenger\'s frequent flyer program membership number used during check-in for mileage accrual and status recognition.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The timestamp when this check-in event record was last updated or modified. Used for audit trail and change tracking.']
  minutes_before_departure varchar [note: 'The number of minutes before scheduled departure time that the passenger completed check-in. Used for OTP analysis and no-show prediction.']
  mobile_boarding_pass_issued_flag boolean [note: 'Boolean indicator of whether a mobile boarding pass was issued to the passenger\'s mobile device during check-in.']
  priority_boarding_flag boolean [note: 'Boolean indicator of whether the passenger is entitled to priority boarding based on ticket class, status, or purchased service.']
  seat_assignment varchar [note: 'The seat number assigned to the passenger during check-in (e.g., 12A, 23F). May be null if seat assignment is deferred.']
  special_service_requests varchar [note: 'Comma-separated list of SSR codes confirmed or added during check-in (e.g., WCHR for wheelchair, VGML for vegetarian meal, PETC for pet in cabin).']
  upgrade_at_check_in_flag boolean [note: 'Boolean indicator of whether the passenger received a cabin class upgrade during the check-in process.']
  upgraded_cabin_class varchar [note: 'The cabin class to which the passenger was upgraded during check-in (e.g., Business, First). Null if no upgrade occurred.']

  Note: 'Check-in transaction record capturing the act of a passenger checking in for a flight. Stores check-in timestamp, check-in channel (web/mobile/airport kiosk/counter/CUSS/API), check-in agent ID, check-in station (airport/city), check-in status (completed/cancelled/no-show), number of bags checked, total checked baggage weight, excess baggage fee amount, upgrade at check-in flag, APIS submission status, document verification status, and check-in close time. Enables OTP analysis, no-show tracking, and ground operations coordination.'
}

Table reservation.pnr_remark {
  pnr_remark_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the PNR remark element. Primary key.']
  pnr_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the parent PNR containing this remark element.']
  reservation_booking_passenger_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to reservation.booking_passenger. Business justification: PNR remark can be associated with a specific passenger name element (e.g., meal preference note, special assistance note). While pnr_remark has associated_passenger_number (INT), it should have a prop']
  associated_passenger_number varchar [note: 'Passenger sequence number within the PNR to which this remark applies. Null if the remark applies to all passengers or the booking as a whole.']
  associated_segment_number varchar [note: 'Flight segment number within the PNR to which this remark applies. Null if the remark applies to the entire PNR rather than a specific segment.']
  creating_agent_sign varchar [note: 'Agent sign-in code or initials of the individual who created this remark element. Used for audit trail and accountability.']
  creating_office_iata_number varchar [note: 'Nine-character IATA office identifier (Office ID) of the agency or airline office that created this remark. Format: 4-character city code + 5-character office identifier.']
  creation_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this remark element was first added to the PNR. Recorded in UTC.']
  deleting_agent_sign varchar [note: 'Agent sign-in code or initials of the individual who deleted this remark element. Null if remark is active.']
  deleting_office_iata_number varchar [note: 'Nine-character IATA office identifier of the agency or airline office that deleted this remark. Null if remark is active.']
  deletion_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this remark element was deleted or marked inactive. Null for active remarks. Supports audit trail and historical analysis.']
  is_confidential boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this remark is restricted to specific user roles or offices. True for confidential remarks that should not be visible to all agents or external parties.']
  is_printable_on_itinerary boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this remark should appear on customer-facing itinerary documents. True for itinerary remarks; false for internal operational remarks.']
  is_transmitted_to_carrier boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this remark is transmitted to the operating carrier(s) via PNR distribution. True for OSI elements and operational remarks requiring carrier visibility.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this remark element was last updated. Null if never modified after creation. Recorded in UTC.']
  modifying_agent_sign varchar [note: 'Agent sign-in code or initials of the individual who last modified this remark element. Null if never modified.']
  modifying_office_iata_number varchar [note: 'Nine-character IATA office identifier of the agency or airline office that last modified this remark. Null if never modified.']
  osi_carrier_code varchar [note: 'IATA two-letter or ICAO three-letter airline code identifying the carrier to which this OSI element is directed. Populated only when remark_type is \'osi\'.']
  remark_category varchar [note: 'Business category or subject area of the remark (e.g., special service request, operational note, customer preference, billing instruction, ground handling instruction).']
  remark_sequence_number varchar [note: 'Sequential ordering of this remark within the PNR. Used to maintain display order and support remark modification or deletion operations.']
  remark_source_system varchar [note: 'Identifier of the system or channel that originated this remark (e.g., Amadeus, Sabre, direct entry, web booking, mobile app, GDS interface).']
  remark_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the remark element. Active remarks are visible and operational; deleted remarks are soft-deleted for audit purposes; archived remarks are retained for historical analysis.']
  remark_text varchar [note: 'Free-text content of the remark element. May contain operational instructions, customer notes, special handling requirements, or inter-carrier communication. Confidential due to potential inclusion of customer preferences or operational details.']
  remark_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the remark element. General remarks are visible to all agents; confidential remarks are restricted; invoice remarks support billing; itinerary remarks appear on customer documents; OSI (Other Service Information) elements are directed to carriers; accounting remarks support financial reconciliation.']

  Note: 'Free-text and structured remark elements stored within a PNR. Captures remark type (general/confidential/invoice/itinerary/OSI/accounting), remark text, remark category, associated segment or passenger, creating office IATA number, creating agent sign, creation timestamp, and remark sequence number. Includes OSI (Other Service Information) elements directed to specific carriers. Supports operational communication between agents, carriers, and ground handlers.'
}

Table reservation.fare_quote {
  fare_quote_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the fare quote record within the PNR (Passenger Name Record). Primary key.']
  campaign_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to marketing.campaign. Business justification: Fare quotes generated during promotional periods need campaign attribution for discount effectiveness tracking and promotional fare performance analysis. Revenue management uses this to evaluate promo']
  fare_class_bucket_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to inventory.fare_class_bucket. Business justification: Fare quotes are priced against specific RBD buckets with defined availability and fare rules. Revenue management requires this link to validate bucket availability at quote time, track which buckets a']
  fare_family_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to revenue.fare_family. Business justification: Branded fare product selection: fare quotes are built from specific fare families (Basic Economy, Main Cabin, etc.). Revenue management and reporting require tracking which branded product was quoted.']
  original_fare_quote_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the original fare quote ID if this is a repricing. Null for original quotes.']
  pnr_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the parent PNR (Passenger Name Record) containing this fare quote.']
  base_fare_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'Total base fare amount before taxes, fees, and surcharges. Sum of all segment fare basis amounts.']
  commission_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'Calculated commission amount payable to the booking agent or distribution partner based on the fare and commission rate.']
  commission_rate decimal(5,2) [note: 'Percentage commission rate applicable to this fare quote for travel agency or distribution partner compensation.']
  corporate_account_code varchar [note: 'Corporate discount or account code applied to this fare quote for negotiated corporate pricing.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this fare quote record was first created in the system.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code in which the fare quote is denominated (e.g., USD, EUR, GBP).']
  discount_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'Total discount applied to the base fare, including promotional codes, corporate discounts, or negotiated reductions.']
  endorsement_text varchar [note: 'Free-text endorsement or restriction box content printed on the ticket, specifying fare rules, non-refundable conditions, or carrier restrictions.']
  fare_basis_summary varchar [note: 'Concatenated or summarized list of fare basis codes applied across all segments in this quote (e.g., YLOW/YLOW/MLOW). Detailed segment-level fare basis stored in related segment records.']
  fare_calculation_line varchar [note: 'Encoded fare construction string showing city pairs, fare basis codes, and amounts per IATA fare calculation standards. Used for ticket validation and interline proration.']
  fare_quote_number varchar [note: 'Business identifier for the fare quote, typically assigned by the PSS (Passenger Service System) or GDS (Global Distribution System) at time of pricing.']
  fare_quote_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the fare quote: active (valid for ticketing), ticketed (converted to ticket), expired (past TTL), repriced (superseded by new quote), cancelled, or voided.']
  fare_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the fare structure: published (public tariff), negotiated (corporate contract), private (confidential rate), award (FFP redemption), net (wholesale), bulk (group), or opaque (hidden carrier). [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: published|negotiated|private|award|net|bulk|opaque — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  gds_code varchar [note: 'Identifier of the GDS used for pricing: 1A (Amadeus), 1B (Abacus), 1S (Sabre), 1G (Galileo), 1P (Apollo), 1V (Worldspan). Null if not GDS-sourced.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this fare quote record was last updated or modified.']
  number_of_passengers varchar [note: 'Count of passengers covered by this fare quote. Typically 1 for individual pricing, or multiple for group or family fares.']
  passenger_type_code varchar [note: 'IATA passenger type code for which this fare quote applies: ADT (adult), CHD (child), INF (infant), SRC (senior), STU (student), MIL (military), YTH (youth). [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: ADT|CHD|INF|SRC|STU|MIL|YTH — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  plating_carrier_code varchar [note: 'Two-character IATA code of the airline whose ticket stock (plate) will be used to issue the ticket. May differ from validating carrier in interline scenarios.']
  pricing_command varchar [note: 'The cryptic command or API call used to generate this fare quote (e.g., FXP, WPNC, pricing modifiers). Used for audit and repricing.']
  pricing_date date [note: 'Date when the fare quote was calculated and stored. Critical for fare validity and repricing scenarios.']
  pricing_source varchar [note: 'Channel or system from which the fare quote was retrieved: GDS (Global Distribution System), NDC (New Distribution Capability), direct airline system, metasearch engine, OTA (Online Travel Agency), or consolidator.']
  pricing_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Precise timestamp when the fare quote was generated, including time zone. Used for ticketing time limit enforcement and audit trails.']
  private_fare_indicator boolean [note: 'Flag indicating whether this fare quote uses a private or negotiated fare (true) versus a published public fare (false).']
  promotional_code varchar [note: 'Promotional or discount code applied at time of pricing to obtain special fare rates.']
  repricing_indicator boolean [note: 'Flag indicating whether this fare quote is a repricing of a previous quote (true) or an original pricing (false).']
  ticketing_time_limit timestamp [note: 'Deadline by which the ticket must be issued to honor this fare quote. After this time, the PNR may be cancelled or repriced.']
  total_fare_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'Grand total fare amount payable by passenger: base fare + taxes + surcharges - discounts. The ticketing amount.']
  total_surcharge_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'Sum of all carrier-imposed surcharges including YQ (fuel surcharge) and YR (carrier surcharge) codes.']
  total_tax_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'Sum of all government taxes, airport fees, and regulatory charges applicable to this fare quote.']
  tour_code varchar [note: 'Bulk or inclusive tour code applied to the fare, indicating special tour operator or package pricing.']
  validating_carrier_code varchar [note: 'Two-character IATA airline code of the carrier responsible for fare validation, ticketing, and revenue settlement.']

  Note: 'Stored fare quote (pricing record) within a PNR capturing the fare calculation at time of booking or repricing. Stores fare quote number, pricing date, fare basis code per segment, published fare amount, applicable discount, total fare, tax itemisation, surcharges (YQ/YR fuel surcharge), pricing command used, validating carrier, ticketing time limit, fare type (published/negotiated/private/award), tour code, endorsement text, and pricing source (GDS/NDC/direct). Supports ticketing, repricing, and revenue accounting.'
}

Table reservation.pnr_history {
  pnr_history_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for each PNR history entry. Primary key. Inferred role: EVENT_LOG.']
  pnr_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key reference to the PNR master record. Links this history entry to the parent booking.']
  reservation_booking_passenger_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to reservation.booking_passenger. Business justification: PNR history entry can be passenger-specific (e.g., name change, SSR addition). While pnr_history has passenger_number (INT), it should have a proper FK to booking_passenger to enable passenger-specifi']
  agent_sign varchar [note: 'Agent sine or user identifier of the person or system that performed the PNR modification. Used for accountability and audit trail.']
  change_reason_code varchar [note: 'Standardized code indicating the business reason for the PNR modification. Used for categorizing change patterns and dispute resolution.']
  change_reason_text varchar [note: 'Free-text description providing additional context or explanation for the PNR modification.']
  change_source varchar [note: 'Channel or system interface through which the PNR modification was initiated. Identifies the origin of the change for audit and analytics.']
  change_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Exact date and time when this PNR modification occurred. Principal event timestamp for audit trail reconstruction.']
  commission_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'Agent commission amount associated with this change if applicable. Captured for revenue accounting and settlement.']
  commission_percentage decimal(5,2) [note: 'Agent commission percentage applied to this transaction if applicable. Used for settlement and revenue reconciliation.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this history record was created in the data warehouse. Audit field for data lineage tracking.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'ISO 4217 three-letter currency code for any monetary values in this history entry.']
  endorsement_text varchar [note: 'Endorsement or restriction text added or modified in this change. Captures fare rule restrictions and ticket validity conditions.']
  fare_basis_code varchar [note: 'Fare basis code if this change involved fare repricing or fare class modification. Captures the fare product applied.']
  field_name varchar [note: 'Name of the PNR field or element that was modified. Identifies the specific data element affected by this change.']
  form_of_payment_type varchar [note: 'Payment method type if this change involved payment modification. Identifies the instrument used for transaction settlement.']
  gds_code varchar [note: 'Code identifying the GDS through which the change was processed. 1A=Amadeus, 1B=Abacus, 1G=Galileo, 1P=Worldspan, 1S=Sabre, 1V=Apollo.']
  history_entry_type varchar [note: 'Type of PNR modification captured in this history entry. Discriminates the nature of the change event. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: segment_add|segment_cancel|segment_modify|name_change|ticket_issuance|ticket_void|remark_add|remark_delete|ssr_add|ssr_cancel|seat_assignment|seat_change|payment_add|payment_modify|contact_change|split_pnr|merge_pnr|queue_placement|queue_removal|fare_quote|price_override|commission_override|endorsement_add|form_of_payment_change|ticketing_time_limit_set|ticketing_time_limit_extend|group_allocation|waitlist_add|waitlist_confirm|upgrade_request|upgrade_confirm — promote to reference product]']
  history_sequence_number varchar [note: 'Sequential ordering number of this history entry within the PNR lifecycle. Enables chronological reconstruction of all changes.']
  ip_address varchar [note: 'IP address of the client or system that initiated the change. Used for security audit and fraud detection.']
  is_automated_change boolean [note: 'Boolean flag indicating whether this change was performed by an automated system process rather than manual agent action. True=automated, False=manual.']
  new_value decimal(18,2) [note: 'New value of the field after the modification. Captures the after-state for audit trail and change tracking.']
  office_iata_number varchar [note: 'IATA-assigned office identification number where the change was made. 9-character alphanumeric code identifying the travel agency or airline office.']
  old_value decimal(18,2) [note: 'Previous value of the field before the modification. Captures the before-state for audit comparison and rollback capability.']
  passenger_number varchar [note: 'Passenger sequence number within the PNR that was affected by this change. Null if change applies to all passengers.']
  queue_number varchar [note: 'Queue identifier if this change resulted in the PNR being placed on a work queue for follow-up action.']
  record_locator varchar [note: 'Six-character alphanumeric PNR record locator at the time of this change. Captures the booking reference for cross-system correlation.']
  remark_text varchar [note: 'Free-text remark or note added or modified in this change. Captures agent comments and special instructions.']
  seat_number varchar [note: 'Seat assignment if this change involved seat selection or modification. Format: row number followed by seat letter.']
  segment_number varchar [note: 'Flight segment number within the PNR itinerary that was affected by this change. Null if change applies to entire PNR rather than specific segment.']
  session_reference varchar [note: 'Session identifier for the user or system session during which this change was made. Enables correlation of related changes within a session.']
  ssr_code varchar [note: 'Four-letter SSR code if this change involved adding or canceling a special service request. Examples: WCHR, VGML, PETC.']
  ticket_number varchar [note: '13-digit e-ticket number if this change involved ticket issuance, void, or exchange. Links history entry to ticketing transaction.']
  transaction_reference varchar [note: 'Unique transaction identifier from the source system that processed this change. Enables correlation with external system logs.']
  waiver_code varchar [note: 'Code indicating any fare rule waiver or exception applied during this modification. Used for revenue integrity and policy compliance tracking.']

  Note: 'Audit history log of all changes made to a PNR throughout its lifecycle. Captures history entry type (segment add/cancel/modify, name change, ticket issuance, remark add, SSR add/cancel, seat change, payment change), old value, new value, change timestamp, agent sign, office IATA number, change source (GDS/API/web/mobile/airport), and history sequence number. Provides full PNR audit trail for dispute resolution, revenue integrity, and regulatory compliance.'
}

Table reservation.booking_payment {
  booking_payment_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the booking payment record. Primary key for the booking payment entity.']
  campaign_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to marketing.campaign. Business justification: Payment transactions require campaign attribution for calculating campaign-driven revenue and marketing ROI. Finance uses payment-to-campaign linkage to reconcile marketing spend against actual collec']
  corporate_contract_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to revenue.corporate_contract. Business justification: Corporate account billing reconciliation: payments made under corporate contracts must link to the contract for soft-dollar credit tracking, volume commitment measurement, and invoice generation. Fina']
  fare_quote_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to reservation.fare_quote. Business justification: Payment is made for a specific fare quote within the PNR. Linking to fare_quote enables tracking which pricing the payment was made against and supports payment reconciliation with the priced itinerar']
  ffp_member_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the frequent flyer program member account from which miles were redeemed. Applicable when payment method is miles redemption.']
  mileage_redemption_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to loyalty.mileage_redemption. Business justification: Award booking copay payments reference the redemption transaction. Business process: award payment reconciliation, copay collection, refund processing for cancelled awards.']
  pnr_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the PNR (Passenger Name Record) associated with this payment transaction. Links payment to the booking record.']
  agency_iata_number varchar [note: 'IATA-assigned numeric identifier for the travel agency that processed the payment. Applicable when payment method is agency account.']
  authorization_code varchar [note: 'Authorization code returned by the payment gateway or card issuer upon successful payment authorization. Used for transaction tracking and reconciliation.']
  billing_address_line1 varchar [note: 'First line of the billing address associated with the payment method. Used for payment verification and fraud prevention.']
  billing_address_line2 varchar [note: 'Second line of the billing address (apartment, suite, etc.). Optional additional address detail.']
  billing_city varchar [note: 'City of the billing address associated with the payment method.']
  billing_country_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 country code of the billing address (e.g., USA, GBR, CAN).']
  billing_postal_code varchar [note: 'Postal or ZIP code of the billing address. Used for address verification and fraud prevention.']
  billing_state_province varchar [note: 'State or province of the billing address. Format varies by country (e.g., two-letter US state codes, full province names).']
  card_expiry_month varchar [note: 'Expiration month of the payment card (1-12). Used for payment authorization and validation.']
  card_expiry_year varchar [note: 'Expiration year of the payment card (four-digit year). Used for payment authorization and validation.']
  card_type varchar [note: 'Brand of the payment card used (e.g., Visa, Mastercard, American Express). Applicable only when payment method is credit or debit card. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: visa|mastercard|amex|discover|jcb|diners|unionpay — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  cardholder_name varchar [note: 'Name of the cardholder as it appears on the payment card. Used for payment verification and fraud prevention.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this payment record was first created in the system. Audit field for data lineage and compliance.']
  fraud_score decimal(5,2) [note: 'Fraud risk score assigned by the payment gateway or fraud detection system (typically 0-100 scale). Higher scores indicate higher fraud risk.']
  gateway_transaction_reference varchar [note: 'Unique transaction identifier assigned by the payment gateway or processor. Used for reconciliation, dispute resolution, and gateway-level tracking.']
  instalment_count varchar [note: 'Total number of instalments in the payment plan. Null if not an instalment payment.']
  instalment_number varchar [note: 'Current instalment number in the sequence (e.g., 1 of 3, 2 of 3). Null if not an instalment payment.']
  instalment_plan_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this payment is part of an instalment plan. True if the payment is structured as multiple instalments, false otherwise.']
  masked_card_number varchar [note: 'Masked payment card number showing only the last four digits (e.g., ****1234) for identification and customer service purposes while maintaining PCI compliance.']
  miles_redeemed_quantity varchar [note: 'Number of frequent flyer miles redeemed for this payment. Applicable when payment method is miles redemption.']
  payment_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Total monetary amount of the payment transaction in the specified currency. Represents the gross payment value before any adjustments.']
  payment_currency_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for the payment amount (e.g., USD, EUR, GBP). Defines the currency in which the payment was processed.']
  payment_method_type varchar [note: 'Type of payment instrument used for the transaction. Indicates the form of payment selected by the customer or agent. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: credit_card|debit_card|cash|agency_account|voucher|miles_redemption|corporate_account|bank_transfer|digital_wallet — 9 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  payment_reference_number varchar [note: 'Externally-known unique reference number for this payment transaction, used for reconciliation and customer service inquiries.']
  payment_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the payment transaction. Tracks the payment from authorization through settlement or failure. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: pending|authorized|captured|settled|declined|failed|refunded|partially_refunded|chargeback|voided — 10 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  payment_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the payment transaction was initiated or authorized. Represents the business event time of the payment in ISO 8601 format (yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX).']
  processor_response_code varchar [note: 'Response code returned by the payment processor indicating the result of the transaction (e.g., approval, decline reason). Used for troubleshooting and reporting.']
  processor_response_message varchar [note: 'Human-readable message from the payment processor describing the transaction result or decline reason.']
  refund_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Total amount refunded to the customer from this payment. Null if no refund has been processed.']
  refund_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the refund was processed. Null if no refund has been issued.']
  settlement_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the payment was settled and funds were transferred. Null if payment has not yet been settled.']
  three_ds_authentication_status varchar [note: 'Status of 3D Secure authentication for the card transaction. Indicates whether additional cardholder verification was performed to reduce fraud risk.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this payment record was last modified. Audit field for tracking changes and data lineage.']
  voucher_code varchar [note: 'Unique code of the voucher or travel credit used for payment. Applicable when payment method is voucher.']

  Note: 'Payment record associated with a PNR capturing the form of payment and transaction details for ticket and ancillary purchases. Stores payment method type (credit card/debit card/cash/agency account/voucher/miles redemption/corporate account), card type, masked card number, card expiry, cardholder name, payment amount, currency, payment status (authorised/settled/declined/refunded/chargeback), payment gateway transaction ID, 3DS authentication status, payment timestamp, and instalment plan details. SSOT for reservation-level payment capture.'
}

Table reservation.refund_transaction {
  refund_transaction_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for refund_transaction']
  arc_settlement_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the ARC settlement record for this refund transaction, used for financial reconciliation with ARC in the US market.']
  baggage_irregularity_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to airport.baggage_irregularity. Business justification: Refunds may be triggered by baggage delays, loss, or damage. Compensation processing, liability tracking, and claims reconciliation require this link for financial and customer service operations.']
  bsp_settlement_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the BSP settlement record for this refund transaction, used for financial reconciliation with IATA BSP.']
  consumer_protection_case_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.consumer_protection_case. Business justification: Refunds often resolve consumer protection violations (involuntary denied boarding, cancellations, significant delays). Finance and legal teams link refund to originating case for regulatory reporting ']
  employee_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the airline agent or system user who processed the refund transaction.']
  itinerary_segment_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to reservation.itinerary_segment. Business justification: Refund transaction can be for specific unused segments in partial refund scenarios. Linking to itinerary_segment enables tracking which booked segments were refunded and supports segment-level refund ']
  mileage_redemption_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to loyalty.mileage_redemption. Business justification: Award ticket refunds must reference original redemption for miles restoration. Business process: award cancellation, miles refund posting, penalty assessment, redemption reversal.']
  pnr_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the Passenger Name Record associated with this refund transaction.']
  reservation_booking_passenger_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to reservation.booking_passenger. Business justification: Refund transaction is issued for a specific passenger\'s ticket within the PNR context. While refund_transaction has pax_profile_id, it should also link to the booking-context passenger to maintain the']
  revenue_emd_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the Electronic Miscellaneous Document being refunded, if applicable. Null for ticket-only refunds.']
  ticket_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the original ticket being refunded.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this refund transaction record was first created in the system.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for all monetary amounts in this refund transaction.']
  fee_refund_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'The amount of carrier-imposed fees and surcharges being refunded.']
  gds_code varchar [note: 'Code identifying the GDS through which the refund was processed, if applicable (e.g., 1A for Amadeus, 1B for Sabre, 1G for Galileo).']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this refund transaction record was last updated or modified.']
  miles_refunded varchar [note: 'Number of frequent flyer miles refunded to the passenger\'s FFP account, if refund method is miles.']
  original_emd_number varchar [note: 'The 13-digit EMD number of the original Electronic Miscellaneous Document being refunded, if applicable.']
  original_fare_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'The original base fare amount from the ticket or EMD before any refund calculations.']
  original_form_of_payment varchar [note: 'The original payment method used when the ticket or EMD was purchased. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: credit_card|debit_card|cash|bank_transfer|check|voucher|miles — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  original_ticket_number varchar [note: 'The 13-digit ticket number of the original e-ticket being refunded.']
  penalty_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'The penalty or cancellation fee deducted from the original fare amount as per fare rules.']
  processing_channel varchar [note: 'The channel through which the refund was requested and processed.']
  refund_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'The total amount being refunded to the passenger, after deducting penalties and fees.']
  refund_approval_date date [note: 'Date when the refund was approved by the airline or authorized agent.']
  refund_method varchar [note: 'The method by which the refund is issued: original form of payment (credit card, cash), travel voucher, frequent flyer miles, credit shell for future travel, or direct bank transfer.']
  refund_notes varchar [note: 'Free-text notes and comments regarding the refund transaction, including special circumstances or agent remarks.']
  refund_processing_date date [note: 'Date when the refund was fully processed and payment was issued or credited.']
  refund_processing_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Precise timestamp when the refund processing was completed.']
  refund_reason_code varchar [note: 'Standardized code indicating the business reason for the refund (e.g., schedule change, flight cancellation, passenger no-show, medical emergency, death in family, fare difference).']
  refund_reason_description varchar [note: 'Detailed textual explanation of the refund reason, providing additional context beyond the reason code.']
  refund_reference_number varchar [note: 'Unique business identifier for the refund transaction, used for customer communication and tracking.']
  refund_request_date date [note: 'Date when the refund was requested by the passenger or initiated by the airline.']
  refund_request_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Precise timestamp when the refund request was submitted or created in the system.']
  refund_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the refund transaction in the processing workflow.']
  refund_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the refund: full (entire ticket value), partial (portion of ticket value), involuntary (airline-initiated due to schedule change, cancellation, or IROP), or voluntary (passenger-initiated).']
  refund_voucher_number varchar [note: 'Unique voucher number issued when refund method is voucher or credit shell, used for future travel redemption.']
  tax_refund_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'The total amount of taxes and government fees being refunded to the passenger.']
  total_refund_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'The total refund amount including base fare refund, tax refund, and fee refund, after all deductions.']
  voucher_expiry_date date [note: 'Expiration date of the refund voucher or credit shell, after which it cannot be redeemed.']
  waiver_code varchar [note: 'Code indicating if penalty fees were waived and the authorization reason (e.g., IROP, medical emergency, airline error, executive waiver).']
  waiver_flag boolean [note: 'Boolean indicator whether penalty waiver was applied to this refund transaction.']

  Note: 'Refund transaction record for voided, cancelled, or partially refunded tickets and EMDs. Stores refund type (full/partial/involuntary/voluntary), original ticket or EMD number, refund amount, penalty amount deducted, tax refund amount, refund method (original form of payment/voucher/miles), refund request date, refund processing date, refund status (requested/approved/processed/rejected), refund reason code, waiver code (for penalty waivers), processing agent ID, and refund reference number. Supports revenue accounting and BSP/ARC settlement.'
}

Table reservation.schedule_change_notification {
  schedule_change_notification_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the schedule change notification record. Primary key.']
  consumer_protection_case_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.consumer_protection_case. Business justification: Schedule changes exceeding thresholds trigger EU261/DOT passenger rights (rebooking, refunds, compensation). Customer service creates consumer protection cases from notifications to track regulatory o']
  flight_number_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to route.flight_number. Business justification: Schedule change notifications reference specific flight numbers. Required for passenger notifications, rebooking workflows, operational recovery, and regulatory compliance (EU261, DOT). Standard airli']
  itinerary_segment_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to reservation.itinerary_segment. Business justification: Schedule change notification is generated when a flight schedule change impacts a specific segment in an existing PNR. Linking to itinerary_segment enables tracking which booked segment was affected b']
  original_aircraft_type_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to fleet.aircraft_type. Business justification: Schedule changes and IROPs often driven by aircraft swaps (gauge changes, maintenance substitutions). Tracking original vs new aircraft type is core to disruption management, passenger re-accommodatio']
  pnr_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key reference to the PNR impacted by the schedule change.']
  route_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to route.route. Business justification: Schedule changes affect specific routes. Critical for IRROPS management, passenger reaccommodation, network disruption analysis, and operational recovery planning. Airlines track schedule changes by r']
  acceptance_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the passenger accepted or rejected the schedule change, formatted as yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX in UTC. Null if still pending.']
  change_reason_code varchar [note: 'Standardized code indicating the root cause of the schedule change (e.g., MAINT for maintenance, WX for weather, CREW for crew unavailability, SLOT for slot time adjustment, COMM for commercial decision).']
  codeshare_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the impacted flight is a codeshare operation (true) or not (false). Codeshare flights require coordination between marketing and operating carriers for schedule change notifications.']
  compensation_amount decimal(10,2) [note: 'Monetary compensation amount offered or paid to the passenger due to the schedule change. Null if no compensation applicable.']
  compensation_currency_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for the compensation amount (e.g., USD, EUR, GBP). Null if no compensation applicable.']
  compensation_eligible_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the schedule change qualifies the passenger for compensation under applicable regulations (true) or not (false).']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the schedule change notification record was first created in the system, formatted as yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX in UTC.']
  gds_record_locator varchar [note: 'Six-character alphanumeric GDS booking reference if the PNR was created through a GDS (Amadeus, Sabre, Travelport). Used for GDS notification routing.']
  impact_severity varchar [note: 'Business assessment of the schedule change impact on the passenger: minor (less than 30 minutes), moderate (30 minutes to 2 hours), major (more than 2 hours or missed connection), or cancellation (flight no longer operating).']
  marketing_airline_code varchar [note: 'Two-character IATA airline code of the carrier whose flight number appears on the ticket (relevant for codeshare flights).']
  modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the schedule change notification record was last updated, formatted as yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX in UTC.']
  new_aircraft_type_code varchar [note: 'Three-character IATA aircraft type code for the revised equipment after the change. Null if flight is cancelled or equipment unchanged.']
  new_arrival_airport_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter IATA airport code for the revised arrival airport. Null if flight is cancelled.']
  new_arrival_datetime timestamp [note: 'The revised arrival date and time in local airport time after the schedule change, formatted as yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX. Null if flight is cancelled.']
  new_departure_airport_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter IATA airport code for the revised departure airport. Null if flight is cancelled.']
  new_departure_datetime timestamp [note: 'The revised departure date and time in local airport time after the schedule change, formatted as yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX. Null if flight is cancelled.']
  new_flight_number varchar [note: 'The revised flight number after the schedule change, formatted as two-character airline code followed by 1-4 digit flight number and optional suffix. Null if flight is cancelled.']
  notification_channel varchar [note: 'Primary communication channel used to notify the passenger: email, SMS, GDS (Global Distribution System) message, travel agency notification, mobile app push notification, airport counter, or phone call. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: email|sms|gds|travel_agency|mobile_app|airport_counter|phone_call — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  notification_number varchar [note: 'Externally-visible unique business identifier for the schedule change notification, formatted as SCN followed by 10 digits.']
  notification_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the notification: pending (queued for delivery), notified (successfully sent), acknowledged (passenger confirmed receipt), failed (delivery unsuccessful), or cancelled (notification voided).']
  notification_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the notification was sent to the passenger, formatted as yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX in UTC.']
  notification_type varchar [note: 'Category of schedule change that triggered the notification: schedule change (general), flight cancellation, equipment change (aircraft type swap), routing change (different flight path), time change (departure/arrival adjustment), or gate change.']
  operating_airline_code varchar [note: 'Two-character IATA airline code of the carrier actually operating the flight (relevant for codeshare flights where operating carrier differs from marketing carrier).']
  original_arrival_airport_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter IATA airport code for the original scheduled arrival airport.']
  original_arrival_datetime timestamp [note: 'The originally scheduled arrival date and time in local airport time, formatted as yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX.']
  original_departure_airport_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter IATA airport code for the original scheduled departure airport.']
  original_departure_datetime timestamp [note: 'The originally scheduled departure date and time in local airport time, formatted as yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX.']
  original_flight_number varchar [note: 'The flight number as originally booked before the schedule change, formatted as two-character airline code followed by 1-4 digit flight number and optional suffix.']
  passenger_acceptance_status varchar [note: 'Passenger response to the schedule change: accepted (passenger confirmed new itinerary), rejected (passenger declined and requested alternative), pending (awaiting passenger response), or auto_accepted (automatically accepted per policy after timeout period).']
  passenger_notification_status varchar [note: 'Status of passenger notification delivery: notified (successfully delivered to passenger), pending (queued for delivery), failed (delivery attempt unsuccessful), or not_required (notification waived per business rules).']
  reaccommodation_action varchar [note: 'Action taken to reaccommodate the passenger: none (no action required), rebooked (moved to different flight), refunded (ticket refunded), voucher_issued (travel credit provided), upgraded (moved to higher cabin class), or alternate_routing (rerouted via different city pair).']
  reaccommodation_flight_number varchar [note: 'Flight number of the alternate flight if passenger was rebooked. Null if no rebooking occurred.']
  time_difference_minutes varchar [note: 'Net change in departure time expressed in minutes. Positive values indicate delay, negative values indicate earlier departure. Null if flight is cancelled.']
  travel_agency_iata_number varchar [note: 'Eight-digit IATA agency numeric code if the booking was made through a travel agency. Used for agency notification routing.']

  Note: 'Schedule change notification record generated when a flight schedule change impacts an existing PNR. Stores notification type (schedule change/cancellation/equipment change/routing change), original flight details, new flight details, change reason code, impact severity (minor/major/cancellation), passenger notification status (notified/pending/failed), notification channel (email/SMS/GDS/agency), notification timestamp, passenger acceptance status (accepted/rejected/pending), and reaccommodation action taken. Supports IROP management and DOT/EASA schedule change compliance.'
}

Table reservation.travel_document {
  travel_document_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for travel_document']
  employee_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the agent or system user who collected or entered the travel document information. Used for audit and quality control.']
  org_unit_id varchar [note: 'IATA office identifier or internal office code where the travel document information was collected. Used for audit trail and compliance reporting.']
  pax_profile_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key reference to the passenger to whom this travel document belongs.']
  pnr_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key reference to the PNR this travel document is associated with.']
  regulatory_requirement_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.regulatory_requirement. Business justification: Travel documents must satisfy APIS, visa, passport validity requirements mandated by destination countries. Border control compliance validation at check-in references regulatory requirements for docu']
  reservation_booking_passenger_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to reservation.booking_passenger. Business justification: Travel document (passport, visa) is collected for a specific passenger name element in the PNR for APIS compliance. While travel_document has pax_profile_id, it should also link to the booking-context']
  apis_response_code varchar [note: 'Response code received from the border control authority after APIS transmission. Used to identify acceptance, rejection, or error conditions.']
  apis_response_message varchar [note: 'Detailed response message received from the border control authority after APIS transmission. Contains error details or acceptance confirmation.']
  apis_transmission_status varchar [note: 'Status of the APIS data transmission to the destination country border control authority. Tracks whether passenger travel document data has been successfully submitted and acknowledged.']
  apis_transmission_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the APIS data was transmitted to the border control authority.']
  boarding_eligibility_status varchar [note: 'Indicates whether the passenger is eligible to board based on travel document validation and APIS clearance. Used by gate agents and automated boarding systems.']
  collection_method varchar [note: 'Channel through which the travel document information was collected from the passenger.']
  compliance_validation_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the travel document record was last validated for regulatory compliance.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the travel document record was first created in the system.']
  date_of_birth date [note: 'Date of birth of the passenger as stated on the travel document. Required for APIS and border control identity verification.']
  document_expiry_date date [note: 'Date on which the travel document expires. Critical for validating passenger eligibility for international travel and compliance with destination country entry requirements (typically 6-month validity rule).']
  document_issue_date date [note: 'Date on which the travel document was issued by the issuing authority. Used for document validity verification.']
  document_number varchar [note: 'Unique alphanumeric identifier of the travel document as issued by the issuing authority. Required for border control and APIS transmission.']
  document_type varchar [note: 'Type of travel document presented by the passenger. Used for APIS (Advance Passenger Information System) compliance.']
  document_verification_status varchar [note: 'Status of the travel document verification process. Indicates whether the document has been validated against issuing authority databases or manual inspection.']
  gds_record_locator varchar [note: 'GDS-specific record locator or confirmation code associated with the travel document collection. Used for cross-system reconciliation.']
  gender varchar [note: 'Gender of the passenger as stated on the travel document. M=Male, F=Female, X=Unspecified/Non-binary, U=Unknown. Required for APIS transmission.']
  given_names varchar [note: 'Given name(s) (first and middle names) of the passenger exactly as they appear on the travel document. Required for APIS and border control matching.']
  ineligibility_reason varchar [note: 'Detailed reason why the passenger is ineligible to board, if boarding_eligibility_status is ineligible. May include expired document, APIS rejection, or missing information.']
  issuing_country_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 code of the country that issued the travel document.']
  modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the travel document record was last modified or updated.']
  nationality_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 code representing the nationality of the passenger as stated on the travel document.']
  place_of_birth varchar [note: 'City and/or country of birth of the passenger as stated on the travel document. May be required by certain destination countries for visa and entry validation.']
  regulatory_compliance_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the travel document record meets all regulatory requirements for international travel (APIS, Secure Flight, destination country rules). True if compliant, False if deficient.']
  source_system varchar [note: 'Name or identifier of the source system from which the travel document record originated (e.g., Amadeus Altéa, Sabre, mobile app, web check-in).']
  ssr_docs_element varchar [note: 'Raw SSR DOCS element string as received from the GDS or PSS. Contains structured travel document information in IATA format for APIS transmission.']
  surname varchar [note: 'Surname (family name) of the passenger exactly as it appears on the travel document. Required for APIS and border control matching.']
  verification_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the travel document verification was completed or last attempted.']

  Note: 'Passenger travel document record collected for APIS (Advance Passenger Information System) and border control compliance. Stores document type (passport/national ID/visa/resident permit/travel permit), document number, issuing country ISO code, nationality, document expiry date, document issue date, surname as on document, given names as on document, gender, date of birth, place of birth, and document verification status. Collected via SSR DOCS/DOCA/DOCO elements. Required for international travel regulatory compliance with TSA, CBP, and national border agencies.'
}

Table reservation.voluntary_change {
  voluntary_change_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the voluntary ticket change transaction. Primary key.']
  booking_pnr_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the booking record being modified through this voluntary change.']
  employee_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the agent who processed the voluntary change. Null for self-service channels.']
  fare_quote_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to reservation.fare_quote. Business justification: Voluntary change transaction generates a new fare quote for repricing the modified itinerary. Linking to fare_quote enables tracking the pricing used for the change transaction and supports fare diffe']
  itinerary_segment_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to reservation.itinerary_segment. Business justification: Voluntary change transaction affects specific segments in the PNR itinerary. Linking to itinerary_segment enables tracking which booked segments were modified, cancelled, or rebooked as part of the vo']
  mileage_redemption_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to loyalty.mileage_redemption. Business justification: Award ticket changes reference original redemption for reissue processing. Business process: award reissue, additional miles collection, change fee assessment, redemption adjustment.']
  org_unit_id varchar [note: 'IATA office identification code (9-character alphanumeric) of the office that processed the change. Format: 4-char city code + 5-char office identifier.']
  pax_profile_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the passenger initiating the voluntary change request.']
  pnr_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the PNR associated with this voluntary change transaction.']
  regulatory_requirement_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.regulatory_requirement. Business justification: Voluntary change policies must comply with DOT/EU consumer protection rules (fee transparency, refund rights). Revenue management validates change fees and restrictions against regulatory requirements']
  route_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to route.route. Business justification: Voluntary changes often involve route changes. Essential for revenue management, change fee policy application, network demand analysis, and fare rule validation. Airlines track voluntary changes by r']
  change_channel varchar [note: 'Distribution channel through which the voluntary change was requested: web (airline website), mobile_app (airline mobile application), call_center (phone reservation center), airport_counter (airport ticket counter), travel_agent (travel agency), GDS (Global Distribution System), chatbot (automated chat assistant), or email (email request). [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: web|mobile_app|call_center|airport_counter|travel_agent|gds|chatbot|email — 8 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  change_fee_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'Fee charged by the airline for processing the voluntary ticket change. Zero if waived.']
  change_fee_waiver_code varchar [note: 'Code indicating the reason for change fee waiver if applicable (e.g., FFP elite status, fare family benefit, promotional waiver, irregular operations). Null if no waiver applied.']
  change_processed_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the voluntary change was successfully processed and the new ticket was issued. Null if not yet completed. Format: yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX.']
  change_reason_code varchar [note: 'Standardized code indicating the reason for the voluntary change (e.g., SCHED for schedule preference, PERS for personal reasons, BUSI for business needs).']
  change_request_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the passenger initiated the voluntary change request. Format: yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX.']
  change_status varchar [note: 'Current status of the voluntary change transaction in its lifecycle: pending (awaiting processing), processing (being handled), completed (successfully processed and new ticket issued), rejected (change request denied), cancelled (passenger cancelled the change request), or refunded (change reversed and fees refunded).']
  change_type varchar [note: 'Category of voluntary change requested: date change (travel date modification), routing change (origin/destination/connection modification), name correction (passenger name spelling correction), cabin upgrade (economy to business/first), cabin downgrade (business/first to economy), carrier change (operating airline change), or mixed change (combination of multiple types). [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: date_change|routing_change|name_correction|cabin_upgrade|cabin_downgrade|carrier_change|mixed_change — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for all monetary amounts in this voluntary change transaction.']
  fare_difference_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'Difference between the new fare and the original fare. Positive if new fare is higher (additional collection), negative if new fare is lower (refund due), zero if fares are equal.']
  fare_rule_category varchar [note: 'IATA fare rule category number governing the voluntary change (typically Category 16 for penalties/changes). Format: 1-2 digit number.']
  form_of_payment_code varchar [note: 'IATA standard form of payment code (e.g., CA for cash, CC for credit card, MS for miles, INV for invoice).']
  gds_record_locator varchar [note: 'Six-character alphanumeric GDS booking reference code if the change was processed through a GDS (Amadeus, Sabre, Travelport).']
  interline_agreement_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the voluntary change involves an interline agreement between multiple carriers. True if interline, False if single carrier.']
  new_cabin_class varchar [note: 'Cabin class of the new ticket after the voluntary change. Null if cabin class was not changed.']
  new_departure_date date [note: 'Scheduled departure date of the new itinerary after the voluntary change. Null if departure date was not changed. Format: yyyy-MM-dd.']
  new_destination_airport_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter IATA airport code of the destination airport in the new itinerary. Null if routing was not changed.']
  new_fare_basis_code varchar [note: 'Fare basis code of the new ticket after the voluntary change.']
  new_origin_airport_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter IATA airport code of the origin airport in the new itinerary. Null if routing was not changed.']
  new_ticket_number varchar [note: 'The 13-digit ticket number of the newly issued e-ticket after the voluntary change is processed.']
  original_cabin_class varchar [note: 'Cabin class of the original ticket: first (first class), business (business class), premium_economy (premium economy), or economy (economy class).']
  original_departure_date date [note: 'Scheduled departure date of the original itinerary before the voluntary change. Format: yyyy-MM-dd.']
  original_destination_airport_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter IATA airport code of the destination airport in the original itinerary.']
  original_fare_basis_code varchar [note: 'Fare basis code of the original ticket indicating fare type, booking class, and fare rules.']
  original_origin_airport_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter IATA airport code of the origin airport in the original itinerary.']
  original_ticket_number varchar [note: 'The 13-digit ticket number of the original e-ticket being changed. Format: 3-digit airline code + 10-digit serial number.']
  payment_method varchar [note: 'Payment instrument used for the voluntary change transaction: credit_card, debit_card, cash, voucher (travel voucher/credit), miles (frequent flyer miles), bank_transfer, or wallet (digital wallet). [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: credit_card|debit_card|cash|voucher|miles|bank_transfer|wallet — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  rejection_reason_code varchar [note: 'Standardized code indicating why the voluntary change was rejected (e.g., fare rule restriction, no availability, payment failure, outside change window). Null if not rejected.']
  rejection_reason_text varchar [note: 'Free-text explanation of why the voluntary change was rejected. Null if not rejected.']
  remarks varchar [note: 'Free-text remarks or notes about the voluntary change transaction, including special handling instructions or passenger comments.']
  total_amount_collected decimal(15,2) [note: 'Total amount collected from the passenger for the voluntary change, including change fee and positive fare difference. Zero if no collection was required.']
  total_amount_refunded decimal(15,2) [note: 'Total amount refunded to the passenger if the new fare is lower than the original fare (after deducting change fee). Zero if no refund was issued.']

  Note: 'Voluntary ticket change transaction record capturing passenger-initiated modifications to an existing booking. Stores change type (date change/routing change/name correction/cabin change/carrier change), original itinerary details, new itinerary details, change fee amount, fare difference amount, total amount collected or refunded, change fee waiver code, change request timestamp, change processed timestamp, change channel, agent ID, change status (pending/completed/rejected), and new ticket number issued. Supports revenue integrity and change fee revenue tracking.'
}

Table reservation.group_booking {
  group_booking_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the group booking record. Primary key for coordinated reservations of 10+ passengers travelling together under a single group contract.']
  employee_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the airline sales representative or agent responsible for managing the group booking relationship and contract execution.']
  org_unit_id varchar [note: 'IATA office identifier or internal office code where the group booking was created and is managed. Supports organizational accountability and reporting.']
  cabin_class varchar [note: 'Service class or cabin category contracted for the group booking. Defines the level of service and amenities provided to group passengers.']
  cancellation_reason varchar [note: 'Free-text explanation or coded reason for group booking cancellation. Supports operational analysis and customer service documentation.']
  cancelled_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the group booking was cancelled. Null if the booking has not been cancelled. Critical for refund and penalty processing.']
  contract_number varchar [note: 'Unique contract reference number assigned to the group booking agreement. Used for legal documentation and financial settlement tracking.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the group booking record was initially created in the reservation system. Audit trail for booking lifecycle tracking.']
  departure_date date [note: 'Scheduled departure date for the outbound travel segment of the group booking. Primary travel date for group coordination and planning.']
  deposit_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'Initial deposit amount required to secure the group booking. Represents the upfront payment obligation per the group contract terms.']
  deposit_due_date date [note: 'Deadline by which the deposit payment must be received to maintain the group booking option or confirmation status.']
  deposit_received_flag boolean [note: 'Indicator of whether the required deposit payment has been received and processed. True indicates deposit obligation fulfilled.']
  destination_station_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter IATA airport code representing the primary arrival point for the group travel. Defines the ending location of the group itinerary.']
  fare_basis_code varchar [note: 'Negotiated fare basis code applicable to the group booking. Defines the fare rules, restrictions, and conditions governing the group travel contract.']
  fare_currency_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for the negotiated fare amount. Defines the currency in which group pricing is denominated.']
  final_payment_due_date date [note: 'Deadline by which full payment for all confirmed seats must be received. Critical milestone for group booking financial settlement.']
  group_booking_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the group booking indicating progression through reservation, confirmation, ticketing, and fulfillment stages.']
  group_coordinator_email varchar [note: 'Primary email address of the group coordinator for all booking communications, updates, and documentation delivery.']
  group_coordinator_name varchar [note: 'Full name of the primary contact person responsible for coordinating the group booking on behalf of the organizing entity.']
  group_coordinator_phone varchar [note: 'Primary contact phone number for the group coordinator. Used for urgent communications and operational coordination.']
  group_name varchar [note: 'Business or descriptive name assigned to the group booking for identification and reference purposes.']
  group_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the group booking based on the nature of travel and organizational affiliation. Determines applicable policies, fare structures, and service requirements. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: leisure|corporate|incentive|tour_operator|sports_team|military|government — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time of the most recent modification to the group booking record. Tracks changes throughout the booking lifecycle.']
  marketing_airline_code varchar [note: 'IATA or ICAO airline code of the carrier marketing and selling the group booking. May differ from operating carrier in codeshare scenarios.']
  name_list_deadline date [note: 'Deadline by which the complete passenger name list must be submitted to the airline. Critical for inventory management and seat release policies.']
  negotiated_fare_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'Contracted fare amount per passenger for the group booking. Represents the negotiated rate agreed between the airline and group organizer.']
  operating_airline_code varchar [note: 'IATA or ICAO airline code of the carrier operating the flights for this group booking. Identifies the airline providing the actual air transportation service.']
  origin_station_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter IATA airport code representing the primary departure point for the group travel. Defines the starting location of the group itinerary.']
  pnr_locator_list varchar [note: 'Comma-separated list of PNR record locators associated with individual passenger bookings within this group. Links group master record to individual reservations.']
