  employee_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the customer service agent or system user who processed the redemption. Null for self-service redemptions.']
  ffp_member_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the FFP member who redeemed miles. Links to the loyalty member master.']
  general_ledger_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.general_ledger. Business justification: Mile redemptions trigger deferred revenue recognition in GL under IFRS 15. Each redemption posts to GL accounts to recognize previously deferred revenue, essential for accurate financial statements an']
  partner_program_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the partner loyalty program or coalition program if redemption was made with a partner (e.g., hotel chain, car rental, retail partner). Null for airline-only redemptions.']
  ticket_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to revenue.ticket. Business justification: Award redemptions result in ticket issuance. Direct link required for revenue recognition, refund processing, and loyalty liability reconciliation. Replaces denormalized e_ticket_number with proper FK']
  award_booking_reference varchar [note: 'Passenger Name Record (PNR) or booking reference for award flight redemptions. Null for non-flight redemptions.']
  cancellation_reason varchar [note: 'Free-text or coded reason for redemption cancellation (e.g., member request, flight cancellation, partner unavailability). Null if not cancelled.']
  co_pay_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'Cash amount paid by the member in addition to miles for this redemption (e.g., taxes, fees, or partial cash payment for award).']
  co_pay_currency_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for the co-pay amount (e.g., USD, EUR, GBP).']
  expiry_date date [note: 'Date by which the redeemed award must be used or the redemption expires (e.g., award ticket validity end date). Null for non-expiring redemptions.']
  miles_discount_applied varchar [note: 'Number of miles discounted due to promotional campaign or elite status benefit. Zero if no discount applied.']
  miles_redeemed varchar [note: 'Total number of loyalty miles debited from the member account for this redemption transaction.']
  partner_transaction_reference varchar [note: 'External transaction reference provided by the partner program for reconciliation. Null for airline-only redemptions.']
  processing_fee_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'Fee charged by the airline or partner for processing the redemption transaction (e.g., award booking fee, transfer fee).']
  processing_fee_currency_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for the processing fee.']
  promotion_code varchar [note: 'Promotional campaign code applied to the redemption (e.g., discounted award miles, bonus transfer). Null if no promotion applied.']
  record_created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this redemption record was first created in the source system.']
  record_updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this redemption record was last modified in the source system.']
  redemption_channel varchar [note: 'Channel through which the member initiated the redemption: airline website, mobile app, call center, airport counter, partner portal, or self-service kiosk.']
  redemption_notes varchar [note: 'Free-text notes or comments about the redemption transaction, captured by agent or system for audit and customer service purposes.']
  redemption_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the redemption transaction: requested (pending), confirmed (approved), ticketed (award ticket issued), cancelled (member cancelled), refunded (miles returned), or expired (award not used).']
  redemption_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the redemption transaction was initiated by the member or agent.']
  redemption_transaction_number varchar [note: 'Externally-visible unique transaction number for the redemption event, used for customer service and reconciliation.']
  redemption_type varchar [note: 'Category of redemption: award flight booking, cabin upgrade, partner airline/hotel reward, merchandise purchase, charity donation, peer-to-peer miles transfer, miles purchase with cash co-pay, or co-pay booking combining miles and cash. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: award_flight|upgrade|partner_reward|merchandise|charity_donation|miles_transfer|purchase_miles|co_pay_booking — 8 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  refund_miles varchar [note: 'Number of miles returned to the member account upon cancellation or refund of the redemption. Zero if no refund occurred.']
  refund_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when miles were refunded to the member account. Null if no refund occurred.']
  source_system varchar [note: 'Name or code of the operational system that originated this redemption record (e.g., Oracle Loyalty Cloud, partner API, legacy FFP system).']
  transfer_fee_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'Fee charged for transferring miles to another member. Null for non-transfer redemptions.']
  transfer_fee_currency_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for the transfer fee.']
  transfer_recipient_ffp_number varchar [note: 'FFP membership number of the recipient member for peer-to-peer miles transfers or family pooling. Null for non-transfer redemptions.']
  transfer_type varchar [note: 'Type of miles transfer: gift (peer-to-peer gifting), family_pool (pooling within family account), purchase (buying miles for another member), or none (not a transfer redemption).']

  Note: 'Transactional record of every mile-debiting event for an FFP member, including award flights, upgrades, partner rewards, merchandise, charity donations, miles transfers (peer-to-peer gifting, family pooling), and purchase-based transfers. Captures redemption type, award booking reference, redemption miles deducted, co-pay cash amount, redemption date, redemption channel, redemption status (requested, confirmed, ticketed, cancelled, refunded), refund miles on cancellation, processing fees, transfer recipient FFP number (for transfers), transfer fee, and transfer type (gift, family pool, purchase). Core SSOT ledger for all debit-side loyalty currency movements.'
}

Table loyalty.miles_balance {
  miles_balance_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the miles balance ledger record. Primary key for the miles balance entity.']
  ffp_member_id varchar [note: 'Unique identifier of the FFP member to whom this miles balance belongs. Links to the member master record.']
  account_inception_date date [note: 'Date when the member first enrolled in the FFP and the miles balance account was created. Used for member tenure analysis and lifetime value calculation.']
  balance_as_of_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp indicating when this balance snapshot was calculated. Critical for point-in-time balance queries and historical balance reconstruction.']
  balance_calculation_method varchar [note: 'Method used to calculate this balance snapshot. Real-time balances are updated transactionally; batch balances are calculated in nightly runs; reconciled balances include manual adjustments.']
  balance_last_updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this balance record was last modified by any transaction or adjustment. Used for data freshness validation and audit trail.']
  balance_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the miles balance account. Active accounts allow accrual and redemption; suspended accounts are temporarily restricted; frozen accounts are under investigation; closed accounts are terminated.']
  balance_version_number varchar [note: 'Version number of this balance record, incremented with each update. Used for optimistic locking and concurrency control in real-time balance updates.']
  bonus_miles_balance varchar [note: 'Subset of total miles balance earned through promotional campaigns, partner bonuses, or special offers. Tracked separately for marketing attribution analysis.']
  elite_bonus_miles_ytd varchar [note: 'Additional miles earned year-to-date as elite tier status bonuses (e.g., 25%, 50%, 100% bonus on base miles). Separate from base miles for tier benefit tracking.']
  is_lifetime_status_qualified boolean [note: 'Boolean flag indicating whether the member has achieved lifetime elite status qualification based on lifetime miles earned or lifetime qualifying criteria. True if qualified, False otherwise.']
  last_accrual_date date [note: 'Date of the most recent miles accrual transaction posted to this account. Used for account activity monitoring and dormancy detection.']
  last_redemption_date date [note: 'Date of the most recent miles redemption transaction posted to this account. Used for member engagement analysis and redemption pattern tracking.']
  last_transaction_date date [note: 'Date of the most recent transaction (accrual or redemption) posted to this account. Used for account activity monitoring and expiration policy enforcement.']
  lifetime_miles_earned varchar [note: 'Cumulative total of all miles earned by the member since program enrollment. Used for lifetime status qualification and member recognition. Never decreases.']
  lifetime_miles_redeemed varchar [note: 'Cumulative total of all miles redeemed by the member since program enrollment. Used for member engagement analytics and redemption behavior analysis.']
  member_number varchar [note: 'The externally-known FFP membership number assigned to the member. Used for member identification across all touchpoints.']
  miles_expiring_180_days varchar [note: 'Total miles scheduled to expire within the next 180 days based on program expiration policy. Used for proactive member engagement and redemption encouragement.']
  miles_expiring_90_days varchar [note: 'Total miles scheduled to expire within the next 90 days based on program expiration policy. Used for member communication and retention campaigns.']
  next_expiration_date date [note: 'Date when the next batch of miles is scheduled to expire based on program expiration policy. Used for member notification and retention campaigns.']
  on_hold_miles varchar [note: 'Miles temporarily held and unavailable for redemption due to fraud investigation, dispute resolution, or account review. Released once issue is resolved.']
  partner_miles_balance varchar [note: 'Subset of total miles balance earned through partner airline flights, hotel stays, car rentals, credit card spend, or coalition program activities. Tracked for partner settlement.']
  pending_miles varchar [note: 'Miles that have been earned but not yet posted to the redeemable balance. Typically includes miles from recent flights or partner transactions awaiting verification.']
  qualification_year_end_date date [note: 'End date of the current qualification year for elite tier status. QM, QS, and QSP counters reset after this date.']
  qualification_year_start_date date [note: 'Start date of the current qualification year for elite tier status. Typically calendar year or membership anniversary date depending on program rules.']
  qualifying_miles_ytd varchar [note: 'Total qualifying miles earned year-to-date that count toward elite tier status qualification. QM are typically based on distance flown and fare class, excluding bonus miles.']
  qualifying_segments_ytd varchar [note: 'Total number of qualifying flight segments flown year-to-date that count toward elite tier status qualification. Each eligible flight leg counts as one segment.']
  qualifying_spend_currency varchar [note: 'ISO 4217 three-letter currency code for the qualifying spend amount. Typically the program base currency (e.g., USD, EUR, GBP).']
  qualifying_spend_ytd decimal(15,2) [note: 'Total qualifying spend amount year-to-date that counts toward elite tier status qualification. Typically based on ticket price paid, excluding taxes and fees.']
  record_created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this miles balance record was first created in the data warehouse. Used for data lineage and audit trail.']
  redeemable_miles varchar [note: 'Subset of total miles balance that is currently available for redemption. Excludes miles that are pending, on hold, or not yet eligible for use.']
  source_system_code varchar [note: 'Code identifying the source system that last updated this balance record. Typically Oracle Loyalty Cloud for operational balances, or partner feed systems for coalition programs.']
  total_miles_balance varchar [note: 'Total accumulated miles currently available in the member account, including all earned miles minus all redeemed miles. This is the authoritative running balance.']

  Note: 'Current and historical miles balance ledger for each FFP member. Maintains total miles balance, redeemable miles, qualifying miles (QM) year-to-date, qualifying segments (QS) year-to-date, qualifying spend (QSP) year-to-date, lifetime miles earned, lifetime miles redeemed, miles expiring in next 90/180 days, last transaction date, and balance as-of timestamp. Serves as the authoritative running balance for member account statements and real-time eligibility checks.'
}

Table loyalty.award_booking {
  award_booking_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the award flight booking. Primary key for the award booking record.']
  award_inventory_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to loyalty.award_inventory. Business justification: award_booking consumes award seat inventory from award_inventory. The award_inventory_bucket attribute (STRING) indicates which inventory bucket was used. Adding award_inventory_id FK links the bookin']
  cabin_class_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to inventory.cabin_class. Business justification: Award bookings require cabin class specification for pricing, availability, and seat allocation. Normalizes existing cabin_class text column. Essential for award inventory management and FFP redemptio']
  campaign_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to marketing.campaign. Business justification: Award seat promotions (reduced-mile requirements, waived fees) are marketing campaigns. Linking enables tracking of promotional award inventory consumption, revenue displacement analysis, and measurem']
  consumer_protection_case_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.consumer_protection_case. Business justification: Award booking denials, involuntary cancellations, or service failures (downgrade, denied boarding on award tickets) trigger consumer protection cases. Links booking to case for investigation, compensa']
  ffp_member_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the FFP member who made the award booking. Links to the loyalty program member whose miles were redeemed.']
  mileage_redemption_id varchar [note: 'Identifier linking to the mileage redemption transaction that debited the miles for this award booking.']
  occurrence_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to safety.occurrence. Business justification: Safety occurrences (delays, cancellations, diversions, aircraft swaps) directly impact award bookings, triggering rebooking, compensation miles, and customer service recovery workflows. Airlines track']
  partner_program_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to loyalty.partner_program. Business justification: award_booking has is_partner_award flag, operating_carrier_code, and marketing_carrier_code attributes. When is_partner_award is true, the booking is made through a partner airline agreement. Adding p']
  scheduled_flight_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to flight.scheduled_flight. Business justification: Award bookings must reference the scheduled flight master for inventory control, schedule changes, cancellation processing, and award seat allocation. Current flight_number/date denormalization is ins']
  ticket_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to revenue.ticket. Business justification: Award bookings generate tickets upon confirmation. Link required for ticketing workflow, refund processing, and revenue accounting. Replaces denormalized award_ticket_number with proper FK.']
  award_pnr varchar [note: 'Six-character alphanumeric Passenger Name Record locator for the award booking. Unique identifier used across reservation systems for this award travel.']
  award_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the award booking type. Saver awards require fewer miles but have limited availability; standard awards have higher mileage cost but greater availability; upgrade awards convert paid tickets to higher cabin; companion awards allow a second passenger at reduced miles; mixed cabin combines different service classes; partner awards are on codeshare or alliance airlines.']
  booking_channel varchar [note: 'Channel through which the award booking was made. Web indicates airline website; mobile app is the carrier\'s mobile application; call center is telephone reservation; airport counter is in-person booking; partner portal is through alliance or coalition partner; GDS is Global Distribution System booking.']
  booking_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the award booking. Confirmed indicates space is held; waitlisted means awaiting availability; ticketed means e-ticket issued; cancelled indicates member-initiated cancellation; expired means booking lapsed without ticketing; pending indicates awaiting payment or verification.']
  booking_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the award booking was created in the reservation system. Represents the moment the member initiated the award redemption.']
  cancellation_reason varchar [note: 'Reason code for award booking cancellation. Member request is voluntary cancellation; schedule change is airline-initiated due to flight changes; IROP is irregular operations; fraud is security cancellation; system timeout is automatic expiry; payment failure is co-pay processing issue.']
  cancellation_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the award booking was cancelled. Null if booking has not been cancelled. Represents when the member or system terminated the reservation.']
  copay_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'Monetary amount paid by the member in addition to miles for this award booking. Covers taxes, fees, and carrier-imposed surcharges not covered by miles.']
  copay_currency_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for the co-pay amount. Indicates the currency in which taxes and fees were charged.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'System timestamp when this award booking record was first created in the loyalty management system. Audit field for data lineage.']
  departure_date date [note: 'Scheduled date of departure for the outbound award flight. First travel date of the award itinerary.']
  destination_airport_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter IATA airport code for the arrival airport of the award journey. Represents the final destination of the award travel itinerary.']
  is_partner_award boolean [note: 'Boolean flag indicating whether this award booking involves travel on a partner airline (alliance member or codeshare partner) rather than the marketing carrier\'s own metal.']
  is_round_trip boolean [note: 'Boolean flag indicating whether this is a round-trip award booking (true) or one-way (false). Round-trip awards include both outbound and return travel.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'System timestamp when this award booking record was last updated. Tracks the most recent change to any field in the record for audit purposes.']
  marketing_carrier_code varchar [note: 'Two-character IATA airline code of the carrier whose FFP miles were redeemed. Represents the loyalty program under which the award was booked.']
  number_of_passengers varchar [note: 'Total count of passengers included in this award booking. Represents how many travelers are covered by this single award PNR.']
  number_of_segments varchar [note: 'Total count of flight segments (individual flight legs) included in this award booking itinerary. Multi-segment awards involve connections.']
  operating_carrier_code varchar [note: 'Two-character IATA airline code of the carrier operating the award flight. May differ from the marketing carrier if this is a codeshare or partner award.']
  origin_airport_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter IATA airport code for the departure airport of the award journey. Represents the starting point of the award travel itinerary.']
  refund_fee_miles varchar [note: 'Number of FFP miles charged as a cancellation or change fee when refunding the award booking. Represents the penalty for award cancellation.']
  refund_miles_amount varchar [note: 'Number of FFP miles returned to the member account upon cancellation or refund of the award booking. May be less than original redemption if cancellation fees apply.']
  return_date date [note: 'Scheduled date of return for the inbound award flight. Null for one-way award bookings. Represents the return travel date for round-trip awards.']
  ticketing_deadline timestamp [note: 'Date and time by which the award booking must be ticketed or it will be automatically cancelled. Enforces time limits for award space holds.']
  ticketing_status varchar [note: 'Current status of e-ticket issuance for the award booking. Not ticketed means reservation exists but no ticket issued; ticketed means e-ticket generated; voided indicates ticket cancelled before travel; refunded means ticket cancelled with miles returned; exchanged means ticket modified for different travel.']
  ticketing_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the award e-ticket was issued. Null if booking has not been ticketed. Represents when the reservation was converted to a confirmed ticket.']
  total_miles_redeemed varchar [note: 'Total number of FFP miles debited from the member account for this award booking. Represents the mileage cost across all passengers and segments.']
  waitlist_position varchar [note: 'Numeric position in the waitlist queue for this award booking. Lower numbers indicate higher priority. Null if not waitlisted.']
  waitlist_status varchar [note: 'Status of waitlist request for award space. Not waitlisted means booking was immediately confirmed; waitlisted indicates awaiting availability; confirmed from waitlist means space opened and booking confirmed; waitlist expired means request timed out without confirmation.']

  Note: 'Master record for award flight bookings made using FFP miles. Captures award PNR, award type (saver, standard, upgrade, companion), origin, destination, travel dates, cabin class, number of passengers, total miles cost, co-pay amount, ticketing status, award ticket number, booking channel, waitlist status, award inventory bucket used, partner airline involvement, and cancellation/refund details. Links to mileage_redemption for the miles debit event.'
}

Table loyalty.partner_program {
  partner_program_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the FFP (Frequent Flyer Program) partner program agreement. Primary key.']
  foreign_carrier_permit_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.foreign_carrier_permit. Business justification: Foreign airline partners must hold valid operating permits in airline\'s home jurisdiction. Links partner program to permit for eligibility verification, partnership approval workflow, and ongoing comp']
  regulatory_authority_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.regulatory_authority. Business justification: Partner programs operate under regulatory oversight in partner jurisdictions (bilateral air service agreements, data protection authorities, consumer protection agencies). Tracks primary regulatory au']
  accrual_delay_days varchar [note: 'Number of days after a qualifying partner transaction before earned miles are posted to the member account. Allows for transaction validation and fraud prevention.']
  agreement_effective_date date [note: 'Date when the partner agreement becomes active and earn/burn transactions are permitted. Format: yyyy-MM-dd.']
  agreement_expiry_date date [note: 'Date when the partner agreement terminates. Null for evergreen agreements with no fixed end date. Format: yyyy-MM-dd.']
  agreement_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the partner agreement. Active agreements permit earn/burn transactions; suspended/terminated agreements block new activity.']
  auto_enrollment_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether Airlines FFP members are automatically enrolled in the partner loyalty program for seamless earn/burn. True if auto-enrollment is enabled.']
  bonus_mile_eligibility_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether transactions with this partner are eligible for promotional bonus mile campaigns and tier multipliers. True if eligible.']
  burn_rate decimal(10,4) [note: 'Redemption rate for using FFP miles with this partner, expressed as miles required per reward unit (e.g., miles per free night, miles per rental day, miles per dollar value).']
  burn_unit varchar [note: 'Unit of measure for the burn rate calculation (e.g., per hotel night redeemed, per car rental day, per dollar value of merchandise). [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: night|day|dollar|euro|pound|item|service — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this partner program record was first created in the system. Format: yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX.']
  earn_rate decimal(10,4) [note: 'Base mileage accrual rate for member transactions with this partner, expressed as miles earned per unit (dollar, euro, night, rental day, etc.). Subject to tier multipliers and promotions.']
  earn_unit varchar [note: 'Unit of measure for the earn rate calculation (e.g., per dollar spent, per hotel night, per car rental day). [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: dollar|euro|pound|night|day|transaction|point — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to this partner program record. Format: yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX.']
  maximum_earn_per_transaction decimal(12,2) [note: 'Maximum number of miles that can be earned in a single transaction with this partner. Null if no cap applies. Prevents abuse and controls liability.']
  minimum_transaction_amount decimal(12,2) [note: 'Minimum transaction value (in settlement currency) required for a partner transaction to qualify for mileage accrual. Null if no minimum applies.']
  partner_contact_email varchar [note: 'Primary email address for the partner business contact. Used for operational communications, settlement queries, and program updates.']
  partner_contact_name varchar [note: 'Full name of the primary business contact at the partner organization responsible for FFP program coordination and issue resolution.']
  partner_contact_phone varchar [note: 'Primary telephone number for the partner business contact, including country code and extension where applicable.']
  partner_country_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 country code for the partners primary country of operation or headquarters location.']
  partner_iata_code varchar [note: 'Two-character IATA airline designator code for airline codeshare partners. Null for non-airline partners.']
  partner_icao_code varchar [note: 'Three-character ICAO airline designator code for airline codeshare partners. Null for non-airline partners.']
  partner_name varchar [note: 'Legal or trading name of the FFP earn/burn partner organization (e.g., Marriott Hotels, Hertz Car Rental, Chase Bank).']
  partner_program_code varchar [note: 'Short alphanumeric code used in operational systems to identify this partner program in transaction records, reports, and member statements.']
  partner_tax_number varchar [note: 'Tax identification number or business registration number for the partner organization. Used for financial settlement and regulatory compliance.']
  partner_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the partner organization by business category. Determines applicable earn/burn rules and integration requirements. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: airline_codeshare|hotel|car_rental|credit_card|retail|telecom|dining|travel_services — 8 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  partner_website_url varchar [note: 'Primary website URL for the partner organization. Used for member communications and marketing materials promoting partner earn/burn opportunities.']
  redemption_blackout_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this partner imposes blackout dates or capacity restrictions on award redemptions. True if blackouts apply.']
  settlement_currency_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for financial settlement of earn/burn transactions between Airlines and the partner (e.g., USD, EUR, GBP).']
  settlement_frequency varchar [note: 'Frequency at which financial reconciliation and settlement occurs between Airlines and the partner for accrued earn/burn transactions.']
  tier_recognition_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the partner honors Airlines FFP elite tier status with reciprocal benefits (e.g., priority service, upgrades, bonus earn). True if tier benefits apply.']

  Note: 'Master record for FFP earn/burn partner agreements. Captures partner name, partner type (airline codeshare, hotel, car rental, credit card, retail, telecom), partner IATA/ICAO code where applicable, earn rate (miles per dollar/unit), burn rate (miles per reward unit), partner tier recognition flag, partner bonus mile eligibility, agreement effective date, agreement expiry date, settlement currency, settlement frequency, and partner contact details. SSOT for all loyalty partner configurations.'
}

Table loyalty.partner_transaction {
  partner_transaction_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the partner transaction record. Primary key.']
  campaign_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to marketing.campaign. Business justification: Partner earn/burn promotions (e.g., bonus miles at hotels, retail) are joint marketing campaigns. Attribution enables partner settlement reconciliation, co-marketing ROI analysis, and promotional cost']
  ffp_member_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the FFP member who earned or redeemed miles through this partner transaction.']
  loyalty_promotion_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to loyalty.loyalty_promotion. Business justification: partner_transaction has promotion_code (STRING) attribute for promotional bonus miles earned through partners. loyalty_promotion defines promotional campaigns. Adding loyalty_promotion_id FK enables j']
  original_transaction_partner_transaction_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the original partner transaction ID if this record is a reversal or adjustment of a prior transaction.']
  partner_program_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the partner program (hotel chain, car rental, credit card issuer, retail coalition) through which the transaction occurred.']
  base_miles_awarded varchar [note: 'Number of base FFP miles earned or redeemed through this partner transaction, calculated per the partner agreement earn/burn rate.']
  bonus_miles_awarded varchar [note: 'Number of bonus or promotional miles awarded in addition to base miles, based on member tier status, promotional campaigns, or partner-specific bonuses.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this partner transaction record was first created in the Airlines FFP system.']
  dispute_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this partner transaction is under dispute by the member or partner (true if disputed, false otherwise).']
  dispute_reason varchar [note: 'Description of the reason for dispute if the transaction is flagged for dispute (e.g., miles not posted, incorrect amount, transaction not recognized).']
  dispute_resolution_date date [note: 'Date when the dispute was resolved, if applicable.']
  earn_rate_multiplier decimal(5,2) [note: 'Multiplier applied to calculate miles earned per unit of partner transaction amount, as defined in the partner agreement (e.g., 1.5 miles per dollar spent).']
  expiration_date date [note: 'Date when the miles awarded in this partner transaction will expire if not used, per FFP expiration policy.']
  last_updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this partner transaction record was last modified in the Airlines FFP system.']
  member_tier_at_transaction varchar [note: 'FFP tier status of the member at the time of the partner transaction (e.g., Silver, Gold, Platinum), which may affect bonus miles calculation.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text notes or comments related to this partner transaction, used for operational context or special handling instructions.']
  partner_category varchar [note: 'Category of partner business type (hotel, car rental, credit card issuer, retail merchant, dining, travel services).']
  partner_location_city varchar [note: 'City where the partner transaction occurred.']
  partner_location_code varchar [note: 'Code identifying the specific partner location where the transaction occurred (e.g., hotel property code, car rental branch code, retail store identifier).']
  partner_location_country_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO country code where the partner transaction occurred.']
  partner_location_name varchar [note: 'Descriptive name of the partner location where the transaction occurred (e.g., Marriott Times Square, Hertz LAX Airport).']
  posting_date date [note: 'Date when the miles from this partner transaction were posted to the member FFP account ledger.']
  posting_status varchar [note: 'Current status of the transaction in the FFP posting workflow indicating whether miles have been successfully credited or debited to the member account.']
  reconciliation_date date [note: 'Date when this transaction was reconciled with the partner for settlement purposes.']
  reconciliation_status varchar [note: 'Status of financial reconciliation between Airlines and the partner for this transaction.']
  reference varchar [note: 'External reference number or confirmation code provided by the partner for this transaction. Used for reconciliation and dispute resolution.']
  reversal_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this transaction is a reversal of a previous partner transaction (true if reversal, false otherwise).']
  settlement_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Amount paid or received by Airlines for this partner transaction as part of the partner revenue-sharing or cost-sharing agreement.']
  settlement_currency_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO currency code for the settlement amount between Airlines and the partner.']
  source_system varchar [note: 'Name of the source system or partner integration platform from which this transaction was received (e.g., Oracle Loyalty Cloud, Partner API Gateway).']
  source_system_transaction_code varchar [note: 'Unique transaction identifier in the source system or partner platform, used for traceability and audit.']
  total_miles varchar [note: 'Total miles awarded or redeemed in this transaction (sum of base miles and bonus miles).']
  transaction_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Monetary value of the partner transaction in the original transaction currency (e.g., hotel bill amount, car rental charge, credit card purchase amount).']
  transaction_currency_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO currency code for the transaction amount (e.g., USD, EUR, GBP).']
  transaction_date date [note: 'Date when the partner transaction occurred (e.g., hotel checkout date, car rental return date, credit card purchase date).']
  transaction_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Precise timestamp when the partner transaction was recorded in the partner system, including time zone information.']
  transaction_type varchar [note: 'Type of partner transaction indicating whether miles were earned (accrual) or redeemed (burn), or if this is a reversal or adjustment.']

  Note: 'Transactional record of miles earned or redeemed through non-airline FFP partners (hotels, car rentals, credit cards, retail). Captures partner program ID, member FFP number, partner transaction reference, transaction date, transaction amount, transaction currency, miles awarded or redeemed, bonus miles, partner location/property, transaction type (earn/burn), posting status, and dispute flag. Feeds into mileage_accrual and mileage_redemption for consolidated member ledger.'
}

Table loyalty.loyalty_promotion {
  loyalty_promotion_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the FFP (Frequent Flyer Program) promotional campaign. Primary key. Inferred role: MASTER_RESOURCE (promotional campaign as a managed marketing resource).']
  budget_plan_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.budget_plan. Business justification: Loyalty promotions require budget allocation for bonus miles and campaign costs. Links promotion to budget plan for cost tracking, variance analysis, and financial planning. Essential for marketing sp']
  parent_campaign_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to marketing.campaign. Business justification: Loyalty promotions are often tactical components of broader marketing campaigns (e.g., summer travel campaign includes bonus miles promotion). Parent campaign link enables budget consolidation, strate']
  regulatory_requirement_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.regulatory_requirement. Business justification: Loyalty promotions must comply with advertising regulations, consumer protection laws (e.g., EU Unfair Commercial Practices Directive), and jurisdictional marketing rules. Links promotion to applicabl']
  actual_cost decimal(18,2) [note: 'Actual total financial cost incurred by the promotion after completion, calculated from bonus miles awarded and their valuation. Used for ROI (Return on Investment) analysis. Business-sensitive financial data.']
  approval_status varchar [note: 'Workflow approval state for the promotion before it can be scheduled or activated. Used for governance and financial control over promotional spend.']
  approved_by varchar [note: 'User ID or name of the manager or executive who approved the promotion. Null if not yet approved. Used for accountability and audit.']
  approved_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the promotion was approved. Null if not yet approved. Distinct lifecycle event from creation and modification.']
  bonus_miles_formula varchar [note: 'Business rule or formula defining how bonus miles are calculated (e.g., \'2x base miles\', \'flat 5000 miles\', \'base_miles * 1.5 + 1000\'). Free-text expression used by accrual engine. Measurement/value category for the promotion.']
  communication_channel varchar [note: 'Primary channel(s) used to communicate the promotion to members (email, SMS, push notification, in-app message, website banner, or all). Used for campaign execution and member engagement.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the promotion record was first created in the system. Record audit created timestamp.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for estimated_cost and actual_cost (e.g., USD, EUR, GBP). Used for multi-currency financial reporting.']
  eligible_member_tiers varchar [note: 'Comma-separated list of FFP (Frequent Flyer Program) member tier codes eligible to participate (e.g., \'SILVER,GOLD,PLATINUM\' or \'ALL\'). Empty or \'ALL\' indicates no tier restriction.']
  eligible_partners varchar [note: 'Comma-separated list of partner airline IATA codes or coalition partner identifiers eligible for bonus earn (e.g., \'AA,BA,QF\' for airline partners or \'HOTEL_CHAIN_X\' for non-air partners). Empty or \'ALL\' indicates no partner restriction.']
  end_date date [note: 'Date when the promotion expires and no further bonus accruals are allowed. Effective-until date for the promotion. Nullable for open-ended promotions.']
  estimated_cost decimal(18,2) [note: 'Estimated total financial cost or liability of the promotion in the airline\'s reporting currency (e.g., USD). Used for budgeting and financial planning. Business-sensitive financial data.']
  external_promotion_code varchar [note: 'Unique identifier for the promotion in the source operational system (e.g., Oracle Loyalty Cloud promotion ID). Used for cross-system reconciliation and data lineage.']
  last_modified_by varchar [note: 'User ID or name of the staff who last updated the promotion record. Used for audit trail and change tracking.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the promotion record was last updated. Record audit updated timestamp.']
  maximum_bonus_cap varchar [note: 'Maximum total bonus miles a member can earn under this promotion. Null indicates no cap. Used to control promotion liability and prevent abuse.']
  opt_in_count varchar [note: 'Total number of members who have opted into this promotion. Updated in real-time or batch. Used for campaign performance measurement.']
  opt_in_required_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether members must explicitly opt-in or register for the promotion (true) or if it is automatically applied to eligible members (false). Used for campaign engagement tracking.']
  priority_rank varchar [note: 'Numeric priority or precedence rank when multiple promotions are eligible for the same transaction. Lower number indicates higher priority. Used by accrual engine to resolve conflicts.']
  promotion_code varchar [note: 'Externally-known unique alphanumeric code identifying the promotion (e.g., DOUBLE2024, TIERBOOST). Used by members to opt-in or by system to auto-apply. Business identifier for the promotion.']
  promotion_description varchar [note: 'Detailed marketing description of the promotion, including terms, conditions, and member benefits. Used for customer communication and internal reference.']
  promotion_name varchar [note: 'Human-readable marketing name of the promotion (e.g., \'Summer Double Miles\', \'Elite Status Challenge\'). Identity label for the promotion.']
  promotion_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the promotion: draft (being designed), scheduled (approved, not yet started), active (currently running), paused (temporarily suspended), completed (ended successfully), cancelled (terminated before completion). Lifecycle status for the promotion.']
  promotion_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the promotional campaign mechanism: double_miles (2x earn rate), triple_miles (3x earn rate), tier_challenge (accelerated tier qualification), status_match (match competitor status), bonus_on_route (extra miles on specific routes), partner_bonus (bonus on partner airline or coalition earn), upgrade_bonus (complimentary or discounted upgrades). Classification category for the promotion. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: double_miles|triple_miles|tier_challenge|status_match|bonus_on_route|partner_bonus|upgrade_bonus — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  redemption_count varchar [note: 'Total number of transactions or bookings where this promotion was successfully applied and bonus miles were awarded. Used for ROI (Return on Investment) analysis and campaign effectiveness.']
  source_system varchar [note: 'Name of the operational system where the promotion was originally created (e.g., \'Oracle Loyalty Cloud\', \'Amadeus Altéa\', \'Custom Promo Engine\'). Used for data lineage and integration troubleshooting.']
  start_date date [note: 'Date when the promotion becomes active and members can begin earning bonus miles or benefits. Effective-from date for the promotion.']
  target_segment varchar [note: 'Marketing segment or customer cohort targeted by this promotion (e.g., \'Inactive Members\', \'High-Value Flyers\', \'New Enrollees\', \'Corporate Travelers\'). Used for campaign targeting and performance analysis.']
  terms_and_conditions_url varchar [note: 'Web URL linking to the full legal terms and conditions document for the promotion. Used for regulatory compliance and member transparency.']
  created_by varchar [note: 'User ID or name of the marketing or loyalty operations staff who created the promotion record. Used for audit trail and accountability.']

  Note: 'Master record for FFP promotional campaigns offering bonus miles, tier accelerators, or special rewards. Captures promotion code, promotion name, promotion type (double miles, tier challenge, status match, bonus on route, partner bonus), target segment, eligible member tiers, eligible routes or partners, promotion start date, promotion end date, bonus miles formula, maximum bonus cap, opt-in requirement flag, opt-in count, redemption count, and promotion status. Aligns with Oracle Loyalty Cloud promotions module.'
}

Table loyalty.loyalty_promotion_enrollment {
  loyalty_promotion_enrollment_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the promotion enrollment record. Primary key.']
  ffp_member_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the FFP member who enrolled in the promotion.']
  loyalty_promotion_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the loyalty promotion in which the member enrolled.']
  source_campaign_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to marketing.campaign. Business justification: Members enroll in loyalty promotions via marketing campaign touchpoints (email, web, app). Linking enables funnel analysis from campaign exposure to enrollment, measures campaign effectiveness in driv']
  auto_enrolled_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the member was automatically enrolled based on tier status or other eligibility criteria, versus explicit opt-in.']
  bonus_miles_earned varchar [note: 'Total bonus miles or points awarded to the member upon successful completion of the promotion.']
  bonus_posting_date date [note: 'Date when bonus miles or rewards were posted to the member\'s FFP account.']
  bonus_posting_status varchar [note: 'Status of the bonus miles posting transaction to the member\'s account.']
  cancellation_date date [note: 'Date when the enrollment was cancelled by the member or system.']
  cancellation_reason varchar [note: 'Reason for enrollment cancellation (e.g., member request, account closure, fraud, terms violation).']
  completion_date date [note: 'Date when the promotion enrollment was marked as completed and rewards were posted or scheduled.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this enrollment record was first created in the system.']
  enrollment_channel varchar [note: 'Channel or touchpoint through which the member enrolled in the promotion.']
  enrollment_date date [note: 'Date when the member enrolled in the promotion.']
  enrollment_number varchar [note: 'Business-facing unique enrollment reference number for tracking and customer service inquiries.']
  enrollment_source_campaign varchar [note: 'Marketing campaign code or identifier that drove the enrollment, used for campaign effectiveness tracking.']
  enrollment_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the promotion enrollment. Tracks progression from initial enrollment through completion or termination. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: enrolled|in_progress|qualified|completed|expired|cancelled|disqualified — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  enrollment_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Precise date and time when the member enrolled in the promotion, including timezone information.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this enrollment record was last updated, used for audit trail and data synchronization.']
  notification_sent_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether enrollment confirmation or completion notification was sent to the member.']
  notification_sent_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when enrollment or completion notification was sent to the member.']
  opt_in_consent_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the member provided explicit consent to participate in the promotion and receive related communications.']
  partner_code varchar [note: 'IATA or internal code of the partner organization for co-branded promotions.']
  partner_promotion_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this is a co-branded promotion with a partner airline, hotel, or coalition program.']
  promotion_end_date date [note: 'Date when the promotion period ends and qualification window closes.']
  promotion_start_date date [note: 'Date when the promotion period begins for this enrollment, may differ from enrollment date for future-dated promotions.']
  qualification_criteria_type varchar [note: 'Type of qualification criteria the member must meet to complete the promotion (e.g., number of flights, miles earned, spend threshold).']
  qualification_current_value decimal(18,2) [note: 'Current progress value toward the qualification target, updated as qualifying activities are completed.']
  qualification_deadline_date date [note: 'Final date by which the member must complete qualification criteria to earn promotion rewards.']
  qualification_percentage decimal(5,2) [note: 'Percentage of qualification criteria completed, calculated as (current_value / target_value) * 100.']
  qualification_target_value decimal(18,2) [note: 'Target value the member must achieve to qualify for promotion completion (e.g., 5 flights, 10000 miles, 500 USD spend).']
  qualification_unit varchar [note: 'Unit of measure for qualification progress (e.g., flights, miles, currency units). [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: flights|segments|miles|kilometers|currency|points|nights|transactions — 8 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  qualified_date date [note: 'Date when the member met all qualification criteria and became eligible for promotion rewards.']
  qualifying_activities_count varchar [note: 'Number of qualifying activities (flights, transactions, partner activities) completed toward promotion requirements.']
  terms_version varchar [note: 'Version identifier of the promotion terms and conditions that the member agreed to at enrollment.']
  tier_bonus_multiplier decimal(5,2) [note: 'Additional multiplier applied to promotion rewards based on the member\'s elite tier status at time of qualification.']

  Note: 'Tracks individual member opt-ins and participation in loyalty promotions. Records member FFP number, promotion ID, enrollment date, enrollment channel, qualification progress (e.g., flights completed vs. required), bonus miles awarded, promotion completion date, and promotion outcome status (enrolled, in-progress, completed, expired, cancelled). Enables targeted promotion tracking and bonus posting automation.'
}

Table loyalty.member_benefit {
  member_benefit_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the member benefit entitlement record. Primary key.']
  employee_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the system user or process that last modified this benefit entitlement record, for audit trail purposes.']
  ffp_member_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the FFP member who holds this benefit entitlement.']
  tier_status_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the FFP tier status to which this benefit is attached.']
  activation_trigger varchar [note: 'Event or condition that triggered the activation of this benefit entitlement (e.g., tier qualification, manual grant by admin, promotional campaign, lifetime status achievement, status challenge match).']
  auto_renew_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the benefit automatically renews upon tier re-qualification or expiration (True) or requires manual re-issuance (False).']
  benefit_code varchar [note: 'Unique alphanumeric code identifying the benefit type (e.g., LOUNGE_ACCESS, PRIORITY_CHECKIN, EXTRA_BAG).']
  benefit_description varchar [note: 'Detailed description of the benefit entitlement, including terms, conditions, and usage instructions.']
  benefit_name varchar [note: 'Human-readable name of the benefit (e.g., Priority Lounge Access, Extra Baggage Allowance).']
  benefit_quantity varchar [note: 'Numeric quantity or limit of the benefit entitlement (e.g., 2 lounge passes, 20kg extra baggage). Null indicates unlimited.']
  benefit_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the benefit entitlement: active (usable), suspended (temporarily unavailable), expired (validity period ended), revoked (administratively removed), or pending activation.']
  benefit_terms varchar [note: 'Full text of terms and conditions governing the use of this benefit, including restrictions, blackout dates, and eligibility criteria.']
  benefit_type varchar [note: 'Category of benefit entitlement: lounge access, priority check-in, priority boarding, extra baggage allowance, seat upgrade priority, companion ticket, bonus mile multiplier, or dedicated phone support. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: lounge_access|priority_checkin|priority_boarding|extra_baggage|seat_upgrade|companion_ticket|bonus_miles|dedicated_support — 8 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  benefit_unit varchar [note: 'Unit of measurement for the benefit quantity (e.g., passes, kg for baggage, segments, upgrade certificates, multiplier for bonus miles). [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: passes|kg|segments|upgrades|multiplier|tickets|hours — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  benefit_validity_end_date date [note: 'Date on which the benefit entitlement expires. Null indicates no expiration.']
  benefit_validity_start_date date [note: 'Date from which the benefit entitlement becomes active and usable by the member.']
  blackout_dates varchar [note: 'Comma-separated list of date ranges or specific dates when the benefit cannot be used (e.g., peak holiday periods). Null if no blackout dates apply.']
  cabin_class_restriction varchar [note: 'Cabin class restriction for benefit usage (e.g., benefit applies only to business class bookings). Value \'any\' indicates no restriction.']
  cost_currency varchar [note: 'ISO 4217 three-letter currency code for the cost_to_airline amount.']
  cost_to_airline decimal(15,2) [note: 'Estimated cost to the airline for providing this benefit entitlement, used for financial planning and benefit valuation analysis.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this benefit entitlement record was first created in the system.']
  delivery_channel varchar [note: 'Channel through which the benefit is delivered or redeemed (e.g., airport counter, online portal, mobile app, call center, partner location, automatic application).']
  geographic_restriction varchar [note: 'Geographic scope or restriction for benefit usage (e.g., domestic only, international only, specific regions or airport codes). Null if no restriction.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this benefit entitlement record was most recently updated.']
  last_usage_date date [note: 'Date when the benefit was most recently used or redeemed by the member.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text notes for internal use, capturing special circumstances, manual adjustments, or member service interactions related to this benefit.']
  partner_airline_code varchar [note: 'IATA two-letter or ICAO three-letter airline code of the partner airline where this benefit is valid. Null if benefit applies only to the issuing airline.']
  priority_rank varchar [note: 'Numeric rank indicating the priority level of this benefit when multiple members compete for limited resources (e.g., upgrade waitlist priority). Lower numbers indicate higher priority.']
  remaining_balance varchar [note: 'Remaining quantity of the benefit available for use (benefit_quantity minus usage_count). Null if unlimited.']
  revocation_reason varchar [note: 'Reason for benefit revocation if status is \'revoked\' (e.g., tier downgrade, policy violation, account closure). Null if benefit has not been revoked.']
  tier_applicability varchar [note: 'Comma-separated list of tier codes to which this benefit applies (e.g., GOLD,PLATINUM,DIAMOND).']
  transferable_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the benefit can be transferred to another FFP member or companion (True) or is non-transferable (False).']
  usage_count varchar [note: 'Number of times this benefit has been used or redeemed by the member during the current validity period.']
  usage_history_summary varchar [note: 'Summary text of benefit usage history, including dates, locations, and transaction references for audit and member inquiry purposes.']

  Note: 'Defines the specific benefit entitlements associated with each FFP tier or membership type and tracks their consumption. Captures benefit type (lounge access, priority check-in, extra baggage allowance, seat upgrade priority, companion ticket, bonus mile multiplier, dedicated phone line), benefit tier applicability, benefit quantity/limit, benefit validity period, benefit delivery channel, benefit terms, usage count, last usage date, remaining balance, and usage history summary. Reference entity linking tier levels to their operational benefit entitlements with consumption tracking for benefit cap enforcement and utilization reporting.'
}

Table loyalty.upgrade_request {
  upgrade_request_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the upgrade request. Primary key for the upgrade request entity.']
  award_booking_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to loyalty.award_booking. Business justification: upgrade_request can be linked to award_booking when the upgrade is processed using FFP miles (miles-based cabin upgrade on an award ticket). This FK will be NULL for revenue ticket upgrades and compli']
  campaign_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to marketing.campaign. Business justification: Upgrade promotions (discounted co-pay, bonus priority, complimentary upgrades) are marketing campaigns. Attribution measures promotional upgrade take-up rates, revenue impact, and operational costs of']
  consumer_protection_case_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.consumer_protection_case. Business justification: Upgrade denials (especially paid upgrades or confirmed upgrades later revoked) and involuntary downgrades can trigger consumer protection cases under passenger rights regulations. Links request to cas']
  current_cabin_class_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to inventory.cabin_class. Business justification: Upgrade eligibility rules depend on current cabin class. Normalizes current_cabin_class text column. Required for upgrade policy enforcement and operational upgrade clearance workflows.']
  employee_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the airline agent or system user who processed or approved the upgrade request. Null for automated system processing.']
  ffp_member_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the FFP member who submitted the upgrade request.']
  flight_inventory_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to inventory.flight_inventory. Business justification: Upgrade requests target specific flight inventory for clearance processing. Critical for upgrade waitlist management, elite benefit fulfillment, and revenue management decisions on premium cabin inven']
  flight_leg_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to flight.flight_leg. Business justification: Upgrade requests target specific operated flight legs for real-time inventory management, waitlist clearance processing, and operational upgrade execution at departure. Required for departure control ']
  loyalty_promotion_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to loyalty.loyalty_promotion. Business justification: upgrade_request has promotion_code (STRING) attribute. Upgrade requests can be part of promotional campaigns offering discounted or complimentary upgrades. Adding loyalty_promotion_id FK enables joini']
  occurrence_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to safety.occurrence. Business justification: Flight disruptions from safety occurrences affect upgrade clearance, waitlist processing, and request expiry. Airlines track which occurrences impacted which upgrade requests to manage operational rec']
  origin_station_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to airport.station. Business justification: Requests track origin_airport_code; FK enables station-level upgrade inventory management, hub capacity planning, and operational reporting on upgrade demand by station. Role prefix for clarity.']
  ticket_coupon_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to revenue.ticket_coupon. Business justification: Upgrades apply to specific flight coupons. Link required for inventory control, revenue recognition (upgrade fees), and operational execution. New attribute; no existing candidate.']
  cancellation_reason varchar [note: 'Free-text explanation or standardized reason for why the upgrade request was cancelled.']
  cancellation_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the upgrade request was cancelled by the member or system.']
  co_pay_amount decimal(10,2) [note: 'Monetary amount paid by the member in addition to miles for a co-pay upgrade. Null for complimentary or pure miles-based upgrades.']
  co_pay_currency_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for the co-pay amount.']
  companion_upgrade_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this upgrade request includes a companion passenger traveling on the same PNR and eligible for upgrade under the member\'s benefits.']
  denial_reason_code varchar [note: 'Standardized code indicating the reason an upgrade request was denied: no availability in requested cabin, insufficient miles balance, ineligible fare class, cabin at capacity, operational restrictions, or request expired.']
  destination_airport_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter IATA airport code representing the arrival airport for the flight segment.']
  expiry_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the upgrade request will automatically expire if not cleared, typically set relative to departure time or check-in deadline.']
  fare_class_code varchar [note: 'Single-letter fare class booking code of the original ticket, which influences upgrade eligibility and priority.']
  flight_number varchar [note: 'Flight number for which the upgrade is requested, consisting of two-letter airline code followed by numeric flight identifier.']
  miles_deducted varchar [note: 'Number of frequent flyer miles deducted from the member\'s account for a miles-based or co-pay upgrade. Null for complimentary elite upgrades.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Additional free-text notes or comments related to the upgrade request, typically entered by agents or system for special handling instructions.']
  origin_airport_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter IATA airport code representing the departure airport for the flight segment.']
  pnr varchar [note: 'Six-character alphanumeric Passenger Name Record reference associated with the booking for which the upgrade is requested.']
  record_created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this upgrade request record was first created in the system.']
  record_updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this upgrade request record was last modified in the system.']
  request_channel varchar [note: 'Channel through which the upgrade request was submitted: web portal, mobile application, call center agent, airport self-service kiosk, gate agent, or partner airline portal.']
  request_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the upgrade request was initially submitted by the member or system.']
  request_type varchar [note: 'Type of upgrade request indicating the method or entitlement basis: complimentary elite upgrade based on tier status, miles-based redemption, co-pay upgrade combining miles and cash, operational upgrade at airline discretion, or revenue upgrade purchased at gate.']
  requested_cabin_class varchar [note: 'The target cabin class to which the passenger is requesting an upgrade.']
  seat_number_assigned varchar [note: 'Specific seat number assigned to the passenger in the upgraded cabin once the upgrade is cleared. Format: row number followed by seat letter.']
  source_system varchar [note: 'Name or identifier of the operational system that originated this upgrade request record, such as Oracle Loyalty Cloud or Amadeus Altéa PSS.']
  travel_date date [note: 'Scheduled date of travel for the flight segment being upgraded.']
  upgrade_cleared_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the upgrade was confirmed and cleared, allowing the passenger to board in the upgraded cabin.']
  upgrade_priority_score decimal(10,2) [note: 'Calculated priority score determining the member\'s position in the upgrade queue, based on factors such as tier status, fare class, lifetime miles, and request timing.']
  upgrade_status varchar [note: 'Current status of the upgrade request in its lifecycle: requested (initial submission), waitlisted (pending availability), confirmed (upgrade space allocated), cleared (passenger checked in with upgrade), denied (request rejected), expired (request lapsed), or cancelled (passenger withdrew request). [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: requested|waitlisted|confirmed|cleared|denied|expired|cancelled — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  waitlist_position varchar [note: 'Current position of the member in the upgrade waitlist queue for the flight, with lower numbers indicating higher priority.']

  Note: 'Tracks member requests for complimentary or miles-based cabin upgrades. Captures request type (complimentary elite upgrade, miles upgrade, co-pay upgrade), member FFP number, PNR reference, flight number, travel date, origin, destination, requested cabin, current cabin, upgrade priority score, waitlist position, upgrade status (requested, waitlisted, confirmed, cleared, denied, expired), upgrade clearing timestamp, miles deducted if applicable, and upgrade source channel.'
}

Table loyalty.miles_transfer {
  miles_transfer_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the peer-to-peer miles transfer transaction. Primary key.']
  employee_id varchar [note: 'System user identifier of the FFP operations agent who approved the transfer. Null if auto-approved or still pending approval.']
  primary_miles_sender_ffp_member_id varchar [note: 'FFP member identifier of the member sending/gifting miles. References the member initiating the transfer.']
  approval_required_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this transfer requires manual approval by FFP operations team (e.g., high-value transfers, fraud risk, first-time transfer). True if approval required, False if auto-approved.']
  approval_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the transfer was approved. Null if auto-approved (approval timestamp equals transfer timestamp) or still pending.']
  bonus_miles_awarded varchar [note: 'Additional bonus miles awarded to sender or recipient as part of a transfer promotion. Zero if no bonus awarded.']
  cancellation_reason varchar [note: 'Free-text explanation for why the transfer was cancelled by sender or system before completion. Null if not cancelled.']
  completion_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the miles were successfully posted to recipient account and deducted from sender account. Null if transfer not yet completed.']
  expiry_date date [note: 'Date when the transferred miles will expire in the recipient account, if different from standard expiry rules. Null if standard expiry applies.']
  fee_discount_applied decimal(10,2) [note: 'Monetary discount amount applied to the standard transfer fee due to promotion, tier status, or relationship type. Zero if no discount applied.']
  fraud_review_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this transfer was flagged for manual fraud review. True if flagged, False otherwise.']
  fraud_risk_score decimal(5,2) [note: 'Automated fraud detection risk score assigned to this transfer at time of initiation (0.00 = no risk, 100.00 = high risk). Used to flag suspicious transfers for review.']
  miles_transferred varchar [note: 'Quantity of loyalty miles transferred from sender to recipient in this transaction. Must be positive integer value.']
  payment_method varchar [note: 'Payment instrument used by sender to pay the transfer fee: credit_card, debit_card, miles_deduction (fee paid in miles), bank_transfer, paypal, wallet (digital wallet).']
  payment_reference_number varchar [note: 'External payment gateway or processor transaction reference number for the transfer fee payment. Null if no fee charged.']
  promotion_code varchar [note: 'Marketing promotion or campaign code applied to this transfer, potentially offering reduced fees or bonus miles. Null if no promotion applied.']
  recipient_annual_receipt_consumed varchar [note: 'Cumulative miles the recipient has received in the current calendar year after this transaction completes. Used to enforce annual receipt limits.']
  recipient_annual_receipt_limit varchar [note: 'Maximum miles the recipient is allowed to receive per calendar year according to FFP program rules. Captured at time of transfer for audit purposes.']
  record_created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this record was first created in the source system. Audit field for data lineage.']
  record_updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this record was last modified in the source system. Audit field for change tracking.']
  rejection_reason varchar [note: 'Free-text explanation for why the transfer was rejected (e.g., insufficient miles, limit exceeded, fraud detected, policy violation). Null if not rejected.']
  relationship_type varchar [note: 'Declared relationship between sender and recipient. Some FFP programs offer preferential transfer terms for family members. Self-declared by sender at time of transfer. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: family|friend|spouse|partner|child|parent|sibling|unrelated — 8 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  relationship_verified_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the declared relationship between sender and recipient has been verified by the airline (e.g., through documentation submission). True if verified, False if unverified or not required.']
  reversal_reason varchar [note: 'Free-text explanation for why a completed transfer was reversed (e.g., fraud investigation, payment chargeback, system error). Null if not reversed.']
  reversal_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the transfer was reversed and miles returned to sender. Null if not reversed.']
  sender_annual_transfer_consumed varchar [note: 'Cumulative miles the sender has transferred in the current calendar year after this transaction completes. Used to enforce annual transfer limits.']
  sender_annual_transfer_limit varchar [note: 'Maximum miles the sender is allowed to transfer per calendar year according to FFP program rules and sender tier status. Captured at time of transfer for audit purposes.']
  source_system varchar [note: 'Name of the operational system that originated this transfer record (e.g., Oracle Loyalty Cloud, legacy FFP system). Used for data lineage and reconciliation.']
  transfer_channel varchar [note: 'Digital or physical channel through which the transfer was initiated: web (airline website), mobile_app, call_center (agent-assisted), partner_portal (coalition partner interface), kiosk (airport self-service).']
  transfer_fee_amount decimal(10,2) [note: 'Monetary fee charged to the sender for executing the miles transfer. Zero for fee-waived transfers (e.g., family pool contributions).']
  transfer_fee_currency_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for the transfer fee amount (e.g., USD, EUR, GBP).']
  transfer_notes varchar [note: 'Free-text notes or comments about the transfer, entered by sender (e.g., gift message) or operations agent (e.g., special handling instructions). Null if no notes.']
  transfer_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the miles transfer: pending (awaiting approval), approved (authorized but not yet posted), completed (miles successfully transferred), rejected (transfer denied), cancelled (sender cancelled before completion), reversed (transfer reversed after completion due to fraud or error).']
  transfer_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the miles transfer was initiated by the sender. Represents the business event time of the transfer request.']
  transfer_transaction_number varchar [note: 'Externally visible unique transaction reference number for the miles transfer, used for customer service inquiries and audit trails.']
  transfer_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the miles transfer transaction: gift (one-time transfer to another member), family_pool (contribution to family pooling account), purchase (bought miles transferred to recipient), share (reciprocal sharing arrangement), donation (charitable contribution of miles).']

  Note: 'Records peer-to-peer miles transfer transactions between FFP members (gifting, pooling, family sharing). Captures sender FFP number, recipient FFP number, miles transferred, transfer fee charged, transfer date, transfer type (gift, family pool contribution, purchase), transfer status, and applicable transfer limits consumed. Distinct from partner_transaction as it represents member-to-member movement of loyalty currency.'
}

Table loyalty.coalition_program {
  coalition_program_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the coalition loyalty program. Primary key.']
  employee_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the system user or process that last modified this coalition program record.']
  regulatory_authority_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.regulatory_authority. Business justification: Coalition loyalty programs operate under data protection regulations (GDPR, CCPA), consumer protection laws, and financial services oversight in multiple jurisdictions. Tracks primary regulatory autho']
  api_authentication_method varchar [note: 'Authentication protocol used to secure API communications between the airline and coalition operator systems.']
  api_endpoint_url varchar [note: 'Base URL of the coalition operator\'s API endpoint used for real-time member data synchronization and transaction processing.']
  auto_renewal_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the coalition program agreement automatically renews at expiration unless terminated by either party.']
  burn_rules_summary varchar [note: 'High-level description of how members redeem coalition currency for airline rewards (e.g., \'Redeem points for award flights at standard rates\').']
  burn_settlement_rate decimal(10,4) [note: 'Financial rate (in settlement currency) that the airline charges the coalition operator per unit of coalition currency redeemed for airline rewards.']
  coalition_operator_country_code varchar [note: 'ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 country code of the coalition operator\'s headquarters.']
  coalition_operator_name varchar [note: 'Legal name of the organization that operates and governs the coalition program (e.g., \'Star Alliance Services GmbH\').']
  contact_email varchar [note: 'Email address of the primary contact person at the coalition operator.']
  contact_person_name varchar [note: 'Name of the primary contact person at the coalition operator for operational and commercial matters.']
  contact_phone varchar [note: 'Phone number of the primary contact person at the coalition operator.']
  conversion_rate_to_airline_miles decimal(10,4) [note: 'Exchange rate for converting coalition program currency to the airline\'s native FFP miles (e.g., 1.0000 means 1:1, 0.5000 means 2 coalition points = 1 airline mile).']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this coalition program record was first created in the system.']
  data_sharing_agreement_reference varchar [note: 'Reference number or identifier of the legal data sharing agreement governing member data exchange between the airline and coalition operator.']
  earn_rules_summary varchar [note: 'High-level description of how members earn coalition currency through airline activity (e.g., \'Earn 1 point per dollar spent on eligible flights\').']
  earn_settlement_rate decimal(10,4) [note: 'Financial rate (in settlement currency) that the coalition operator pays the airline per unit of coalition currency earned by members through airline activity.']
  effective_date date [note: 'Date when the airline\'s participation in the coalition program became or will become active.']
  expiration_date date [note: 'Date when the airline\'s participation in the coalition program ends or is scheduled to end. Null for open-ended agreements.']
  gdpr_compliant_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the coalition program\'s data handling practices are compliant with GDPR requirements for EU member data.']
  governance_model varchar [note: 'Organizational governance structure of the coalition program defining decision-making authority and member airline roles.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this coalition program record was last updated in the system.']
  marketing_visibility_level varchar [note: 'Level of prominence given to the coalition program in the airline\'s customer-facing marketing materials and FFP communications.']
  member_enrollment_url varchar [note: 'Web URL where airline FFP members can enroll in or link their accounts to the coalition program.']
  member_linkage_mechanism varchar [note: 'Technical method used to link airline FFP members to their coalition program accounts.']
  minimum_commitment_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'Minimum financial commitment (in settlement currency) the airline must meet annually to maintain participation in the coalition program.']
  notice_period_days varchar [note: 'Number of days advance notice required by either party to terminate participation in the coalition program.']
  program_code varchar [note: 'Unique business identifier code for the coalition program, used in external communications and partner integrations.']
  program_currency_name varchar [note: 'Name of the loyalty currency used within the coalition program (e.g., \'Alliance Miles\', \'Coalition Points\').']
  program_name varchar [note: 'Official marketing name of the coalition loyalty program (e.g., \'Star Alliance Rewards\', \'SkyTeam Elite Plus\').']
  program_status varchar [note: 'Current operational status of the airline\'s participation in the coalition program.']
  program_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the coalition program by industry vertical or partnership model.']
  settlement_currency_code varchar [note: 'ISO 4217 three-letter currency code used for financial settlements between the airline and coalition operator.']
  settlement_frequency varchar [note: 'Frequency at which financial settlements occur between the airline and the coalition operator for earned and redeemed currency.']
  terms_and_conditions_url varchar [note: 'Web URL hosting the official terms and conditions governing the airline\'s participation in the coalition program.']

  Note: 'Master record for coalition loyalty programs in which the airline participates (e.g., multi-brand earn/burn coalitions). Captures coalition program name, coalition operator, program currency name, conversion rate to airline miles, earn rules, burn rules, member linkage mechanism, settlement terms, data sharing agreement reference, program effective date, and program status. Distinct from bilateral partner_program as coalitions involve multi-party governance.'
}

Table loyalty.status_match {
  status_match_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the status match or tier challenge request. Primary key.']
  campaign_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to marketing.campaign. Business justification: Status match challenges are targeted marketing campaigns to acquire competitors\' elite members. Campaign attribution tracks acquisition costs, conversion rates, and lifetime value of matched members, ']
  employee_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the loyalty operations staff member who reviewed and adjudicated the status match request.']
  ffp_member_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the FFP member requesting the status match or tier challenge.']
