  loyalty_promotion_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to loyalty.loyalty_promotion. Business justification: status_match has promotion_code (STRING) attribute. Status match and tier challenge offers can be part of promotional campaigns (e.g., accelerated status match during promotional periods). Adding loya']
  requested_tier_status_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to loyalty.tier_status. Business justification: status_match has requested_tier_code and requested_tier_name attributes indicating which tier level the member is requesting to match. tier_status is the master definition table for tier levels. Addin']
  approval_notes varchar [note: 'Internal notes recorded by the reviewer regarding the approval or denial decision, including any special conditions or considerations.']
  challenge_duration_months varchar [note: 'Duration of the tier challenge period in months (typically 3, 6, or 12 months). Null for instant match requests.']
  challenge_end_date date [note: 'End date of the tier challenge period by which the member must complete qualifying activity. Null for instant match requests.']
  challenge_start_date date [note: 'Start date of the tier challenge period during which the member must meet qualifying activity requirements. Null for instant match requests.']
  competing_airline_code varchar [note: 'Two-letter IATA airline code of the competing carrier where the member holds elite status.']
  competing_airline_name varchar [note: 'Full name of the competing airline where the member holds elite status.']
  competing_tier_held varchar [note: 'Name or code of the elite tier status the member currently holds with the competing airline (e.g., Gold, Platinum, Diamond).']
  competing_tier_level varchar [note: 'Numeric level of the competing tier for comparison purposes, where higher numbers indicate higher status (e.g., 1=Silver, 2=Gold, 3=Platinum).']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the status match record was first created in the system.']
  denial_reason varchar [note: 'Explanation for why the status match request was denied (e.g., insufficient proof, competing tier too low, member not in good standing, program restrictions).']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the status match record was last updated, including progress updates and status changes.']
  match_outcome varchar [note: 'Final outcome of the status match request: matched (instant tier granted), challenged (challenge period active), denied (request rejected), expired (challenge period ended without completion), withdrawn (member cancelled), completed_success (challenge met), completed_failure (challenge not met). [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: matched|challenged|denied|expired|withdrawn|completed_success|completed_failure — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  matched_tier_code varchar [note: 'Code of the tier status actually granted to the member as a result of the status match. May differ from requested tier if a lower tier was approved.']
  matched_tier_effective_date date [note: 'Date when the matched tier status becomes active for the member. Null if request was denied or is still pending.']
  matched_tier_expiry_date date [note: 'Date when the matched tier status expires. For challenge matches, this is typically set after successful challenge completion. Null if request was denied or is still pending.']
  progress_percentage decimal(5,2) [note: 'Percentage of challenge requirements completed, calculated as the minimum progress across all qualifying metrics (miles, segments, spend).']
  proof_of_status_document_type varchar [note: 'Type of documentation provided by the member to prove their elite status with the competing airline.']
  proof_of_status_reference varchar [note: 'Reference identifier or storage location for the uploaded proof of status documentation (e.g., document management system ID, cloud storage path).']
  qualifying_miles_achieved varchar [note: 'Number of qualifying miles the member has earned toward the challenge requirement as of the current date.']
  qualifying_miles_required varchar [note: 'Number of qualifying miles the member must earn during the challenge period to retain the matched tier. Null for instant match requests.']
  qualifying_segments_achieved varchar [note: 'Number of qualifying flight segments the member has completed toward the challenge requirement as of the current date.']
  qualifying_segments_required varchar [note: 'Number of qualifying flight segments the member must complete during the challenge period. Null for instant match requests.']
  qualifying_spend_achieved decimal(15,2) [note: 'Monetary spend amount the member has achieved toward the challenge requirement as of the current date.']
  qualifying_spend_currency varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for the qualifying spend requirement.']
  qualifying_spend_required decimal(15,2) [note: 'Monetary spend amount the member must achieve during the challenge period. Null for instant match requests.']
  request_date date [note: 'Date when the member submitted the status match or tier challenge request.']
  request_number varchar [note: 'Externally visible unique reference number for the status match request, formatted as SM followed by 10 digits.']
  request_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the status match request. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: submitted|under_review|approved|denied|expired|withdrawn|completed — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  request_type varchar [note: 'Type of status match request: instant match (immediate tier grant), challenge with conditions (earn tier by meeting activity requirements), courtesy match (discretionary grant), or reciprocal match (partner airline agreement).']
  requested_tier_code varchar [note: 'Code of the tier status the member is requesting to be matched to in the airline\'s FFP.']
  review_date date [note: 'Date when the status match request was reviewed and a decision was made.']
  source_channel varchar [note: 'Channel through which the member submitted the status match request.']

  Note: 'Tracks status match and tier challenge requests from members holding elite status with a competing airline. Captures applicant FFP number, competing airline name, competing tier held, proof of status document reference, requested tier, challenge type (instant match, challenge with conditions), challenge start date, challenge end date, qualifying activity required, qualifying activity achieved, outcome (matched, challenged, denied, expired), and matched tier granted.'
}

Table loyalty.award_inventory {
  award_inventory_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the award inventory record. Primary key for the award seat allocation entity.']
  cabin_class_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to inventory.cabin_class. Business justification: Award inventory allocation is managed per cabin class with distinct availability and pricing. Normalizes cabin_class text column. Critical for award seat availability management and FFP redemption ope']
  route_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to route.route. Business justification: Award seat allocation strategy by route, route profitability analysis including award redemption costs, and network planning for award availability all require linking award inventory to routes. Essen']
  allocation_effective_date date [note: 'The date from which this award seat allocation becomes effective and available for member booking.']
  allocation_expiry_date date [note: 'The date after which this award seat allocation expires and is no longer available for booking. Null for open-ended allocations.']
  allocation_notes varchar [note: 'Free-text notes regarding special conditions, restrictions, or context for this award inventory allocation. Used by revenue management and FFP operations teams.']
  allocation_source varchar [note: 'The source or method by which award seats were allocated to this inventory bucket. Revenue management indicates standard RM allocation; partner agreement indicates contractual allocation; promotional indicates campaign-driven allocation; dynamic indicates real-time algorithmic allocation.']
  award_bucket_code varchar [note: 'The award inventory bucket code indicating the redemption tier (e.g., saver, standard, premium). Determines mileage cost and availability rules.']
  award_bucket_type varchar [note: 'The award redemption tier type. Saver awards require fewer miles but have limited availability; standard and premium awards offer greater availability at higher mileage cost.']
  blackout_date_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this travel date is designated as a blackout date for award redemptions. True means no award seats are available regardless of inventory.']
  booking_window_close_days varchar [note: 'The minimum number of days before departure that award bookings close. Typically 1-14 days depending on route and cabin.']
  booking_window_open_days varchar [note: 'The number of days in advance that award seats become available for booking. Standard is 330-365 days for most airlines.']
  cancellation_fee_waived_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the standard award cancellation fee is waived for bookings in this inventory bucket. Often applies to elite members or promotional awards.']
  change_fee_waived_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the standard award change fee is waived for bookings in this inventory bucket. Often applies to elite members or promotional awards.']
  co_pay_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'The mandatory cash co-payment amount required in addition to miles for award redemption. Zero if no co-pay is required.']
  co_pay_currency_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for the co-payment amount. Null if no co-pay is required.']
  dynamic_pricing_enabled_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether dynamic award pricing is enabled for this inventory, allowing mileage cost to vary based on demand and availability rather than fixed award chart rates.']
  elite_only_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether award seat availability is restricted to elite tier members only. Used for premium cabin inventory management.']
  flight_number varchar [note: 'The airline flight number for which award seats are allocated. Follows IATA flight number format with carrier code and numeric identifier.']
  inventory_status varchar [note: 'The current operational status of the award inventory. Open means available for booking; closed means no longer accepting reservations; suspended means temporarily unavailable; restricted means limited access.']
  last_updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The timestamp when this award inventory record was last modified. Critical for real-time availability synchronization across distribution channels.']
  marketing_carrier_code varchar [note: 'Two-character IATA airline code of the carrier marketing the award seats. Relevant for codeshare and partner award bookings.']
  miles_required varchar [note: 'The number of FFP miles required to redeem one award seat in this bucket and cabin class. Varies by route, cabin, and bucket type.']
  minimum_tier_required varchar [note: 'The minimum FFP tier status code required to access this award inventory bucket. Null if available to all members.']
  operating_carrier_code varchar [note: 'Two-character IATA airline code of the carrier operating the flight. May differ from marketing carrier in codeshare scenarios.']
  partner_award_availability_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether these award seats are available for redemption by partner FFP members under reciprocal agreements.']
  record_created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The timestamp when this award inventory record was first created in the system. Used for audit trail and data lineage.']
  seats_available varchar [note: 'The current number of award seats available for immediate booking. Decremented upon award reservation confirmation.']
  seats_held varchar [note: 'The number of award seats currently held in pending or unconfirmed reservations. These seats are temporarily unavailable but not yet redeemed.']
  seats_redeemed varchar [note: 'The cumulative number of award seats that have been successfully redeemed and ticketed for this flight, cabin, and bucket.']
  seats_waitlisted varchar [note: 'The number of members currently on the waitlist for award seats on this flight and cabin when availability is zero.']
  total_award_seats_allocated varchar [note: 'The total number of seats allocated to the FFP award inventory pool for this flight, cabin, and bucket combination. Set by revenue management.']
  travel_date date [note: 'The scheduled departure date of the flight for which award inventory is allocated. Key dimension for award seat availability queries.']
  upgrade_eligible_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether these award seats are eligible for mileage upgrade from a lower cabin class. Relevant for upgrade award inventory management.']

  Note: 'Manages the availability of award seats allocated for FFP redemption across flights and cabin classes. Captures flight number, travel date, origin, destination, cabin class, award bucket code (saver/standard/premium), total award seats allocated, seats available, seats held, seats redeemed, blackout date flag, partner award availability flag, and last updated timestamp. Distinct from the revenue inventory domain — this is the loyalty-specific award seat pool managed by the FFP program.'
}

Table loyalty.partner_settlement {
  partner_settlement_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the partner settlement transaction record. Primary key for the partner settlement entity.']
  accounts_payable_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.accounts_payable. Business justification: Partner settlements for miles redeemed via partners create AP obligations. Real cash settlement process requires tracking payables to partners (hotels, car rentals) for miles burned on their services,']
  accounts_receivable_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.accounts_receivable. Business justification: When airline sells miles to partners (credit card companies, hotels), creates AR for amounts due. Real billing and collection process for mile sales revenue, essential for cash flow management and rev']
  employee_id varchar [note: 'User identifier of the person who approved the settlement. Null if approval is not required or not yet granted.']
  partner_program_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the FFP partner program involved in this settlement. Links to the partner program master data defining earn/burn agreements.']
  adjustment_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Any manual adjustments applied to the settlement, including corrections, credits, or penalties agreed between the airline and partner.']
  approval_required_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this settlement requires management approval before invoicing, typically true for settlements above a threshold or with variances.']
  approved_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the settlement was approved. Null if approval is not required or not yet granted.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this settlement record was first created in the system. Audit field for data lineage and compliance.']
  exchange_rate decimal(12,6) [note: 'Exchange rate applied if the settlement currency differs from the partner program base currency. Used for currency conversion in multi-currency settlements.']
  invoice_reference_number varchar [note: 'Reference number of the invoice generated for this settlement. Links the settlement to the accounts receivable or payable invoice in the finance system.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this settlement record was last updated. Audit field for tracking changes and data lineage.']
  miles_redeemed_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Total monetary value of miles redeemed via the partner during the settlement period, calculated using the agreed burn rate.']
  miles_redeemed_via_partner varchar [note: 'Total number of FFP miles redeemed through the partner during the settlement period. Represents burn transactions where members used miles for partner services.']
  miles_sold_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Total monetary value of miles sold to the partner during the settlement period, calculated using the agreed earn rate.']
  miles_sold_to_partner varchar [note: 'Total number of FFP miles sold to the partner during the settlement period. Represents earn transactions where the partner purchased miles for their customers.']
  net_miles_position varchar [note: 'Net miles position for the settlement period, calculated as miles sold minus miles redeemed. Positive indicates partner owes airline; negative indicates airline owes partner.']
  partner_statement_reference varchar [note: 'Reference number from the partners settlement statement or report, used for cross-referencing during reconciliation.']
  payment_due_date date [note: 'Date by which payment is due according to the partner agreement payment terms. Used for aging analysis and collections management.']
  payment_received_date date [note: 'Actual date when payment was received from the partner or paid to the partner. Null if payment has not yet been completed.']
  payment_status varchar [note: 'Current payment status of the settlement. Tracks whether the invoice has been paid, is outstanding, or has been written off.']
  reconciliation_status varchar [note: 'Status of the reconciliation process between airline and partner records. Indicates whether transaction counts and amounts match or require investigation.']
  settlement_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Net settlement amount due, calculated as miles sold amount minus miles redeemed amount plus adjustments. Positive indicates receivable; negative indicates payable.']
  settlement_currency_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for the settlement transaction. Defines the currency in which the settlement is denominated.']
  settlement_date date [note: 'Official date when the settlement was finalized and approved. Represents the business event timestamp for the settlement transaction.']
  settlement_frequency varchar [note: 'Frequency at which settlements are processed with this partner, as defined in the partner agreement.']
  settlement_notes varchar [note: 'Free-text notes regarding the settlement, including special circumstances, dispute details, or communication with the partner.']
  settlement_period_end_date date [note: 'The last date of the period covered by this settlement transaction. Defines the end of the accrual and redemption window being reconciled.']
  settlement_period_start_date date [note: 'The first date of the period covered by this settlement transaction. Defines the beginning of the accrual and redemption window being reconciled.']
  settlement_reference_number varchar [note: 'Externally-known unique business identifier for this settlement transaction, used for partner communication and reconciliation.']
  settlement_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the settlement transaction. Tracks progression from draft through approval, invoicing, payment, or dispute resolution. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: draft|pending_approval|approved|invoiced|paid|disputed|cancelled — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  source_system_code varchar [note: 'Code identifying the operational system that originated this settlement record, typically the loyalty management platform.']
  transaction_count_redeemed varchar [note: 'Number of individual burn transactions included in the miles redeemed via partner total. Used for reconciliation and audit purposes.']
  transaction_count_sold varchar [note: 'Number of individual earn transactions included in the miles sold to partner total. Used for reconciliation and audit purposes.']
  variance_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Monetary variance identified during reconciliation between airline and partner records. Null if no variance exists.']
  variance_reason varchar [note: 'Explanation of any variance identified during reconciliation, including root cause and resolution plan.']

  Note: 'Records financial settlement transactions between the airline and FFP earn/burn partners for miles sold, redeemed, and reconciled. Captures partner program ID, settlement period, miles sold to partner (earn), miles redeemed via partner (burn), net miles position, settlement amount, settlement currency, exchange rate, invoice reference, payment status, and settlement date. Feeds into the finance domain for accounts receivable/payable but is owned by loyalty as the operational source.'
}

Table loyalty.lifetime_status {
  lifetime_status_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the lifetime status record. Primary key.']
  employee_id varchar [note: 'The user ID or employee identifier of the person who approved the lifetime status grant.']
  ffp_member_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the FFP member who holds this lifetime status.']
  tier_status_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to loyalty.tier_status. Business justification: lifetime_status has lifetime_status_level and lifetime_status_code attributes indicating the permanent tier level granted. tier_status is the master definition table for tier levels. Adding tier_statu']
  approval_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when the lifetime status grant was officially approved.']
  benefits_override_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this lifetime status includes custom or enhanced benefits beyond the standard tier benefits (true if custom benefits apply).']
  bonus_mile_multiplier_lifetime decimal(5,2) [note: 'The permanent bonus mile earning multiplier applied to all future flights for this lifetime status holder (e.g., 1.50 for 50% bonus).']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when this lifetime status record was first created in the data platform.']
  external_reference_code varchar [note: 'External system identifier or reference number for this lifetime status record in the source system.']
  extra_baggage_allowance_kg varchar [note: 'Additional baggage allowance in kilograms granted permanently to the lifetime status holder.']
  grant_date date [note: 'The date on which the lifetime status was officially granted to the member.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when this lifetime status record was last updated in the data platform.']
  lifetime_miles_earned varchar [note: 'Cumulative total of all miles earned by the member across their entire FFP membership history at the time of lifetime status grant.']
  lifetime_segments_flown varchar [note: 'Cumulative total of flight segments flown by the member across their entire FFP membership history at the time of lifetime status grant.']
  lifetime_spend_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Cumulative total spend (revenue contribution) by the member across their entire FFP membership history at the time of lifetime status grant.']
  lifetime_spend_currency varchar [note: 'ISO 4217 three-letter currency code for the lifetime spend amount.']
  lifetime_status_code varchar [note: 'Short code representing the lifetime status level for system processing and display.']
  lifetime_status_level varchar [note: 'The permanent tier level achieved by the member (e.g., Lifetime Gold, Lifetime Platinum). This status is non-expiring and represents lifetime achievement.']
  lifetime_status_status varchar [note: 'Current state of the lifetime status. Typically active, but may be suspended or revoked in exceptional circumstances (e.g., program abuse, fraud).']
  lounge_access_lifetime_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the member has permanent lounge access as part of their lifetime status benefits.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text notes or comments regarding the lifetime status grant, special circumstances, or administrative details.']
  partner_tier_equivalent_code varchar [note: 'The equivalent tier code recognized by partner airlines and alliance programs for this lifetime status (e.g., oneworld Emerald, Star Alliance Gold).']
  priority_boarding_lifetime_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the member has permanent priority boarding privileges as part of their lifetime status benefits.']
  qualification_basis varchar [note: 'The primary criterion or method by which the member qualified for lifetime status (e.g., lifetime miles threshold, lifetime segments, special recognition).']
  recognition_type varchar [note: 'The type of recognition or award associated with this lifetime status (e.g., Million Miler, Two Million Miler, Lifetime Achievement Award).']
  revocation_date date [note: 'The date on which the lifetime status was revoked, if applicable. Null if status is active.']
  revocation_reason varchar [note: 'The reason for revoking the lifetime status, if applicable (e.g., program abuse, fraud, terms violation).']
  source_system varchar [note: 'The operational system from which this lifetime status record originated (e.g., Oracle Loyalty Cloud, legacy FFP system).']
  special_benefits_description varchar [note: 'Free-text description of any special or custom benefits granted to this lifetime status holder beyond standard tier benefits.']
  threshold_miles_required varchar [note: 'The lifetime miles threshold that was required to achieve this lifetime status level at the time of grant.']
  threshold_segments_required varchar [note: 'The lifetime segments threshold that was required to achieve this lifetime status level at the time of grant.']
  threshold_spend_required decimal(18,2) [note: 'The lifetime spend threshold that was required to achieve this lifetime status level at the time of grant.']
  upgrade_eligibility_lifetime_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the member has permanent complimentary upgrade eligibility as part of their lifetime status benefits.']
  waitlist_priority_rank varchar [note: 'The priority ranking assigned to this lifetime status holder for upgrade and standby waitlists (lower number = higher priority).']

  Note: 'Tracks lifetime achievement milestones and permanent status designations for long-tenured FFP members. Captures lifetime miles earned (cumulative all-time), lifetime segments flown, lifetime spend, lifetime tier thresholds crossed, lifetime status level achieved (e.g., Lifetime Gold, Lifetime Platinum), lifetime status grant date, and any lifetime status benefits override. Distinct from tier_qualification which tracks annual cycle qualification — lifetime status is permanent and non-expiring.'
}

Table loyalty.miles_purchase {
  miles_purchase_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the miles purchase transaction. Primary key.']
  accounts_receivable_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.accounts_receivable. Business justification: Direct member mile purchases create receivables when payment terms allow or payment processing is pending. Links purchase transaction to AR for collection tracking, aging analysis, and revenue recogni']
  campaign_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to marketing.campaign. Business justification: Buy-miles promotions (bonus percentages, discounted rates) are revenue-generating marketing campaigns. Campaign attribution is essential for ROI calculation, revenue forecasting, and measuring promoti']
  employee_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the customer service agent who processed the transaction, if applicable.']
  ffp_member_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the FFP member who purchased the miles.']
  loyalty_promotion_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the loyalty promotion campaign associated with this purchase.']
  regulatory_requirement_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.regulatory_requirement. Business justification: Miles purchase programs must comply with consumer protection regulations (pricing transparency, refund rights), payment processing rules (PCI-DSS), and tax regulations (VAT/sales tax treatment). Links']
  base_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'Base purchase amount before taxes and fees.']
  bonus_miles_quantity varchar [note: 'Number of promotional bonus miles awarded with this purchase, if applicable.']
  cancellation_reason varchar [note: 'Reason provided for cancelling or refunding the miles purchase.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this record was first created in the system.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for the purchase transaction.']
  device_type varchar [note: 'Type of device used to initiate the purchase.']
  discount_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'Discount amount applied to the purchase due to promotional offers.']
  external_transaction_code varchar [note: 'Transaction identifier from the external payment gateway or partner system.']
  ffp_number varchar [note: 'The member\'s FFP account number associated with this purchase transaction.']
  fraud_check_status varchar [note: 'Result of fraud detection screening for this transaction.']
  fraud_score decimal(5,2) [note: 'Numerical fraud risk score assigned by the fraud detection system.']
  ip_address varchar [note: 'IP address from which the purchase was initiated, used for fraud detection and security.']
  last_updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this record was last modified.']
  miles_expiration_date date [note: 'Date when the purchased miles will expire if not redeemed.']
  miles_quantity varchar [note: 'Number of base miles purchased by the member in this transaction.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text notes or comments related to the miles purchase transaction.']
  payment_authorization_code varchar [note: 'Authorization code returned by the payment processor confirming the transaction.']
  payment_method varchar [note: 'Payment instrument used for the miles purchase (e.g., credit card, PayPal, bank transfer).']
  payment_reference varchar [note: 'External payment gateway or processor transaction reference number.']
  posting_date date [note: 'Date when the purchased miles were posted to the member\'s FFP account.']
  price_per_mile decimal(10,4) [note: 'Unit price charged per mile in the purchase currency.']
  processing_fee_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'Processing or service fee charged for the miles purchase transaction.']
  promotion_code varchar [note: 'Promotional code applied to the purchase for bonus miles or discounts.']
  purchase_channel varchar [note: 'Channel through which the member initiated the miles purchase (e.g., web, mobile app, call center).']
  purchase_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the miles purchase transaction.']
  purchase_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the member initiated the miles purchase transaction.']
  refund_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'Amount refunded to the customer in the original currency.']
  refund_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this purchase has been refunded.']
  refund_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the refund was processed, if applicable.']
  source_system varchar [note: 'Name of the source system that originated this miles purchase record.']
  tax_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'Total tax amount applied to the miles purchase.']
  total_amount_charged decimal(15,2) [note: 'Total amount charged to the customer, including base amount, taxes, and fees.']
  total_miles_credited varchar [note: 'Total miles credited to the member account, including base miles and bonus miles.']
  transaction_number varchar [note: 'Externally-visible unique transaction reference number for the miles purchase, used for customer service and reconciliation.']

  Note: 'Records transactions where members purchase additional miles directly from the airline to top up their balance. Captures member FFP number, miles purchased quantity, price per mile, total amount charged, purchase currency, payment method reference, purchase date, bonus miles included (if promotional), purchase channel, and transaction status. Distinct from mileage_accrual (which covers earned miles) — purchased miles represent a direct commercial revenue transaction.'
}

Table loyalty.miles_adjustment {
  miles_adjustment_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the miles adjustment transaction. Primary key for the miles adjustment record.']
  campaign_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to marketing.campaign. Business justification: Goodwill miles, promotional credits, and campaign-driven adjustments require campaign attribution for cost center allocation, promotional expense tracking, and reconciliation of campaign-promised bene']
  capa_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.compliance_capa. Business justification: When compliance audits discover systematic mileage calculation errors or policy violations, corrective actions require bulk member adjustments. Links adjustment to originating CAPA for audit trail, co']
  employee_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the customer service agent, supervisor, or system user who authorized and processed the miles adjustment.']
  ffp_member_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the FFP member whose miles balance is being adjusted.']
  loyalty_promotion_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to loyalty.loyalty_promotion. Business justification: miles_adjustment has promotion_code (STRING) attribute. Adjustments can be related to promotional campaigns (e.g., crediting promotional bonus miles, correcting promotion-related errors). Adding loyal']
  mileage_accrual_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to loyalty.mileage_accrual. Business justification: miles_adjustment has source_transaction_reference and source_transaction_type attributes. When an adjustment corrects or reverses an accrual transaction, it should reference the original mileage_accru']
  mileage_redemption_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to loyalty.mileage_redemption. Business justification: miles_adjustment can correct or reverse redemption transactions. When source_transaction_type indicates a redemption, this FK should reference the original mileage_redemption record. This FK will be N']
  original_adjustment_miles_adjustment_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the original miles adjustment record that is being reversed by this transaction.']
  partner_program_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the partner program if the adjustment relates to a partner airline or coalition program transaction.']
  adjustment_channel varchar [note: 'Channel or interface through which the adjustment request was initiated or processed. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: call_center|web_portal|mobile_app|email_request|social_media|airport_counter|system_batch — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  adjustment_reason_code varchar [note: 'Standardized code representing the specific reason for the adjustment, used for categorization and reporting purposes.']
  adjustment_reason_description varchar [note: 'Detailed free-text explanation of why the miles adjustment was made, providing context for audit and customer service purposes.']
  adjustment_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the adjustment request indicating whether it has been approved, processed, or rejected.']
  adjustment_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the miles adjustment was processed and applied to the member balance.']
  adjustment_transaction_number varchar [note: 'Externally-visible unique transaction number for the miles adjustment, used for customer service reference and audit trail.']
  adjustment_type varchar [note: 'Category of miles adjustment indicating the business reason for the balance change outside standard earn/burn transactions. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: goodwill_credit|retro_credit_claim|error_correction|fraud_reversal|miles_reinstatement|compensation|expired_miles_restoration|system_migration|partner_reconciliation|promotional_bonus — 10 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  approval_level varchar [note: 'Organizational level of authority that approved the adjustment, indicating the escalation tier required based on adjustment amount or type.']
  approval_reference varchar [note: 'Reference number or identifier for the approval workflow, case number, or authorization ticket that justified the adjustment.']
  audit_notes varchar [note: 'Internal notes and comments recorded by agents or supervisors for audit trail and quality assurance purposes.']
  compliance_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this adjustment was flagged for compliance review due to unusual amount, pattern, or regulatory concern.']
  cost_centre_code varchar [note: 'Financial cost centre to which the liability or expense of this miles adjustment is allocated for accounting purposes.']
  effective_date date [note: 'Date from which the adjusted miles are considered valid and available for redemption or qualification purposes.']
  expiration_date date [note: 'Date when the adjusted miles will expire if not used, applicable for time-limited adjustments such as promotional credits or goodwill gestures.']
  external_reference_code varchar [note: 'External system identifier or reference number from partner systems, GDS, or third-party platforms related to this adjustment.']
  financial_impact_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'Estimated monetary value or liability impact of the miles adjustment, calculated based on the airline internal valuation of miles.']
  financial_impact_currency varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for the financial impact amount.']
  fraud_review_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this adjustment was flagged for fraud investigation due to suspicious activity or pattern detection.']
  member_notification_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the FFP member was notified of this miles adjustment via email, SMS, or account alert.']
  miles_amount varchar [note: 'Quantity of miles being adjusted. Positive values represent credits to the member account; negative values represent debits or reversals.']
  notification_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the member notification was sent regarding this miles adjustment.']
  processing_priority varchar [note: 'Priority level assigned to the adjustment request, often influenced by member tier status or issue severity.']
  record_created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this miles adjustment record was first created in the system.']
  record_updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this miles adjustment record was last modified or updated.']
  request_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the member or agent initially requested the miles adjustment, distinct from when it was processed.']
  reversal_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this adjustment is a reversal of a previous adjustment transaction.']
  source_system varchar [note: 'Name or identifier of the operational system that originated or processed this miles adjustment record.']
  source_transaction_reference varchar [note: 'Reference to the original transaction that is being corrected or adjusted, such as a flight booking reference, partner transaction ID, or redemption number.']
  source_transaction_type varchar [note: 'Type of original transaction that triggered the need for this adjustment, providing context for the correction.']

  Note: 'Records manual or system-initiated adjustments to a member\'s miles balance outside of standard earn/burn transactions. Captures adjustment type (goodwill credit, retro-credit claim, error correction, fraud reversal, miles reinstatement, compensation), miles amount (positive or negative), adjustment reason code, authorizing agent ID, approval reference, adjustment date, source transaction reference, and member notification flag. Provides the audit trail for non-standard balance changes.'
}

Table loyalty.member_audit_sample {
  member_audit_sample_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for this member audit sample record. Primary key.']
  audit_event_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to the audit event during which this member was sampled']
  ffp_member_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to the FFP member record that was sampled in this audit event']
  documentation_provided varchar [note: 'List or description of documentation provided or reviewed for this member during the audit (e.g., transaction history, redemption records, tier qualification evidence, complaint correspondence).']
  examination_status varchar [note: 'Current status of the examination of this member within this audit event. Pending=selected but not yet examined, In Progress=examination underway, Completed=examination finished, Deferred=postponed to follow-up, Excluded=removed from sample.']
  finding_count varchar [note: 'Number of audit findings, discrepancies, or non-conformances identified specifically related to this member account during this audit event. Zero indicates clean examination.']
  interview_date date [note: 'Date when the member interview or account examination was conducted as part of this audit event. May be null if no direct interview occurred.']
  interview_notes varchar [note: 'Auditor notes from member interview or account examination. May contain sensitive information about member behavior, complaints, or account irregularities discovered during audit.']
  risk_category varchar [note: 'Risk classification assigned to this member for audit sampling purposes (e.g., high-value accounts, frequent redeemers, recent complaints, tier qualification edge cases). Drives sample stratification.']
  sample_selection_date date [note: 'Date when this member was selected for inclusion in the audit sample. Used to track audit planning timeline and sample frame timing.']
  sample_selection_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this member was formally selected as part of the audit sample (true) or examined opportunistically (false). Tracks audit methodology compliance.']

  Note: 'This association product represents the sampling and examination of individual FFP member accounts during compliance audit events. It captures the selection, interview, documentation review, and findings for each member-audit pair. Each record links one ffp_member to one audit_event with attributes that exist only in the context of this audit sampling relationship.. Existence Justification: In airline loyalty program compliance audits, auditors sample multiple member accounts to test controls around revenue recognition, fraud prevention, tier qualification accuracy, and consumer protection compliance. Each member may be sampled across multiple audit events over time (annual audits, regulatory inspections, fraud investigations). The relationship represents the operational audit sampling methodology where each member-audit pair has distinct examination data (selection criteria, interview notes, documentation reviewed, findings specific to that member in that audit).'
}

Table loyalty.qualification_cycle {
  qualification_cycle_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for qualification_cycle']
  predecessor_cycle_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the previous qualification cycle that immediately preceded this one in the program timeline.']
  previous_qualification_cycle_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing FK on qualification_cycle (previous_qualification_cycle_id)']
  program_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the loyalty program to which this qualification cycle belongs.']
  successor_cycle_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the next qualification cycle that immediately follows this one in the program timeline.']
  benefit_effective_date date [note: 'The date when newly qualified tier benefits become active for members who qualified in this cycle.']
  benefit_expiry_date date [note: 'The date when tier benefits earned through this qualification cycle expire.']
  communication_sent_date date [note: 'Date when members were notified about this qualification cycle (e.g., cycle start announcement, rule changes).']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this qualification cycle record was first created in the system.']
  cycle_code varchar [note: 'Business identifier code for the qualification cycle, used for external reference and reporting.']
  cycle_name varchar [note: 'Human-readable name of the qualification cycle (e.g., \'Calendar Year 2024\', \'Q1 2024 Elite Qualification\').']
  cycle_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the qualification cycle period structure (annual, semi-annual, quarterly, rolling 12-month, or custom).']
  description varchar [note: 'Detailed description of the qualification cycle, including any special rules, promotions, or conditions applicable to this cycle.']
  end_date date [note: 'The date when the qualification cycle ends and qualifying activity accrual stops.']
  evaluation_date date [note: 'The date when member qualification status is evaluated and tier promotions/demotions are processed for this cycle.']
  grace_period_days varchar [note: 'Number of days after cycle end date during which late-posted qualifying activity may still be credited to the cycle.']
  is_active boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this is the currently active qualification cycle for the program.']
  is_lifetime_eligible boolean [note: 'Indicates whether qualifying activity in this cycle counts toward lifetime status achievement.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this qualification cycle record was last updated.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Internal operational notes or comments about this qualification cycle for program administrators.']
  partner_activity_eligible boolean [note: 'Indicates whether qualifying activity earned through partner airlines and coalition partners counts toward qualification in this cycle.']
  promotion_multiplier decimal(5,2) [note: 'Default promotional multiplier applied to qualifying activity during this cycle (e.g., 1.50 for 50% bonus).']
  qualification_metric varchar [note: 'The primary metric used to measure member qualification progress during this cycle (e.g., qualifying miles, qualifying points, qualifying segments, qualifying dollars, or hybrid combination).']
  rollover_allowed boolean [note: 'Indicates whether excess qualifying activity beyond the highest tier threshold can roll over to the next qualification cycle.']
  rollover_percentage decimal(5,2) [note: 'Percentage of excess qualifying activity that rolls over to the next cycle if rollover is allowed (e.g., 50.00 for 50%).']
  sequence_number varchar [note: 'Sequential ordering number of this cycle within the program history (e.g., 1 for first cycle, 2 for second cycle).']
  soft_landing_tiers varchar [note: 'Number of tier levels a member drops if soft landing rule applies (e.g., 1 means drop one tier level).']
  start_date date [note: 'The date when the qualification cycle begins and members can start accruing qualifying activity.']
  status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the qualification cycle.']
  terms_and_conditions_url varchar [note: 'URL link to the official terms and conditions document governing this qualification cycle.']
  tier_retention_rule varchar [note: 'Rule governing how members retain or lose tier status if they do not requalify (requalify from scratch, soft landing to lower tier, guaranteed period retention, or no retention).']

  Note: 'Master reference table for qualification_cycle. Referenced by qualification_cycle_id.'
}

Table airport.terminal_facility {
  terminal_facility_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the airport terminal facility record. Primary key.']
  cost_centre_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.cost_centre. Business justification: Terminal facilities are cost centers for budgeting, P&L reporting, and operational expense allocation. Airlines track terminal operating costs (utilities, maintenance, staffing) against budgets for st']
  facility_manager_employee_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to workforce.employee. Business justification: Terminal facilities require operational managers from workforce for day-to-day operations, staff coordination, and incident response. Aviation operations standard practice to assign facility managers.']
  station_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to airport.station. Business justification: terminal_facility exists at a specific station/airport. Currently stores airport_code and icao_code (both STRING). FK to station provides access to station details including both IATA and ICAO codes, ']
  accessibility_compliance_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the terminal facility meets accessibility standards for passengers with reduced mobility (PRM) including wheelchair access, accessible counters, and assistance services.']
  annual_passenger_volume varchar [note: 'Total number of passengers processed through this terminal facility in the most recent full calendar year.']
  bag_drop_counter_count varchar [note: 'Number of dedicated self-service bag drop counters for passengers who have completed online or kiosk check-in.']
  biometric_capability_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the terminal facility is equipped with biometric passenger processing technology (facial recognition, fingerprint scanning) for check-in and boarding.']
  cdm_enabled_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the terminal facility participates in Airport Collaborative Decision Making (A-CDM) for real-time information sharing between airport, airlines, and Air Traffic Control (ATC).']
  check_in_counter_count varchar [note: 'Total number of full-service check-in counters staffed by airline agents for passenger processing and baggage acceptance.']
  contact_gate_count varchar [note: 'Number of contact gates equipped with jet bridges for direct aircraft-to-terminal passenger boarding.']
  counter_zone_designation varchar [note: 'Alphanumeric designation of check-in counter zones within the terminal (e.g., Zone A, Rows 1-20, Island 3).']
  customs_facility_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the terminal has customs and border control facilities for international passenger processing.']
  facility_address varchar [note: 'Physical street address of the terminal facility for operational coordination and ground transportation routing.']
  facility_city varchar [note: 'City where the terminal facility is located.']
  facility_country_code varchar [note: 'ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 country code for the terminal facility location (e.g., USA, GBR, ARE).']
  facility_email varchar [note: 'Primary email address for terminal facility operational communications and coordination.']
  facility_phone varchar [note: 'Primary contact telephone number for terminal facility operations and coordination.']
  facility_postal_code varchar [note: 'Postal or ZIP code for the terminal facility address.']
  facility_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the terminal facility structure type.']
  hub_classification varchar [note: 'Strategic classification of the airport station in the airline\'s network (hub = central connecting airport, spoke = route from hub to destination).']
  last_renovation_date date [note: 'Date of the most recent major renovation or modernization project completed for this terminal facility.']
  lounge_count varchar [note: 'Number of airline lounges operated within this terminal facility for premium passengers and Frequent Flyer Program (FFP) members.']
  opening_date date [note: 'Date when the terminal facility first became operational for passenger services.']
  operating_airline_code varchar [note: 'IATA airline code of the primary operating carrier for this terminal facility (null for joint-use or airport authority facilities).']
  operational_status varchar [note: 'Current operational state of the terminal facility.']
  ownership_model varchar [note: 'Designation of facility ownership and usage rights (airline-owned, joint-use with other carriers, or airport authority managed).']
  passenger_capacity_per_hour varchar [note: 'Design capacity of the terminal facility measured in passengers (PAX) that can be processed per hour during peak operations.']
  record_created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this terminal facility record was first created in the system.']
  record_updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this terminal facility record was last modified in the system.']
  remote_gate_count varchar [note: 'Number of remote gates requiring bus transportation for passenger boarding (no jet bridge connection).']
  security_checkpoint_count varchar [note: 'Number of Transportation Security Administration (TSA) or equivalent security screening checkpoints in the terminal facility.']
  self_service_kiosk_count varchar [note: 'Number of self-service check-in kiosks (CUSS - Common Use Self-Service) available for passenger self-check-in.']
  slot_coordinated_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the airport requires slot coordination (allocated takeoff and landing times) due to capacity constraints.']
  terminal_area_sqm decimal(12,2) [note: 'Total floor area of the terminal facility measured in square meters.']
  terminal_identifier varchar [note: 'Business identifier for the terminal within the airport (e.g., Terminal 1, Terminal A, Concourse B, Satellite Terminal).']
  total_gate_count varchar [note: 'Total number of aircraft gates available in this terminal facility for passenger boarding and deplaning operations.']

  Note: 'Master record for airport terminal facilities including terminal buildings, concourses, piers, satellite structures, and check-in counter/kiosk assets. Captures IATA/ICAO airport codes, terminal identifiers, facility type, total gate count, check-in counter inventory (counter zones, types including full-service/self-service/CUSS/bag-drop, airline assignments, accessibility compliance, biometric capability), lounge count, capacity (PAX per hour), operational status, hub classification (hub vs spoke station), and owning airline or joint-use designation. SSOT for physical airport facility assets and check-in infrastructure managed by the airline.'
}

Table airport.gate {
  gate_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the aircraft gate. Primary key for the gate master record.']
  cost_centre_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.cost_centre. Business justification: Gates incur direct operating costs (ground power, jetbridge maintenance, utilities) that must be allocated to cost centers for financial reporting, station P&L analysis, and turnaround cost modeling u']
  station_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to airport.station. Business justification: gate exists at a specific station/airport. Currently stores airport_code (STRING). FK to station provides access to station details, time zone, and operational context. The airport_code becomes redund']
  terminal_facility_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the terminal facility that contains this gate.']
  aircraft_size_code_max varchar [note: 'Maximum ICAO aircraft size code (A through F) that this gate can accommodate based on wingspan, parking envelope, and structural limitations.']
  aircraft_size_code_min varchar [note: 'Minimum ICAO aircraft size code (A through F) that this gate can accommodate based on wingspan and parking envelope.']
  common_use_gate boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this gate is operated as a common-use facility available to multiple airlines through dynamic assignment, or is dedicated to a specific carrier.']
  concourse varchar [note: 'The concourse or pier designation within the terminal (e.g., A, B, C, D, International).']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this gate master record was first created in the system.']
  customs_facility boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this gate has direct access to customs and immigration facilities for international arrivals processing.']
  domestic_capable boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this gate is equipped and authorized to handle domestic flights.']
  effective_date date [note: 'Date when this gate configuration became or will become operationally effective.']
  expiry_date date [note: 'Date when this gate configuration will cease to be valid (null for indefinite configurations).']
  fuel_hydrant_available boolean [note: 'Indicates whether underground fuel hydrant system is available at this gate position for aircraft refueling operations.']
  gate_number varchar [note: 'The alphanumeric gate identifier displayed to passengers and used in operational systems (e.g., A12, B5, C23).']
  gate_type varchar [note: 'Classification of gate based on aircraft parking configuration: contact (jetbridge-connected), remote (apron position requiring bus), hardstand (unpaved remote stand), bus_gate (passenger bus boarding point), swing_gate (can serve domestic or international).']
  ground_power_unit_available boolean [note: 'Indicates whether fixed ground power unit (GPU) electrical supply is available at this gate for aircraft systems during turnaround.']
  international_capable boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this gate is equipped and authorized to handle international flights with customs, immigration, and border control requirements.']
  jetbridge_available boolean [note: 'Indicates whether a passenger boarding bridge (jetbridge/airbridge) is installed and operational at this gate.']
  jetbridge_count varchar [note: 'Number of passenger boarding bridges available at this gate (0 for remote stands, 1-3 for contact gates serving wide-body aircraft).']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this gate master record was last updated in the system.']
  latitude decimal(10,7) [note: 'Geographic latitude coordinate of the gate parking position in decimal degrees for Ground Support Equipment (GSE) navigation and airport mapping systems.']
  longitude decimal(10,7) [note: 'Geographic longitude coordinate of the gate parking position in decimal degrees for Ground Support Equipment (GSE) navigation and airport mapping systems.']
  max_aircraft_length_meters decimal(5,2) [note: 'Maximum aircraft fuselage length in meters that can be accommodated at this gate position.']
  max_wingspan_meters decimal(5,2) [note: 'Maximum aircraft wingspan in meters that can be safely accommodated at this gate position.']
  minimum_turnaround_time_minutes varchar [note: 'Minimum aircraft turnaround time in minutes required at this gate based on facility capabilities and Ground Support Equipment (GSE) availability.']
  narrow_body_capable boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this gate can accommodate narrow-body aircraft (typically ICAO codes B, C such as A320 family, B737 family).']
  operating_airline_code varchar [note: 'IATA airline code of the carrier that has preferential or exclusive use rights to this gate under lease or operating agreement (null if common-use gate).']
  operational_status varchar [note: 'Current operational availability status of the gate for flight assignments and turnaround operations.']
  parking_stand_designation varchar [note: 'The official apron parking stand identifier used in airport operations and ATC communications (may differ from passenger-facing gate number).']
  potable_water_available boolean [note: 'Indicates whether fixed potable water supply connection is available at this gate for aircraft water system servicing.']
  preconditioned_air_available boolean [note: 'Indicates whether fixed preconditioned air system is available at this gate for aircraft cabin climate control during turnaround, reducing APU usage.']
  regional_jet_capable boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this gate can accommodate regional jet aircraft (typically ICAO code A such as CRJ, ERJ families).']
  remarks varchar [note: 'Free-text operational notes regarding gate restrictions, special handling requirements, or temporary conditions affecting gate usage.']
  schengen_capable boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this gate can handle flights within the Schengen Area (European airports only) without passport control requirements.']
  wide_body_capable boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this gate can accommodate wide-body aircraft (typically ICAO codes D, E, F such as B777, B787, A330, A350, A380).']

  Note: 'Master record for individual aircraft gates within a terminal facility. Captures gate identifier, terminal reference, concourse, gate type (contact/remote/hardstand), compatible aircraft categories (ICAO size codes A–F), jetbridge availability, ground power unit (GPU) availability, potable water availability, operational status, and maximum wingspan clearance. Serves as the SSOT for gate asset definitions used in gate assignment and turnaround planning.'
}

Table airport.gate_assignment {
  gate_assignment_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the gate assignment record. Primary key for the gate assignment transaction.']
  aircraft_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to fleet.aircraft. Business justification: Gate compatibility verification (aircraft size vs gate capability), stand planning, and ground equipment deployment require aircraft linkage. Critical for operational safety and efficient gate utiliza']
  employee_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the operations dispatcher or system user who created or last modified this gate assignment. Used for audit trail and operational accountability.']
  flight_leg_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the specific flight leg being assigned to the gate. Links to the flight operations domain.']
  gate_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to airport.gate. Business justification: gate_assignment references a specific gate but currently uses gate_identifier (STRING) and terminal_code. Normalizing to FK allows joining to gate master for all gate attributes (gate_number, concours']
  actual_gate_in_time timestamp [note: 'Actual timestamp when aircraft arrived at gate and set parking brake (Actual On-block from OOOI). Captured via ACARS or manual entry. Critical for OTP calculation and turnaround tracking.']
  actual_gate_out_time timestamp [note: 'Actual timestamp when aircraft pushed back from gate and released parking brake (Actual Off-block from OOOI). Captured via ACARS or manual entry. Marks gate release and availability for next assignment.']
  aircraft_size_category varchar [note: 'Size classification of aircraft assigned to gate, used for gate compatibility validation. Narrow-body: single aisle; Wide-body: twin aisle; Regional: small aircraft; Heavy: large aircraft; Super: A380 class.']
  airport_iata_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter IATA airport code where this gate assignment occurs (e.g., JFK, LHR, DXB). Identifies the operational airport for multi-hub airline networks.']
  arrival_gate varchar [note: 'The gate number or stand identifier at the destination airport where the flight arrived. [Moved from flight_leg: arrival_gate is a STRING attribute in flight_leg that represents the gate assignment at arrival. This should be replaced by the gate_assignment association, as a flight can be reassigned to multiple gates during IROP. The actual gate used is determined by querying gate_assignment with assignment_type=\'arrival\' or \'turnaround\' and assignment_status=\'completed\'.]']
  assignment_end_time timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the gate reservation expires and becomes available for next assignment, typically includes buffer after scheduled gate-out for cleanup and turnaround completion.']
  assignment_number varchar [note: 'Business identifier for the gate assignment, formatted as GA followed by 8 digits. Used for operational reference and CDM messaging.']
  assignment_source varchar [note: 'Origin of the gate assignment decision. CDM: Collaborative Decision Making system; Manual: dispatcher override; Auto-assign: automated allocation; Rescheduled: changed from original; IROP: irregular operations reassignment.']
  assignment_start_time timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the gate was reserved for this flight, typically earlier than scheduled gate-in to allow for ground equipment setup and early arrival buffer.']
  assignment_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the gate assignment. Planned: initial assignment; Confirmed: verified by operations; Active: aircraft at gate; Completed: turnaround finished; Released: gate available; Cancelled: assignment voided.']
  assignment_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this gate assignment record was initially created in the system. Represents the business event time of assignment decision, distinct from technical audit timestamps.']
  cdm_milestone_status varchar [note: 'Current CDM milestone achieved for this gate assignment. TOBT: Target Off-Block Time set; TSAT: Target Start-Up Approval Time issued; AOBT: Actual Off-Block Time; AIBT: Actual In-Block Time; ALDT: Actual Landing Time.']
  conflict_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this gate assignment has a scheduling conflict with another assignment (overlapping time windows). True when conflict detected; requires resolution by operations.']
  conflict_resolution_notes varchar [note: 'Free-text notes documenting how a gate assignment conflict was resolved, including dispatcher actions, alternative gate assignments, or operational adjustments made.']
  departure_gate varchar [note: 'The gate number or stand identifier at the origin airport from which the flight departed. [Moved from flight_leg: departure_gate is a STRING attribute in flight_leg that represents the gate assignment at departure. This should be replaced by the gate_assignment association, as a flight can be reassigned to multiple gates during IROP. The actual gate used is determined by querying gate_assignment with assignment_type=\'departure\' or \'turnaround\' and assignment_status=\'completed\'.]']
  domestic_international_flag varchar [note: 'Indicates whether the flight assigned to this gate is domestic or international. Schengen applies to intra-European flights with no border control. Determines gate area assignment and customs/immigration requirements.']
  gate_hold_reason varchar [note: 'Free-text explanation for any gate hold or delay in releasing the gate assignment. Documents operational reasons such as late arriving aircraft, maintenance issues, or ATC delays.']
  gate_type varchar [note: 'Classification of gate facility type. Contact: jet bridge equipped; Remote: bus boarding; Hardstand: tarmac parking; Cargo: freight operations. Determines boarding method and ground service requirements.']
  ground_power_available_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether ground power unit (GPU) is available at this gate for this assignment. Required for aircraft electrical power during turnaround when APU is shut down.']
  hub_spoke_indicator varchar [note: 'Classification of airport operational role in airline network. Hub: major connecting airport; Spoke: destination from hub; Focus city: secondary hub; Outstation: remote station with limited operations.']
  jet_bridge_available_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether a jet bridge (passenger boarding bridge) is available and operational at this gate for this assignment. Critical for passenger boarding operations and accessibility requirements.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this gate assignment record was last updated. Tracks changes to assignment details, status transitions, or operational adjustments.']
  passenger_facility_charge_applicable_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether Passenger Facility Charge applies to this gate assignment based on airport authority regulations. Used for financial settlement and regulatory reporting to DOT.']
  preconditioned_air_available_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether preconditioned air (PCA) system is available at this gate for cabin climate control during turnaround. Reduces APU usage and fuel burn.']
  priority_override_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this assignment received priority treatment overriding normal allocation rules. True for VIP flights, IROP recovery, or operational priority situations.']
  priority_override_reason varchar [note: 'Business reason for priority override of normal gate assignment rules. Documents justification for preferential gate allocation outside standard procedures. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: vip_flight|irop_recovery|maintenance_priority|diplomatic|medical_emergency|operational_necessity|none — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  remarks varchar [note: 'Free-text operational notes and comments related to this gate assignment. Used by dispatchers and ground operations for coordination and situational awareness.']
  scheduled_gate_in_time timestamp [note: 'Planned timestamp when aircraft is scheduled to arrive at the gate and set parking brake (On-block time from OOOI). Used for gate allocation planning and OTP reporting.']
  scheduled_gate_out_time timestamp [note: 'Planned timestamp when aircraft is scheduled to push back from gate and release parking brake (Off-block time from OOOI). Used for gate release planning and next assignment scheduling.']
  slot_coordination_required_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this gate assignment requires coordination with airport slot allocation due to capacity constraints at slot-controlled airport. True for Level 3 coordinated airports.']
  special_handling_code varchar [note: 'Special Service Request code indicating special handling requirements for this gate assignment (e.g., WCHR for wheelchair, DEPA for deportee, MEDA for medical). Drives ground service and security coordination.']
  stand_compatibility_code varchar [note: 'ICAO aerodrome reference code indicating maximum aircraft size the gate can accommodate (A through F). Ensures aircraft-gate physical compatibility for safe operations.']
  turnaround_duration_minutes varchar [note: 'Planned duration in minutes for aircraft turnaround at the gate, from gate-in to gate-out. Varies by aircraft type, service level, and operational requirements.']

  Note: 'Transactional record linking a specific flight leg to a gate for a defined time window. Captures flight identifier, tail number, scheduled and actual gate-in/gate-out timestamps (OOOI-aligned), gate identifier, assignment source (CDM/manual/auto-assign), conflict flag, priority override reason, and assignment status (planned/confirmed/released). Supports turnaround sequencing, CDM messaging, and OTP reporting.'
}

Table airport.turnaround {
  turnaround_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the aircraft turnaround event. Primary key for the turnaround record.']
  aircraft_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to fleet.aircraft. Business justification: Turnaround performance analysis by aircraft, maintenance event correlation, and technical delay attribution require aircraft linkage. Ground operations planning and aircraft-specific service requireme']
  cost_centre_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.cost_centre. Business justification: Turnaround operations are cost-tracked activities for CASK (cost per available seat kilometer) calculation, station profitability analysis, and ground handling cost allocation. Essential for financial']
  gate_assignment_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to airport.gate_assignment. Business justification: turnaround occurs at a specific gate assignment (time-bound gate allocation). Currently stores gate_assignment as STRING. FK to gate_assignment provides access to gate details, assignment times, and a']
  ground_handler_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to airport.ground_handler. Business justification: turnaround services are performed by a specific ground handler. Currently stores ground_handler_code (STRING). FK to ground_handler provides access to handler name, capabilities, performance tier, SLA']
  station_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to airport.station. Business justification: Aircraft turnaround events occur at specific airport stations. The turnaround table currently has airport_code as a STRING denormalized attribute, but no FK to station. Adding station_id normalizes th']
  turnaround_inbound_flight_leg_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the arriving flight leg that initiates this turnaround event.']
  turnaround_outbound_flight_leg_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the departing flight leg that concludes this turnaround event.']
  actual_block_in_time timestamp [note: 'Actual timestamp when the inbound aircraft arrived at the gate and parking brake was set (OOOI - On event).']
  actual_block_out_time timestamp [note: 'Actual timestamp when the outbound aircraft pushed back from the gate and began taxi (OOOI - Off event).']
  actual_turnaround_time_minutes varchar [note: 'Actual duration in minutes from block-in to block-out. Calculated as the difference between actual_block_out_time and actual_block_in_time.']
  all_services_completed_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether all ordered ground services have been completed and signed off. True when turnaround is ready for departure.']
  baggage_pieces_loaded varchar [note: 'Total number of baggage pieces loaded onto the outbound flight during turnaround.']
  baggage_pieces_offloaded varchar [note: 'Total number of baggage pieces offloaded from the inbound flight during turnaround.']
  cargo_weight_loaded_kg decimal(10,2) [note: 'Total weight in kilograms of cargo and mail loaded onto the outbound flight.']
  cargo_weight_offloaded_kg decimal(10,2) [note: 'Total weight in kilograms of cargo and mail offloaded from the inbound flight.']
  cdm_milestone_status varchar [note: 'JSON or delimited string capturing CDM milestone timestamps (TOBT, TSAT, TTOT, AOBT, ASAT, ATOT) for Airport CDM integration.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this turnaround record was first created in the system.']
  delay_code varchar [note: 'Two-digit IATA delay code indicating the primary cause of any turnaround delay (e.g., 31=aircraft defect, 32=lack of crew, 81=weather, 82=ATC).']
  delay_minutes varchar [note: 'Total delay in minutes attributed to turnaround operations. Calculated as actual_turnaround_time_minutes minus target_turnaround_time_minutes when positive.']
  deviation_notes varchar [note: 'Free-text description of any deviations from standard turnaround procedures, service failures, or operational irregularities encountered.']
  fuel_uplift_kg decimal(10,2) [note: 'Quantity of fuel in kilograms added to the aircraft during this turnaround.']
  ground_support_equipment_assigned varchar [note: 'Comma-separated list of GSE unit identifiers assigned to this turnaround (e.g., GPU-123, belt loader-456, tug-789).']
  handler_instructions varchar [note: 'Special instructions or notes for the ground handler regarding this specific turnaround (e.g., VIP passenger, special cargo, MEL items, security requirements).']
  last_updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this turnaround record was most recently modified.']
  otp_impact_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this turnaround contributed to an on-time performance failure (departure delay >15 minutes). Used for OTP root-cause analysis.']
  passenger_count_boarded varchar [note: 'Number of passengers who boarded the outbound flight during this turnaround.']
  passenger_count_deplaned varchar [note: 'Number of passengers who deplaned from the inbound flight during this turnaround.']
  ramp_service_order_number varchar [note: 'Unique identifier for the ramp service order that defines all ground services to be performed during this turnaround.']
  scheduled_block_in_time timestamp [note: 'Planned timestamp when the inbound aircraft is scheduled to arrive at the gate (OOOI - On event).']
  scheduled_block_out_time timestamp [note: 'Planned timestamp when the outbound aircraft is scheduled to push back from the gate (OOOI - Off event).']
  service_window_end_time timestamp [note: 'Latest timestamp by which all ground service operations must be completed to meet scheduled block-out time.']
  service_window_start_time timestamp [note: 'Earliest timestamp when ground service operations can commence, typically shortly after block-in.']
  services_completion_time timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when all ground services were completed and aircraft was declared ready for departure.']
  services_ordered varchar [note: 'Comma-separated list of ground services ordered for this turnaround (e.g., fueling, catering, cleaning, water service, lavatory service, GPU, air start, deicing, baggage handling, cargo loading).']
  target_turnaround_time_minutes varchar [note: 'Planned duration in minutes for the complete turnaround cycle from block-in to block-out, based on aircraft type and turnaround type.']
  turnaround_status varchar [note: 'Current operational status of the turnaround event. Tracks lifecycle from scheduled through completion or cancellation.']
  turnaround_type varchar [note: 'Classification of turnaround based on duration and service scope. Standard (60-90 min), quick (<45 min), overnight (>8 hours), transit (international connection), maintenance (scheduled service), extended (unplanned delay).']

  Note: 'Master operational record for an aircraft turnaround event at a gate, representing the full ground servicing cycle between inbound and outbound flight legs. Captures inbound and outbound flight references, tail number, gate assignment, scheduled and actual block-in/block-out times, turnaround type (standard/quick/overnight/transit), target turnaround time (TTT), actual turnaround time, overall status, responsible ground handler, ramp service order details (ordered services, service window, handler instructions, completion status, deviation notes), and OTP contribution metrics. Central entity for ground operations coordination, handler SLA measurement, and OTP root-cause analysis.'
}

Table airport.turnaround_task {
  turnaround_task_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the individual ground servicing task within a turnaround event. Primary key.']
  employee_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the individual ground service operator or technician assigned to execute this task. Used for accountability and certification tracking.']
  flight_leg_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the flight leg associated with this turnaround task. Connects the ground service task to the specific flight operation.']
  gse_asset_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the primary ground support equipment asset deployed for this task (e.g., fuel truck, catering truck, GPU, tug, belt loader).']
  gse_deployment_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to airport.gse_deployment. Business justification: turnaround_task may involve a specific gse_deployment. While turnaround_task already has gse_asset_id, the gse_deployment record captures the transactional deployment details (planned/actual times, op']
  member_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the ground crew or team assigned to perform this turnaround task.']
  occurrence_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to safety.occurrence. Business justification: Individual tasks (fueling, catering, pushback) are the specific activity during which safety occurrences happen. Task-level occurrence linking supports detailed root cause analysis and task-specific s']
  station_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to airport.station. Business justification: Individual turnaround tasks (fueling, catering, cleaning, etc.) occur at specific airport stations. The turnaround_task table currently has airport_code as a STRING denormalized attribute, but no FK t']
  turnaround_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the parent turnaround event that this task belongs to. Links this task to the overall aircraft turnaround operation.']
  actual_end_time timestamp [note: 'Actual timestamp when the ground servicing task was completed. Used for SLA compliance measurement and operational efficiency analysis.']
  actual_start_time timestamp [note: 'Actual timestamp when the ground servicing task commenced. Used for performance measurement and delay analysis.']
