Captures lounge name, IATA airport code, access date and time, access method (purchased, complimentary, day-pass, credit card partner), amount charged if applicable, guest count, and passenger reference. Supports lounge revenue and capacity management.'
}

Table ancillary.ancillary_refund {
  ancillary_refund_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the ancillary refund transaction. Primary key.']
  ancillary_emd_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to ancillary.ancillary_emd. Business justification: Refunds for EMD-linked ancillary services should reference the EMD master record. The ancillary_refund table has emd_number (STRING) but no FK. Adding this FK enables proper EMD refund tracking and se']
  ancillary_order_item_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the original ancillary order item being refunded. Links to the purchased ancillary service record.']
  booking_pnr_id varchar [note: 'Internal booking identifier associated with the refunded ancillary purchase.']
  employee_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the customer service agent or automated system that processed the refund request.']
  flight_irop_event_id varchar [note: 'Reference to an irregular operations event (flight cancellation, delay, diversion) that triggered the refund, if applicable.']
  general_ledger_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.general_ledger. Business justification: Ancillary refunds post to contra-revenue GL accounts for accurate net revenue reporting. Critical for refund liability tracking, revenue reversal accounting, and DOT refund compliance reporting.']
  pax_profile_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the passenger who originally purchased the ancillary service being refunded.']
  pnr_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to reservation.pnr. Business justification: Associates ancillary refunds with booking for refund processing, revenue adjustment, and passenger account management. Essential for ancillary refund reporting and financial reconciliation.']
  reservation_booking_passenger_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to reservation.booking_passenger. Business justification: Links ancillary refunds to booking passengers for passenger-specific refund processing, account management, and service history. Essential for passenger refund tracking.']
  amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'The net amount being refunded to the customer after deducting any applicable penalties or fees.']
  ancillary_category_code varchar [note: 'Code identifying the category of ancillary service being refunded (e.g., baggage, seat, lounge, meal, Wi-Fi).']
  approval_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the refund was approved by the system or authorized personnel.']
  completed_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the refund was confirmed as completed and funds were successfully returned to the customer.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'System timestamp when this refund record was first created in the database.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for all monetary amounts in this refund transaction.']
  emd_coupon_number varchar [note: 'Coupon sequence number within the EMD being refunded. Identifies the specific service coupon.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'System timestamp when this refund record was last updated, tracking the most recent change to any field.']
  miles_credited varchar [note: 'Number of frequent flyer miles credited to the passenger account if the refund is issued as mileage credit.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text notes or comments entered by the processing agent providing additional context or special handling instructions for the refund.']
  original_purchase_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'The original gross amount paid for the ancillary service before any refund adjustments.']
  payment_method_original varchar [note: 'The payment method used for the original ancillary purchase, relevant for refund routing. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: credit_card|debit_card|bank_transfer|paypal|miles|voucher|cash|corporate_account — 8 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  payment_reference_original varchar [note: 'Masked or tokenized reference to the original payment transaction (e.g., last 4 digits of card, transaction ID).']
  penalty_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'The penalty or cancellation fee amount deducted from the original purchase amount, if applicable.']
  processed_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the refund was processed and submitted to the payment gateway or settlement system.']
  processing_channel varchar [note: 'The channel through which the refund request was submitted and processed. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: web|mobile_app|call_center|airport_counter|travel_agent|gds|chatbot — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  processing_location_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter IATA airport or city code indicating where the refund was processed (e.g., call center location, airport counter).']
  reason_code varchar [note: 'Standardized code indicating the business reason for the refund (e.g., service not used, flight cancellation, customer request, service failure, involuntary downgrade).']
  reason_description varchar [note: 'Detailed textual explanation of the reason for the refund, providing additional context beyond the reason code.']
  reference_number varchar [note: 'Unique external reference number assigned to this refund transaction for customer service tracking and reconciliation.']
  refund_method varchar [note: 'The method by which the refund is being issued (e.g., back to original payment method, travel voucher, frequent flyer miles, loyalty points).']
  refund_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the refund transaction in the processing workflow. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: pending|approved|processed|completed|rejected|cancelled|reversed — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  refund_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the refund indicating whether it is a full refund, partial refund, penalty-adjusted refund, or fee waiver.']
  request_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the refund request was initiated by the customer or agent.']
  revenue_accounting_code varchar [note: 'General ledger or revenue accounting code used to classify the refund for financial reporting and revenue adjustment.']
  rfisc_code varchar [note: 'Four-character IATA RFISC code identifying the specific ancillary service type for standardized reporting and distribution.']
  settlement_date date [note: 'Date when the refund transaction was included in the BSP/ARC settlement batch for financial reconciliation.']
  settlement_status varchar [note: 'Status of the refund in the BSP/ARC settlement cycle, tracking financial reconciliation with payment processors and distribution channels.']
  tax_refund_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'The portion of the refund amount representing taxes and government fees being returned to the customer.']
  voucher_number varchar [note: 'Unique voucher or credit memo number issued if the refund is provided as a travel voucher rather than monetary refund.']
  waiver_code varchar [note: 'Code indicating any fee waiver or penalty exemption applied to the refund (e.g., elite status waiver, operational disruption waiver).']

  Note: 'Transactional record of a refund or reversal processed for a previously purchased ancillary service. Captures original ancillary order item reference, EMD coupon reference, refund reason code, refund amount, currency, refund method (original payment, voucher, miles), processing agent, refund request timestamp, and settlement status. Supports ancillary revenue adjustments, BSP/ARC refund processing, and customer service workflows.'
}

Table ancillary.sales_channel {
  sales_channel_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for sales_channel']
  channel_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to marketing.marketing_channel. Business justification: Ancillary sales channels (GDS, NDC, direct web, mobile app) map to marketing channels for omnichannel campaign execution and attribution. Real business need to link distribution channels with marketin']
  api_endpoint_url varchar [note: 'API endpoint URL for programmatic integration with the channel. Confidential technical information.']
  bundling_supported_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the channel supports bundled ancillary product offerings (e.g., fare families, branded fares).']
  channel_category varchar [note: 'Categorization of the channel as direct (airline-owned), indirect (third-party), or hybrid.']
  channel_owner varchar [note: 'Entity that owns and operates the sales channel (e.g., Airline for direct channels, GDS Provider for GDS, OTA for online travel agencies). [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: Airline|GDS Provider|OTA|TMC|Aggregator|Airport Authority|Third Party — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  channel_status varchar [note: 'Current operational status of the sales channel.']
  channel_type varchar [note: 'High-level classification of the distribution channel type.']
  commission_applicable_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether commission payments apply to ancillary sales made through this channel.']
  commission_rate_percent decimal(5,2) [note: 'Standard commission rate percentage applied to ancillary revenue generated through this channel. Null if commission is not applicable or varies by product.']
  commission_structure_type varchar [note: 'Type of commission structure applied to this channel (e.g., fixed percentage, tiered based on volume, product-specific rates).']
  contact_email varchar [note: 'Primary email address for channel support and business inquiries. Organizational contact data classified as confidential.']
  contact_phone varchar [note: 'Primary phone number for channel support and business inquiries. Organizational contact data classified as confidential.']
  contract_reference_number varchar [note: 'Reference number of the legal or commercial agreement governing the channel relationship. Confidential business information.']
  country_code varchar [note: 'ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 country code if the channel is country-specific. Null for multi-country or global channels.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the channel record was first created in the system.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Primary ISO 4217 three-letter currency code used for transactions in this channel.']
  dynamic_pricing_enabled_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether dynamic or personalized pricing for ancillaries is enabled for this channel.']
  effective_date date [note: 'Date when the channel became or will become operational for ancillary sales.']
  emd_issuance_capable_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the channel can issue EMDs for ancillary service charges.']
  expiration_date date [note: 'Date when the channel is scheduled to be decommissioned or contract expires. Null for indefinite channels.']
  gds_code varchar [note: 'Standard GDS identifier code if the channel is a GDS (e.g., 1A for Amadeus, 1B for Sabre, 1G for Galileo, 1P for Worldspan). Null for non-GDS channels.']
  geographic_scope varchar [note: 'Geographic reach and availability of the sales channel.']
  integration_method varchar [note: 'Technical integration method used to connect the channel to airline systems.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the channel record was last updated.']
  merchandising_capability varchar [note: 'Level of ancillary merchandising capability supported by the channel (Full = rich content, images, bundling; Limited = text-only offers; Basic = minimal ancillary support; None = no ancillary support).']
  multi_currency_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the channel supports transactions in multiple currencies.']
  ndc_capability_level varchar [note: 'IATA NDC certification level achieved by the channel (Level 1 through Level 4), or None if not NDC-capable.']
  ndc_capable_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the channel supports IATA NDC API standards for rich content and ancillary merchandising.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text field for additional information, special conditions, or operational notes about the channel.']
  payment_methods_supported varchar [note: 'Comma-separated list of payment methods accepted through this channel (e.g., Credit Card, Debit Card, PayPal, Miles, Points, Bank Transfer).']
  platform_type varchar [note: 'Technology platform or interface type through which the channel operates. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: Web|Mobile App|Kiosk|Agent Desktop|API|Voice|In-Flight System — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  priority_rank varchar [note: 'Numeric ranking indicating the strategic priority or preference for this channel in merchandising and revenue attribution (lower number = higher priority).']
  real_time_inventory_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the channel has real-time access to ancillary inventory and availability.']
  region_code varchar [note: 'IATA region code or ISO region code if the channel is region-specific (e.g., EUR for Europe, NAM for North America). Null for global channels.']
  service_level_agreement varchar [note: 'Summary of service level commitments for channel availability, response time, and support (e.g., 99.9% uptime, 24/7 support).']
  settlement_method varchar [note: 'Financial settlement mechanism used for transactions through this channel (e.g., BSP for IATA Billing and Settlement Plan, ARC for Airlines Reporting Corporation, Direct Billing).']
  target_customer_segment varchar [note: 'Primary customer segment or persona targeted by this channel (e.g., Business Travelers, Leisure Travelers, Corporate Accounts, Travel Agents).']

  Note: 'Reference data defining the sales and distribution channels through which ancillary products are offered and sold — including GDS, NDC API, airline direct website, mobile app, airport kiosk, check-in agent, in-flight crew, and partner portals. Captures channel code, channel type, NDC capability level, commission structure applicability, and active status. Used for channel-level ancillary revenue attribution and merchandising rule configuration.'
}

Table ancillary.eligibility_rule {
  eligibility_rule_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for eligibility_rule']
  product_catalog_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the specific ancillary product or service to which this eligibility rule applies.']
  route_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to route.route. Business justification: Eligibility rules restrict ancillary availability by specific routes (e.g., premium seats only on long-haul, lounge access on international). Real business process: merchandising rules engine evaluate']
  sales_channel_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to ancillary.sales_channel. Business justification: Eligibility rules can be channel-specific (e.g., certain products only available via mobile app). The eligibility_rule table has sales_channel_list (STRING) which is a multi-value field. Adding a sing']
  aircraft_type_list varchar [note: 'Comma-separated list of IATA aircraft type codes (e.g., 737, 777, A320) for which this ancillary product is eligible. Empty indicates no aircraft restriction.']
  booking_date_from date [note: 'Start date of the booking window during which this eligibility rule is active. Null indicates no booking start date restriction.']
  booking_date_to date [note: 'End date of the booking window during which this eligibility rule is active. Null indicates no booking end date restriction.']
  cabin_class_list varchar [note: 'Comma-separated list of cabin classes (First, Business, Premium Economy, Economy) eligible for this ancillary product. Empty indicates no cabin restriction.']
  created_by_user varchar [note: 'User identifier or system account that created this eligibility rule record.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this eligibility rule record was first created in the system.']
  destination_airport_list varchar [note: 'Comma-separated list of IATA airport codes representing eligible destination points. Empty indicates no destination restriction.']
  destination_country_list varchar [note: 'Comma-separated list of ISO 3166-1 alpha-2 country codes representing eligible destination countries. Empty indicates no destination country restriction.']
  effective_date date [note: 'Date from which this eligibility rule becomes active and enforceable in all sales channels.']
  exclusion_rule_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this is an exclusion rule (true) that prevents eligibility, or an inclusion rule (false) that grants eligibility.']
  expiration_date date [note: 'Date after which this eligibility rule is no longer active. Null indicates an open-ended rule.']
  fare_class_list varchar [note: 'Comma-separated list of booking class codes (RBD - Revenue Booking Designator) to which this rule applies. Empty indicates no fare class restriction.']
  ffp_tier_list varchar [note: 'Comma-separated list of loyalty program tier codes (e.g., Gold, Platinum, Diamond) eligible for this ancillary product. Empty indicates no tier restriction.']
  is_combinable boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this eligibility rule can be combined with other rules (true) or must be evaluated independently (false).']
  last_modified_by_user varchar [note: 'User identifier or system account that most recently modified this eligibility rule record.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this eligibility rule record was most recently updated.']
  marketing_carrier_list varchar [note: 'Comma-separated list of IATA airline codes representing marketing carriers for which this rule applies. Empty indicates all carriers.']
  maximum_advance_purchase_days varchar [note: 'Maximum number of days before departure that the ancillary product may be purchased. Null indicates no maximum advance purchase restriction.']
  minimum_advance_purchase_days varchar [note: 'Minimum number of days before departure that the ancillary product must be purchased. Null indicates no minimum advance purchase requirement.']
  operating_carrier_list varchar [note: 'Comma-separated list of IATA airline codes representing operating carriers for which this rule applies. Empty indicates all carriers.']
  origin_airport_list varchar [note: 'Comma-separated list of IATA airport codes representing eligible origin points. Empty indicates no origin restriction.']
  origin_country_list varchar [note: 'Comma-separated list of ISO 3166-1 alpha-2 country codes representing eligible origin countries. Empty indicates no origin country restriction.']
  override_allowed_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether authorized agents or systems may override this eligibility rule under exceptional circumstances.']
  passenger_type_code_list varchar [note: 'Comma-separated list of IATA passenger type codes (ADT, CHD, INF, MIL, SRC, etc.) eligible for this ancillary product. Empty indicates no passenger type restriction.']
  priority_rank varchar [note: 'Numeric priority order for rule evaluation when multiple rules apply to the same ancillary product. Lower numbers indicate higher priority.']
  rule_code varchar [note: 'Business-assigned unique code identifying this eligibility rule for operational reference and system integration.']
  rule_description varchar [note: 'Detailed business description of the eligibility rule, including rationale, business context, and any special conditions or exceptions.']
  rule_logic_expression varchar [note: 'Complex logical expression or formula defining advanced eligibility conditions beyond standard attribute filters. Used for multi-condition rules requiring AND/OR/NOT logic.']
  rule_name varchar [note: 'Human-readable name describing the purpose and scope of this eligibility rule.']
  rule_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the eligibility rule indicating whether it is actively enforced in sales channels.']
  rule_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the eligibility rule defining the primary dimension of restriction or entitlement (fare class, route, FFP tier, booking window, passenger type, or cabin).']
  sales_channel_list varchar [note: 'Comma-separated list of sales channels (web, mobile, GDS, call_center, airport, agency) through which this ancillary product may be purchased. Empty indicates all channels.']
  terms_and_conditions_url varchar [note: 'Web address linking to the full terms and conditions document governing this eligibility rule for customer transparency and regulatory compliance.']
  travel_date_from date [note: 'Start date of the travel period during which this eligibility rule applies. Null indicates no start date restriction.']
  travel_date_to date [note: 'End date of the travel period during which this eligibility rule applies. Null indicates no end date restriction.']

  Note: 'Business rules defining which passengers, fare classes, routes, cabin types, booking windows, and FFP tiers are eligible to purchase specific ancillary products. Captures rule type (fare-class restriction, route restriction, tier-based entitlement, advance-purchase window, passenger-type restriction), effective date range, priority order, and override flags. Drives real-time ancillary offer eligibility checks across all sales channels.'
}

Table ancillary.channel_vendor_agreement {
  channel_vendor_agreement_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for this channel-vendor distribution agreement record. Primary key.']
  sales_channel_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to the sales channel through which the vendor\'s products are distributed']
  vendor_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to the vendor whose products are sold through this channel']
  agreement_status varchar [note: 'Current operational status of this channel-vendor distribution agreement.']
  commission_rate_percent decimal(5,2) [note: 'Commission rate percentage applied to this specific vendor\'s ancillary sales through this channel. Overrides the channel\'s default commission rate when vendor-specific terms are negotiated.']
  created_date timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this channel-vendor agreement record was created in the system.']
  effective_date date [note: 'The date from which this channel-vendor distribution agreement becomes active and the specified terms apply.']
  expiration_date date [note: 'The date on which this channel-vendor distribution agreement expires or is scheduled for renewal. Null indicates an evergreen agreement.']
  integration_method varchar [note: 'Technical integration method used to connect this vendor\'s ancillary inventory to this specific channel (e.g., API, File Transfer, Manual, EDI, NDC). May vary by vendor-channel pair based on technical capabilities.']
  last_modified_date timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent modification to this channel-vendor agreement record.']
  priority_rank varchar [note: 'Numeric ranking indicating the priority or preference for displaying this vendor\'s products in this channel. Used for merchandising rules and product sorting.']
  service_level_agreement varchar [note: 'Text description or reference to the SLA terms governing this channel-vendor relationship, including response times, availability guarantees, and performance metrics specific to this partnership.']
  settlement_method varchar [note: 'Financial settlement mechanism used for transactions between this vendor and channel (e.g., BSP, ARC, Direct Billing, Net Settlement, Gross Settlement). May differ from channel default based on vendor agreements.']

  Note: 'This association product represents the commercial agreement between a sales channel and a vendor for distributing the vendor\'s ancillary products through that channel. It captures channel-specific commission rates, settlement terms, integration methods, and service level agreements that exist only in the context of this channel-vendor partnership. Each record links one sales channel to one vendor with the commercial and technical terms governing their distribution relationship.. Existence Justification: In airline ancillary distribution, vendors (catering providers, lounge operators, ground service providers) distribute their ancillary products through multiple sales channels (GDS, NDC APIs, airline.com, mobile app, airport kiosks), and each channel sells products from multiple vendors. The airline actively manages channel-vendor agreements that specify channel-specific commission rates, settlement terms, integration methods, and SLAs for each vendor-channel partnership. This is an operational business process involving contract negotiation, commission reconciliation, and multi-channel merchandising strategy.'
}

Table inventory.flight_inventory {
  flight_inventory_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate identifier for the flight inventory record representing the sellable seat inventory for a specific flight-date combination in the Amadeus Altéa Inventory system.']
  aircraft_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to fleet.aircraft. Business justification: Flight inventory is opened on a specific tail number for each departure. Operations Control Center assigns aircraft to flights; inventory management needs tail number for MEL item applicability, actua']
  campaign_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to marketing.campaign. Business justification: Marketing campaigns target specific flights for promotional fares and seat sales. Campaign managers track which inventory is being promoted for performance measurement and ROI analysis. Essential for ']
  cost_centre_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.cost_centre. Business justification: Flight operations are cost centers in airline accounting. CASK (Cost per Available Seat Kilometer) calculations require linking flight inventory to cost centers for profitability analysis and manageme']
  fleet_assignment_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to route.route_fleet_assignment. Business justification: Fleet assignment determines aircraft type, seat configuration, and cabin class capacity that inventory must control. Revenue management bucket authorization, overbooking limits, and codeshare allocati']
  flight_number_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to route.flight_number. Business justification: Flight inventory controls real-time seat availability for specific flight numbers defined in route planning. Revenue management, codeshare allocation, and GDS distribution all require linking daily in']
  operating_certificate_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.operating_certificate. Business justification: Every flight operates under a specific air operator certificate (AOC) that authorizes the carrier\'s operations. Flight inventory must reference the certificate authorizing that flight\'s operation for ']
  operational_approval_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.operational_approval. Business justification: Flights requiring special operational approvals (ETOPS, CAT III, RNP AR) must reference the specific approval authorizing that operation. Critical for dispatch validation, crew qualification verificat']
  promotional_fare_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to marketing.promotional_fare. Business justification: Promotional fares are allocated against specific flight inventory for capacity control and seat cap enforcement. Airlines track promotional seat utilization per flight for yield management. Essential ']
  route_id varchar [note: 'FK to route.route.route_id — Flight inventory must reference the route for network-level availability queries, codeshare allocation management, and seasonal capacity planning.']
  scheduled_flight_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the scheduled flight leg for which this inventory record is maintained. Links to the flight master entity in the flight operations domain.']
  seasonal_schedule_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to route.seasonal_schedule. Business justification: Flight inventory operationalizes a specific seasonal schedule template. Linking inventory to seasonal_schedule enables variance analysis (planned vs actual capacity), schedule change impact assessment']
  altea_inventory_key varchar [note: 'Native primary key or reference identifier assigned by the Amadeus Altéa Inventory system to this flight inventory record. Used for system-of-record traceability and reconciliation between the lakehouse Silver layer and the operational PSS.']
  authorized_capacity varchar [note: 'Revenue management-approved seat count authorized for sale on this flight-date, which may differ from total physical capacity due to operational holds, crew seat requirements, or regulatory restrictions. This is the effective inventory ceiling used by Sabre AirVision Revenue Management.']
  availability_snapshot_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent real-time availability snapshot broadcast to GDS and direct booking channels. Reflects the point-in-time at which seats_available and fare class bucket statuses were last synchronized with Amadeus Altéa.']
  booking_curve_day varchar [note: 'Number of days prior to departure at which this inventory snapshot was captured, representing the position on the booking curve (e.g., 90, 60, 30, 14, 7, 1). Used by Sabre AirVision Revenue Management for demand forecasting and fare class optimization.']
  business_class_capacity varchar [note: 'Total number of seats allocated to the Business Class cabin on this flight-date. Reflects the aircraft configuration and any cabin reconfiguration applied for this specific flight.']
  channel_restriction_code varchar [note: 'Code indicating any channel-specific sales restrictions applied to this flight inventory (e.g., \'DIRECT_ONLY\' for web/app exclusive fares, \'GDS_BLOCKED\' for GDS channel closure, \'NDC_ONLY\' for IATA NDC channel exclusivity). Controls distribution channel access to inventory.']
  codeshare_indicator boolean [note: 'Flag indicating whether this flight inventory record includes codeshare seat allocations from partner airlines. When true, a portion of the authorized capacity is reserved for codeshare partner bookings under interline ticketing agreements.']
  codeshare_seats_allocated varchar [note: 'Number of seats within the authorized capacity ring-fenced for codeshare partner airline bookings on this flight-date. Managed under bilateral codeshare agreements and tracked separately from own-metal inventory for revenue accounting and BSP settlement.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this flight inventory record was first created in the Amadeus Altéa Inventory system, typically at the time of schedule publication or inventory initialization for the flight-date.']
  departure_date date [note: 'Scheduled local departure date of the flight leg at the origin station. Together with flight_number, forms the primary business key for inventory lookup by GDS and booking channels.']
  destination_airport_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter IATA airport code for the arrival station of this flight leg (e.g., LAX, CDG). Used to identify the inventory leg destination in route network and GDS availability queries.']
  economy_capacity varchar [note: 'Total number of seats allocated to the Economy Class cabin on this flight-date. Typically the largest cabin and primary driver of load factor and revenue management decisions.']
  equipment_change_indicator boolean [note: 'Flag indicating whether the aircraft type assigned to this flight-date has been changed from the originally scheduled equipment. Equipment changes may alter cabin configuration, total capacity, and fare class bucket allocations requiring inventory rebalancing.']
  fare_class_bucket_config varchar [note: 'Serialized representation of the active fare class bucket (booking class) configuration for this flight-date, listing each IATA booking class code and its authorized seat allocation (e.g., \'Y:20,B:15,M:10,Q:5\'). Managed by Sabre AirVision Revenue Management and consumed by Amadeus Altéa for availability control.']
  first_class_capacity varchar [note: 'Total number of seats allocated to the First Class cabin on this flight-date. Reflects the aircraft configuration and any cabin reconfiguration applied for this specific flight.']
  flight_number varchar [note: 'IATA-format airline designator and numeric flight number (e.g., AA101) identifying the marketed flight for which inventory is being managed. Used by GDS and direct channels to query availability.']
  gds_availability_code varchar [note: 'Single-letter availability status code broadcast to Global Distribution Systems (GDS) representing the highest open booking class on this flight-date (e.g., \'Y\' for full availability, \'L\' for restricted availability). Conforms to IATA availability message standards.']
  group_seats_held varchar [note: 'Number of seats currently held under group booking reservations (typically 10+ passengers under a single PNR group) on this flight-date. Group holds reduce available inventory for individual bookings and are subject to group ticketing deadlines.']
  interline_seats_allocated varchar [note: 'Number of seats allocated for interline ticketing partner bookings on this flight-date under IATA interline agreements. Distinct from codeshare allocations and tracked for ARC/BSP proration and revenue accounting.']
  inventory_control_mode varchar [note: 'Indicates whether inventory management for this flight-date is operating under MANUAL (revenue analyst override), AUTOMATED (Sabre AirVision algorithmic control), or HYBRID (automated with manual overrides) mode. Affects how fare class bucket changes are applied.']
  inventory_status varchar [note: 'Current operational status of the flight inventory record in Amadeus Altéa. OPEN indicates seats are available for sale; CLOSED indicates no further sales permitted; WAITLIST indicates demand exceeds supply and waitlist is active; SUSPENDED indicates temporary hold on sales; CANCELLED indicates the flight has been cancelled and inventory is frozen.']
  last_seat_availability_date date [note: 'Date on which the last available seat on this flight-date was sold or the inventory was closed. Used for post-departure revenue analysis and booking curve benchmarking.']
  last_updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent modification to this flight inventory record, including any changes to seat counts, fare class buckets, overbooking parameters, or inventory status. Critical for real-time availability synchronization with GDS.']
  load_factor_pct decimal(5,2) [note: 'Current load factor expressed as a percentage, calculated as seats_sold divided by authorized_capacity multiplied by 100. A key revenue management KPI representing the proportion of available seat capacity that has been sold. Used by Sabre AirVision for yield optimization decisions.']
  operational_seats_held varchar [note: 'Number of seats withheld from sale for operational purposes including deadhead crew positioning, airline staff travel, and aircraft ferry requirements. These seats are excluded from the sellable inventory pool.']
  origin_airport_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter IATA airport code for the departure station of this flight leg (e.g., JFK, LHR). Used to identify the inventory leg origin in route network and GDS availability queries.']
  overbooking_factor decimal(5,2) [note: 'Revenue management-approved overbooking multiplier expressed as a percentage above physical capacity (e.g., 105.00 means 5% overbooking allowed). Set by Sabre AirVision Revenue Management based on historical no-show and cancellation rates to maximize load factor while managing denied boarding risk.']
  overbooking_limit varchar [note: 'Absolute maximum number of bookings permitted on this flight-date including the overbooking allowance. Computed from authorized_capacity multiplied by overbooking_factor and used as the hard ceiling in the Amadeus Altéa booking engine.']
  premium_economy_capacity varchar [note: 'Total number of seats allocated to the Premium Economy cabin on this flight-date. Nullable for aircraft configurations that do not include a Premium Economy cabin.']
  revenue_management_class varchar [note: 'Revenue management classification assigned by Sabre AirVision to this flight-date based on demand forecast, booking curve position, and competitive pricing environment. Drives automated fare class bucket opening and closing decisions for yield optimization.']
  schedule_change_indicator boolean [note: 'Flag indicating whether this flight-date has undergone a schedule change (time, equipment, or routing modification) since the original inventory record was created. Schedule changes may trigger IROP (Irregular Operations) rebooking workflows and inventory reallocation.']
  seats_available varchar [note: 'Current number of seats available for sale across all cabins on this flight-date, calculated as authorized_capacity minus seats_sold minus overbooking_buffer. This is the real-time availability figure broadcast to GDS and direct booking channels.']
  seats_sold varchar [note: 'Total number of confirmed seat bookings (Pax) across all cabins and fare classes on this flight-date. Updated in real-time as reservations are created, modified, or cancelled in the Amadeus Altéa Reservation system.']
  total_capacity varchar [note: 'Total number of physical seats available on the aircraft for this flight-date combination across all cabins. Derived from the aircraft configuration assigned to the flight and represents the maximum sellable inventory ceiling.']
  waitlist_count varchar [note: 'Current number of passengers on the waitlist for this flight-date when inventory_status is WAITLIST or when specific fare classes are closed. Waitlisted passengers are cleared in priority order as cancellations occur.']

  Note: 'Core master entity representing the sellable seat inventory for a specific flight-date combination. Owns the authoritative record of total capacity, cabin breakdown, fare class bucket configuration, and overall availability status for each flight leg in the Amadeus Altéa Inventory system. This is the primary inventory control record consumed by GDS, direct channels, and revenue management yield optimization.'
}

Table inventory.cabin_class {
  cabin_class_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate identifier for the cabin class record within the airline\'s inventory system. Primary key for this reference master entity.']
  cabin_configuration_part_master_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to procurement.part_master. Business justification: Cabin configurations require specific parts (seats, IFE units, galley equipment). Procurement must know which parts are compatible with each cabin class for refurbishment projects, spare parts plannin']
  cost_centre_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.cost_centre. Business justification: Each cabin class has distinct cost structures (service, catering, crew ratios). Airlines track cabin-level profitability by allocating costs to cabin class cost centers for yield management and produc']
  applicable_flight_range varchar [note: 'Flight range category for which this cabin class configuration is applicable: \'short_haul\' (under 3 hours), \'medium_haul\' (3–6 hours), \'long_haul\' (6–12 hours), \'ultra_long_haul\' (over 12 hours), or \'all\'. Drives route-specific product deployment and inventory segmentation.']
  baggage_allowance_kg decimal(5,2) [note: 'Standard checked baggage allowance in kilograms included with the base fare for this cabin class. Drives ancillary revenue calculations, passenger service standards, and GDS fare display. Governed by IATA Resolution 302 weight concept.']
  baggage_pieces_allowed varchar [note: 'Number of checked baggage pieces included in the standard allowance for this cabin class under the piece concept. Applicable for routes governed by IATA piece concept (primarily transatlantic and transpacific). Complements baggage_allowance_kg for weight-concept routes.']
  cabin_class_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the cabin class record. \'active\' indicates the cabin is currently offered and sellable; \'inactive\' indicates temporarily suspended; \'retired\' indicates permanently discontinued; \'pending\' indicates configured but not yet activated for sale.']
  cabin_code varchar [note: 'Airline-internal alphanumeric code uniquely identifying the cabin class within the carrier\'s Passenger Service System (PSS). Used in Amadeus Altéa Inventory for cabin-level availability and inventory management. May differ from the IATA designator when the airline operates sub-cabin variants.']
  cabin_name varchar [note: 'Commercial marketing name of the cabin class as presented to passengers and travel agents (e.g., \'First Class\', \'Business Class\', \'Premium Economy\', \'Economy\'). Used in passenger-facing communications, booking flows, and GDS displays.']
  cabin_type varchar [note: 'Standardized classification of the cabin into one of the four IATA-recognized commercial cabin tiers: first, business, premium_economy, or economy. Drives revenue management segmentation, yield optimization in Sabre AirVision, and inventory bucket hierarchy.']
  carry_on_allowance_kg decimal(5,2) [note: 'Maximum weight in kilograms permitted for carry-on baggage in the cabin for this cabin class. Used in ground handling operations, security screening coordination with TSA/EASA, and passenger service standards.']
  codeshare_sellable boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this cabin class can be sold on codeshare flights operated by partner airlines. When true, inventory allocations for this cabin are made available to codeshare partners through interline agreements. Drives GDS availability display for codeshare itineraries.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this cabin class record was first created in the system. Supports data lineage, audit trail, and regulatory record-keeping requirements. Format: yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX.']
  discontinuation_date date [note: 'Date on which this cabin class definition is retired and no longer available for new bookings. Null for currently active cabin classes. Supports historical inventory analysis and regulatory record retention requirements.']
  effective_date date [note: 'Date from which this cabin class definition becomes commercially active and available for sale. Used for seasonal product changes, new aircraft type introductions, and cabin reconfiguration projects. Aligns with IATA Season scheduling conventions.']
  fare_basis_prefix varchar [note: 'Standard alphabetic prefix used in fare basis codes for this cabin class (e.g., \'Y\' for Economy, \'C\' for Business, \'F\' for First). Aligns fare construction with IATA fare basis code conventions and drives revenue management fare class bucket assignment in Sabre AirVision.']
  ffp_accrual_multiplier decimal(5,2) [note: 'Multiplier applied to base miles flown to calculate Frequent Flyer Program (FFP) miles or points accrued for travel in this cabin class. For example, a value of 2.0 means passengers earn double miles. Feeds Oracle Loyalty Cloud FFP accrual engine.']
  gds_display_code varchar [note: 'Code used to represent this cabin class in Global Distribution System (GDS) availability displays (e.g., Amadeus, Sabre, Travelport). May differ from the internal cabin_code for legacy GDS compatibility. Ensures correct cabin identification in travel agent booking workflows.']
  iata_cabin_designator varchar [note: 'Single-letter IATA standard cabin designator code identifying the commercial cabin class (e.g., F = First, C = Business, W = Premium Economy, Y = Economy). Used as the industry-standard booking class grouping code across Global Distribution Systems (GDS) and interline agreements.']
  ife_type varchar [note: 'Type of In-Flight Entertainment (IFE) system available in this cabin class: \'seatback_screen\' = personal seatback display, \'overhead_screen\' = shared overhead monitors, \'personal_device\' = bring-your-own-device streaming, \'none\' = no IFE. Used in product definition and passenger experience standards.']
  is_lie_flat boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the seats in this cabin class convert to a fully flat sleeping surface (180-degree recline). True for premium long-haul First and Business class products. Critical for product differentiation, revenue management premium pricing, and GDS display.']
  lounge_access_entitlement varchar [note: 'Level of airport lounge access included with this cabin class: \'none\' = no lounge access, \'home_carrier\' = access to the airline\'s own lounges only, \'partner\' = access to partner airline lounges, \'all_alliance\' = full alliance lounge network access. Drives ground handling instructions and passenger service delivery.']
  max_overbooking_pct decimal(5,2) [note: 'Maximum percentage above physical seat capacity that bookings may be accepted for this cabin class under the airline\'s overbooking policy. For example, 10.00 means up to 110% of seats may be sold. Null when overbooking_allowed is false. Governed by DOT regulations.']
  meal_service_type varchar [note: 'Standard meal service type provided in this cabin class: \'full_meal\' = multi-course meal service, \'light_meal\' = single-course meal, \'snack\' = snack and beverage only, \'buy_on_board\' = no complimentary meal, \'none\' = no food service. Drives catering uplift planning and galley configuration.']
  overbooking_allowed boolean [note: 'Indicates whether overbooking is permitted for this cabin class. When true, the inventory system may accept bookings beyond physical seat capacity based on no-show forecasting models. Governed by DOT overbooking regulations and airline revenue management policy.']
  power_outlet_type varchar [note: 'Type of in-seat power outlet available in this cabin class: \'ac_110v\', \'ac_220v\', \'usb_a\', \'usb_c\', or \'none\'. Used in product specification, passenger communication, and aircraft configuration management in AMOS.']
  priority_boarding boolean [note: 'Indicates whether passengers in this cabin class receive priority boarding privileges ahead of general boarding groups. Drives boarding sequence configuration in SITA Airport Management Platform and ground handling SOPs.']
  priority_check_in boolean [note: 'Indicates whether passengers in this cabin class are entitled to dedicated priority check-in counters or lanes. Drives airport operations configuration in SITA Airport Management Platform and ground handling service level agreements.']
  revenue_class_hierarchy varchar [note: 'Numeric rank defining the commercial hierarchy of this cabin class relative to others (1 = highest/First, 4 = lowest/Economy). Used by Sabre AirVision Revenue Management for yield optimization, upgrade logic, and inventory nesting rules. Lower number = higher revenue class.']
  seat_configuration_code varchar [note: 'Standard seat layout configuration code for this cabin class expressed as a row-abreast pattern (e.g., \'2-2-2\' for Business, \'3-3-3\' for Economy). Reflects the physical seat arrangement across the aircraft cross-section. Sourced from aircraft type configuration in AMOS.']
  seat_pitch_inches decimal(5,2) [note: 'Distance in inches between a point on one seat and the same point on the seat directly in front, representing legroom standard for this cabin class. Key product differentiation metric used in marketing, regulatory DOT reporting, and passenger experience benchmarking.']
  seat_recline_inches decimal(5,2) [note: 'Maximum recline distance in inches for seats in this cabin class. Distinguishes flat-bed (Business/First) from recliner (Premium Economy) and standard recline (Economy) product tiers. Used in product definition and passenger experience standards.']
  seat_width_inches decimal(5,2) [note: 'Width of the seat in inches for this cabin class, measured at the widest point of the seat cushion. Used in product specification, passenger comfort benchmarking, and marketing collateral.']
  service_level_description varchar [note: 'Narrative description of the service standards, amenities, and passenger experience associated with this cabin class. Includes details such as meal service type, lounge access entitlement, priority boarding, and onboard entertainment offerings. Used for product definition and customer communication.']
  source_system_code varchar [note: 'Code identifying the operational system of record from which this cabin class definition originates (e.g., \'ALTEA_INV\' for Amadeus Altéa Inventory, \'SABRE_RM\' for Sabre AirVision). Supports data lineage tracking in the Databricks Silver Layer lakehouse.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent modification to this cabin class record. Used for change tracking, data synchronization with downstream systems (GDS, Revenue Management), and audit compliance. Format: yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX.']
  upgrade_eligible boolean [note: 'Indicates whether passengers booked in this cabin class are eligible to receive complimentary or paid upgrades to a higher cabin class. Drives upgrade logic in Amadeus Altéa Inventory and Oracle Loyalty Cloud FFP upgrade award processing.']
  upgrade_target_cabin_code varchar [note: 'Cabin code of the next higher cabin class to which passengers in this cabin may be upgraded. Null for the highest cabin class (First). Used in automated upgrade processing and FFP award redemption workflows.']
  wifi_available boolean [note: 'Indicates whether in-flight Wi-Fi connectivity is available in this cabin class. Drives ancillary service offerings, passenger communication, and product marketing. Complements IFE type for digital connectivity product definition.']

  Note: 'Reference master defining the physical and commercial cabin classes operated by the airline (e.g., First, Business, Premium Economy, Economy). Captures IATA cabin designator codes, seat configuration standards, service level definitions, and applicable aircraft type associations. Used as the classification backbone for inventory segmentation and revenue management.'
}

Table inventory.fare_class_bucket {
  fare_class_bucket_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate identifier for the fare class bucket record in the Silver layer. Primary key for this entity.']
  cabin_class_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to inventory.cabin_class. Business justification: fare_class_bucket defines RBD buckets within a cabin class. cabin_class is the reference master for cabin definitions. This FK establishes the cabin hierarchy for fare class buckets. cabin_code is red']
  catering_order_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to procurement.catering_order. Business justification: Fare class bookings drive catering order meal counts by service class. Revenue management bucket changes trigger catering order adjustments. Daily operational link between inventory yield management a']
  inventory_leg_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the flight inventory leg record to which this fare class bucket belongs. Links the bucket to a specific flight-date-leg combination managed in Altéa Inventory.']
  promotional_fare_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to marketing.promotional_fare. Business justification: Promotional fares must be mapped to specific RBD buckets for inventory control. Revenue management systems authorize promotional inventory at the bucket level. Critical for integrating marketing promo']
  advance_purchase_days varchar [note: 'Minimum number of days before departure that a ticket must be purchased to qualify for fares in this booking class (e.g., 7, 14, 21). A value of 0 indicates no advance purchase requirement. Governs ticketing time limit enforcement in Altéa.']
  allotment_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the inventory allocation type for this bucket: own (operating carrier direct sale), codeshare (partner airline marketing), interline (interline ticketing agreement), group (group booking block), charter (charter operator block), blocked (operationally blocked seats). Drives revenue accounting and settlement routing.']
  authorized_units varchar [note: 'Authorized capacity (AU) — the maximum number of seats the revenue management system has authorized for sale in this booking class bucket for the flight leg. Set by Sabre AirVision yield optimization and loaded into Altéa Inventory. Core inventory control parameter.']
  availability_code varchar [note: 'IATA standard availability status code transmitted to GDS systems (e.g., A = available, L = waitlist, R = request, C = closed). Distinct from bucket_status in that this is the externally-broadcast GDS code per IATA PADIS standards, while bucket_status is the internal operational state.']
  availability_snapshot_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp at which the current availability figures (seats_available, current_bookings, load_factor_pct) were captured from Altéa Inventory. Critical for GDS cache freshness validation and real-time availability SLA monitoring.']
  booking_limit varchar [note: 'Booking limit (BL) — the maximum cumulative number of bookings permitted across this bucket and all lower-yield buckets nested beneath it. Enforced in real time by Altéa Inventory to prevent overselling beyond the authorized level.']
  bucket_rank varchar [note: 'Ordinal position of this RBD bucket within its cabin hierarchy, from highest-yield (rank 1) to lowest-yield. Used by Sabre AirVision and Altéa Inventory to determine availability cascade — a lower-ranked (higher-yield) bucket must be open before a higher-ranked (lower-yield) bucket can sell.']
  bucket_status varchar [note: 'Current availability status of the fare class bucket as broadcast to GDS and direct channels: open (seats available for sale), closed (no availability — bucket is shut by RM or capacity), waitlist (no confirmed seats but waitlist accepted), request_only (requires manual approval), suspended (temporarily removed from sale by operations).']
  change_fee_amount decimal(10,2) [note: 'Monetary penalty charged to the passenger for a voluntary itinerary change on a ticket issued under this booking class, expressed in the filing currency. A value of 0.00 indicates no change fee. Commercially sensitive pricing parameter.']
  change_fee_currency varchar [note: 'ISO 4217 three-letter currency code in which the change fee amount is denominated (e.g., USD, EUR, GBP). Required for multi-currency fare filing and BSP/ARC settlement.']
  codeshare_indicator boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this fare class bucket is allocated for codeshare inventory (True) — i.e., seats sold under a partner airline\'s flight number — or is for the operating carrier\'s own inventory (False). Governs interline proration and codeshare revenue accounting.']
  codeshare_partner_code varchar [note: 'IATA two-letter airline designator of the codeshare marketing partner for whom this bucket allocation is reserved (e.g., LH, BA, AA). Null for own-carrier inventory. Used for interline settlement and codeshare capacity reporting.']
  current_bookings varchar [note: 'Number of confirmed bookings currently held against this fare class bucket. Updated in near-real-time from Altéa Inventory as PNRs are created, modified, or cancelled. Used to compute remaining availability.']
  effective_from date [note: 'Calendar date from which this fare class bucket configuration becomes active for sale. Used for seasonal inventory planning and schedule-period bucket management in Altéa Inventory.']
  effective_until date [note: 'Calendar date after which this fare class bucket configuration is no longer active. Null indicates the bucket remains open-ended until explicitly closed. Supports seasonal and schedule-period inventory lifecycle management.']
  fare_basis_code varchar [note: 'IATA fare basis code associated with the primary fare filed under this booking class bucket (e.g., YOWUS, BAPUS21). Links the inventory bucket to the fare tariff filed with ATPCO. Used for ticketing, interline proration, and BSP/ARC settlement.']
  fare_family_code varchar [note: 'Branded fare family or product bundle code associated with this booking class (e.g., BASIC, STANDARD, FLEX, BUSINESS_PLUS). Aligns the RBD bucket with the airline\'s branded fare product hierarchy for ancillary bundling and NDC offer construction.']
  group_block_seats varchar [note: 'Number of seats within this bucket reserved as a group block allocation. A value of 0 indicates no group block. Group blocks are managed separately from individual seat availability and are subject to group contract terms.']
  is_changeable boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the itinerary booked under this fare class may be voluntarily changed (True) or is a fixed non-changeable ticket (False). Relevant for IROP handling, voluntary rebooking, and ancillary change-fee revenue.']
  is_refundable boolean [note: 'Indicates whether tickets issued under this booking class are eligible for a full or partial refund (True) or are non-refundable (False). Drives customer service handling, involuntary rerouting compensation, and DOT consumer protection compliance.']
  last_rm_update_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent authorized unit or booking limit update pushed from Sabre AirVision to Altéa Inventory. Enables audit of RM optimization cycle frequency and latency monitoring between the two systems.']
  load_factor_pct decimal(5,2) [note: 'Current load factor for this fare class bucket expressed as a percentage (current_bookings / authorized_units × 100). Stored as a snapshot field for real-time RM monitoring and GDS availability optimization. Aligns with IATA load factor (percentage of seats filled) definition.']
  max_stay_days varchar [note: 'Maximum number of days the passenger may remain at the destination before return travel must commence for fares in this booking class. A null value indicates no maximum stay restriction. Standard fare restriction filed with ATPCO.']
  min_stay_days varchar [note: 'Minimum number of days the passenger must remain at the destination before return travel is permitted for fares in this booking class. A value of 0 indicates no minimum stay restriction. Standard fare restriction filed with ATPCO.']
  overbooking_factor decimal(5,4) [note: 'Fractional multiplier applied to physical seat capacity to derive the authorized overbooking level for this bucket (e.g., 1.0500 = 5% overbooking). Set by revenue management based on historical no-show and cancellation rates. Commercially sensitive — drives denied boarding exposure. Governed by DOT 14 CFR Part 250.']
  protection_level varchar [note: 'Number of seats protected (reserved) for higher-yield demand in this bucket, as computed by the EMSR (Expected Marginal Seat Revenue) optimization in Sabre AirVision. Prevents lower-yield bookings from displacing higher-yield passengers. Core RM optimization output.']
  rbd_code varchar [note: 'Single uppercase IATA letter identifying the booking class (e.g., Y, B, M, Q, V, H, K, L, S, N, O, G). The RBD is the atomic unit of inventory control in Altéa Inventory and the direct input to Sabre AirVision yield optimization. Governed by IATA Resolution 728.']
  record_created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this fare class bucket record was first created in the Silver layer data product. Supports data lineage, audit trail, and SLA compliance tracking per enterprise data governance standards.']
  record_updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to this fare class bucket record in the Silver layer. Used for incremental load detection, change data capture, and audit compliance.']
  seats_available varchar [note: 'Current number of seats available for sale in this booking class bucket at the time of the last inventory snapshot. Derived operationally as booking_limit minus current_bookings, but stored as a first-class field for GDS availability response performance. Consumed by GDS channels and direct booking engines.']
  sell_up_target_rbd varchar [note: 'The preferred higher-yield RBD code to which Sabre AirVision should attempt to sell up when this bucket is closed or constrained. Commercially sensitive RM strategy parameter used in availability cascade logic.']
  source_system_code varchar [note: 'Identifies the operational system of record that originated or last updated this fare class bucket record: ALTEA (Amadeus Altéa Inventory), SABRE_AV (Sabre AirVision Revenue Management), MANUAL (manually entered override), MIGRATION (data migration load). Supports data lineage and audit in the Silver layer.']
  waitlist_count varchar [note: 'Current number of waitlisted booking requests queued against this fare class bucket. When seats become available (e.g., cancellations), Altéa Inventory automatically clears waitlisted PNRs in queue order.']
  yield_class varchar [note: 'Revenue management yield classification assigned to this bucket by Sabre AirVision, grouping RBDs into broad yield tiers for reporting and optimization. Commercially sensitive as it reveals pricing strategy segmentation.']

  Note: 'Master entity defining individual booking class (RBD — Reservation Booking Designator) buckets within a cabin for a flight inventory record. Captures the single-letter IATA booking class code (e.g., Y, B, M, Q, V), the hierarchical nesting within the cabin, authorized capacity (AU), booking limit (BL), overbooking factor, and current availability status. The atomic unit of inventory control in Altéa Inventory and the direct input to Sabre AirVision yield optimization.'
}

Table inventory.seat_availability {
  seat_availability_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate identifier for each seat availability snapshot record in the inventory system. Primary key for this data product.']
  catering_order_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to procurement.catering_order. Business justification: Real-time seat availability snapshots determine final catering order quantities. Catering cutoff decisions use latest availability data to minimize waste and ensure adequate provisioning. Critical for']
  fare_class_bucket_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to inventory.fare_class_bucket. Business justification: seat_availability tracks availability for a specific fare class bucket (RBD). fare_class_bucket is the master entity defining RBD buckets. This FK links transactional availability records to the bucke']
  flight_inventory_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to inventory.flight_inventory. Business justification: seat_availability records are real-time availability snapshots for a specific flight-date combination. flight_inventory is the master entity for that flight-date. This FK establishes the natural paren']
  flight_leg_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the operating flight record for which seat availability is being tracked. Links to the flight master entity.']
  availability_status varchar [note: 'Current booking status of the fare class bucket as broadcast to distribution channels. Open = seats available for sale; Closed = bucket closed by revenue management; Waitlist = waitlist accepted; Request = booking requires airline confirmation; Sold Out = no seats remain. Core inventory control signal consumed by GDS, NDC, and direct channels.']
  availability_version varchar [note: 'Monotonically increasing version counter for this flight-date-fare-class availability record. Enables ordering of multiple snapshots for the same key and supports optimistic concurrency control in the Silver layer.']
  booking_class_availability_map varchar [note: 'Availability status string (AVS) representing the full cabin availability across all booking classes at the time of snapshot, as broadcast in GDS availability responses (e.g., \'Y9B9M9Q4V0\'). Enables reconstruction of the complete availability display seen by travel agents.']
  broadcast_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this availability update was broadcast to GDS and distribution channels. Distinct from snapshot_timestamp (capture time) and last_updated_timestamp (system update time). Used for GDS broadcast latency measurement and SLA verification.']
  cabin_class varchar [note: 'Physical cabin designation to which the fare class bucket belongs (e.g., First, Business, Premium Economy, Economy). Used for cabin-level load factor reporting and capacity management.']
  codeshare_allotment varchar [note: 'Number of seats allocated to the codeshare marketing partner within this fare class bucket. Null when codeshare_indicator is false. Used to track partner inventory consumption against agreed allotment caps.']
  codeshare_indicator boolean [note: 'Flag indicating whether this availability record pertains to a codeshare flight operation where the marketing carrier differs from the operating carrier. When true, inventory is managed under a codeshare allocation agreement.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this availability snapshot record was first created in the data platform. Audit trail field for Silver layer data lineage and regulatory compliance.']
  days_to_departure varchar [note: 'Number of calendar days between the snapshot_timestamp date and the flight_date. Critical dimension for revenue management booking curve analysis and yield optimization model training.']
  distribution_channel varchar [note: 'Channel context for which this availability snapshot was generated or broadcast. GDS = Global Distribution System (Amadeus, Sabre, Travelport); NDC = IATA New Distribution Capability; Direct = airline.com or mobile app; API = B2B direct connect; Interline = partner carrier ticketing; Codeshare = shared flight operation partner.']
  forecast_demand varchar [note: 'Revenue management system\'s demand forecast for this fare class bucket at the time of the snapshot, expressed as expected number of bookings. Captured alongside actual availability for model accuracy tracking and yield optimization.']
  gds_provider_code varchar [note: 'IATA-assigned GDS provider code identifying the specific GDS to which this availability was broadcast: 1A = Amadeus, 1S = Sabre, 1G = Galileo (Travelport), 1P = Worldspan (Travelport). Null when distribution_channel is not GDS.']
  inventory_control_mode varchar [note: 'Method by which inventory is controlled for this availability record: Leg = physical leg-based control; Segment = segment-level control; OD = Origin-Destination (virtual nesting / O&D control). Determines how Amadeus Altéa manages seat allocation across connecting itineraries.']
  is_current_snapshot boolean [note: 'Flag indicating whether this record represents the most recent (current) availability snapshot for the flight-date-fare-class combination. Enables efficient point-in-time queries without full history scan. Set to false when superseded by a newer snapshot.']
  is_interline_eligible boolean [note: 'Flag indicating whether this fare class bucket is open for interline ticketing by partner carriers under bilateral interline agreements. Governs availability visibility in interline booking flows.']
  last_updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this availability record was last modified in the source inventory system (Amadeus Altéa). Used for GDS broadcast verification, change detection, and Silver layer incremental processing.']
  load_factor_pct decimal(5,2) [note: 'Percentage of authorized seats sold at the time of this snapshot, calculated as (seats_sold / seats_authorized) × 100. Captured at snapshot time for historical revenue management model training and GDS broadcast audit. Aligns with IATA load factor (percentage of seats filled) definition.']
  marketing_carrier_code varchar [note: 'Two-character IATA airline designator of the carrier under whose flight number the seat is sold to passengers. On non-codeshare flights this equals the operating carrier code.']
  nesting_level varchar [note: 'Hierarchical nesting position of this fare class bucket within the cabin\'s virtual nesting structure used by the revenue management system. Lower values indicate higher-yield buckets. Governs availability inheritance between fare classes.']
  operating_carrier_code varchar [note: 'Two-character IATA airline designator of the carrier physically operating the flight. Differs from the marketing carrier on codeshare flights. Required for interline and codeshare inventory allocation tracking.']
  overbooking_factor decimal(5,2) [note: 'Authorized overbooking multiplier applied to physical seat capacity for this fare class bucket, expressed as a percentage above 100 (e.g., 105.00 = 5% overbooking). Set by revenue management to compensate for expected no-shows and cancellations.']
  physical_capacity varchar [note: 'Total number of physical seats configured in the cabin for this flight-date, before overbooking authorization. Provides the denominator for cabin-level load factor calculations and capacity planning.']
  protection_level varchar [note: 'Number of seats protected (reserved) for higher-yield fare classes above this bucket in the nesting hierarchy, as set by the revenue management optimizer. Core EMSR (Expected Marginal Seat Revenue) output used in Altéa and Sabre inventory control.']
  rm_decision_code varchar [note: 'Code identifying the revenue management optimization decision or rule that triggered the current availability status or booking limit for this fare class bucket (e.g., FCST_CLOSE, MANUAL_OVERRIDE, AUTO_OPEN). Supports RM model audit and performance attribution.']
  seats_authorized varchar [note: 'Maximum number of bookings authorized for this fare class bucket as set by the revenue management system. Represents the booking limit or allotment cap controlling inventory exposure for this RBD.']
  seats_available varchar [note: 'Number of seats currently available for sale in this fare class bucket at the time of the snapshot. A value of zero with status Open may indicate overbooking authorization. Negative values indicate oversold position.']
  seats_held varchar [note: 'Number of seats temporarily held under option, group block, or tour operator allotment that are not yet confirmed bookings but reduce net availability. Distinct from seats_sold as these may be released.']
  seats_sold varchar [note: 'Number of confirmed bookings (including checked-in passengers) held against this fare class bucket at the time of the snapshot. Used to compute load factor and remaining availability.']
  snapshot_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Exact date and time (ISO 8601 with timezone offset) at which this availability record was captured or broadcast. The principal business event timestamp for this EVENT_LOG record. Used for GDS broadcast verification and revenue management time-series analysis.']
  snapshot_trigger varchar [note: 'Business event or system action that caused this availability snapshot to be generated and broadcast. Enables audit of why availability changed at a given point in time. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: Booking|Cancellation|Schedule Change|RM Override|GDS Poll|NDC Request|Overbooking Adjustment|Batch Interval — promote to reference product]']
  source_system_code varchar [note: 'Code identifying the operational system of record that originated this availability record: ALTEA = Amadeus Altéa Inventory (PSS); SABRE = Sabre AirVision Revenue Management; NETLINE = Lufthansa Systems NetLine/Ops; MANUAL = manually entered override.']
  waitlist_count varchar [note: 'Number of passengers currently on the waitlist for this fare class bucket at the time of the snapshot. Relevant when availability_status is Waitlist. Used to assess demand pressure and inform overbooking decisions.']

  Note: 'Real-time and historical transactional record of seat availability by fare class bucket for a flight-date, as broadcast to distribution channels and captured at scheduled intervals for audit and analytics. Captures available seat count, status code (Open/Closed/Waitlist), last update timestamp, channel context (GDS, NDC, direct), snapshot trigger reason, and load factor at capture time. Serves as both the live availability signal consumed by Amadeus, Sabre, and Travelport systems and the historical availability audit trail for revenue management model training and GDS broadcast verification.'
}

Table inventory.overbooking_control {
  overbooking_control_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate identifier for the overbooking control record. Primary key for this entity.']
  cabin_class_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to inventory.cabin_class. Business justification: overbooking_control stores overbooking authorization parameters for a specific cabin class. cabin_class is the reference master for cabin definitions. This FK links control parameters to the cabin mas']
  catering_order_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to procurement.catering_order. Business justification: Overbooking authorization changes affect expected passenger counts and thus catering requirements. Catering supervisors adjust meal orders based on overbooking limits to balance service levels and was']
  cost_centre_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.cost_centre. Business justification: Denied boarding compensation (voluntary and involuntary) is a direct cost tracked by cost center for financial reporting and DOT/regulatory compliance. Airlines monitor IDB costs against overbooking r']
  employee_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the revenue management analyst who applied the manual override. Populated only when rm_system_override_flag is True. Supports audit trail and accountability requirements.']
  flight_inventory_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the flight inventory record (specific flight departure date and flight number combination) to which this overbooking control applies. Links to the sellable inventory unit in Altéa Inventory.']
  regulatory_requirement_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.regulatory_requirement. Business justification: Overbooking limits are constrained by passenger rights regulations (EU261, DOT Part 250). Overbooking control records must reference the regulatory requirement defining maximum oversale thresholds and']
  authorized_overbooking_pct decimal(5,2) [note: 'The approved percentage above physical cabin seat capacity that the revenue management system is authorized to sell. For example, a value of 10.00 means up to 10% more seats than physically available may be booked. Derived from historical no-show rates and spoilage risk models in Sabre AirVision Revenue Management.']
  availability_snapshot_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The timestamp of the most recent real-time availability snapshot published to GDS and direct booking channels by Altéa Inventory. Indicates the currency of the current_booking_count and booking_limit values.']
  avg_fare_value_usd decimal(10,2) [note: 'The average fare value in USD for bookings in this cabin on this flight, used as an input to the overbooking revenue optimization model. Enables the model to weigh the revenue benefit of additional bookings against the cost of denied boarding compensation.']
  booking_limit varchar [note: 'The computed maximum number of bookings permitted for this cabin on this flight, equal to physical seat capacity plus authorized oversale seats. This is the operative value consumed by Altéa Inventory and GDS availability responses to control seat sales.']
  cancellation_rate_input decimal(5,4) [note: 'Historical cancellation rate (expressed as a decimal proportion) for this route, cabin, and season used as an actuarial input alongside the no-show rate in the overbooking model. Distinct from no-show rate as it captures pre-departure cancellations.']
  codeshare_inventory_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this overbooking control applies to a codeshare inventory allocation rather than the operating carrier\'s own inventory. Codeshare overbooking controls may differ from mainline controls due to interline agreements and partner airline seat block commitments.']
  control_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of this overbooking control record. Active indicates the parameters are live and being applied by Altéa Inventory booking limit calculations. Suspended means overbooking is temporarily halted for this cabin. Overridden indicates a manual revenue management analyst override is in effect.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when this overbooking control record was first created in the system. Supports audit trail and data lineage requirements.']
  current_booking_count varchar [note: 'The real-time count of confirmed bookings (PNRs) held against this cabin at the time of the last availability snapshot. Used to compute remaining availability and monitor oversale exposure.']
  day_of_week_pattern varchar [note: 'Bitmask or string pattern (e.g., \'1234567\' where each digit represents Mon–Sun) indicating which days of the week this overbooking control applies to. Allows differentiated overbooking levels for weekday versus weekend departures.']
  denied_boarding_risk_threshold decimal(5,4) [note: 'The maximum acceptable probability (expressed as a decimal, e.g., 0.0200 = 2%) that at least one passenger will be involuntarily denied boarding due to oversale. Balances revenue optimization against regulatory denied boarding compensation exposure under DOT 14 CFR Part 250 and IATA Resolution 830d.']
  departure_date date [note: 'The scheduled departure date of the flight to which this overbooking control record applies. Combined with the flight inventory reference, uniquely identifies the specific flight departure instance.']
  effective_from date [note: 'The date from which this overbooking control record becomes operative. Supports scheduling of future overbooking parameter changes in advance of the departure date.']
  effective_until date [note: 'The date after which this overbooking control record is no longer operative. Null indicates the record remains active indefinitely until superseded. Supports time-bounded overbooking strategies.']
  go_show_rate_input decimal(5,4) [note: 'Historical rate of passengers who arrive at the gate without a prior reservation (go-shows) for this route and cabin, expressed as a decimal proportion. Used in the overbooking model to account for demand that materialises at departure.']
  group_booking_seats_reserved varchar [note: 'Number of seats within the cabin that are reserved for confirmed group bookings and excluded from the overbooking calculation. Group bookings typically have lower no-show rates and are treated separately in the overbooking model.']
  historical_no_show_rate decimal(5,4) [note: 'The input no-show rate (expressed as a decimal proportion, e.g., 0.0850 = 8.50%) derived from historical passenger behaviour for this route, cabin, and season. Used as the primary actuarial input to the overbooking authorization model in Sabre AirVision.']
  idb_compensation_exposure_usd decimal(10,2) [note: 'Estimated maximum financial exposure in USD for involuntary denied boarding (IDB) compensation if all oversold seats result in IDB events. Calculated based on max_oversale_seats and applicable DOT/IATA compensation rates. Used in revenue management risk assessment.']
  last_reviewed_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when a revenue management analyst last reviewed and confirmed or adjusted this overbooking control record. Supports governance workflows requiring periodic review of overbooking authorizations.']
  last_updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when this overbooking control record was most recently modified. Tracks parameter changes by revenue management analysts or automated system recalculations.']
