  load_factor_forecast decimal(5,4) [note: 'The forecasted load factor (proportion of physical seats expected to be occupied at departure, expressed as a decimal e.g., 0.9200 = 92%) for this cabin on this flight. Input from Sabre AirVision demand forecasting, used to calibrate the overbooking level.']
  max_oversale_seats varchar [note: 'The absolute maximum number of seats above physical cabin capacity that may be sold, regardless of the authorized overbooking percentage. Acts as a hard cap to limit denied boarding exposure. Set by revenue management analysts in Altéa Inventory.']
  next_review_date date [note: 'The scheduled date by which this overbooking control record must be reviewed by a revenue management analyst. Drives workflow queuing in Sabre AirVision to ensure timely recalibration before departure.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text field for additional operational notes or context related to this overbooking control record, such as special event considerations, IROP recovery instructions, or coordination notes with ground handling teams.']
  overbooking_model_version varchar [note: 'Version identifier of the statistical overbooking model used to compute the authorized overbooking percentage for this record. Enables reproducibility, model governance, and comparison of model performance across versions in Sabre AirVision.']
  override_reason varchar [note: 'Free-text justification provided by the revenue management analyst when rm_system_override_flag is True. Documents the business rationale for departing from the system-recommended overbooking level (e.g., special event, group booking, IROP recovery).']
  physical_seat_capacity varchar [note: 'The actual number of physical seats available in the cabin for this flight departure, as configured in the aircraft fleet assignment. This is the baseline against which the overbooking percentage is applied.']
  regulatory_market_code varchar [note: 'ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 country code or regulatory jurisdiction code (e.g., USA, EU) identifying the regulatory regime governing denied boarding compensation obligations for this flight. Determines applicable DOT, EASA EC 261/2004, or national civil aviation authority rules.']
  rm_system_override_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether a revenue management analyst has manually overridden the system-calculated overbooking parameters. When True, the authorized_overbooking_pct and max_oversale_seats reflect analyst-entered values rather than the automated Sabre AirVision model output.']
  season_code varchar [note: 'IATA scheduling season code (e.g., S25 for Summer 2025, W24 for Winter 2024) during which this overbooking control is applicable. Overbooking parameters are typically calibrated per season based on seasonal demand and no-show patterns.']
  source_system_code varchar [note: 'Identifies the originating system that created or last updated this overbooking control record. ALTEA = Amadeus Altéa Inventory automated calculation, SABRE_AV = Sabre AirVision Revenue Management model output, MANUAL = direct analyst entry.']
  spoilage_risk_threshold decimal(5,4) [note: 'The maximum acceptable probability (expressed as a decimal, e.g., 0.0500 = 5%) that the flight departs with empty seats due to under-booking. Revenue management uses this threshold to calibrate the overbooking level against the risk of seat spoilage versus denied boarding.']
  upgrade_buffer_seats varchar [note: 'Number of seats held back from the overbooking pool to accommodate anticipated operational upgrades (e.g., elite frequent flyer upgrades, compensation upgrades). Reduces the effective booking limit to preserve upgrade capacity.']
  voluntary_denied_boarding_target varchar [note: 'The target number of passengers expected to accept voluntary denied boarding (VDB) compensation offers at the gate in the event of an oversale. Used in the overbooking model to offset the involuntary denied boarding risk.']

  Note: 'Master entity storing the overbooking authorization parameters for a flight inventory record by cabin class. Captures the authorized overbooking percentage, maximum oversale seats, historical no-show rate inputs, spoilage risk threshold, and the revenue management system override flag. Directly managed by revenue management analysts and consumed by Altéa Inventory to set booking limits above physical capacity.'
}

Table inventory.codeshare_allocation {
  codeshare_allocation_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate identifier for the codeshare or interline inventory allocation record within the Amadeus Altéa Inventory system. Primary key for this entity.']
  bilateral_asa_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the governing bilateral codeshare or interline agreement between the operating carrier and the marketing carrier that authorises this allocation.']
  cabin_class_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to inventory.cabin_class. Business justification: codeshare_allocation defines seat allocations granted to codeshare partners for a specific cabin class. cabin_class is the reference master for cabin definitions. This FK links allocation records to t']
  flight_inventory_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the specific flight-date inventory record in the host carrier\'s Altéa Inventory for which this codeshare allocation is ring-fenced.']
  operating_carrier_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the airline that physically operates the flight on which this inventory allocation is granted. The operating carrier owns the aircraft and crew.']
  operating_carrier_org_unit_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to workforce.org_unit. Business justification: Operating carriers in codeshare agreements are airline organizational units (divisions/subsidiaries). Essential for codeshare revenue allocation, bilateral agreement compliance tracking, and operation']
  operating_carrier_vendor_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to procurement.vendor. Business justification: Operating carrier in codeshare is a vendor for settlement purposes. Prorate agreements, revenue sharing, and interline billing require vendor master linkage. Finance uses this for accounts payable/rec']
  allocation_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the codeshare allocation record within Altéa Inventory. \'active\' means the allocation is live and available for booking; \'suspended\' means temporarily halted; \'cancelled\' means permanently withdrawn; \'pending\' means awaiting activation; \'expired\' means the flight date has passed.']
  allocation_type varchar [note: 'Defines the inventory control mechanism governing this codeshare allocation. \'block_space\' means a fixed seat block is ring-fenced for the marketing carrier regardless of overall availability; \'free_flow\' means the marketing carrier draws from the operating carrier\'s shared availability pool without a hard block; \'hybrid\' combines a guaranteed minimum block with free-flow access above it.']
  altea_allocation_reference varchar [note: 'Native allocation reference identifier assigned by the Amadeus Altéa Inventory system for this codeshare block. Used for reconciliation and traceability back to the operational PSS source record.']
  authorized_seat_count varchar [note: 'Maximum number of seats the marketing carrier is authorised to sell on this flight-date and cabin under this allocation. For block-space agreements this is the contracted block size; for free-flow it represents the cap agreed in the bilateral.']
  availability_snapshot_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent real-time availability snapshot captured from Altéa Inventory for this allocation. Used by GDS and direct channels to serve current seat availability to booking agents.']
  bilateral_agreement_reference varchar [note: 'Human-readable external reference number or code of the bilateral codeshare/interline agreement (e.g., contract number from the commercial agreement). Stored for operational traceability; the full agreement detail is held in the bilateral_agreement master.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this codeshare allocation record was first created in the Altéa Inventory system, representing the business event of the allocation being established.']
  days_of_operation varchar [note: 'IATA-standard days-of-week string indicating on which days of the week this allocation applies within the effective date range (e.g., \'1234567\' = daily, \'135\' = Mon/Wed/Fri). Encoded as ISO weekday numbers 1=Monday through 7=Sunday.']
  destination_iata_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter IATA airport code for the arrival airport of the operating flight segment covered by this allocation (e.g., CDG, SIN, ORD).']
  effective_from date [note: 'First date from which this codeshare allocation record is valid and active, as defined in the bilateral agreement. Supports seasonal schedule periods aligned with IATA scheduling seasons.']
  effective_until date [note: 'Last date through which this codeshare allocation record is valid, as defined in the bilateral agreement. Null for open-ended allocations. Aligns with IATA scheduling season end dates.']
  fare_class_bucket varchar [note: 'IATA booking class (RBD — Reservation Booking Designator) within the cabin to which this allocation is mapped in Altéa Inventory (e.g., Y, B, M, Q, V). Determines the yield tier at which the marketing carrier\'s seats are ring-fenced.']
  flight_date date [note: 'Scheduled departure date of the operating flight for which this codeshare allocation applies. Combined with the operating flight number, uniquely identifies the flight-date instance.']
  gds_availability_indicator boolean [note: 'Flag indicating whether the marketing carrier\'s availability under this allocation is published and visible in Global Distribution Systems (GDS) such as Amadeus, Sabre, and Travelport. When False, the allocation is restricted to direct or NDC channels only.']
  interline_indicator boolean [note: 'Flag indicating whether this allocation is governed by an interline ticketing agreement (True) rather than a pure codeshare arrangement (False). Interline allocations follow IATA BSP/ARC settlement rules.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent modification to this codeshare allocation record, such as a change to seat counts, thresholds, or status. Supports audit trail and change tracking in the Silver layer.']
  last_seat_date date [note: 'Latest date on which the marketing carrier may sell the last available seat under this allocation before the booking window closes. May differ from the flight date due to advance purchase restrictions in the bilateral.']
  marketing_carrier_code varchar [note: 'Reference to the partner airline that markets and sells seats under its own flight designator code on the operating carrier\'s flight. The marketing carrier holds this allocation.']
  marketing_flight_number varchar [note: 'IATA-format flight designator under which the marketing carrier sells the codeshare seats (e.g., BA5678). This is the flight number visible to passengers booking through the marketing carrier.']
  maximum_seat_threshold varchar [note: 'Hard upper limit on the number of seats the marketing carrier may sell on this flight-date and cabin, enforced by Altéa Inventory to prevent over-allocation beyond the bilateral agreement cap.']
  minimum_seat_threshold varchar [note: 'Minimum number of seats guaranteed to the marketing carrier that the operating carrier must hold available regardless of overall flight demand. Relevant for block-space and hybrid allocation types. Null for pure free-flow allocations.']
  operating_flight_number varchar [note: 'IATA-format flight designator of the operating carrier (e.g., AA1234). Identifies the physical flight on which the codeshare seats are allocated.']
  origin_iata_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter IATA airport code for the departure airport of the operating flight segment covered by this allocation (e.g., LHR, JFK, DXB).']
  overbooking_factor decimal(5,4) [note: 'Multiplier applied to the authorized seat count to permit controlled overbooking of the marketing carrier\'s allocation (e.g., 1.0500 = 5% overbooking). Managed by the revenue management system in alignment with Sabre AirVision overbooking controls.']
  prorate_agreement_code varchar [note: 'Code referencing the revenue proration agreement between the operating and marketing carriers that governs how ticket revenue is split for seats sold under this codeshare allocation. Aligns with IATA BSP prorate rules.']
  release_date date [note: 'Date on which any unsold seats in a block-space allocation are automatically released back to the operating carrier\'s general inventory pool for unrestricted sale. Null for free-flow allocations where no block is held.']
  release_days_prior varchar [note: 'Number of days before scheduled departure at which unsold block-space seats are automatically released to the operating carrier\'s open inventory. Derived from the bilateral agreement terms and used to compute release_date.']
  remarks varchar [note: 'Free-text operational notes or remarks associated with this codeshare allocation, such as special handling instructions, commercial exceptions, or temporary override justifications entered by inventory controllers.']
  revenue_share_percentage decimal(5,2) [note: 'Percentage of ticket revenue attributable to the operating carrier for seats sold by the marketing carrier under this allocation, as stipulated in the bilateral prorate agreement. Commercially sensitive.']
  seats_available varchar [note: 'Current number of seats remaining available for sale by the marketing carrier under this allocation (authorized_seat_count minus seats_sold, subject to overbooking controls). Reflects the real-time availability figure published to GDS and direct channels.']
  seats_sold varchar [note: 'Current count of seats already sold or confirmed by the marketing carrier against this allocation as of the last inventory snapshot. Used to compute remaining availability and monitor allocation utilisation.']

  Note: 'Master entity governing the seat block or free-flow inventory allocation granted to a codeshare or interline partner airline on a specific flight-date and cabin. Captures the operating carrier flight, the marketing carrier code, allocation type (block space vs. free-flow), authorized seat count, minimum/maximum thresholds, and the bilateral agreement reference. Ensures partner inventory is correctly ring-fenced within the host carrier\'s Altéa Inventory.'
}

Table inventory.inventory_leg {
  inventory_leg_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate identifier for the inventory leg record within the Amadeus Altéa Inventory system. Serves as the primary key for this entity in the Silver Layer lakehouse.']
  aircraft_type_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to fleet.aircraft_type. Business justification: Revenue management systems configure fare class bucket authorization limits based on aircraft type\'s physical capacity, cabin configuration, and range. Inventory leg planning requires structured refer']
  flight_inventory_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the parent flight inventory record that owns this leg. Supports multi-leg itinerary nesting and through-availability calculations within the inventory domain.']
  arrival_terminal varchar [note: 'Terminal identifier at the destination airport where this leg arrives (e.g., \'T2\', \'T4\', \'C\'). Used for connecting passenger MCT validation and ground handling coordination.']
  authorized_capacity varchar [note: 'Revenue management-approved total booking authorization for this leg, incorporating overbooking allowances above physical seat capacity. Set by Sabre AirVision Revenue Management and consumed by Altéa Inventory for availability control.']
  availability_snapshot_datetime timestamp [note: 'UTC timestamp of the most recent real-time availability snapshot for this leg as published to GDS and direct booking channels. Supports audit of availability state at a point in time and feeds through-availability calculations.']
  booked_count varchar [note: 'Current total number of confirmed bookings (Pax) held against this leg across all fare classes and cabins. Updated in real time by the Amadeus Altéa PSS as reservations are created, modified, or cancelled.']
  cabin_configuration_code varchar [note: 'Identifier for the specific cabin layout configuration assigned to this leg (e.g., \'Y-only\', \'J/Y\', \'F/J/Y\'). Links to the cabin configuration master to determine per-cabin seat counts and fare class bucket assignments.']
  codeshare_blocked_count varchar [note: 'Number of seats allocated to codeshare partner airlines on this operating leg. Represents inventory carved out for marketing carrier partners under codeshare agreements, reducing net availability for the operating carrier.']
  codeshare_indicator boolean [note: 'Flag indicating whether this leg is operated under a codeshare agreement, meaning one or more marketing carriers sell seats on this operating leg under their own flight numbers. True = codeshare arrangements exist for this leg.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'UTC timestamp when this inventory leg record was first created in the system, either by schedule publication or manual entry. Provides the audit trail creation anchor for data lineage and compliance purposes.']
  departure_slot_time timestamp [note: 'Allocated airport departure slot time (UTC) assigned by the slot coordinator for this leg at the origin airport. May differ from scheduled departure time. Null for airports without slot coordination (non-Level 2/3 airports).']
  departure_terminal varchar [note: 'Terminal identifier at the origin airport from which this leg departs (e.g., \'T1\', \'T5\', \'B\'). Used for passenger wayfinding, ground handling coordination, and SITA airport management integration.']
  destination_airport_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter IATA airport code for the arrival airport of this inventory leg (e.g., \'LAX\', \'CDG\', \'SIN\'). Defines the physical destination point of the leg for inventory, pricing, and slot management.']
  flight_number varchar [note: 'The operating carrier\'s flight number for this leg, combining the IATA carrier code and numeric designator (e.g., \'AA101\', \'LH400\'). Used as the primary commercial identifier for the flight leg in GDS, OTA, and direct channels.']
  group_blocked_count varchar [note: 'Number of seats currently blocked and held for group bookings on this leg. Reduces the net availability exposed to individual GDS and direct channel requests.']
  interline_indicator boolean [note: 'Flag indicating whether this leg is eligible for interline ticketing, allowing passengers to be ticketed on a single document across multiple carriers. True = interline agreements apply to this leg.']
  inventory_close_datetime timestamp [note: 'Date and time (UTC) when inventory for this leg was closed to new bookings. Typically triggered at departure or by revenue management closure rules. Null if inventory is still open.']
  inventory_open_datetime timestamp [note: 'Date and time (UTC) when inventory for this leg was first opened for sale across GDS and direct channels. Marks the start of the revenue management availability window.']
  irop_indicator boolean [note: 'Flag indicating whether this leg is currently subject to an Irregular Operations (IROP) event such as a delay, cancellation, diversion, or equipment swap. True = IROP condition active. Triggers passenger re-accommodation workflows in Amadeus Altéa.']
  last_updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'UTC timestamp of the most recent modification to this inventory leg record, including availability updates, status changes, capacity adjustments, or schedule revisions. Essential for incremental ETL processing and Silver Layer change detection.']
  leg_status varchar [note: 'Current operational and inventory lifecycle status of this leg. \'active\' — open for sale; \'cancelled\' — flight cancelled, inventory closed; \'diverted\' — flight diverted to alternate airport; \'suspended\' — temporarily removed from sale; \'closed\' — inventory closed post-departure.']
  load_factor decimal(5,2) [note: 'Current load factor for this leg expressed as a percentage: (booked_count / total_seat_capacity) × 100. Key revenue management KPI representing the percentage of available seats filled. Updated in real time as bookings change.']
  operating_carrier_code varchar [note: 'Two-character IATA airline designator code of the carrier physically operating this flight leg (e.g., \'AA\', \'LH\', \'EK\'). Distinguishes the operating carrier from any marketing/codeshare carrier.']
  operating_day_of_week varchar [note: 'IATA day-of-week code on which this leg operates, where 1=Monday through 7=Sunday per IATA SSIM convention. Used for schedule frequency analysis and inventory pattern matching. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: 1|2|3|4|5|6|7 — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  origin_airport_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter IATA airport code for the departure airport of this inventory leg (e.g., \'JFK\', \'LHR\', \'DXB\'). Defines the physical origin point of the leg for inventory, pricing, and slot management.']
  overbooking_factor decimal(5,2) [note: 'The overbooking multiplier applied to physical seat capacity for this leg, expressed as a percentage above 100 (e.g., 105.50 means 5.5% overbooking). Derived from Sabre AirVision Revenue Management no-show and cancellation forecasting models.']
  schedule_period_code varchar [note: 'IATA seasonal schedule period code under which this leg is published (e.g., \'W24\' for Winter 2024, \'S25\' for Summer 2025). Aligns with IATA SSIM scheduling seasons for slot coordination and network planning.']
  scheduled_arrival_datetime timestamp [note: 'Scheduled gate arrival date and time for this leg in UTC (ISO 8601 format). Represents the planned OOOI \'In\' time used for connection logic, MCT calculations, and inventory closure triggers.']
  scheduled_block_hours decimal(5,2) [note: 'Planned block time for this leg in decimal hours, measured from scheduled gate departure (Out) to scheduled gate arrival (In). Block hours are the primary unit for aircraft utilization, crew duty time, and cost accounting (CASK calculations).']
  scheduled_departure_datetime timestamp [note: 'Scheduled gate departure date and time for this leg in UTC (ISO 8601 format). Represents the planned OOOI \'Out\' time used for inventory availability windows, GDS display, and OTP measurement.']
  scheduled_flight_distance_km varchar [note: 'Great-circle distance of this leg in kilometres between origin and destination airports. Used in RPK (Revenue Passenger Kilometre) and ASK (Available Seat Kilometre) calculations, yield analysis, and CORSIA carbon emissions reporting.']
  sequence_number varchar [note: 'Ordinal position of this physical leg within the parent flight inventory record (e.g., 1 for the first leg, 2 for the second leg of a multi-leg service). Used to reconstruct itinerary order and support through-availability logic.']
  service_type_code varchar [note: 'IATA single-character service type code indicating the nature of the flight service (e.g., \'J\' for scheduled passenger, \'F\' for scheduled freight, \'C\' for charter). Drives inventory partitioning between passenger and cargo domains.']
  through_availability_indicator boolean [note: 'Flag indicating whether this leg participates in through-availability calculations, allowing connecting itinerary availability to be computed across multiple legs without individual leg-level availability checks. True = through-availability enabled.']
  total_seat_capacity varchar [note: 'Total number of sellable passenger seats on this leg across all cabins, as configured in the inventory system for the assigned aircraft type. This is the gross capacity before overbooking authorization is applied.']
  waitlisted_count varchar [note: 'Number of passengers currently on the waitlist for this leg, pending availability opening due to cancellations or capacity adjustments. Managed within Amadeus Altéa Inventory waitlist queues.']
  wet_lease_indicator boolean [note: 'Flag indicating whether this leg is operated under a wet lease (ACMI) arrangement, where the aircraft, crew, maintenance, and insurance are provided by a lessor airline. Affects operating carrier attribution and regulatory reporting.']

  Note: 'Master entity representing a single physical flight leg as the unit of inventory management, distinct from the commercial flight segment. Captures origin and destination airport IATA codes, scheduled departure and arrival datetimes, operating carrier, flight number, service type, and the link to the parent flight inventory record. Supports multi-leg itinerary inventory nesting and through-availability calculations.'
}

Table inventory.segment {
  segment_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate identifier for the commercial Origin-Destination (O&D) inventory segment record in the Amadeus Altéa Inventory system. Primary key for this entity.']
  aircraft_type_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to fleet.aircraft_type. Business justification: Segment-level inventory control and nesting logic depend on aircraft type\'s seat capacity and cabin class distribution. GDS availability calculations and codeshare seat allocation require aircraft typ']
  cabin_class_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to inventory.cabin_class. Business justification: inventory_segment represents a commercial O&D segment sold in a specific cabin class. cabin_class is the reference master for cabin definitions. This FK links segment records to the cabin master. cabi']
  inventory_leg_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the underlying physical inventory leg (flight leg) from which this commercial segment derives its availability. A segment may span one or more legs in a connecting itinerary.']
  scheduled_flight_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the scheduled flight record associated with this inventory segment, linking to the flight operations domain for operational context.']
  authorized_capacity varchar [note: 'Total number of seats authorized for sale on this segment in the specified cabin and booking class, including any overbooking allowance approved by revenue management. This is the gross sellable inventory before netting sold seats.']
  availability_control_method varchar [note: 'Method used to control seat availability for this segment: \'leg_based\' = availability derived from individual leg bucket counts; \'od_based\' = availability controlled at the O&D segment level using virtual nesting; \'hybrid\' = combination of leg and O&D controls.']
  availability_snapshot_time timestamp [note: 'Timestamp (UTC) at which the current availability figures (seats_sold, seats_available, load_factor) were last refreshed from the Amadeus Altéa Inventory system. Critical for GDS cache synchronization and real-time availability accuracy.']
  booking_class varchar [note: 'Single-letter IATA Reservation Booking Designator (RBD) representing the fare class bucket for this segment (e.g., Y, B, M, Q, V, W). Directly controls availability nesting and revenue management yield optimization in Sabre AirVision and Amadeus Altéa.']
  codeshare_seat_allocation varchar [note: 'Number of seats allocated to the marketing carrier under a block-space codeshare agreement for this segment. Applicable only when codeshare_type = \'block_space\'. Null for free-flow or non-codeshare segments.']
  codeshare_type varchar [note: 'Type of codeshare arrangement governing inventory allocation for this segment: \'free_flow\' = marketing carrier draws from operating carrier\'s open inventory; \'block_space\' = a fixed seat block is allocated to the marketing carrier; \'none\' = not a codeshare segment.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp (UTC) when this inventory segment record was first created in the system, typically when the flight schedule was loaded into Amadeus Altéa Inventory for the season.']
  departure_date date [note: 'Scheduled departure date of the segment at the origin airport in local time (yyyy-MM-dd). Used as the primary date dimension for inventory availability queries and revenue management yield optimization.']
  destination_airport_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter IATA airport code for the destination station of this commercial O&D segment as sold to the passenger. Defines the arrival point of the sellable unit.']
  fare_basis_code varchar [note: 'IATA fare basis code identifying the specific fare product associated with this inventory segment (e.g., YLOWUS, BAPOW). Governs fare rules, restrictions, and the booking class bucket to which availability is mapped.']
  gds_availability_code varchar [note: 'Availability status code published to Global Distribution Systems (GDS) such as Amadeus, Sabre, and Travelport for this segment and booking class. Standard codes include numeric seat counts (0-9) or alpha codes (A=available, C=closed, L=waitlist) per IATA AIRIMP standards.']
  group_seats_held varchar [note: 'Number of seats currently held under group booking reservations for this segment. Group holds reduce available inventory and are subject to release deadlines managed by the group desk.']
  interline_eligible boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this inventory segment is eligible for interline ticketing agreements, allowing passengers to book connecting itineraries across multiple carriers on a single ticket. Governed by bilateral interline agreements and IATA BSP/ARC settlement rules.']
  load_factor decimal(5,2) [note: 'Current load factor for this inventory segment expressed as a percentage of physical cabin capacity that is sold or confirmed (seats_sold / physical_capacity * 100). Key performance indicator for revenue management and capacity planning. Note: this is a point-in-time snapshot value, not a computed aggregate.']
  marketed_flight_number varchar [note: 'The IATA-format flight number as sold to passengers and published in GDS channels (e.g., AA1234). For codeshare segments, this is the marketing carrier\'s flight number, which may differ from the operating carrier\'s flight number.']
  marketing_carrier_code varchar [note: 'Two-character IATA airline designator of the carrier that markets and sells this segment to passengers. For codeshare flights, this is the airline whose flight number appears on the ticket.']
  min_connecting_time_minutes varchar [note: 'Minimum Connecting Time (MCT) in minutes required at the connecting airport for this segment to be valid in a connecting itinerary. Enforced during availability display and booking to prevent infeasible connections. Sourced from IATA SSIM MCT tables.']
  nesting_level varchar [note: 'Hierarchical nesting level of this booking class within the cabin\'s availability nesting structure (e.g., 1=highest yield class, 10=lowest yield class). Governs through-availability rules: higher-level classes inherit availability from lower-level buckets per revenue management nesting policy.']
  operating_carrier_code varchar [note: 'Two-character IATA airline designator of the carrier that physically operates the aircraft for this segment. Differs from marketing_carrier_code on codeshare flights.']
  origin_airport_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter IATA airport code for the origin station of this commercial O&D segment as sold to the passenger. Defines the departure point of the sellable unit.']
  overbooking_limit varchar [note: 'Maximum number of seats that may be sold beyond the physical cabin capacity for this segment, as authorized by revenue management overbooking controls. Expressed as an absolute seat count above physical capacity.']
  overbooking_rate decimal(5,2) [note: 'Overbooking authorization expressed as a percentage above physical cabin capacity (e.g., 5.00 = 5% overbooking). Used by revenue management to calibrate no-show risk models and optimize load factor.']
  sale_close_date date [note: 'Date after which this inventory segment is no longer available for new bookings. Typically set to the departure date or earlier based on advance purchase restrictions and revenue management close-out rules.']
  sale_open_date date [note: 'Date from which this inventory segment is open for sale in GDS and direct booking channels. Defines the advance purchase window start for revenue management and distribution control.']
  scheduled_arrival_time timestamp [note: 'Scheduled arrival date and time of the segment at the destination airport in UTC (yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX). Used for MCT enforcement and connecting itinerary feasibility validation.']
  scheduled_departure_time timestamp [note: 'Scheduled departure date and time of the segment at the origin airport in UTC (yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX). Used for precise GDS availability display and MCT (Minimum Connecting Time) enforcement calculations.']
  season_code varchar [note: 'IATA scheduling season code for this inventory segment (e.g., S25 = Summer 2025, W24 = Winter 2024). Aligns inventory records with the IATA Standard Schedules Information Manual (SSIM) scheduling seasons used for slot management and route planning.']
  seats_available varchar [note: 'Current count of seats available for sale on this segment, computed as authorized_capacity minus seats_sold minus seats_blocked. This is the real-time availability figure published to GDS and direct channels.']
  seats_blocked varchar [note: 'Number of seats held back from general sale for operational purposes such as crew positioning, group holds, codeshare allocations, or special service requests (SSR). Reduces net available inventory without being sold.']
  seats_sold varchar [note: 'Number of seats currently confirmed as sold (booked and ticketed) on this inventory segment. Updated in real-time from the Passenger Service System (PSS) as bookings are made or cancelled.']
  segment_status varchar [note: 'Current availability status of this inventory segment as published to GDS and direct booking channels. \'open\' = seats available for sale; \'closed\' = no availability; \'waitlisted\' = demand exceeds supply, waitlist active; \'cancelled\' = flight/segment cancelled; \'suspended\' = temporarily removed from sale.']
  segment_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the commercial segment by its operational and commercial structure: \'direct\' = single leg O&D; \'connecting\' = multi-leg through-availability segment; \'codeshare\' = marketed by partner airline; \'interline\' = cross-carrier ticketing agreement; \'virtual\' = constructed availability for display purposes.']
  through_availability_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this segment participates in through-availability nesting, meaning its availability is derived from the aggregated availability of underlying leg buckets rather than being independently controlled. True = through-availability applies.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp (UTC) of the most recent update to this inventory segment record, including availability changes, overbooking adjustments, or status transitions. Used for incremental data pipeline processing in the Databricks Silver layer.']
  waitlist_count varchar [note: 'Number of passengers currently on the waitlist for this inventory segment and booking class. Used by revenue management to assess latent demand and inform overbooking and upgrade decisions.']

  Note: 'Master entity representing a commercial O&D (origin-destination) segment as sold to passengers, which may span one or more physical inventory legs. Captures the marketed flight number, O&D city pair, cabin, fare basis, and the through-availability nesting rules that govern how segment availability is derived from underlying leg buckets. Critical for connecting-itinerary inventory control and MCT (Minimum Connecting Time) enforcement.'
}

Table inventory.availability_snapshot {
  availability_snapshot_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate identifier for each point-in-time availability snapshot record captured for a flight inventory record.']
  cabin_class_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to inventory.cabin_class. Business justification: availability_snapshot captures point-in-time availability state for a specific cabin class. cabin_class is the reference master for cabin definitions. This FK links snapshot records to the cabin maste']
  fare_class_bucket_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to inventory.fare_class_bucket. Business justification: availability_snapshot captures availability state for a specific fare class bucket (RBD). fare_class_bucket is the master entity for RBD buckets. This FK links snapshot records to the bucket master. b']
  flight_inventory_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the flight inventory record (flight-date-leg combination) for which this availability snapshot was captured. Links to the parent inventory entity in Amadeus Altéa.']
  scheduled_flight_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the scheduled flight (operating flight number and departure date) associated with this snapshot, enabling direct join to flight operations data.']
  advance_purchase_days varchar [note: 'The minimum number of days before departure that a booking must be made to qualify for this fare class bucket at snapshot time. A fare restriction parameter that governs bucket eligibility.']
  authorized_capacity varchar [note: 'The total number of seats authorized for sale in this cabin at snapshot time, incorporating the overbooking factor applied to physical cabin capacity. Represents the effective sellable ceiling for the cabin.']
  availability_code varchar [note: 'The availability indicator code broadcast to GDS channels at snapshot time (e.g., A = available, L = limited, C = closed, R = request). Follows IATA/GDS standard availability display codes used in Amadeus, Sabre, and Travelport.']
  bucket_status varchar [note: 'The availability status of this fare class bucket as broadcast to GDS and direct channels at snapshot time. Open = seats available for sale; Closed = bucket closed by RM; Waitlist = no seats but waitlist accepted; Request_only = availability subject to manual approval.']
  channel_restriction_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this fare class bucket has channel-specific distribution restrictions applied at snapshot time (e.g., web-only, GDS-excluded, direct-only). True = one or more channel restrictions are active.']
  codeshare_seats_allocated varchar [note: 'The number of seats in this fare class bucket allocated to codeshare partner airlines at snapshot time. Represents inventory blocked for partner distribution and reduces the seats available to the operating carrier\'s own channels.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The UTC timestamp when this snapshot record was first written to the Silver layer data store. Serves as the audit creation timestamp for data lineage and ETL traceability. Format: yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'The ISO 4217 three-letter currency code in which the rm_bid_price is denominated (e.g., USD, EUR, GBP). Necessary for multi-currency RM analysis across international routes.']
  days_before_departure varchar [note: 'The number of days between the snapshot timestamp and the scheduled departure date at the time of capture. A critical dimension for revenue management booking curve analysis and demand forecasting model training.']
  departure_date date [note: 'The scheduled local departure date of the flight for which availability is being snapshotted. Used as a primary partitioning and filtering dimension for historical availability analysis.']
  destination_airport_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter IATA airport code for the arrival station of the flight leg being snapshotted (e.g., LAX, CDG). Denormalized for direct availability analysis by destination market.']
  flight_number varchar [note: 'The IATA-format operating flight number (e.g., AA101, BA0293) at the time of the snapshot. Denormalized here for direct query access without joining to the flight schedule table.']
  gds_broadcast_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The UTC timestamp at which this availability state was broadcast to Global Distribution Systems (Amadeus, Sabre, Travelport). Used for GDS audit trails and latency analysis between snapshot capture and distribution. Format: yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX.']
  group_seats_blocked varchar [note: 'The number of seats in this cabin blocked for confirmed or tentative group bookings at snapshot time. Group blocks reduce the net availability for individual (PNR-level) bookings.']
  is_active_snapshot boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this record represents the most current (latest) availability snapshot for the given flight inventory record and fare class bucket combination. True = this is the current state; False = superseded by a later snapshot. Supports efficient current-state queries without full history scans.']
  load_factor decimal(5,4) [note: 'The cabin-level load factor at snapshot time, expressed as a ratio of seats sold to physical cabin capacity (e.g., 0.8500 = 85%). A key revenue management KPI used to assess fill rate and trigger yield optimization actions. Calculated as seats_sold / physical_capacity.']
  minimum_stay_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether a minimum stay condition (e.g., Saturday night stay) is required for bookings in this fare class bucket at snapshot time. True = minimum stay restriction is active on this bucket.']
  origin_airport_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter IATA airport code for the departure station of the flight leg being snapshotted (e.g., JFK, LHR). Denormalized for direct availability analysis by origin market.']
  overbooking_factor decimal(5,4) [note: 'The overbooking multiplier applied to this cabin at snapshot time, expressed as a ratio (e.g., 1.0500 = 5% overbooking above physical capacity). Set by revenue management to account for expected no-shows and cancellations.']
  physical_capacity varchar [note: 'The actual number of physical seats installed in this cabin on the aircraft assigned to this flight at snapshot time. Reflects the aircraft configuration at the time of capture, which may differ from the scheduled configuration.']
  rm_bid_price decimal(10,2) [note: 'The revenue management bid price (opportunity cost threshold) for this cabin at snapshot time, expressed in the operating currency. A booking is accepted only if its revenue exceeds the bid price. Core RM optimization output from Sabre AirVision.']
  rm_forecast_demand varchar [note: 'The revenue management system\'s demand forecast (unconstrained) for this fare class bucket at the time of the snapshot, expressed as number of expected bookings. Sourced from Sabre AirVision RM forecasting module. Used for model training and forecast accuracy measurement.']
  seats_authorized varchar [note: 'The maximum number of seats authorized by revenue management for sale in this fare class bucket (the bucket cap set by the RM system). Distinct from physical capacity; reflects yield optimization controls from Sabre AirVision.']
  seats_available varchar [note: 'The number of seats available for sale in this fare class bucket at the moment of the snapshot. A value of zero indicates the bucket is closed. This is the primary sellable inventory count broadcast to GDS and direct channels.']
  seats_held varchar [note: 'Number of seats in this bucket currently held under option or waitlist status (not yet confirmed) at snapshot time. Represents tentative demand that may convert to confirmed bookings.']
  seats_sold varchar [note: 'The cumulative number of confirmed bookings (paid and held) in this fare class bucket at the time of the snapshot. Includes codeshare and interline bookings allocated to this bucket.']
  snapshot_sequence_number varchar [note: 'An ordinal sequence number for snapshots taken for the same flight inventory record and fare class bucket, ordered by snapshot_timestamp ascending. Enables reconstruction of the full availability history and booking curve for a given flight-bucket combination.']
  snapshot_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The precise UTC date and time at which this availability state was captured. Serves as the primary temporal anchor for point-in-time analysis and GDS broadcast audit. Format: yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX.']
  source_system_code varchar [note: 'Identifies the operational system that generated or triggered this availability snapshot. ALTEA = Amadeus Altéa Inventory (primary PSS); SABRE_AV = Sabre AirVision RM override; NETLINE = Lufthansa Systems NetLine/Ops operational change; MANUAL = manually entered override.']
  trigger_reason varchar [note: 'The business event or condition that caused this snapshot to be taken. Distinguishes between routine scheduled captures, threshold-driven captures (booking or cancellation crossing a bucket boundary), manual revenue management overrides, reaccommodation events (IROPs), and overbooking adjustments. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: scheduled|booking_threshold|cancellation_threshold|manual_override|reaccommodation|overbooking_adjustment — promote to reference product]']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The UTC timestamp when this snapshot record was last modified in the Silver layer (e.g., due to late-arriving corrections or reprocessing). Format: yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX.']
  upgrade_seats_used varchar [note: 'The number of seats in this cabin occupied by passengers who have been upgraded from a lower cabin class (operational, complimentary, or mileage-based upgrades) at snapshot time. Affects net revenue yield for the cabin.']
  waitlist_count varchar [note: 'The number of passengers currently on the waitlist for this fare class bucket at snapshot time. A non-zero value when bucket_status is \'waitlist\' or \'closed\', indicating latent demand that may be cleared if cancellations occur.']

  Note: 'Transactional point-in-time capture of the full availability state across all fare class buckets for a flight inventory record, taken at scheduled intervals or triggered by significant booking events. Used for historical availability analysis, revenue management model training, and audit of GDS availability broadcasts. Stores the complete bucket-level seat count array, load factor at snapshot time, and the trigger reason (scheduled, booking threshold, manual override).'
}

Table inventory.bucket_adjustment {
  bucket_adjustment_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate identifier for each inventory bucket adjustment audit record in the silver layer lakehouse. Primary key for this transactional audit entity.']
  cabin_class_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to inventory.cabin_class. Business justification: bucket_adjustment records changes to inventory state affecting a specific cabin class. cabin_class is the reference master for cabin definitions. This FK links adjustment records to the cabin master. ']
  employee_id varchar [note: 'Employee or system account identifier of the analyst or automated process that initiated this inventory adjustment. Populated for manual overrides and analyst-driven actions; may be a system service account for automated RM adjustments. Confidential as it identifies internal workforce activity.']
  fare_class_bucket_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the fare class bucket (booking class) whose capacity or status was adjusted. Links to the fare class bucket master defining the booking class code and cabin association.']
  rm_forecast_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the revenue management demand forecast record in Sabre AirVision that triggered or informed this automated inventory adjustment. Links the audit record to the RM optimization decision for post-departure forecast accuracy analysis.']
  scheduled_flight_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the specific flight leg whose inventory was adjusted. Identifies the flight-date-origin-destination combination affected by this adjustment.']
  adjustment_reference_number varchar [note: 'Externally-known alphanumeric reference number assigned to this inventory adjustment event, used for cross-system traceability between Altéa Inventory, Sabre AirVision Revenue Management, and GDS channels.']
  adjustment_scope varchar [note: 'Defines the granularity level at which this inventory adjustment was applied: \'flight\' (entire flight stop-sale or reopen), \'cabin\' (all buckets within a cabin class), or \'bucket\' (a specific fare class booking designator). Determines which of the cabin_code and booking_class_code fields are applicable.']
  adjustment_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of this adjustment record. \'applied\' indicates the change is live in inventory; \'reversed\' indicates a subsequent correction nullified this action; \'superseded\' indicates a later adjustment replaced this one.']
  adjustment_type varchar [note: 'Categorical discriminator identifying the nature of the inventory control action applied. Drives downstream revenue analysis and audit classification. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: bucket_limit_change|status_change|cabin_closure|flight_stop_sale|flight_reopen|codeshare_reallocation — promote to reference product]']
  after_authorization_level varchar [note: 'The bucket authorization level (seat limit / cap) set by this adjustment. Represents the maximum number of seats authorized for sale in this booking class after the change is applied. Delta from before_authorization_level quantifies the inventory impact.']
  after_bucket_status varchar [note: 'The availability status of the fare class bucket set by this adjustment. Captures whether the bucket is now open for sale, closed, on waitlist, or request-only after the change is applied.']
  before_authorization_level varchar [note: 'The bucket authorization level (seat limit / cap) in effect immediately before this adjustment was applied. Represents the maximum number of seats authorized for sale in this booking class prior to the change. Used for before/after delta analysis in revenue management.']
  before_bucket_status varchar [note: 'The availability status of the fare class bucket immediately before this adjustment. Captures whether the bucket was open for sale, closed, on waitlist, or request-only prior to the change.']
  booking_class_code varchar [note: 'Single-letter IATA Reservation Booking Designator (RBD) identifying the fare class bucket affected by this adjustment (e.g., Y, B, M, Q, V, G). Null for flight-level or cabin-level adjustments that span all buckets.']
  channel_restriction varchar [note: 'Specifies whether this inventory adjustment applies to all distribution channels or is restricted to a specific channel subset: GDS (Global Distribution System), direct (airline.com and mobile), or NDC (New Distribution Capability) channels only. \'none\' indicates no channel restriction is applied.']
  codeshare_partner_code varchar [note: 'Two-character IATA airline designator of the codeshare marketing partner whose inventory allocation is affected by this adjustment. Populated only when is_codeshare_affected is True. Used to reconcile codeshare inventory reallocation events with partner settlement.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Audit timestamp recording when this bucket adjustment record was first captured in the silver layer data product. Distinct from effective_timestamp which reflects the business event time. Format: yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX.']
  days_to_departure varchar [note: 'Number of days between the effective_timestamp of this adjustment and the scheduled flight departure date. Critical revenue management context metric used to analyze booking curve behavior and evaluate the appropriateness of inventory control decisions at each booking horizon.']
  destination_airport_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter IATA airport code for the arrival station of the flight leg affected by this inventory adjustment (e.g., LAX, CDG). Together with origin_airport_code defines the O&D pair for revenue analysis.']
  effective_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The principal real-world timestamp at which the inventory adjustment became effective in the live inventory system. This is the business event time, distinct from the audit record creation timestamp. Format: yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX.']
  flight_date date [note: 'Scheduled departure date of the flight leg whose inventory was adjusted. Combined with flight_number provides the flight-date key used in GDS availability queries. Format: yyyy-MM-dd.']
  flight_number varchar [note: 'IATA-format airline designator and numeric flight number (e.g., AA1234) identifying the operating flight affected by this inventory adjustment. Denormalized for direct audit readability without joining to the flight entity.']
  initiating_system varchar [note: 'Identifies the operational system or channel that triggered this inventory adjustment. Distinguishes automated revenue management actions (Sabre AirVision) from inventory system actions (Altéa Inventory), operations control actions (NetLine/Ops for IROPs), and manual analyst overrides.']
  irop_event_reference varchar [note: 'Reference identifier linking this inventory adjustment to a specific Irregular Operations (IROP) event such as a cancellation, diversion, or significant delay that necessitated inventory reprotection or stop-sale actions. Null for non-IROP-driven adjustments.']
  is_automated boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this inventory adjustment was triggered automatically by a revenue management system rule or algorithm (True) versus initiated manually by a revenue management analyst (False). Enables segregation of automated vs. manual control decisions in audit and performance analysis.']
  is_codeshare_affected boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this inventory adjustment affects codeshare inventory allocations (True) in addition to or instead of the operating carrier\'s own inventory. When True, the codeshare_partner_code field identifies the affected marketing carrier.']
  load_factor_at_adjustment decimal(5,2) [note: 'The cabin or flight load factor (percentage of seats filled) at the time this adjustment was triggered. Expressed as a decimal percentage (e.g., 0.85 = 85%). Key context metric for revenue management yield optimization analysis and threshold-triggered automation.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Supplementary free-text notes attached to this inventory adjustment record by the revenue management analyst or operations control team. May include escalation context, regulatory compliance notes, or cross-team coordination details not captured in structured fields.']
  origin_airport_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter IATA airport code for the departure station of the flight leg affected by this inventory adjustment (e.g., JFK, LHR). Used to scope the adjustment to the correct flight-leg inventory.']
  overbooking_factor_after decimal(5,2) [note: 'The overbooking authorization factor (as a percentage above physical capacity) set by this adjustment for this cabin/bucket. Compared against overbooking_factor_before to quantify the overbooking control change applied.']
  overbooking_factor_before decimal(5,2) [note: 'The overbooking authorization factor (as a percentage above physical capacity) in effect for this cabin/bucket immediately before this adjustment. For example, 1.10 represents 110% of physical seat capacity authorized. Used to audit overbooking control decisions.']
  reason_code varchar [note: 'Standardized reason code classifying the business driver behind this inventory adjustment. Enables post-departure revenue analysis by adjustment cause. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: rm_optimization|irop_reprotection|group_block|codeshare_reallocation|load_factor_threshold|schedule_change — promote to reference product]']
  reason_description varchar [note: 'Free-text narrative providing additional context for the inventory adjustment beyond the standardized reason_code. Entered by the revenue management analyst or populated by the automated system (e.g., \'Load factor exceeded 85% threshold — closing Q class\').']
  reversal_reference_number varchar [note: 'The adjustment_reference_number of the subsequent adjustment record that reversed or superseded this action. Populated only when adjustment_status is \'reversed\' or \'superseded\'. Enables full audit chain reconstruction for compliance and revenue analysis.']
  seats_available_after varchar [note: 'The count of seats available for sale in this fare class bucket immediately after this adjustment was applied. Compared against seats_available_before to measure the net availability impact on GDS and direct channel inventory.']
  seats_available_before varchar [note: 'The count of seats available for sale in this fare class bucket immediately before this adjustment was applied. Snapshot of the real-time availability figure consumed by GDS channels prior to the change.']
  source_system_transaction_reference varchar [note: 'The native transaction or message identifier assigned by the originating operational system (Altéa Inventory, Sabre AirVision, or NetLine/Ops) for this adjustment event. Enables traceability back to the system-of-record for reconciliation and dispute resolution.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Audit timestamp recording the last modification to this bucket adjustment record in the silver layer, for example when a reversal or status correction is applied. Format: yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX.']

  Note: 'Transactional audit record of every manual or automated change to inventory state, including fare class bucket capacity/limit changes, bucket open/close actions, cabin closures, flight-level stop-sale events, and reopen actions. Captures before and after values, adjustment type (bucket limit change, status change, flight closure, cabin closure, stop-sale), reason code (RM optimization, IROP reprotection, group block, codeshare reallocation, load factor threshold, schedule change), the initiating system or analyst (Sabre AirVision, Altéa Inventory, manual override), affected scope (flight, cabin, bucket), and effective timestamp. Provides the single authoritative audit trail of all inventory control decisions for post-departure revenue analysis and regulatory compliance.'
}

Table inventory.group_block {
  group_block_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate identifier for the group block record in the inventory system. Primary key for this entity.']
  cabin_class_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to inventory.cabin_class. Business justification: group_block allocates seats in a specific cabin class. cabin_class is the reference master for cabin definitions. This FK links group block records to the cabin master. cabin_code is redundant and ava']
  campaign_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to marketing.campaign. Business justification: Group bookings are often generated through marketing campaigns targeting tour operators, corporate travel programs, and event organizers. Airlines attribute group sales to campaigns for ROI measuremen']
  catering_order_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to procurement.catering_order. Business justification: Group bookings trigger special catering orders with bulk meal requirements and custom menus. Group coordinators link blocks to catering orders for accurate provisioning and cost allocation. Essential ']
  created_by_employee_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to workforce.employee. Business justification: Group sales agents create and manage group blocks. Essential for sales commission calculation, performance metrics, accountability, and customer relationship management. Replaces agent_code with prope']
  fare_class_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the fare class bucket (booking class) under which the group block seats are held. Determines the yield and revenue management bucket impacted by this block.']
  flight_inventory_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to inventory.flight_inventory. Business justification: group_block reserves a seat block for a group booking on a specific flight-date. flight_inventory is the master inventory record for that flight-date. This FK establishes the natural parent-child rela']
  flight_leg_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the specific flight-date instance on which this group block is reserved. Links to the flight schedule/operations entity.']
  pnr_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the master Passenger Name Record (PNR) associated with this group block. A group block is linked to a single group PNR in the Passenger Service System (PSS).']
  block_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the group block. \'tentative\' = seats held pending confirmation; \'confirmed\' = group has committed; \'ticketed\' = tickets issued; \'cancelled\' = block released back to inventory; \'expired\' = option date passed without confirmation; \'waitlisted\' = requested but not yet allocated. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: tentative|confirmed|ticketed|cancelled|expired|waitlisted — promote to reference product]']
  booking_class_code varchar [note: 'Single-letter IATA booking class (fare class bucket) code under which the group seats are held (e.g., \'G\' for group fare class). Directly controls which revenue management bucket is debited when seats are allocated.']
  codeshare_indicator boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this group block is associated with a codeshare flight operation, where the operating carrier differs from the marketing carrier. True = codeshare flight; False = own-metal operation. Affects inventory allocation and interline settlement.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The timestamp when this group block record was first created in the inventory system. Supports audit trail, SLA tracking, and data lineage requirements.']
  deposit_amount decimal(12,2) [note: 'The monetary deposit amount required or received for this group block, expressed in fare_currency_code. Null if deposit_required is False.']
  deposit_paid boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the required deposit has been received from the group organizer. True = deposit received; False = deposit outstanding. Drives follow-up and cancellation workflows.']
  deposit_paid_date date [note: 'The date on which the deposit payment was received from the group organizer. Null if deposit has not been paid.']
  deposit_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether a deposit payment is required from the group organizer to hold or confirm this group block. True = deposit required; False = no deposit required.']
  fare_currency_code varchar [note: 'ISO 4217 three-letter currency code in which the group_fare_amount is denominated (e.g., \'USD\', \'EUR\', \'GBP\').']
  final_payment_due_date date [note: 'The date by which the full fare payment for all ticketed seats in the group block must be settled. Distinct from the deposit due date and ticketing deadline.']
  free_seat_ratio decimal(5,4) [note: 'The ratio of complimentary (free) seats granted to the group organizer relative to the total seats purchased, expressed as a decimal (e.g., 0.1000 = 1 free seat per 10 paid). Drives revenue accounting and yield calculations.']
  free_seats_allocated varchar [note: 'The actual number of complimentary (free) seats allocated to the group organizer based on the free_seat_ratio and confirmed group size. Impacts revenue accounting and load factor reporting.']
  group_fare_amount decimal(12,2) [note: 'Negotiated per-seat fare amount agreed for this group block, expressed in the currency specified by fare_currency_code. Represents the contracted price per passenger for revenue accounting and settlement.']
  group_fare_basis varchar [note: 'The fare basis code applicable to the group block, defining the fare rules, restrictions, and pricing conditions under which the group seats are sold (e.g., \'GEXCAP\', \'YGROUP\'). Aligns with IATA fare construction rules.']
  group_name varchar [note: 'The commercial or organizational name of the group for whom the seat block is reserved (e.g., \'Smith Wedding Party\', \'ACME Corp Sales Conference\'). Used for identification and communication with the group organizer.']
  group_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the group by purpose or segment. Used for revenue management analysis, pricing strategy, and reporting. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: leisure|corporate|incentive|military|religious|sports|charter — promote to reference product]']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The timestamp when this group block record was most recently updated in the inventory system. Used for change detection, ETL delta processing, and audit compliance.']
  minimum_group_size varchar [note: 'The minimum number of passengers required to qualify for group fare pricing on this block, as defined by the applicable fare rules. Typically 10 passengers per IATA group fare conventions.']
  names_due_date date [note: 'The date by which the group organizer must submit individual passenger names (Pax names) for all seats in the confirmed block. Required for check-in, security compliance, and advance passenger information (API) submission.']
  operating_carrier_code varchar [note: 'Two-character IATA airline designator of the carrier actually operating the flight (relevant for codeshare blocks where the operating carrier may differ from the marketing carrier). Required for interline settlement and ACARS reporting.']
  option_date date [note: 'The deadline date by which the group organizer must confirm the number of seats and provide passenger names. If not confirmed by this date, the block may be automatically released back to inventory. Critical for inventory management and revenue optimization.']
  overbooking_eligible boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this group block is subject to overbooking controls. True = the block counts against overbooking limits; False = the block is protected from overbooking displacement (e.g., guaranteed allocation). Feeds revenue management overbooking models.']
  reference varchar [note: 'Externally-known alphanumeric reference code assigned to this group block by the Passenger Service System (PSS) or reservations team. Used by travel agents and group coordinators to identify and manage the block.']
  remarks varchar [note: 'Free-text remarks or special instructions associated with this group block, entered by the reservations agent or group coordinator. May include special service requests (SSR), handling notes, or commercial conditions.']
  sales_channel varchar [note: 'The distribution channel through which the group block was requested and booked. \'direct\' = airline reservations team; \'gds\' = Global Distribution System (GDS) such as Amadeus or Sabre; \'online_travel_agency\' = OTA; \'corporate\' = corporate travel desk; \'charter\' = charter operator.']
  seats_blocked varchar [note: 'Current number of seats held (blocked) in inventory for this group. Reduces available seat count for individual sale. May differ from seats_requested if the airline partially approved the block.']
  seats_confirmed varchar [note: 'Number of seats within the block that have been formally confirmed by the group organizer. May be less than seats_blocked during the tentative phase. Drives revenue recognition and ticketing obligations.']
  seats_released varchar [note: 'Number of seats that have been released back to general inventory from this group block, either due to partial cancellation, reduction, or expiry of the option. Supports inventory reconciliation.']
  seats_requested varchar [note: 'Total number of seats originally requested by the group organizer at the time of the initial group block request. Baseline for tracking changes to the block size.']
  seats_ticketed varchar [note: 'Number of seats within the confirmed block for which electronic tickets (e-tickets) have been issued. Used to track ticketing progress against the ticketing deadline.']
  ticketing_deadline date [note: 'The final date by which all confirmed seats in the group block must be ticketed (e-tickets issued). Failure to ticket by this date may result in automatic cancellation of unticketed seats and release to general inventory.']

  Note: 'Master entity representing a reserved seat block allocated to a group booking request on a specific flight-date and cabin. Captures the group name, number of seats blocked, option date (deadline for confirmation), ticketing deadline, applicable fare class, deposit status, and the linked PNR reference. Group blocks reduce available inventory for individual sale and must be tracked separately from regular fare class bucket availability.'
}

Table inventory.waitlist_entry {
  waitlist_entry_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate identifier for each waitlist entry record in the Altéa Inventory waitlist processing engine. Primary key for this data product.']
  campaign_response_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to marketing.campaign_response. Business justification: Passengers may waitlist after responding to a campaign offer when inventory is sold out. Airlines track campaign-driven demand including waitlisted requests for demand forecasting. Essential for measu']
  fare_class_bucket_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the specific fare class booking class bucket (e.g., Y, B, M, Q) for which availability was closed and the waitlist entry was created. Drives revenue management yield optimization.']
  fare_class_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to revenue.fare_class. Business justification: Waitlist entries specify the requested fare class (RBD) for clearance processing. Waitlist clearance algorithms prioritize by fare class yield, eligibility rules, and bucket authorization. Essential f']
  flight_inventory_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the specific flight inventory record (flight-date-cabin combination) for which this waitlist entry is held. Identifies the sellable inventory unit in Altéa Inventory.']
  group_block_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the group booking record when the waitlist entry originates from a group overflow scenario (waitlist_reason = GROUP_OVERFLOW). Links the individual waitlist entry back to the parent group contract for group management reporting.']
  pax_profile_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the individual passenger profile associated with this waitlist entry. A single PNR may contain multiple passengers, each generating a separate waitlist entry.']
  pnr_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the Passenger Name Record (PNR) booking that is placed on the waitlist. Links the waitlist entry to the originating reservation in the Passenger Service System (PSS).']
  altea_waitlist_sequence varchar [note: 'Native waitlist sequence number assigned by the Amadeus Altéa Inventory waitlist processing engine. Serves as the business identifier for this waitlist entry within the Altéa system, used for reconciliation between the lakehouse Silver layer and the source PSS.']
  booking_class varchar [note: 'Single-letter IATA booking class (fare class) code within the requested cabin for which the waitlist entry was created (e.g., Y, B, M, H, Q, K, L, V, W, S, N, G). Directly maps to a revenue management fare class bucket in Altéa Inventory.']
  cabin_code varchar [note: 'IATA single-character cabin designator for the cabin class requested by the waitlisted passenger. F: First Class; C: Business Class; W: Premium Economy; Y: Economy. Determines the inventory pool from which clearance will be attempted.']
  channel_code varchar [note: 'Distribution channel through which the waitlist entry was created. GDS: Global Distribution System (e.g., Amadeus, Sabre, Travelport); NDC: IATA New Distribution Capability channel; DIRECT_WEB: airline direct website; MOBILE: airline mobile application; AIRPORT: airport check-in or gate agent; CALL_CENTER: reservations call center; INTERLINE: interline partner booking. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: GDS|NDC|DIRECT_WEB|MOBILE|AIRPORT|CALL_CENTER|INTERLINE — promote to reference product]']
  clearance_booking_class varchar [note: 'The actual booking class in which the waitlist entry was cleared and confirmed, which may differ from the originally requested booking class if the revenue management system cleared the passenger into a higher-availability bucket. Null until clearance occurs.']
  clearance_method varchar [note: 'Method by which the waitlist entry was cleared. AUTOMATIC: Altéa Inventory engine cleared automatically when bucket availability opened; MANUAL: airport or reservations agent manually cleared the entry; UPGRADE_PROCESSING: cleared as part of an upgrade processing run; IROP_REACCOMMODATION: cleared during Irregular Operations (IROP) reaccommodation workflow.']
  clearance_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time (UTC) when the waitlist entry was successfully cleared and the seat confirmed. Null if the entry has not yet been cleared. Used to measure waitlist clearance latency and revenue recovery timing. Format: yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time (UTC) when the waitlist entry was first created in the Altéa Inventory system. Serves as the business event timestamp for queue ordering and audit purposes. Format: yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX.']
  destination_airport_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter IATA airport code for the arrival airport of the waitlisted flight segment (e.g., LAX, CDG, SIN). Used for route-level waitlist analysis and GDS availability messaging.']
  expiry_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time (UTC) at which the waitlist entry will automatically expire if not cleared, typically aligned with the flight departure time or a configurable pre-departure deadline set by revenue management policy. Format: yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX.']
  fare_basis_code varchar [note: 'IATA fare basis code associated with the waitlisted booking (e.g., YLOWUS, BAPUS21). Encodes the fare family, booking class, and applicable restrictions. Used by revenue management to validate clearance eligibility and by pricing to re-price upon confirmation.']
  ffp_member_number varchar [note: 'Frequent Flyer Program (FFP) membership number of the waitlisted passenger. Used to apply loyalty-based priority rules and to credit miles upon clearance and travel. Classified as confidential PII as it is a unique personal identifier.']
  ffp_tier varchar [note: 'Frequent Flyer Program (FFP) membership tier of the waitlisted passenger at the time of waitlist entry creation (e.g., NONE, SILVER, GOLD, PLATINUM, ELITE). Higher tiers receive elevated priority rank in the waitlist queue per revenue management policy.']
  flight_date date [note: 'Scheduled local departure date of the flight for which the waitlist entry is held. Format: yyyy-MM-dd. Used for inventory snapshot reporting and waitlist aging analysis.']
  flight_number varchar [note: 'IATA flight number (carrier designator + numeric suffix, e.g., AA101) for the flight on which the waitlist entry is held. Denormalized from the flight inventory record for operational reporting and GDS availability messaging.']
  gds_queue_number varchar [note: 'Queue number assigned by the Global Distribution System (GDS) for this waitlist entry, used to route clearance notifications back to the originating travel agent or booking system. Null for direct-channel bookings.']
  is_codeshare boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the waitlist entry is associated with a codeshare flight operation where the marketing carrier differs from the operating carrier. True: codeshare booking; False: own-metal operation. Affects inventory allotment rules and clearance authority.']
  marketing_carrier_code varchar [note: 'Two-character IATA airline designator of the carrier under whose flight number the ticket was sold (marketing carrier). For codeshare flights, this may differ from the operating carrier. Determines which inventory pool and allotment rules apply.']
  notification_sent boolean [note: 'Indicates whether a clearance or status-change notification has been dispatched to the passenger or booking agent following a waitlist status transition. True: notification sent; False: notification pending or not applicable. Supports DOT consumer notification compliance.']
  notification_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time (UTC) when the clearance or status-change notification was dispatched to the passenger or booking agent. Null if no notification has been sent. Used for DOT consumer protection compliance reporting. Format: yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX.']
  operating_carrier_code varchar [note: 'Two-character IATA airline designator of the carrier actually operating the flight (e.g., AA, BA, EK). Relevant for codeshare flights where the marketing carrier differs from the operating carrier.']
  origin_airport_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter IATA airport code for the departure airport of the waitlisted flight segment (e.g., JFK, LHR, DXB). Used for route-level waitlist analysis and GDS availability messaging.']
  priority_rank varchar [note: 'Numeric rank of this waitlist entry within the queue for the same flight, cabin, and fare class bucket. Lower values indicate higher priority for clearance. Determined by Altéa Inventory based on booking time, fare paid, and frequent flyer tier.']
  revenue_management_override boolean [note: 'Indicates whether a revenue management analyst manually overrode the standard Altéa Inventory waitlist clearance rules for this entry (e.g., to prioritise a high-value passenger or honour a corporate contract). True: manual override applied; False: standard automated rules applied.']
  seat_count_requested varchar [note: 'Number of seats requested on the waitlist for this entry. For individual bookings this is typically 1; for group bookings this may be greater than 1. Determines the inventory units required for clearance.']
  source_system_code varchar [note: 'Code identifying the operational system of record that originated this waitlist entry. Primarily ALTEA_INV (Amadeus Altéa Inventory) but may include GDS source codes for externally originated waitlist requests. Used for data lineage and reconciliation in the Silver layer.']
  ssr_codes varchar [note: 'Comma-separated list of IATA Special Service Request (SSR) codes associated with the waitlisted passenger (e.g., WCHR, VGML, UMNR). These SSRs must be honoured upon clearance and seat assignment. Stored as a denormalized string for operational reference.']
  ticket_number varchar [note: '13-digit IATA electronic ticket (e-ticket) number associated with the waitlisted booking segment. Follows the IATA 13-digit format (3-digit airline code + 10-digit serial). May be null if the ticket has not yet been issued at the time of waitlist creation.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time (UTC) when the waitlist entry record was last modified in the source system, including status transitions, priority re-ranking, or clearance processing. Format: yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX.']
  upgrade_from_cabin_code varchar [note: 'For upgrade-request waitlist entries, the cabin code of the originally booked cabin from which the passenger is requesting an upgrade (e.g., Y for Economy). Null for sold-out or group overflow waitlist reasons. Used by upgrade processing logic in Altéa Inventory.']
  waitlist_reason varchar [note: 'Business reason why the passenger or booking was placed on the waitlist rather than confirmed. SOLD_OUT: requested fare class bucket was closed; UPGRADE_REQUEST: passenger requested cabin upgrade pending availability; GROUP_OVERFLOW: group booking exceeded available allotment; OVERBOOKING_CONTROL: revenue management overbooking limit reached; CODESHARE_ALLOTMENT: codeshare partner inventory allocation exhausted.']
  waitlist_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the waitlist entry as managed by the Altéa Inventory waitlist processing engine.
