OPEN: actively queued awaiting availability; CLEARED: bucket opened and seat confirmed; CANCELLED: passenger or agent cancelled the waitlist request; EXPIRED: flight departed or deadline passed without clearance; REJECTED: system or revenue management rule prevented clearance.']

  Note: 'Transactional record of a passenger or booking placed on the waitlist for a specific fare class bucket when availability is closed. Captures the PNR reference, requested cabin and fare class, waitlist priority rank, waitlist reason (sold-out, upgrade request, group overflow), creation timestamp, and clearance status. Managed by Altéa Inventory\'s waitlist processing engine and cleared automatically when bucket availability opens.'
}

Table inventory.upgrade_inventory {
  upgrade_inventory_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate identifier for the upgrade inventory record. Primary key for the upgrade_inventory data product within the Amadeus Altéa Inventory system.']
  aircraft_type_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to fleet.aircraft_type. Business justification: Elite upgrade clearance algorithms determine upgrade seat pool size from aircraft type\'s cabin configuration (business class seat count, lie-flat availability). Automated upgrade processing at departu']
  cabin_class_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to inventory.cabin_class. Business justification: upgrade_inventory manages upgrades TO a target cabin class. cabin_class is the reference master for cabin definitions. This FK links upgrade inventory to the target cabin master. cabin_class_code is r']
  catering_order_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to procurement.catering_order. Business justification: Upgrade clearances change cabin meal requirements post-catering cutoff. Catering must adjust premium meal counts when upgrades clear. Operational link ensures service delivery to upgraded passengers a']
  flight_inventory_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to inventory.flight_inventory. Business justification: upgrade_inventory manages the pool of seats designated for upgrades on a specific flight-date. flight_inventory is the master inventory record for that flight-date. This FK establishes the natural par']
  flight_leg_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the specific flight instance (flight-date combination) for which this upgrade inventory pool is managed. Links to the flight operations domain.']
  auto_upgrade_enabled boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the system is configured to automatically process and clear eligible upgrade requests for this flight-cabin pool without manual agent intervention.']
  bid_upgrade_max_price decimal(10,2) [note: 'Maximum monetary bid amount cap for a paid bid-upgrade auction seat in this cabin on this flight. Prevents outlier bids and anchors the auction ceiling for revenue management.']
  bid_upgrade_min_price decimal(10,2) [note: 'Minimum monetary bid amount accepted for a paid bid-upgrade auction seat in this cabin on this flight. Expressed in the operating currency. Feeds ancillary revenue management.']
  bid_upgrade_seats varchar [note: 'Number of seats allocated to the paid bid-upgrade auction channel (e.g., Plusgrade or equivalent ancillary upgrade product). Feeds the ancillary services domain for revenue capture.']
  clearance_cutoff_hours varchar [note: 'Number of hours before scheduled departure at which upgrade clearance processing closes. After this threshold, no further upgrades are processed and remaining pool seats revert to general inventory.']
  clearance_window_hours varchar [note: 'Number of hours before scheduled departure at which the upgrade clearance processing window opens. Defines when the system begins auto-clearing eligible waitlisted upgrade requests.']
  codeshare_upgrade_seats varchar [note: 'Number of upgrade seats within the pool allocated to codeshare partner airline passengers. Governs interline and codeshare upgrade entitlements per bilateral agreements.']
  confirmed_upgrades varchar [note: 'Current count of upgrade seats that have been confirmed and assigned to passengers across all upgrade types. Used to compute remaining availability in real time.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this upgrade inventory record was first created in the system, typically at schedule publication or inventory initialization for the flight-date.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code in which bid upgrade prices are denominated (e.g., USD, EUR, GBP). Aligns with BSP settlement currency.']
  elite_upgrade_processing_order varchar [note: 'Primary sort criterion used when sequencing elite FFP upgrade candidates from the waitlist. TIER_FIRST=highest loyalty tier processed first; FARE_FIRST=highest paid fare class first; CHECKIN_FIRST=earliest check-in time first; MILES_FIRST=highest accrued miles balance first.']
  elite_upgrade_seats varchar [note: 'Number of seats within the upgrade pool reserved exclusively for complimentary upgrades to elite-tier Frequent Flyer Program (FFP) members. Interfaces with Oracle Loyalty Cloud for tier eligibility.']
  fare_class_eligibility varchar [note: 'Pipe-delimited list of booking class (fare class bucket) codes whose holders are eligible for upgrade processing from this pool (e.g., Y|B|M|H). Aligns with Amadeus Altéa fare class bucket configuration. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: promote to reference product for full fare class list]']
  inventory_snapshot_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent real-time availability snapshot for this upgrade inventory pool, as published to GDS and direct booking channels. Aligns with Amadeus Altéa real-time availability feeds.']
  inventory_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the upgrade inventory pool for this flight-cabin combination. OPEN=upgrades available for processing; CLOSED=no further upgrades accepted; WAITLIST=pool exhausted but waitlist active; SUSPENDED=temporarily halted by revenue management; EXPIRED=clearance window has passed.']
  last_clearance_run_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent automated upgrade clearance processing run for this flight-cabin pool. Used for operational monitoring and audit of clearance execution.']
  load_factor_at_snapshot decimal(5,2) [note: 'Cabin load factor (percentage of seats filled) in the target upgrade cabin at the time of the most recent inventory snapshot. Expressed as a percentage (0.00–100.00). Used by revenue management to calibrate upgrade pool sizing.']
  min_elite_tier_required varchar [note: 'Minimum Frequent Flyer Program (FFP) elite tier level a member must hold to be eligible for a complimentary upgrade from this pool. Tiers below this threshold are excluded from elite upgrade processing.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text operational notes entered by revenue management or inventory control staff regarding special handling instructions, exceptions, or context for this upgrade inventory pool.']
  operational_upgrade_seats varchar [note: 'Number of seats held for operational upgrades issued by the airline at check-in or gate (e.g., IROP recovery, oversell resolution, VIP handling). Managed by ground operations and airport staff.']
  overbooking_buffer_seats varchar [note: 'Number of additional upgrade seats held as an overbooking buffer above physical cabin capacity to account for expected no-shows and cancellations. Managed by revenue management yield optimization.']
  override_reason_code varchar [note: 'Standardized reason code explaining why a revenue management override was applied to this upgrade inventory pool. Applicable only when revenue_management_override is TRUE. IROP=Irregular Operations; VIP=VIP passenger handling; COMMERCIAL=commercial agreement; CAPACITY_CHANGE=aircraft swap or gauge change.']
  pool_closed_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp at which the upgrade inventory pool was closed, either by reaching the clearance cutoff or by manual revenue management action. Null if pool is still open.']
  pool_open_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp at which the upgrade inventory pool was opened and made available for upgrade request intake. Marks the start of the upgrade acceptance window.']
  priority_rule_code varchar [note: 'Code referencing the upgrade priority rule set applied to this inventory pool. Governs the sequencing logic for elite FFP tier, fare class paid, check-in time, and other tiebreaker criteria.']
  revenue_management_override boolean [note: 'Indicates whether a revenue management analyst has manually overridden the system-calculated upgrade seat allocation for this flight-cabin pool, superseding automated yield optimization rules.']
  source_cabin_class_code varchar [note: 'IATA cabin class code of the originating cabin from which passengers are being upgraded. Defines the upgrade direction (e.g., Y→C means Economy to Business). Complements cabin_class_code which is the target cabin.']
  total_upgrade_seats varchar [note: 'Total number of seats in the target cabin designated as available for upgrade processing across all upgrade types (elite FFP, bid, operational). This is the gross pool before allocation by upgrade type.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent modification to this upgrade inventory record, including seat count adjustments, status changes, or override actions.']
  waitlisted_upgrades varchar [note: 'Number of upgrade requests currently on the waitlist for this flight-cabin pool, pending seat availability or clearance processing.']

  Note: 'Master entity managing the pool of seats designated for complimentary and paid upgrade processing within a cabin on a specific flight-date. Captures the number of seats held for elite FFP upgrades, bid-upgrade auction seats, operational upgrade pool, upgrade priority rules, and the clearance window (hours before departure). Interfaces with the loyalty domain for elite tier upgrade prioritization and the ancillary domain for paid upgrade products.'
}

Table inventory.irop_reprotection {
  irop_reprotection_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate identifier for each IROP reprotection action record in the inventory system. Primary key for this transactional entity.']
  authorized_by_employee_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to workforce.employee. Business justification: Operations Control Center staff authorize IROP passenger reprotections and inventory ring-fencing. Required for operational accountability, regulatory compliance audit trails (EU261, DOT), and cost ce']
  cabin_class_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to inventory.cabin_class. Business justification: irop_reprotection records inventory reprotection actions targeting a specific cabin class during irregular operations. cabin_class is the reference master for cabin definitions. This FK links reprotec']
  catering_order_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to procurement.catering_order. Business justification: IROP events trigger catering order cancellations for disrupted flights and adjustments for recovery flights. Operations Control Center uses this link to manage catering changes during irregular operat']
  consumer_protection_case_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.consumer_protection_case. Business justification: IROP reprotection events that trigger passenger rights obligations (EU261 delay/cancellation compensation, DOT tarmac delay) must link to consumer protection cases for compensation tracking, regulator']
  cost_centre_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.cost_centre. Business justification: IROP recovery costs (passenger compensation, rebooking, hotel vouchers) are tracked by cost center for financial impact analysis and operational performance reporting. Airlines monitor IROP cost expos']
  fare_class_bucket_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to inventory.fare_class_bucket. Business justification: irop_reprotection records target a specific fare class bucket for reprotection. fare_class_bucket is the master entity for RBD buckets. This FK links reprotection records to the bucket master. fare_cl']
  flight_irop_event_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the parent IROP event (e.g., cancellation, diversion, significant delay) that triggered this reprotection action. Links to the IROP event master record.']
  primary_irop_scheduled_flight_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the original flight segment that was disrupted (cancelled, diverted, or significantly delayed), causing the need for passenger reprotection. Directly impacts inventory on the disrupted flight.']
  authorized_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time (UTC) when the Operations Control Center (OCC) formally authorized this reprotection action. Represents the principal business event time for this transaction.']
  codeshare_indicator boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the recovery flight operates under a codeshare agreement. When true, inventory ring-fencing must be coordinated with the operating carrier\'s inventory system, adding complexity to the reprotection process.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time (UTC) when this reprotection record was first created in the inventory system. Audit trail field for data lineage and compliance.']
  destination_airport_iata_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter IATA airport code for the arrival airport of the disrupted flight (e.g., LAX, CDG). Denormalized for IROP reporting and route-level disruption analysis.']
  disrupted_flight_date date [note: 'Scheduled departure date (local airport time) of the disrupted flight. Used for day-of-operations reporting and IROP timeline analysis.']
  disrupted_flight_number varchar [note: 'IATA flight number of the disrupted flight (e.g., AA1234). Denormalized for operational reporting and IROP dashboards without requiring a join to the flight segment table.']
  downgrade_compensation_applicable boolean [note: 'Indicates whether passengers in this reprotection block are entitled to downgrade compensation (e.g., fare difference refund or EMD issuance) because they are being accommodated in a lower cabin class than originally booked.']
  gds_availability_updated boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the real-time availability snapshot on the recovery flight has been updated and broadcast to GDS channels (e.g., Amadeus, Sabre, Travelport) to reflect the ring-fenced seats. Critical for preventing oversell on the recovery flight.']
  interline_reprotection_indicator boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this reprotection action involves rebooking passengers onto a flight operated by a different carrier under an interline agreement. Triggers BSP/ARC settlement and interline billing processes.']
  inventory_impact_acknowledged boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the Revenue Management system has acknowledged and processed the inventory reduction on the recovery flight caused by this reprotection action. Ensures yield optimization models reflect the ring-fenced seats.']
  irop_cause_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the root cause of the IROP event that triggered reprotection. Drives downstream analytics on disruption patterns and recovery cost attribution. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: CANCELLATION|DIVERSION|SIGNIFICANT_DELAY|EQUIPMENT_SWAP|ATC_GROUND_STOP|WEATHER|CREW_SHORTAGE|SECURITY|MEDICAL|MECHANICAL — promote to reference product]']
  last_updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time (UTC) when this reprotection record was last modified. Tracks status changes, seat count adjustments, and authorization updates for audit purposes.']
  occ_authorization_reference varchar [note: 'Reference to the Operations Control Center authorization record that approved this reprotection action. Ensures accountability and traceability of inventory ring-fencing decisions.']
  operating_carrier_code varchar [note: 'Two-letter IATA airline designator code of the carrier actually operating the recovery flight. May differ from the marketing carrier in codeshare scenarios. Required for interline reprotection coordination.']
  origin_airport_iata_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter IATA airport code for the departure airport of the disrupted flight (e.g., JFK, LHR). Denormalized for IROP reporting and passenger communication workflows.']
  overbooking_override_applied boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the OCC applied an overbooking override to accommodate reprotected passengers beyond the recovery flight\'s standard authorized overbooking limit. Requires elevated authorization and triggers denied boarding risk monitoring.']
  pax_displaced_count varchar [note: 'Total number of passengers displaced by the IROP event who are eligible for reprotection under this action. May exceed seats_ring_fenced if partial reprotection is applied.']
  pnr_count varchar [note: 'Number of distinct Passenger Name Records (PNRs) included in this reprotection action. Used to track the scope of the reprotection and reconcile against seats_ring_fenced.']
  priority_class varchar [note: 'Priority tier assigned to this reprotection block, determining the order in which displaced passengers are accommodated on the recovery flight. ELITE: frequent flyer elite members; CONNECTING: passengers with onward connections at risk; MEDICAL: passengers with medical needs or SSR codes; UNACCOMPANIED_MINOR: UM passengers; STANDARD: general passengers.']
  recovery_flight_date date [note: 'Scheduled departure date (local airport time) of the recovery flight. Used to determine inventory impact window and reprotection timeline.']
  recovery_flight_number varchar [note: 'IATA flight number of the recovery (reprotection) flight on which seats are ring-fenced (e.g., AA1456). Denormalized for operational dashboards and GDS availability messaging.']
  release_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time (UTC) when unused ring-fenced seats were released back to general inventory. Populated when reprotection_status transitions to RELEASED or EXPIRED. Null if all seats were utilized or reprotection is still active.']
  reprotection_notes varchar [note: 'Free-text operational notes entered by the OCC agent describing special circumstances, exceptions, or instructions related to this reprotection action (e.g., VIP handling, diplomatic passengers, group reprotection conditions).']
  reprotection_reference_number varchar [note: 'Externally-known alphanumeric reference number assigned by the Passenger Service System (PSS) to uniquely identify this reprotection action across operational systems and GDS channels. Used for cross-system traceability.']
  reprotection_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the reprotection action. PENDING: awaiting OCC authorization; ACTIVE: seats ring-fenced and available for displaced passengers; CONFIRMED: passengers booked onto recovery flight; RELEASED: unused seats returned to general inventory; EXPIRED: reprotection window passed without use; CANCELLED: reprotection voided.']
  reprotection_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the reprotection strategy applied. SAME_DAY: recovery on same calendar day; NEXT_DAY: recovery on following day; MULTI_LEG: recovery requires multiple connecting flights; INTERLINE: recovery on partner carrier; UPGRADE: passenger accommodated in higher cabin; DOWNGRADE: passenger accommodated in lower cabin with compensation.']
  reprotection_window_close_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time (UTC) when the reprotection inventory block expires. After this time, unused ring-fenced seats are released back to general inventory. Nullable if the window is open-ended until flight departure.']
  reprotection_window_open_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time (UTC) when the reprotection inventory block was activated and seats became available for displaced passenger booking. Marks the start of the reprotection window.']
  seats_released varchar [note: 'Number of ring-fenced seats that were released back to general inventory after the reprotection window closed or the action was cancelled. Equals seats_ring_fenced minus seats_utilized when fully resolved.']
  seats_ring_fenced varchar [note: 'Number of seats on the recovery flight that have been ring-fenced (blocked from general sale) for displaced passengers under this reprotection action. Directly reduces available inventory on the recovery flight.']
  seats_utilized varchar [note: 'Number of ring-fenced seats that were actually used by displaced passengers. Compared against seats_ring_fenced to measure reprotection efficiency and inventory recovery.']
  source_system_code varchar [note: 'Identifies the operational system of record that originated this reprotection record. ALTEA: Amadeus Altéa Inventory PSS; NETLINE: Lufthansa Systems NetLine/Ops; SABRE: Sabre AirVision; MANUAL: manually entered by OCC agent. Used for data lineage and reconciliation.']
  ssr_codes varchar [note: 'Comma-separated list of IATA four-letter Special Service Request (SSR) codes applicable to passengers in this reprotection block (e.g., WCHR, UMNR, MEDA). Ensures special handling requirements are preserved during reprotection.']

  Note: 'Transactional record of inventory reprotection actions taken during IROP (Irregular Operations) events such as flight cancellations, diversions, or significant delays. Captures the disrupted flight reference, the reprotection flight(s) offered, number of seats ring-fenced for displaced passengers, priority class (elite, connecting, medical), reprotection status, and the operations control center authorization. Directly impacts available inventory on recovery flights.'
}

Table inventory.season_inventory_plan {
  season_inventory_plan_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate identifier for the seasonal inventory plan record. Primary key for this entity in the Silver Layer lakehouse.']
  aircraft_type_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to fleet.aircraft_type. Business justification: Strategic network planning allocates aircraft types to seasonal routes based on fleet composition, range capability, and seat capacity. Season inventory plans drive schedule filings and slot coordinat']
  budget_plan_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.budget_plan. Business justification: Seasonal capacity plans (ASK, frequencies, aircraft deployment) are budgeted in financial planning cycles. Links planned inventory to revenue/cost budgets for variance analysis and board reporting. Co']
  campaign_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to marketing.campaign. Business justification: Seasonal marketing campaigns are planned against seasonal inventory plans (summer schedules, winter routes). Integrated commercial planning requires alignment between capacity deployment and marketing']
  fuel_contract_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to procurement.fuel_contract. Business justification: Seasonal flight programs determine fuel contract volumes and airport coverage requirements. Fuel procurement uses seasonal plans for contract negotiations, minimum offtake commitments, and supplier se']
  route_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the route master record for the origin-destination pair covered by this inventory plan. Enables route-level aggregation and network planning analysis.']
  schedule_season_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the flight schedule record from which this seasonal inventory plan was derived. Links the inventory plan to the underlying schedule planning entity (route, frequency, and timing) as defined in the schedule planning system.']
  supply_contract_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to procurement.supply_contract. Business justification: Seasonal capacity plans drive long-term catering and ground service contracts. Procurement negotiates seasonal supply contracts based on planned flight programs. Essential for contract volume commitme']
  approved_by varchar [note: 'Name or employee identifier of the Revenue Management or Inventory Control authority who formally approved this seasonal inventory plan for activation. Supports audit trail and governance requirements.']
  approved_date date [note: 'The date on which this seasonal inventory plan was formally approved by the authorized Revenue Management or Inventory Control authority. Represents the BUSINESS_EVENT_TIMESTAMP for the approval milestone.']
  authorized_capacity varchar [note: 'Total number of bookings authorized for acceptance on this flight for the season, incorporating the overbooking strategy applied to total_planned_capacity. This is the effective sellable inventory ceiling used by the Passenger Service System.']
  business_class_capacity varchar [note: 'Number of seats planned in the Business Class cabin (including premium business variants such as Clipper Class, Club World) for this seasonal inventory plan. Zero if no Business Class cabin exists.']
  codeshare_allocation_seats varchar [note: 'Number of seats within the total authorized capacity allocated to codeshare marketing partners for sale under their own flight numbers. Null or zero if codeshare_indicator is False.']
  codeshare_indicator boolean [note: 'Flag indicating whether this inventory plan includes codeshare seat allocations (True) or is a purely operating-carrier plan (False). When True, codeshare partner allocations are governed by this plan.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this seasonal inventory plan record was first created in the system, in ISO 8601 format (yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX). Serves as RECORD_AUDIT_CREATED for audit trail purposes.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'ISO 4217 three-letter currency code in which yield targets and revenue metrics for this inventory plan are denominated (e.g., \'USD\', \'EUR\', \'GBP\').']
  days_of_operation varchar [note: 'IATA SSIM-standard days-of-week string indicating which days this flight operates within the season (e.g., \'1234567\' = daily, \'135\' = Mon/Wed/Fri). Uses IATA day numbering: 1=Monday through 7=Sunday.']
  destination_airport_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter IATA airport code for the arrival station of the flight covered by this inventory plan (e.g., \'JFK\', \'CDG\'). Used to identify the route destination.']
  economy_class_capacity varchar [note: 'Number of seats planned in the Economy Class cabin for this seasonal inventory plan. Typically the largest cabin and primary driver of load factor and yield management decisions.']
  first_class_capacity varchar [note: 'Number of seats planned in the First Class cabin for this seasonal inventory plan. Zero if the aircraft configuration does not include a First Class cabin.']
  flight_number varchar [note: 'IATA-format operating flight number (carrier designator + numeric suffix, e.g., \'AA101\') for which this seasonal inventory plan is defined. Identifies the specific flight or flight series covered by this plan.']
  gds_availability_indicator boolean [note: 'Flag indicating whether this flight\'s inventory is made available for sale through Global Distribution Systems (GDS) such as Amadeus, Sabre, and Travelport (True) or is restricted to direct channels only (False).']
  iata_season_code varchar [note: 'IATA-standard season designator indicating the schedule period this plan covers. Format: S=Summer, W=Winter followed by 2-digit year (e.g., \'S24\' for Summer 2024, \'W24\' for Winter 2024). Aligns with IATA SSIM season definitions.']
  initial_bucket_authorization varchar [note: 'Serialized representation of the initial booking class (fare bucket) authorization levels at season open, as configured in Amadeus Altéa Inventory (e.g., \'Y:180,B:120,M:90,Q:60,V:30\'). Defines the starting inventory allocation across RBD (Reservation Booking Designator) buckets before dynamic RM optimization. Confidential as it encodes pricing strategy. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: promote bucket detail to a child product season_inventory_bucket_plan — promote to reference product]']
  interline_indicator boolean [note: 'Flag indicating whether this flight and its inventory plan participates in interline ticketing agreements with other carriers (True) or is restricted to own-carrier ticketing only (False).']
  last_updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent modification to this seasonal inventory plan record, in ISO 8601 format (yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX). Serves as RECORD_AUDIT_UPDATED for change tracking and data lineage.']
  load_factor_target decimal(5,2) [note: 'The target load factor (percentage of seats filled) set by Revenue Management for this flight during the season, expressed as a percentage (e.g., 85.00 = 85%). Used as the baseline KPI against which actual load factor performance is measured. Confidential as it reflects commercial strategy.']
  marketing_carrier_code varchar [note: 'Two-character IATA airline designator of the carrier marketing and selling seats on this flight. May differ from operating_carrier_code in codeshare arrangements. Null if same as operating carrier.']
  minimum_connecting_time_minutes varchar [note: 'Minimum Connecting Time (MCT) in minutes applicable at the origin airport for this flight, as published for the season. Used by GDS and booking systems to validate connection feasibility for itinerary construction.']
  operating_carrier_code varchar [note: 'Two-character IATA airline designator of the carrier actually operating the flight (e.g., \'AA\', \'BA\', \'LH\'). Distinguishes the operating carrier from marketing carriers in codeshare arrangements.']
  origin_airport_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter IATA airport code for the departure station of the flight covered by this inventory plan (e.g., \'LHR\', \'JFK\'). Used to identify the route origin.']
  overbooking_rate decimal(5,2) [note: 'The planned overbooking percentage applied to total capacity for this seasonal inventory plan, expressed as a percentage above 100% (e.g., 5.00 means 5% overbooking above physical capacity). Set by Revenue Management based on historical no-show and cancellation rates. Confidential as it reflects pricing strategy.']
  plan_code varchar [note: 'Externally-known alphanumeric business identifier for this seasonal inventory plan, used in Amadeus Altéa Inventory and cross-system references (e.g., \'SIP-W24-LHR-JFK-Y\'). Serves as the BUSINESS_IDENTIFIER for this master resource.']
  plan_name varchar [note: 'Human-readable descriptive name for the seasonal inventory plan (e.g., \'Winter 2024 LHR-JFK Economy Inventory Plan\'). Serves as the IDENTITY_LABEL for this master resource.']
  plan_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the seasonal inventory plan. \'draft\' = under construction; \'approved\' = authorized for use; \'active\' = currently governing live inventory; \'suspended\' = temporarily halted; \'closed\' = season ended; \'superseded\' = replaced by a revised plan. Serves as LIFECYCLE_STATUS.']
  plan_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the inventory plan by its operational purpose. \'baseline\' = initial seasonal plan; \'revised\' = updated mid-season plan; \'codeshare\' = plan governing codeshare inventory allocation; \'charter\' = charter flight inventory; \'adhoc\' = ad-hoc or supplemental plan. Serves as CLASSIFICATION_OR_TYPE.']
  planned_arrival_time timestamp [note: 'Planned scheduled arrival time in local time at the destination airport, in HHMM format (e.g., \'1145\' for 11:45). Represents the published schedule time for the season as filed in IATA SSIM.']
  planned_departure_time timestamp [note: 'Planned scheduled departure time in local time at the origin airport, in HHMM format (e.g., \'0830\' for 08:30). Represents the published schedule time for the season as filed in IATA SSIM.']
  premium_economy_capacity varchar [note: 'Number of seats planned in the Premium Economy cabin for this seasonal inventory plan. Zero if no Premium Economy cabin exists on the planned aircraft type.']
  revenue_management_system_code varchar [note: 'The identifier assigned to this inventory plan within the Sabre AirVision Revenue Management system. Used for cross-system traceability between the inventory plan in Altéa and the corresponding RM optimization model in Sabre AirVision.']
  season_end_date date [note: 'The last date of the IATA schedule season for which this inventory plan is effective. Corresponds to the IATA SSIM-defined season end date. Serves as EFFECTIVE_UNTIL.']
  season_start_date date [note: 'The first date of the IATA schedule season for which this inventory plan is effective. Corresponds to the IATA SSIM-defined season start date (typically last Sunday of March for Summer, last Sunday of October for Winter). Serves as EFFECTIVE_FROM.']
  total_planned_capacity varchar [note: 'Total number of sellable seats planned for this flight for the season, across all cabins. Represents the principal quantitative capacity fact (MEASUREMENT_OR_VALUE) for this inventory plan. Derived from aircraft configuration but stored here as the authoritative planned figure.']
  yield_target decimal(10,4) [note: 'The target yield (revenue per Revenue Passenger Kilometer — RPK) set by Revenue Management for this flight for the season, expressed in the operating currency per RPK. Used as the baseline revenue efficiency target. Confidential as it encodes pricing strategy.']

  Note: 'Master entity representing the pre-departure seasonal inventory plan for a flight or route, defining the planned capacity deployment, cabin mix, initial bucket authorization levels, and overbooking strategy for an IATA season (summer/winter schedule period). Created during schedule planning and used as the baseline against which actual inventory performance is measured. Aligns with IATA SSIM season definitions.'
}

Table inventory.lease_cost_allocation {
  lease_cost_allocation_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate identifier for the lease cost allocation record']
  flight_inventory_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to the flight inventory record receiving the allocated lease cost']
  gl_journal_entry_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the SAP S/4HANA general ledger journal entry document generated from this allocation record. Supports audit trail and reconciliation between cost allocation system and financial ledger.']
  lease_contract_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to the lease contract from which costs are being allocated']
  allocation_method varchar [note: 'Methodology used to allocate lease costs to this flight. FLIGHT_HOURS: proportional to block hours; CYCLE_COUNT: proportional to takeoff/landing cycles; REVENUE_SHARE: proportional to revenue generated; SEAT_MILES: proportional to available seat miles; MANUAL: finance-approved override allocation.']
  allocation_percentage decimal(7,4) [note: 'Percentage of the lease contract\'s periodic cost allocated to this specific flight inventory record, expressed as a decimal (e.g., 0.0125 = 1.25%). Sum of allocation percentages across all flights for a given lease-period should equal 1.0.']
  allocation_period varchar [note: 'Accounting period (YYYY-MM format) for which this lease cost allocation applies. Supports monthly close processes and period-over-period cost variance analysis.']
  allocation_status varchar [note: 'Lifecycle status of the allocation record. DRAFT: preliminary calculation; APPROVED: finance-reviewed and approved; POSTED: GL journal entries generated; REVERSED: allocation reversed due to correction or schedule change.']
  amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Monetary amount of lease expense allocated to this flight inventory record for the effective period, denominated in the lease contract currency. Calculated as periodic_lease_payment × allocation_percentage, adjusted for any variable payment components.']
  approved_by varchar [note: 'User ID or employee identifier of the finance analyst or manager who approved this allocation for posting to the general ledger.']
  approved_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this allocation record was approved for GL posting.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'System timestamp when this allocation record was initially created by the lease cost allocation batch process or manual entry.']
  effective_date date [note: 'Date from which this lease cost allocation is effective. Typically aligns with the flight departure date or the beginning of the accounting period for which costs are being allocated.']
  flight_hours_attributed decimal(8,2) [note: 'Block hours (departure gate to arrival gate) attributed to this flight for the purpose of lease cost allocation. Used in utilization-based allocation methods where lease cost is distributed proportionally to flight hours operated.']

  Note: 'This association product represents the allocation of aircraft lease contract costs to specific flight inventory records for IFRS 16 compliance, route profitability analysis, and unit cost accounting. Each record links one lease contract to one flight-date inventory record, capturing the proportion of lease expense, flight hours attributed, allocation methodology, and effective period. This enables accurate P&L attribution at the flight level and supports revenue management yield optimization decisions.. Existence Justification: Airlines operate under IFRS 16 lease accounting requirements that mandate allocation of aircraft lease costs to individual flight operations for accurate unit cost accounting and route profitability analysis. A single lease contract (covering an aircraft, engine, or APU) generates costs that must be allocated across hundreds or thousands of flight inventory records over the lease term, while a single flight may consume resources from multiple leased assets (primary aircraft lease, spare engine lease, APU lease). The allocation relationship is actively managed by finance teams who calculate, approve, and post allocation entries monthly, with specific allocation percentages, flight hours, cost amounts, and methodology tracked for each lease-flight pairing.'
}

Table finance.general_ledger {
  general_ledger_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate identifier for each general ledger journal entry line item record in the SAP S/4HANA FI-GL module. Serves as the immutable primary key for the silver layer lakehouse table.']
  company_code_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.company_code. Business justification: Each GL entry belongs to ONE company code. The general_ledger table has company_code (STRING) which should be replaced with a FK to the company_code master. This is a mandatory dimension in SAP FI-GL.']
  cost_centre_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.cost_centre. Business justification: Each GL entry can be assigned to ONE cost center for CO allocation. The general_ledger table has cost_centre (STRING) which should be replaced with a FK to the cost_centre master.']
  accounting_principle varchar [note: 'The accounting standard under which this posting is recorded. Supports dual-reporting requirements for airlines operating across multiple jurisdictions. IFRS16 specifically tracks right-of-use asset and lease liability postings for aircraft operating leases.']
  amount_company_currency decimal(18,2) [note: 'The posting amount converted to the company code\'s local currency (BSEG-DMBTR). Used for statutory financial reporting, local GAAP compliance, and period-end balance sheet preparation.']
  amount_group_currency decimal(18,2) [note: 'The posting amount translated to the group reporting currency (BSEG-KURSF-based calculation, stored as parallel ledger amount). Supports consolidated financial statements and group-level CASK/RASK reporting across all airline entities.']
  amount_transaction_currency decimal(18,2) [note: 'The gross posting amount in the original transaction currency (BSEG-WRBTR). Represents the raw financial value before currency conversion. Used for interline billing settlements, BSP reconciliation, and multi-currency financial reporting.']
  assignment_number varchar [note: 'Free-form assignment field used for sorting and grouping line items in account statements (BSEG-ZUONR). Typically populated with flight number, route code, or cost object reference to facilitate operational cost reconciliation.']
  company_currency varchar [note: 'ISO 4217 three-letter currency code of the company code\'s local (functional) currency (T001-WAERS). The currency in which statutory accounts are prepared for the legal entity.']
  cost_element varchar [note: 'SAP CO cost element corresponding to the GL account for cost and revenue postings (BSEG-KOSTL-linked KSTAR). Bridges FI and CO modules, enabling detailed cost analysis for fuel uplift, crew costs, MRO expenses, and lease payments.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when the accounting document was first created in SAP (BKPF-CPUDT + BKPF-CPUTM combined). Provides the immutable audit creation timestamp for the journal entry, distinct from the business posting_date.']
  debit_credit_indicator varchar [note: 'SAP indicator specifying whether the line item is a debit (S = Soll) or credit (H = Haben) posting (BSEG-SHKZG). Fundamental to double-entry bookkeeping and ensures the document balances to zero.']
  document_date date [note: 'The date of the original source document (invoice, receipt, contract) that triggered the accounting entry (BKPF-BLDAT). May differ from posting_date for accruals, prepayments, or late-received invoices.']
  document_number varchar [note: 'SAP FI accounting document number uniquely identifying the journal entry header within a company code and fiscal year. Corresponds to BKPF-BELNR in SAP. All line items sharing the same document number belong to the same posting event.']
  document_type varchar [note: 'SAP document type code classifying the nature of the accounting entry (BKPF-BLART). Controls number ranges and account types. Common airline values include SA (GL account document), KR (vendor invoice), DR (customer invoice), ZP (payment), AA (asset posting), AB (accounting document). [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: SA|KR|DR|ZP|AA|AB|RV|WA — promote to reference product]']
  exchange_rate decimal(12,6) [note: 'The foreign currency exchange rate applied to convert the transaction currency amount to the company code currency at the time of posting (BKPF-KURSF). Critical for IFRS IAS 21 compliance and interline settlement reconciliation.']
  fiscal_period varchar [note: 'Accounting period (1–16) within the fiscal year to which the posting belongs (BKPF-MONAT in SAP). Periods 13–16 are special closing periods used for year-end adjustments, accruals, and audit corrections.']
  fiscal_year varchar [note: 'The fiscal year to which the accounting document belongs (BKPF-GJAHR in SAP). Used for period-end close, annual financial reporting, and year-over-year variance analysis.']
  functional_area varchar [note: 'SAP functional area classifying the business function of the posting for cost-of-sales accounting (BSEG-FKBER). Enables income statement presentation by function (e.g., Flight Operations, Sales & Distribution, Administration) per IFRS IAS 1.']
  gl_account_code varchar [note: 'The GL account number from the chart of accounts to which this line item is posted (BSEG-HKONT / SKA1-SAKNR). Represents the financial classification of the transaction (e.g., fuel expense, lease liability, revenue, accrued liabilities). Core to CASK reporting and financial consolidation.']
  gl_account_name varchar [note: 'Human-readable description of the GL account (SKA1-TXT50). Denormalised from the chart of accounts for reporting convenience and audit trail completeness. Examples: \'Jet Fuel Expense\', \'Aircraft Lease Liability (IFRS 16)\', \'Interline Receivable\'.']
  header_text varchar [note: 'Free-text description at the accounting document header level (BKPF-BKTXT). Provides overall context for the journal entry, shared across all line items of the same document. Used for audit trail and period-end close documentation.']
  intercompany_indicator boolean [note: 'Flag indicating whether this posting is an intercompany transaction between two legal entities within the airline group (BKPF-BVTYP populated). True = intercompany posting requiring elimination in group consolidation. Critical for IFRS 10 consolidation.']
  internal_order varchar [note: 'SAP CO internal order number used to collect costs for specific projects, campaigns, or temporary cost objects (BSEG-AUFNR). Used in aviation for aircraft modification projects, route launch campaigns, and MRO work orders.']
  last_changed_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when the accounting document was last modified in SAP. Tracks changes to parked documents, header text updates, or clearing events. Supports change audit trail requirements under SOX and IFRS.']
  ledger_code varchar [note: 'SAP parallel ledger identifier (BKPF-RLDNR) supporting dual IFRS and local GAAP reporting. Common values: \'0L\' = leading ledger (IFRS), \'N1\' = local GAAP ledger. Enables simultaneous reporting under multiple accounting standards required for multinational airline operations.']
  line_item_number varchar [note: 'Sequential line item number within the accounting document (BSEG-BUZEI in SAP). Identifies the specific debit or credit posting line within the journal entry. Combined with document_number and company_code forms the natural key of a GL line item.']
  posting_date date [note: 'The date on which the accounting document is posted to the general ledger (BKPF-BUDAT). Determines the fiscal period assignment and is the primary date used for financial reporting and period-end close.']
  posting_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the accounting document. \'posted\' = active GL entry; \'reversed\' = entry has been reversed by a reversal document; \'parked\' = document saved but not yet posted; \'held\' = incomplete document; \'cleared\' = open item has been cleared against a payment or offsetting entry.']
  posting_text varchar [note: 'Free-text description of the individual line item posting (BSEG-SGTXT). Provides narrative context for the accounting entry, e.g., \'Jet fuel uplift JFK-LHR 15-Jan\', \'IFRS 16 lease liability accrual Q1\', \'Interline settlement IATA BSP Jan\'.']
  posting_user varchar [note: 'SAP user ID of the person or system process that created the accounting document (BKPF-USNAM). Essential for SOX audit trail, segregation of duties controls, and financial fraud detection. May be a batch job ID for automated postings.']
  profit_centre varchar [note: 'SAP Controlling profit centre representing a business unit responsible for its own profitability (BSEG-PRCTR / CEPC-PRCTR). Used for route-level, hub-level, or business-segment P&L reporting. Supports RASK and yield analysis by profit centre.']
  reference_document_number varchar [note: 'External reference number from the source document (BKPF-XBLNR), such as a vendor invoice number, AWB (Air Waybill) number, PNR, interline billing statement number, or BSP settlement reference. Enables traceability from the GL back to the originating business transaction.']
  reversal_document_number varchar [note: 'The document number of the corresponding reversal or reversed document (BKPF-STBLG). Populated when reversal_indicator is true, enabling bidirectional traceability between original and reversal entries for audit and compliance purposes.']
  reversal_indicator boolean [note: 'Flag indicating whether this document is a reversal of a previously posted document (BKPF-STBLG is populated). True = this is a reversal document. Supports audit trail integrity and period-end close accuracy.']
  tax_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'The tax amount (VAT/GST/PFC) associated with this line item in the transaction currency (BSEG-WMWST). Supports tax reconciliation, VAT return preparation, and Passenger Facility Charge (PFC) reporting to airport authorities.']
  tax_code varchar [note: 'SAP tax code determining the applicable tax rate and tax account for the posting (BSEG-MWSKZ). Relevant for VAT/GST on airline ancillary services, ground handling charges, and MRO procurement. Supports tax reporting to national revenue authorities.']
  trading_partner_company_code varchar [note: 'Company code of the intercompany trading partner for intercompany postings (BSEG-VBUND). Populated when intercompany_indicator is true. Used for automated intercompany elimination during group financial consolidation.']
  transaction_currency varchar [note: 'ISO 4217 three-letter currency code of the original transaction (BSEG-WAERS). Identifies the currency in which the business event was denominated, e.g., USD for US routes, EUR for European operations, GBP for UK operations.']
  wbs_element varchar [note: 'SAP Project System WBS element for project-based cost assignment (BSEG-PS_PSP_PNR). Used for capital expenditure projects such as fleet acquisition, terminal construction, and major MRO overhauls. Supports IFRS IAS 16 capitalisation tracking.']

  Note: 'SAP S/4HANA FI-GL SSOT for all accounting entries across the airlines chart of accounts. Each record represents a journal entry document (posting) with header attributes (document number, posting date, fiscal period, document type, company code, reference, reversal indicator, posting user) and line items (debit/credit amounts, GL account, cost centre, profit centre, functional area, cost element, currency, exchange rate). Supports IFRS and local GAAP dual reporting, period-end close, intercompany postings, accruals, provisions, and financial consolidation across all legal entities. Serves as the immutable audit trail for regulatory financial reporting and the single authoritative source for all financial postings.'
}

Table finance.journal_entry {
  journal_entry_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate identifier for each journal entry record in the general ledger. Primary key for the journal_entry data product in the finance domain silver layer.']
  company_code_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.company_code. Business justification: Each journal entry belongs to ONE company code (legal entity). The journal_entry table has company_code (STRING) which should be replaced with a FK to the company_code master. Company code is a mandat']
  cost_centre_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.cost_centre. Business justification: Each journal entry can be assigned to ONE cost center for CO allocation. The journal_entry table has cost_center (STRING) which should be replaced with a FK to the cost_centre master. This is a standa']
  general_ledger_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.general_ledger. Business justification: Each journal entry posts to ONE GL account. The journal_entry table has gl_account_code (STRING) which should be replaced with a FK to the general_ledger master. This is a standard SAP FI relationship']
  primary_journal_customer_employee_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the customer master record for accounts receivable postings (e.g., interline billing receivables, corporate travel account receivables, cargo customer invoices). Populated for document types involving customer transactions.']
  vendor_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the vendor/supplier master record for accounts payable postings (e.g., fuel suppliers, MRO providers, catering vendors, airport authorities, ACMI lessors). Populated for document types involving vendor transactions.']
  account_type varchar [note: 'SAP FI account type indicating the subledger category for this posting: A=Asset, D=Customer (Debtor/Accounts Receivable), K=Vendor (Creditor/Accounts Payable), M=Material, S=General Ledger. Determines which subledger is updated.']
  amount_document_currency decimal(18,2) [note: 'The transaction amount in the original document currency (transaction currency). May differ from amount_local_currency when the source document is in a foreign currency, such as interline billing settlements in USD or EUR.']
  amount_local_currency decimal(18,2) [note: 'The gross transaction amount in the company code\'s local (functional) currency. This is the primary monetary value for statutory reporting and forms part of the MONETARY_TRIPLET for this transaction.']
  assignment_number varchar [note: 'SAP FI assignment field used for sorting and matching open items during clearing (e.g., flight number, route code, cost object reference). Supports automated payment matching and interline settlement reconciliation.']
  business_area varchar [note: 'SAP FI business area code representing an organizational unit for which an internal balance sheet and P&L can be produced (e.g., passenger operations, cargo, MRO, loyalty). Supports segment reporting under IFRS 8.']
  clearing_date date [note: 'The date on which this open item was cleared (matched against a payment or offsetting entry). Used for accounts receivable aging analysis, days sales outstanding (DSO) calculation, and cash flow reporting.']
  clearing_document_number varchar [note: 'The SAP FI document number of the clearing transaction that matched and closed this open item (e.g., payment document clearing an invoice, credit memo clearing a debit). Populated when posting_status is \'cleared\'.']
  debit_credit_indicator varchar [note: 'Indicates whether this journal entry line is a debit (D) or credit (C) posting. Fundamental to double-entry bookkeeping and ensures the general ledger remains in balance. Aligns with SAP FI posting key logic.']
  document_currency_code varchar [note: 'ISO 4217 three-letter currency code of the original document currency (e.g., USD, EUR, GBP). Critical for interline billing, BSP settlements, and multi-currency treasury operations.']
  document_date date [note: 'The date of the original source document (invoice date, payment date, contract date) that triggered this journal entry. May differ from posting_date when documents are processed with a lag.']
  document_number varchar [note: 'The externally visible SAP FI document number uniquely identifying this accounting document within the company code and fiscal year. Used as the primary business reference for audit trail and reconciliation.']
  document_type varchar [note: 'SAP FI document type code classifying the nature of the accounting document (e.g., SA=General Ledger, KR=Vendor Invoice, DR=Customer Invoice, ZP=Payment, AB=Accounting Document). Drives number range assignment and posting rules. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: SA|KR|DR|ZP|AB|RE|RV|KZ|DZ — promote to reference product]']
  entry_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The precise date and time when this journal entry record was created in the SAP S/4HANA system. Serves as the RECORD_AUDIT_CREATED timestamp for audit trail and data lineage purposes.']
  exchange_rate decimal(12,6) [note: 'The foreign currency exchange rate applied to translate the document currency amount to the local currency amount at the time of posting. Sourced from SAP exchange rate tables and aligned with treasury hedging policies.']
  fiscal_period varchar [note: 'The accounting period (1–16, including special periods 13–16 for year-end adjustments) within the fiscal year in which this journal entry is posted. Aligns with SAP S/4HANA posting period control.']
  fiscal_year varchar [note: 'The accounting fiscal year in which this journal entry is recorded, as defined by the airline\'s fiscal year variant in SAP S/4HANA. May differ from the calendar year for airlines with non-calendar fiscal years.']
  functional_area varchar [note: 'SAP CO functional area classifying the cost by business function (e.g., PROD=Production/Flight Operations, SELL=Sales, ADMN=Administration, MAINT=Maintenance). Supports cost-of-sales accounting and CASK component analysis.']
  header_text varchar [note: 'Free-text description at the document header level providing business context for the journal entry (e.g., \'Monthly fuel accrual JFK hub\', \'IFRS 16 lease liability Q3\', \'Interline BSP settlement October\'). Supports audit review and financial analysis.']
  internal_order_number varchar [note: 'SAP CO internal order number used to collect costs for specific projects or events (e.g., aircraft heavy maintenance C-check, airport terminal renovation, fleet acquisition project). Enables project-level cost tracking.']
  last_changed_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when this journal entry record was last modified in the source system. Supports incremental data loading, change data capture, and audit trail requirements.']
  line_item_text varchar [note: 'Free-text description at the individual line item level providing specific context for this debit or credit posting (e.g., \'Jet-A fuel uplift LHR 15-Oct\', \'ACMI wet lease payment\', \'Crew hotel deadhead expense\').']
  local_currency_code varchar [note: 'ISO 4217 three-letter currency code of the company code\'s local (functional) currency. Defines the base currency for statutory financial reporting for the legal entity.']
  posting_date date [note: 'The date on which the journal entry is posted to the general ledger and determines the fiscal period assignment. This is the authoritative accounting date for period-end close and financial reporting.']
  posting_key varchar [note: 'SAP FI two-digit posting key controlling the account type, debit/credit indicator, and field selection for this line item (e.g., 40=GL Debit, 50=GL Credit, 31=Vendor Credit, 01=Customer Debit). Determines the accounting treatment applied.']
  posting_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the journal entry in the general ledger workflow. \'Posted\' indicates a completed, immutable entry; \'parked\' is a draft pending approval; \'reversed\' indicates a correcting entry has been applied; \'cleared\' indicates the open item has been matched.']
  profit_center_code varchar [note: 'SAP CO profit center identifying the business segment (e.g., route, hub, cabin class, cargo) responsible for this posting. Supports profitability analysis by route, fleet type, and business unit.']
  reference_document_number varchar [note: 'External reference number from the originating source document (e.g., vendor invoice number, PNR, AWB number, interline billing statement number, lease contract reference). Enables cross-system reconciliation and audit trail linkage.']
  reversal_indicator boolean [note: 'Flag indicating whether this journal entry is a reversal document (True) or an original posting (False). Reversal entries are created to correct erroneous postings and maintain audit integrity in the general ledger.']
  tax_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'The tax component (VAT, GST, PFC, or other applicable taxes) included in or associated with this journal entry posting. Part of the MONETARY_TRIPLET adjustment component. Supports tax reporting and PFC (Passenger Facility Charge) reconciliation.']
  tax_code varchar [note: 'SAP tax code identifying the applicable tax type and rate for this posting (e.g., V1=Input VAT 19%, A1=Output VAT 19%). Drives automatic tax calculation and tax return preparation.']
  wbs_element varchar [note: 'SAP PS Work Breakdown Structure (WBS) element for project-based cost assignments, such as aircraft acquisition, fleet modification programs, or major infrastructure projects. Supports capital expenditure tracking under IFRS IAS 16.']

  Note: 'Individual accounting posting records in the general ledger representing debits and credits for every financial transaction across the airline. Captures document type, posting date, fiscal period, currency, exchange rate, reference document, reversal indicators, and posting user. Aligns with SAP FI document posting model and supports audit trail for regulatory financial reporting.'
}

Table finance.cost_centre {
  cost_centre_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate identifier for the cost centre record in the lakehouse silver layer. Primary key for the cost_centre data product.']
  company_code_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.company_code. Business justification: Each cost center belongs to ONE company code. The cost_centre table has company_code (STRING) which should be replaced with a FK to the company_code master. Cost centers are assigned to company codes ']
  employee_id varchar [note: 'Employee identifier of the manager accountable for this cost centre\'s budget, expenditure, and performance. Used for budget approval workflows, variance reporting escalation, and management accountability in SAP CO. Links to HR employee master.']
  activity_type_code varchar [note: 'Primary SAP CO activity type associated with this cost centre, defining the unit of output for internal activity allocation (e.g., \'FLT-HRS\' = flight hours, \'BLK-HRS\' = block hours, \'MRO-MHR\' = maintenance man-hours, \'GND-TRN\' = ground turns). Used to calculate internal activity rates for cost allocation between centres.']
  annual_budget_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Total approved annual budget allocated to this cost centre for the current fiscal year, in the controlling area currency. Represents the authorised spending limit for departmental P&L management. Used in budget vs. actual variance reporting and financial planning.']
  budget_version varchar [note: 'SAP CO planning version identifier for the budget associated with this cost centre (e.g., \'000\' = actuals, \'001\' = original budget, \'002\' = revised forecast, \'PLAN\' = annual plan). Enables comparison of actuals vs. budget vs. forecast in variance reporting.']
  business_area_code varchar [note: 'SAP FI business area assigned to this cost centre, representing a distinct area of operations for internal balance sheet and P&L reporting (e.g., \'PASS\' = Passenger, \'CARG\' = Cargo, \'MRO\' = Maintenance). Enables segment reporting per IFRS 8 without full profit centre accounting.']
  cask_allocation_key varchar [note: 'Allocation driver used to apportion this cost centre\'s costs to Available Seat Kilometers (ASK) for CASK reporting. \'ask\' = direct ASK-based allocation; \'block_hours\' = by block hours flown; \'departures\' = by number of departures; \'pax\' = by passenger count; \'revenue\' = by revenue share; \'equal\' = equal split; \'manual\' = manually defined percentages. Critical for unit cost benchmarking. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: ask|block_hours|departures|pax|revenue|equal|manual — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  category_code varchar [note: 'SAP CO cost centre category that classifies the functional nature of the centre (e.g., \'F\' = Flight Operations, \'M\' = Maintenance, \'G\' = Ground Handling, \'A\' = Administration, \'C\' = Cargo, \'L\' = Loyalty). Controls which activity types and statistical key figures can be posted. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: F|M|G|A|C|L|S|T — promote to reference product]']
  centre_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the responsibility centre by financial accountability type. \'cost\' centres track expenditure only (e.g., maintenance base); \'profit\' centres track both revenue and cost for P&L reporting (e.g., route group, cargo division); \'investment\' centres include capital employed; \'revenue\' centres track revenue attribution (e.g., lounge, ancillary). Drives CASK/RASK allocation methodology.']
  controlling_area_code varchar [note: 'SAP CO controlling area to which this cost centre belongs. The controlling area defines the organisational unit within which cost accounting is performed (e.g., \'AA01\' for the airline group). Determines currency, fiscal year variant, and chart of accounts applicable to this centre.']
  corsia_emissions_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this cost centre is subject to CORSIA (Carbon Offsetting and Reduction Scheme for International Aviation) emissions monitoring and offset cost accounting. True for international route operations cost centres. Drives inclusion in ICAO CORSIA compliance reporting and carbon offset cost allocation.']
  cost_centre_code varchar [note: 'Externally-known alphanumeric code uniquely identifying the cost centre within the SAP CO controlling area. Used in journal entries, budget allocations, and management reporting (e.g., \'FLT-OPS-001\', \'MRO-BASE-LHR\', \'CARGO-HUB-JFK\'). Aligns with SAP CO cost centre number.']
  cost_centre_description varchar [note: 'Long-form narrative description of the cost centre\'s business purpose, scope of operations, and cost accountability. Provides context for financial analysts and auditors beyond the short name (e.g., \'Responsible for all direct operating costs of widebody long-haul fleet on North Atlantic routes including fuel uplift, crew costs, and ATC charges\').']
  cost_centre_name varchar [note: 'Full descriptive name of the cost centre as maintained in SAP CO master data (e.g., \'Flight Operations — North Atlantic Hub\', \'Cabin Crew Training — Base London\', \'MRO Line Maintenance — Frankfurt\'). Used in financial reports and management dashboards.']
  cost_centre_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the cost centre in SAP CO. \'active\' = open for postings; \'inactive\' = temporarily suspended; \'locked\' = blocked for new postings (period-end); \'pending_activation\' = created but not yet released; \'closed\' = permanently deactivated with no further postings permitted.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this cost centre master record was first created in the source SAP CO system. Used for audit trail, data lineage tracking, and compliance with financial record retention requirements.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'ISO 4217 three-letter currency code for the cost centre\'s controlling currency (e.g., \'USD\', \'EUR\', \'GBP\'). Determines the currency in which budgets are planned, actuals are recorded, and variance reports are produced. May differ from company code currency for international operations.']
  fiscal_year varchar [note: 'The fiscal year to which the cost centre\'s budget and planning data applies (e.g., 2024). Aligns with the SAP CO fiscal year variant configured for the controlling area. Used to version budget records and support multi-year trend analysis.']
  fuel_cost_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this cost centre is designated for fuel cost accounting and uplift tracking. True for flight operations and route cost centres where jet fuel (Jet-A/A1) costs are directly attributed. Enables fuel cost per ASK (fuel CASK) component reporting and hedging P&L attribution.']
  functional_area_code varchar [note: 'SAP FI/CO functional area classifying the cost centre by business function for cost-of-sales accounting and segment reporting (e.g., \'PROD\' = Production/Operations, \'SELL\' = Sales, \'ADMN\' = Administration, \'R&D\' = Research). Required for IFRS income statement by function of expense.']
  gl_account_group varchar [note: 'SAP FI general ledger account group associated with primary cost postings for this cost centre. Defines the range of GL accounts permissible for postings, ensuring cost classification consistency (e.g., \'OPEX\' for operating expenses, \'FUEL\' for fuel costs, \'LABOR\' for crew and staff costs).']
  hierarchy_area varchar [note: 'Node identifier within the standard cost centre hierarchy (SAP CO standard hierarchy) to which this centre is assigned. Enables roll-up reporting by business segment, division, or region (e.g., \'FLIGHT-OPS\', \'MRO-EMEA\', \'CARGO-APAC\'). Used for group-level CASK reporting and segment P&L consolidation.']
  hierarchy_level varchar [note: 'Numeric depth of this cost centre within the standard hierarchy tree (1 = top-level division, 2 = sub-division, 3 = department, 4 = operational unit, etc.). Used to control report aggregation and drill-down navigation in management accounting dashboards.']
  hub_airport_code varchar [note: 'IATA three-letter airport code of the hub or base airport associated with this cost centre, where applicable (e.g., \'LHR\' for London Heathrow hub operations, \'JFK\' for New York hub, \'FRA\' for Frankfurt MRO base). Enables geographic cost attribution and hub-level CASK analysis. Null for non-location-specific centres.']
  iata_cost_category varchar [note: 'IATA standard cost category classification for airline management accounting and industry benchmarking (e.g., \'Flight Operations\', \'Maintenance\', \'Passenger Services\', \'Sales and Distribution\', \'General and Administration\'). Enables CASK component benchmarking against IATA industry data. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: Flight Operations|Maintenance|Passenger Services|Sales and Distribution|General and Administration|Cargo|Ground Operations — promote to reference product]']
  ifrs16_lease_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this cost centre carries IFRS 16 right-of-use asset and lease liability obligations (e.g., aircraft operating leases, airport gate leases, hangar leases). True triggers inclusion in IFRS 16 lease liability reporting and depreciation of right-of-use assets. Critical for airline balance sheet compliance.']
  interline_settlement_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this cost centre participates in interline billing settlement processes (IATA BSP/ARC). True for cost centres associated with interline revenue sharing, prorate agreements, and bilateral settlement with partner carriers. Drives inclusion in interline financial reconciliation workflows.']
  internal_activity_rate decimal(18,6) [note: 'Planned cost rate per unit of activity output for this cost centre (in controlling area currency). Used for internal cost allocation between cost centres (e.g., cost per block hour charged from Flight Operations to Route cost centres). Recalculated each planning cycle. Classified confidential as it reflects internal transfer pricing.']
  last_changed_by varchar [note: 'SAP user ID of the person who last modified this cost centre master record. Supports audit trail requirements for financial master data governance and SOX compliance controls over cost centre maintenance.']
  last_changed_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent modification to this cost centre master record in SAP CO. Used for change tracking, incremental data extraction (delta loads), and audit compliance. Corresponds to SAP CDHDR change document header timestamp.']
  profit_centre_code varchar [note: 'SAP CO profit centre to which this cost centre is assigned for internal P&L reporting. Enables revenue attribution and profitability analysis at route, hub, or business segment level. Supports RASK/CASK analysis by profit centre.']
  rask_attribution_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this cost centre participates in Revenue per Available Seat Kilometer (RASK) attribution for route profitability analysis. True for profit centres and revenue-generating units (e.g., route groups, lounges, ancillary services); False for pure cost centres (e.g., corporate functions, back-office).']
  region_code varchar [note: 'Geographic region code to which this cost centre is assigned for regional management reporting (e.g., \'EMEA\', \'NORAM\', \'APAC\', \'LATAM\', \'DOM\' for domestic). Supports regional P&L roll-up, CASK benchmarking by region, and DOT/EASA regional regulatory reporting.']
  short_name varchar [note: 'Abbreviated name of the cost centre used in condensed reports, dashboards, and system displays where space is limited. Corresponds to SAP CO short text field (up to 20 characters).']
  valid_from date [note: 'Date from which this cost centre is valid and open for financial postings in SAP CO. Supports time-dependent master data management — cost centre attributes may change over time with new validity periods. Aligns with fiscal year planning cycles.']
  valid_to date [note: 'Date until which this cost centre is valid for financial postings. Null or 31-Dec-9999 indicates an open-ended validity. Used to manage cost centre lifecycle, seasonal operations (e.g., charter season cost centres), and organisational restructuring.']

  Note: 'SAP CO organisational unit master representing cost-incurring and profit-bearing responsibility centres within the airline — e.g., flight operations, cabin crew, MRO line/base stations, ground handling, airport lounges, cargo division, route groups (by hub, region), and corporate functions. Stores centre type (cost/profit), hierarchy level and parent, responsible manager, controlling area, validity periods, budget allocation references, revenue attribution rules, CASK/RASK allocation keys, and internal activity rates. Enables departmental P&L reporting, CASK component attribution, route profitability analysis, and management accounting by business segment.'
}

Table finance.accounts_payable {
  accounts_payable_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate primary key for each vendor invoice record in the accounts payable ledger. Generated by the Databricks Silver Layer ingestion pipeline from SAP FI-AP document entries.']
  company_code_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.company_code. Business justification: Each AP invoice belongs to ONE company code. The accounts_payable table has company_code (STRING) which should be replaced with a FK to the company_code master. This is a mandatory dimension in SAP FI']
  cost_centre_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the cost center to which this invoice expense is allocated, supporting CASK (Cost per Available Seat Kilometer) reporting and departmental cost accounting within SAP CO.']
  expense_employee_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to workforce.employee. Business justification: AP invoices for employee expenses (training, relocation, travel advances) require employee linkage for expense management, reimbursement tracking, and payroll integration. Role prefix distinguishes fr']
  goods_receipt_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to procurement.goods_receipt. Business justification: Three-way match (PO-GR-Invoice) requires AP to reference specific goods receipt for invoice verification, payment release authorization, and procurement compliance. Mandatory for accounts payable proc']
  lease_contract_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.lease_contract. Business justification: AP invoices for IFRS 16 lease payments should be linked to the lease contract. The accounts_payable table has ifrs16_lease_flag (BOOLEAN) indicating lease-related invoices, but no explicit FK. Adding ']
  purchase_order_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the originating purchase order document against which this vendor invoice is matched in the three-way match process (PO, goods receipt, invoice). Maps to SAP MM purchasing document.']
  repair_order_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to procurement.repair_order. Business justification: MRO repair invoices must reference the originating repair order for cost validation, warranty claim processing, maintenance cost tracking, and airworthiness compliance. Essential for MRO financial con']
  vendor_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the vendor master record representing the supplier of goods or services (e.g., MRO supplier, fuel vendor, airport authority, GDS provider, catering contractor, ground handling agent). Maps to SAP Vendor Master (LFA1).']
  approval_status varchar [note: 'Current status of the invoice within the internal approval workflow, reflecting authorization levels required before payment execution. Supports segregation of duties and internal controls per SOX compliance requirements.']
  bsp_arc_settlement_ref varchar [note: 'The IATA Billing and Settlement Plan (BSP) or Airlines Reporting Corporation (ARC) settlement reference number for invoices settled through interline or agency billing cycles. Applicable for GDS provider fees, interline billing, and travel agency commission settlements.']
  clearing_document_number varchar [note: 'The SAP accounting document number of the payment document that cleared (settled) this open invoice item. Populated upon successful payment execution. Corresponds to SAP field AUGBL (Clearing Document). Null for unpaid invoices.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The timestamp when this accounts payable record was first created in the SAP FI-AP system, representing the initial document entry time. Used for audit trail, SLA monitoring of invoice processing cycle time, and data lineage.']
  discount_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'The cash discount amount available if payment is made by the discount_due_date, as negotiated in the vendor payment terms. Supports working capital optimization and supplier relationship management.']
  discount_due_date date [note: 'The deadline by which early payment must be made to qualify for the cash discount offered under the vendor\'s payment terms (e.g., 2/10 net 30). Supports cash flow optimization and working capital management.']
  due_date date [note: 'The calculated date by which payment must be made to the vendor to comply with agreed payment terms and avoid late payment penalties. Derived from invoice date plus payment term days. Corresponds to SAP field FAEDT (Net Due Date).']
  exchange_rate decimal(12,6) [note: 'The exchange rate applied to translate the invoice currency amount to the local currency at the time of posting. Sourced from SAP exchange rate table (TCURR). Critical for FX exposure reporting and treasury operations.']
  fiscal_period varchar [note: 'The fiscal posting period (1–16, including special periods) within the fiscal year in which this invoice is recorded. Supports monthly accrual management and period-end close processes in SAP FI.']
  fiscal_year varchar [note: 'The fiscal year in which this vendor invoice is posted in SAP FI. Used for period-end closing, financial reporting, and year-over-year cost analysis. Corresponds to SAP field GJAHR.']
  fuel_uplift_volume_liters decimal(12,2) [note: 'For fuel vendor invoices, the volume of jet fuel (Jet-A or Jet-A1) uplifted in liters as recorded on the fuel receipt. Supports fuel cost per liter analysis, CASK fuel component reporting, and CORSIA carbon emissions accounting. Null for non-fuel invoices.']
  gl_account_code varchar [note: 'The SAP general ledger account number to which the invoice expense is posted, enabling cost classification for financial reporting and CASK component analysis (e.g., fuel cost GL, MRO cost GL, airport charges GL). Corresponds to SAP field HKONT.']
  goods_receipt_date date [note: 'The date on which goods or services were confirmed as received, forming the third leg of the three-way match (PO → Goods Receipt → Invoice). Critical for MRO parts, fuel uplift, and catering deliveries. Corresponds to SAP MIGO goods receipt posting date.']
  gross_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'The total gross amount of the vendor invoice before deduction of taxes, withholding tax, or discounts, expressed in the invoice currency. Represents the full obligation as stated on the vendor\'s invoice document.']
  ifrs16_lease_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this invoice relates to a lease obligation subject to IFRS 16 Leases accounting treatment (e.g., aircraft dry lease, wet lease, ACMI lease payments, airport gate lease). When true, the payment is classified as a lease liability repayment rather than an operating expense.']
  invoice_currency varchar [note: 'The ISO 4217 three-letter currency code in which the vendor invoice is denominated (e.g., USD, EUR, GBP). May differ from the company code local currency, requiring foreign exchange translation for GL posting. Corresponds to SAP field WAERS.']
  invoice_date date [note: 'The date printed on the vendor\'s invoice document, representing the business event date for the supply of goods or services. Used as the baseline for payment term calculation and tax point determination. Corresponds to SAP field BLDAT.']
  invoice_number varchar [note: 'The externally-assigned invoice number as printed on the vendor\'s invoice document. Used for vendor reconciliation, dispute resolution, and audit trail. Corresponds to SAP field BELNR (Accounting Document Number) or XBLNR (Reference Document Number).']
