  invoice_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the vendor invoice within the SAP FI-AP workflow. \'open\' = posted and awaiting payment; \'parked\' = saved but not yet posted; \'blocked\' = payment block applied; \'in_approval\' = pending workflow approval; \'cleared\' = fully paid and cleared; \'cancelled\' = reversed/cancelled document.']
  last_updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The timestamp of the most recent modification to this accounts payable record, capturing changes to approval status, payment block, or clearing information. Supports change data capture (CDC) in the Databricks Silver Layer pipeline.']
  local_currency_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'The net payable amount translated into the company code\'s local (functional) currency using the exchange rate at posting date. Used for GL posting, financial reporting, and CASK calculation in the airline\'s reporting currency.']
  net_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'The net amount payable to the vendor after deducting withholding tax and any applicable cash discounts from the gross invoice amount. Represents the actual cash outflow obligation. Calculated as gross_amount minus withholding_tax_amount minus discount_amount.']
  payment_block_reason varchar [note: 'Reason code explaining why a payment block has been applied to this invoice, preventing it from being included in the next payment run. Corresponds to SAP field ZLSPR (Payment Block Key). Null when no block is active.']
  payment_date date [note: 'The actual date on which payment was executed to the vendor, either via bank transfer, cheque, or BSP/ARC settlement. Populated upon payment run completion. Corresponds to SAP clearing document value date.']
  payment_method varchar [note: 'The method by which payment is executed to the vendor. \'bank_transfer\' = SWIFT/SEPA wire; \'cheque\' = paper cheque; \'bsp_settlement\' = IATA Billing and Settlement Plan net settlement; \'arc_settlement\' = Airlines Reporting Corporation settlement; \'direct_debit\' = automated debit. Corresponds to SAP field ZLSCH.']
  payment_terms_code varchar [note: 'The SAP payment terms key (e.g., NT30, NT60, 2/10N30) defining the agreed payment schedule, discount percentages, and due date calculation rules for this vendor. Corresponds to SAP field ZTERM.']
  posting_date date [note: 'The date on which the vendor invoice is posted to the SAP general ledger, determining the accounting period for recognition. May differ from invoice date due to processing delays. Corresponds to SAP field BUDAT.']
  sap_document_number varchar [note: 'The internal SAP FI accounting document number (BELNR) assigned upon posting of the vendor invoice in the general ledger. Uniquely identifies the FI document within a company code and fiscal year.']
  tax_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'The total tax amount (VAT, GST, or applicable local tax) charged on the vendor invoice. Corresponds to SAP field WMWST. Used for input tax recovery and tax reporting to national revenue authorities.']
  tax_code varchar [note: 'The SAP tax code identifying the applicable tax rate and tax type (input VAT, zero-rated, exempt) for this invoice line. Drives automated tax calculation and input tax recovery reporting. Corresponds to SAP field MWSKZ.']
  three_way_match_status varchar [note: 'Result of the automated three-way match validation comparing the purchase order, goods receipt, and vendor invoice for quantity and price alignment. \'matched\' = all three documents agree; \'price_variance\' = invoice price differs from PO; \'quantity_variance\' = invoice quantity differs from GR; \'pending_gr\' = goods receipt not yet confirmed; \'failed\' = match failed requiring manual review.']
  vendor_category varchar [note: 'Classification of the vendor type supplying goods or services to the airline. Drives cost allocation to CASK components and supports spend analytics by supplier category. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: mro_supplier|fuel_vendor|airport_authority|gds_provider|catering|ground_handling|bsp_arc — promote to reference product]']
  withholding_tax_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'The amount of withholding tax (WHT) to be deducted from the vendor payment and remitted to the tax authority on behalf of the vendor. Applicable for cross-border supplier payments subject to bilateral tax treaties. Corresponds to SAP Extended Withholding Tax (WITH_ITEM).']

  Note: 'SAP FI-AP vendor invoice and payment obligation records for the airline covering MRO suppliers, fuel vendors, airport authorities, GDS providers, catering contractors, and ground handling agents. Each record represents a vendor invoice document with invoice number, vendor ID, invoice date, line items, tax amounts, purchase order reference, goods receipt reference, three-way match status, payment terms, due dates, withholding tax, approval workflow status, payment execution details (method, bank, value date, clearing document), and BSP/ARC settlement obligations. Supports procure-to-pay lifecycle, cash flow forecasting, and supplier payment runs.'
}

Table finance.accounts_receivable {
  accounts_receivable_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate identifier for each accounts receivable record in the SAP FI-AR module. Primary key for the silver layer lakehouse table.']
  company_code_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.company_code. Business justification: Each AR document belongs to ONE company code. The accounts_receivable table has company_code (STRING) which should be replaced with a FK to the company_code master. This is a mandatory dimension in SA']
  staff_travel_employee_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to workforce.employee. Business justification: Staff travel tickets (ID90, ZED fares) sold to employees create AR entries requiring employee linkage for payroll deduction processing, travel entitlement tracking, and revenue recognition per airline']
  ticket_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to revenue.ticket. Business justification: Accounts receivable for ticket sales must be linked to the ticket for revenue recognition and cash collection tracking. This FK enables reconciliation of ticket revenue with cash receipts.']
  ageing_bucket varchar [note: 'Ageing classification of the open receivable based on days past due date: CURRENT (not yet due), 1_30_DAYS, 31_60_DAYS, 61_90_DAYS, 91_120_DAYS, OVER_120_DAYS. Drives credit risk assessment and bad debt provisioning under IFRS 9 expected credit loss model.']
  ar_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the receivable: OPEN (unpaid), PARTIALLY_PAID (partial payment received), CLEARED (fully paid and matched), DISPUTED (customer dispute raised), WRITTEN_OFF (bad debt write-off applied), IN_DUNNING (active dunning process initiated).']
  bank_value_date date [note: 'Value date on which funds were credited to the airline\'s bank account for this receivable. Distinct from posting date; used for treasury cash flow forecasting and bank reconciliation.']
  bsp_arc_billing_period varchar [note: 'IATA BSP or ARC billing period identifier (e.g., 2024-W03 for weekly cycle) to which this agency receivable belongs. Supports settlement reconciliation and dispute management with travel agencies.']
  clearing_date date [note: 'Date on which the receivable was fully cleared (matched against incoming payment or credit memo) in SAP. Null for open items. Used for Days Sales Outstanding (DSO) calculation.']
  clearing_document_number varchar [note: 'SAP document number of the payment or credit memo that cleared this receivable. Enables end-to-end payment reconciliation and audit trail from invoice to settlement.']
  cost_center varchar [note: 'SAP cost center associated with the receivable for internal cost allocation and CASK (Cost per Available Seat Kilometer) reporting purposes.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the AR record was first created in the source SAP FI-AR system. Provides audit trail for record origination and supports data lineage in the lakehouse silver layer.']
  credit_control_area varchar [note: 'SAP credit control area (KKBER) under which the customer\'s credit limit is managed. Groups customers for credit exposure monitoring across company codes.']
  credit_limit decimal(18,2) [note: 'Approved credit limit assigned to the customer in the credit control area, expressed in the credit control area currency. Used for credit exposure monitoring and order block decisions.']
  customer_account_number varchar [note: 'SAP debtor account number (KUNNR) identifying the customer — travel agency, corporate account, interline carrier, or cargo shipper — against whom the receivable is raised.']
  customer_name varchar [note: 'Legal name of the debtor customer as maintained in SAP customer master. Covers travel agencies, corporate accounts, interline carriers, and cargo clients.']
  days_overdue varchar [note: 'Number of calendar days the receivable is past its due date as of the last refresh date. Negative values indicate days until due. Used for DSO reporting and credit risk monitoring.']
  dispute_reason_code varchar [note: 'Standardized code identifying the reason for a customer dispute on this receivable (e.g., pricing discrepancy, duplicate billing, service not rendered, ADM — Agency Debit Memo dispute). Null when no dispute exists. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: PRICE_DISCREPANCY|DUPLICATE_BILLING|SERVICE_NOT_RENDERED|ADM_DISPUTE|CREDIT_NOT_APPLIED|INTERLINE_PRORATE_DIFF — promote to reference product]']
  document_currency varchar [note: 'ISO 4217 three-letter currency code in which the AR document amounts are denominated (e.g., USD, EUR, GBP). Supports multi-currency airline operations and foreign exchange reporting.']
  document_number varchar [note: 'SAP FI-AR accounting document number uniquely identifying the receivable posting within the company code. Corresponds to BELNR field in SAP BSID/BSAD tables.']
  document_type varchar [note: 'SAP document type code classifying the nature of the AR posting: DR (customer invoice), DG (credit memo), DA (customer document), RV (billing document transfer), AB (accounting document). Drives posting logic and account determination.']
  due_date date [note: 'Date by which payment is contractually due from the customer, calculated from invoice date plus payment terms (e.g., BSP weekly settlement cycle, net 30 corporate terms). Key input for ageing bucket classification and dunning.']
  dunning_level varchar [note: 'Current dunning level (1–4) assigned to this receivable in SAP dunning program, indicating the escalation stage of the collection notice. Level 0 means no dunning initiated.']
  fiscal_period varchar [note: 'Accounting period (1–16 including special periods) within the fiscal year in which the AR document was posted. Supports month-end and period-end financial close.']
  fiscal_year varchar [note: 'Fiscal year in which the AR document was posted, as defined in the SAP fiscal year variant. Used for period-end close and financial consolidation reporting.']
  gl_account varchar [note: 'SAP general ledger account number to which the receivable is posted (e.g., trade receivables, interline receivables, cargo receivables). Drives financial statement classification.']
  gross_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Total gross amount of the receivable before any discounts, taxes, or adjustments, expressed in the document currency. Represents the face value of the invoice or billing document.']
  interline_carrier_code varchar [note: 'Two or three-character IATA airline designator code of the interline partner carrier for interline receivable types. Null for non-interline receivables. Supports interline prorate and settlement reconciliation.']
  invoice_date date [note: 'Date on which the invoice or billing document was issued to the customer. Used for revenue recognition, ageing bucket calculation, and payment terms baseline.']
  last_dunning_date date [note: 'Date on which the most recent dunning notice was issued to the customer for this receivable. Used to track collection activity and enforce dunning intervals.']
  last_updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the AR record in the source SAP FI-AR system, including payment postings, dunning updates, or status changes. Supports incremental data loading in the lakehouse.']
  local_currency_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Open receivable amount translated to the company code local currency using the SAP exchange rate type at posting date. Required for statutory financial reporting and consolidation.']
  local_currency_code varchar [note: 'ISO 4217 three-letter code for the company code local (functional) currency to which document amounts are translated for statutory reporting.']
  net_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Net amount due from the customer after deducting applicable discounts and adjustments but before tax. Used for revenue recognition and working capital reporting.']
  open_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Remaining unpaid balance on the receivable at the current point in time, calculated as gross amount minus payments received and credit memos applied. Core field for working capital management and ageing analysis.']
  payment_method varchar [note: 'Method by which payment was or is expected to be received: BANK_TRANSFER (wire/SWIFT), BSP_SETTLEMENT (IATA Billing and Settlement Plan automated settlement), ARC_SETTLEMENT (Airlines Reporting Corporation settlement), CREDIT_CARD, DIRECT_DEBIT, CHEQUE.']
  payment_terms_code varchar [note: 'SAP payment terms key (ZTERM) defining the contractual payment conditions, discount periods, and due date calculation rules applicable to this receivable (e.g., BSP weekly, net 30, net 60).']
  posting_date date [note: 'Date on which the AR document was posted to the general ledger in SAP. Determines the fiscal period assignment and is the basis for period-end cut-off.']
  profit_center varchar [note: 'SAP profit center to which the receivable revenue is attributed, enabling segment-level P&L reporting by route, cabin class, or business unit within the airline.']
  receivable_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the receivable by originating business channel: BSP_AGENCY (IATA Billing and Settlement Plan agency), ARC_AGENCY (Airlines Reporting Corporation agency), CORPORATE (direct corporate account billing), INTERLINE (interline carrier settlement), CARGO_AWB (Air Waybill cargo collection), ANCILLARY (ancillary service charges such as excess baggage, upgrades).']
  reconciliation_status varchar [note: 'Status of the receivable reconciliation against the originating billing system (BSP, ARC, cargo system, or ancillary billing): MATCHED (confirmed and reconciled), UNMATCHED (discrepancy identified), DISPUTED (formal dispute raised), PENDING_REVIEW (awaiting finance review).']
  reference_document varchar [note: 'External reference number associated with the receivable, such as the BSP billing period reference, ARC settlement report number, Air Waybill (AWB) number for cargo, or PNR for passenger billings. Enables cross-system reconciliation.']
  tax_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Tax component of the receivable including applicable government taxes, Passenger Facility Charges (PFC), and other statutory levies included in the invoice. Supports tax reporting and reconciliation.']
  write_off_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Amount written off as bad debt for this receivable, in document currency. Populated when ar_status is WRITTEN_OFF. Supports bad debt expense reporting and IFRS 9 expected credit loss provisioning.']

  Note: 'SAP FI-AR customer receivable and collection records for the airline covering travel agency BSP/ARC settlements, corporate account billings, interline receivables, cargo AWB collections, ancillary service charges, and incoming payment receipts. Tracks invoice issuance, payment receipt (method, bank, value date, clearing reference), dunning status, credit limits, ageing buckets, and reconciliation status. Supports revenue recognition, working capital management, and customer credit risk monitoring.'
}

Table finance.budget_plan {
  budget_plan_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate identifier for the budget plan record in the finance data product. Primary key for the budget_plan entity.']
  approver_employee_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the senior manager, CFO, or board member who approved this budget plan. Supports segregation of duties and audit trail requirements.']
  company_code_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.company_code. Business justification: Each budget plan belongs to ONE company code. The budget_plan table has company_code (STRING) which should be replaced with a FK to the company_code master. Budget plans are company-code specific in S']
  cost_centre_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the SAP CO cost centre to which this budget plan is assigned. Enables cost centre-level budget tracking and variance analysis across airline operational divisions.']
  general_ledger_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the General Ledger account code in SAP FI against which this budget plan is posted. Enables GL-level budget vs. actuals reporting.']
  primary_budget_employee_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the employee or manager responsible for owning and managing this budget plan. Supports accountability tracking and approval workflow routing in SAP BPC.']
  procurement_category_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to procurement.procurement_category. Business justification: Budget planning allocates spend targets by procurement category for category management, variance reporting, and strategic sourcing alignment. Core to procurement financial planning and category strat']
  profit_centre_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the SAP CO-PA profit centre associated with this budget plan. Supports profitability analysis by route, fleet type, or business unit.']
  approval_date date [note: 'The date on which the budget plan received final approval from the designated authority (CFO, board, or budget committee). Marks the transition from \'submitted\' to \'approved\' status in the workflow.']
  ask_budget decimal(18,2) [note: 'Budgeted Available Seat Kilometers (ASK) for the planning period, representing planned capacity output. ASK is the industry-standard capacity metric (seats × kilometers flown). Used as the denominator for CASK and RASK budget calculations.']
  budget_category varchar [note: 'High-level financial category of the budget plan. OPEX = Operating Expenditure; CAPEX = Capital Expenditure; REVENUE = Revenue budget; MIXED = combined OPEX and CAPEX plan. Aligns with SAP CO-PA cost element categories.']
  capex_budget_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Budgeted amount allocated to capital expenditure (CAPEX) within this plan, including aircraft acquisitions, fleet modifications, and infrastructure investments. Supports IFRS 16 lease liability planning.']
  cask_budget decimal(10,6) [note: 'Budgeted Cost per Available Seat Kilometer (CASK), calculated as total OPEX budget divided by ASK budget. Expressed in currency units per ASK. Primary airline unit cost efficiency metric used in board-level financial planning and investor reporting.']
  consolidation_level varchar [note: 'The organisational level at which this budget plan is consolidated. \'entity\' = legal entity; \'division\' = business division (e.g., cargo, MRO); \'group\' = airline group; \'segment\' = IFRS 8 reportable segment. Supports financial consolidation and group reporting.']
  controlling_area_code varchar [note: 'The SAP CO controlling area code under which this budget plan is managed. The controlling area defines the organisational unit for internal cost accounting and profitability analysis in SAP CO-PA.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The timestamp when this budget plan record was first created in the system. Supports audit trail requirements and data lineage tracking in the Databricks Silver Layer.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'ISO 4217 three-letter currency code in which the budget amounts are denominated (e.g., USD, EUR, GBP). Supports multi-currency airline operations and IATA BSP settlement reporting.']
  effective_from date [note: 'The start date from which this budget plan is effective and binding for financial control purposes. Aligns with the fiscal period start in SAP FI.']
  effective_until date [note: 'The end date through which this budget plan remains effective. Null for open-ended multi-year plans. Aligns with fiscal period end in SAP FI.']
  fiscal_year varchar [note: 'The fiscal year to which this budget plan primarily applies (e.g., 2025). Used for period-based financial reporting and alignment with SAP FI fiscal year variants.']
  forecast_cycle varchar [note: 'The frequency at which this rolling forecast budget plan is updated and resubmitted. Applicable only when is_rolling_forecast is True. Supports planning cadence governance.']
  fuel_cost_budget_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Budgeted amount specifically allocated to jet fuel procurement and uplift costs. Fuel is typically the largest single OPEX line for airlines. Supports fuel cost accounting and hedging strategy alignment.']
  interline_settlement_budget_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Budgeted net interline billing settlement amount payable or receivable through IATA BSP/ARC for interline ticketing agreements with partner carriers. Supports interline prorate and billing reconciliation planning.']
  is_rolling_forecast boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this budget plan is part of a rolling forecast cycle (True) or a fixed annual budget (False). Rolling forecasts are updated periodically (monthly/quarterly) to reflect current business conditions.']
  lease_liability_budget_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Budgeted amount for aircraft and facility lease liabilities recognised under IFRS 16. Covers both operating lease right-of-use asset depreciation and interest on lease liabilities for dry lease and ACMI arrangements.']
  load_factor_budget_pct decimal(5,2) [note: 'Budgeted passenger load factor expressed as a percentage (e.g., 82.50 = 82.5%). Load factor is the percentage of available seats filled by revenue passengers. Used in revenue budget planning and yield management alignment.']
  locked_date date [note: 'The date on which the budget plan was locked to prevent further modifications, marking the start of the execution period. Locked budgets serve as the baseline for variance reporting.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text field for budget planners and approvers to record assumptions, justifications, revision rationale, or board commentary associated with this budget plan version.']
  opex_budget_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Budgeted amount allocated to operating expenditure (OPEX) within this plan, including fuel, crew, maintenance, airport charges, and overhead. Key input for CASK (Cost per Available Seat Kilometer) reporting.']
  plan_name varchar [note: 'Human-readable descriptive name of the budget plan (e.g., \'FY2025 Annual Operating Budget\', \'Q3 2025 Rolling Forecast\'). Used for display and reporting purposes.']
  plan_number varchar [note: 'Externally-known alphanumeric identifier for the budget plan, aligned with SAP BPC plan version numbering (e.g., BP-2025-001). Used for cross-system reference and audit trails.']
  plan_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the budget plan within the approval workflow. \'draft\' = in preparation; \'submitted\' = awaiting review; \'under_review\' = being evaluated; \'approved\' = board/CFO approved; \'rejected\' = returned for revision; \'locked\' = frozen for execution; \'archived\' = superseded. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: draft|submitted|under_review|approved|rejected|locked|archived — promote to reference product]']
  plan_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the budget plan by planning methodology. \'annual\' = full-year fixed budget; \'rolling_forecast\' = continuously updated forecast; \'supplemental\' = mid-year adjustment; \'capital\' = CAPEX-specific plan; \'zero_based\' = zero-based budgeting cycle.']
  plan_version varchar [note: 'Version identifier of the budget plan, supporting iterative planning cycles (e.g., \'v1.0\', \'v2.3\', \'RF-Q2\'). Enables tracking of revisions and rolling forecast iterations within SAP BPC.']
  planning_horizon_years varchar [note: 'Number of years covered by this budget plan (e.g., 1 for annual, 3 or 5 for multi-year strategic plans). Supports long-range financial planning and IFRS 16 lease liability projections.']
  rask_budget decimal(10,6) [note: 'Budgeted Revenue per Available Seat Kilometer (RASK), calculated as total revenue budget divided by ASK budget. Expressed in currency units per ASK. Key airline revenue productivity metric for strategic planning and investor reporting.']
  revenue_budget_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Budgeted revenue target for the planning period, covering passenger revenue, cargo revenue, ancillary services, and loyalty program income. Supports RASK (Revenue per Available Seat Kilometer) planning.']
  submission_date date [note: 'The date on which the budget plan was formally submitted for review and approval. Supports planning cycle governance and deadline tracking.']
  total_budget_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'The gross total budgeted amount for this plan in the plan currency, covering all line items. Represents the top-line financial commitment approved for the planning period. Used for board-level financial reporting.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The timestamp when this budget plan record was last modified. Tracks the most recent change for audit trail, version control, and incremental data pipeline processing in the Databricks Silver Layer.']
  variance_threshold_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'The maximum allowable absolute monetary deviation between budgeted and actual amounts before a variance alert is triggered. Complements the percentage threshold for large-value budget lines where a fixed amount cap is more appropriate.']
  variance_threshold_pct decimal(5,2) [note: 'The maximum allowable percentage deviation between budgeted and actual amounts before a variance alert is triggered for management review. Expressed as a percentage (e.g., 5.00 = 5%). Supports financial control and exception reporting.']

  Note: 'Annual and multi-year financial budget plans for the airline covering operating expenditure (OPEX), capital expenditure (CAPEX), and revenue budgets by cost centre, profit centre, and GL account. Stores budget version, planning horizon, approval status, budget owner, and variance thresholds. Supports rolling forecast cycles and board-level financial planning aligned with SAP CO-PA and BPC modules.'
}

Table finance.fuel_cost_transaction {
  fuel_cost_transaction_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate identifier for each fuel cost transaction record in the finance domain silver layer. Primary key for this data product.']
  cost_centre_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the cost centre in SAP S/4HANA CO to which the fuel cost is allocated. Enables departmental cost accountability and CASK fuel component reporting by operating unit.']
  flight_leg_assignment_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to crew.flight_leg_assignment. Business justification: Fuel costs attributed to crew assignments for pilot fuel efficiency tracking, performance evaluation, and fuel conservation KPIs. Real operational metric used in pilot performance reviews and fuel man']
  flight_leg_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the specific flight leg for which fuel was uplifted. Enables CASK (Cost per Available Seat Kilometer) fuel component attribution at the flight level.']
  fuel_contract_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to procurement.fuel_contract. Business justification: Fuel cost accounting requires linking to the pricing contract for variance analysis, invoice reconciliation, and hedge effectiveness testing. Essential for fuel cost management and financial reporting']
  fuel_uplift_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the physical fuel uplift event recorded in flight operations (Jeppesen / NetLine/Ops). Links the financial accounting entry back to the operational fuel uplift record for CASK reconciliation.']
  fuel_uplift_order_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to procurement.fuel_uplift_order. Business justification: Fuel cost accounting must reconcile to operational fuel uplift orders for quantity/price variance analysis, dispute resolution, and invoice-to-delivery matching. Critical for fuel cost control and sup']
  general_ledger_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the General Ledger account in SAP S/4HANA FI to which this fuel cost is posted. Supports financial consolidation and regulatory financial reporting.']
  hedge_contract_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.hedge_contract. Business justification: Each fuel cost transaction can be linked to ONE hedge contract for fuel price hedging. The fuel_cost_transaction table has hedging_contract_reference (STRING) which should be replaced with a FK to the']
  accounting_period varchar [note: 'Financial accounting period (YYYY-MM) to which this fuel cost is assigned in SAP S/4HANA FI. Determines which monthly P&L period absorbs the cost. May differ from the uplift month due to accruals or late invoices.']
  airport_iata_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter IATA airport code identifying the station where the fuel uplift occurred (e.g., LHR, JFK, DXB). Used for geographic cost analysis, into-plane agent reconciliation, and fuel price benchmarking by station.']
  carbon_emission_kg decimal(18,4) [note: 'Estimated CO2-equivalent carbon emissions in kilograms attributable to this fuel uplift, calculated using ICAO/CORSIA emission factors applied to the fuel quantity and SAF blend. Required for CORSIA offsetting obligations and ESG regulatory reporting.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this fuel cost transaction record was first created in the source system (SAP S/4HANA). Supports audit trail and data lineage requirements.']
  dispute_reference varchar [note: 'Reference number of any active or resolved supplier dispute raised against this fuel cost transaction (e.g., quantity discrepancy, pricing error). Populated when transaction_status = \'disputed\'. Used for accounts payable dispute management.']
  fiscal_year varchar [note: 'Fiscal year (e.g., 2024) to which this fuel cost transaction belongs in SAP S/4HANA FI. Used for annual financial reporting, budget vs. actual variance analysis, and year-end close.']
  fuel_density decimal(10,6) [note: 'Measured fuel density in kilograms per litre at the time of uplift, as recorded on the fuel delivery receipt. Used to reconcile volumetric (litres) and mass (kg) quantities and to validate fuel quality compliance.']
  fuel_supplier_name varchar [note: 'Name of the fuel supplier (oil major or independent) who provided the fuel (e.g., Shell Aviation, BP Aviation, ExxonMobil Aviation, World Fuel Services). Used for supplier spend analysis and contract compliance monitoring.']
  fuel_type varchar [note: 'Type of aviation fuel uplifted. \'Jet-A1\' is the standard international jet fuel; \'SAF\' is Sustainable Aviation Fuel; \'Jet-A\' is the US domestic equivalent; \'TS-1\' is Russian-standard jet fuel; \'JP-8\' is military-grade. Critical for CORSIA carbon accounting and SAF blending cost tracking.']
  functional_currency_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Net fuel cost translated into the airline\'s functional (reporting) currency using the exchange rate at the posting date. Required for consolidated P&L reporting and CASK calculation in a single currency.']
  fx_rate decimal(18,8) [note: 'Exchange rate applied to convert the transaction currency amount to the functional currency at the posting date. Sourced from SAP S/4HANA treasury exchange rate tables. Required for IFRS IAS 21 compliance.']
  gross_cost_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Total gross cost of the fuel uplift before any discounts, surcharges, or tax adjustments, in the transaction currency. Calculated as quantity × unit price. Base figure for CASK fuel component reporting.']
  hedge_gain_loss_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Monetary gain or loss on the fuel hedge for this uplift, calculated as (hedged_unit_price - actual unit_price) × quantity. Positive = hedge gain (actual price exceeded hedged price); negative = hedge loss. Posted to the hedge reserve in OCI under IFRS 9.']
  hedged_unit_price decimal(18,6) [note: 'The locked-in fuel price per litre from the hedging contract applicable to this uplift. Compared against the actual unit_price to calculate hedge gain/loss and measure hedge effectiveness per IFRS 9.']
  ifrs16_lease_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the fuel cost is associated with a wet lease or ACMI (Aircraft Crew Maintenance Insurance) arrangement where fuel costs are embedded in the lease liability under IFRS 16. True = IFRS 16 lease-related fuel cost; False = standard fuel purchase.']
  into_plane_agent_name varchar [note: 'Name of the into-plane fuelling agent responsible for the physical delivery of fuel to the aircraft at the airport (may differ from the fuel supplier). Into-plane agents are contracted separately and charge a service fee included in the surcharge amount.']
  net_cost_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Net total cost of the fuel uplift after applying discounts and adjustments but inclusive of taxes and surcharges, in the transaction currency. This is the amount posted to the General Ledger and used for CASK fuel component reporting.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text notes or comments entered by the fuel accounting team regarding this transaction (e.g., reason for reversal, manual adjustment justification, AOG emergency uplift context). Supports audit trail and exception management.']
  posting_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the fuel cost was posted to the General Ledger in SAP S/4HANA FI. Used for period-end close, accrual management, and financial reporting cut-off.']
  purchase_order_number varchar [note: 'SAP S/4HANA purchase order number raised for the fuel procurement at this station. Links the financial posting to the procurement process for three-way matching and spend analytics.']
  quantity_kg decimal(18,4) [note: 'Mass of fuel uplifted expressed in kilograms. The operationally authoritative unit for flight planning and weight-and-balance. Used for CASK fuel component calculation (cost per ASK) and fuel hedge effectiveness measurement.']
  quantity_litres decimal(18,4) [note: 'Volume of fuel uplifted expressed in litres. Standard volumetric unit used for into-plane agent invoicing and fuel supplier billing. Used alongside quantity_kg for density reconciliation.']
  saf_blend_percentage decimal(5,2) [note: 'Percentage of Sustainable Aviation Fuel (SAF) in the total fuel uplift volume (0.00–100.00). Required for CORSIA carbon offsetting calculations, SAF mandate compliance reporting, and ESG/sustainability disclosures.']
  supplier_invoice_number varchar [note: 'Invoice number issued by the fuel supplier or into-plane agent for this uplift. Used for three-way matching (PO / goods receipt / invoice) in SAP S/4HANA MM/FI and for dispute resolution.']
  tax_and_surcharge_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Total taxes, levies, and into-plane service surcharges applied to the fuel uplift in the transaction currency. Includes Passenger Facility Charges (PFC) where applicable, fuel throughput fees, and local fuel taxes.']
  transaction_currency varchar [note: 'ISO 4217 three-letter currency code in which the fuel cost transaction is denominated (e.g., USD, EUR, GBP). May differ from the company code currency requiring FX translation for consolidated reporting.']
  transaction_number varchar [note: 'Externally-known business document number assigned by SAP S/4HANA FI at the time of financial posting (e.g., FI document number or material document number). Used for audit trail and cross-system reconciliation.']
  transaction_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the fuel cost transaction in the financial system. \'posted\' = FI document posted to GL; \'reversed\' = reversal document created; \'pending\' = awaiting invoice matching; \'disputed\' = under supplier dispute; \'cancelled\' = voided prior to posting.']
  unit_price decimal(18,6) [note: 'Price per litre (or per metric tonne where applicable) charged by the fuel supplier or into-plane agent for this uplift. Commercially sensitive. Used for fuel price variance analysis and hedge effectiveness measurement.']
  unit_price_currency varchar [note: 'ISO 4217 three-letter currency code in which the unit price is denominated (e.g., USD, EUR, GBP). Fuel is typically priced in USD at international stations; local currency may apply at domestic stations.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent modification to this fuel cost transaction record. Tracks reversals, price adjustments, and dispute resolutions for audit compliance.']
  uplift_source_type varchar [note: 'Method by which fuel was delivered to the aircraft: \'hydrant\' = airport hydrant system; \'bowser\' = mobile fuel tanker truck; \'pipeline\' = direct pipeline connection; \'barge\' = waterborne fuel barge (seaplane/amphibious operations). Affects into-plane service fee rates.']
  uplift_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time (UTC) when the physical fuel uplift occurred at the airport. This is the principal real-world business event time, distinct from the financial posting timestamp. Critical for matching operational fuel records to financial entries.']

  Note: 'Granular fuel uplift cost records linking each physical fuel uplift event (from flight operations) to its financial accounting entry. Captures fuel quantity (litres/kg), unit price, total cost, currency, fuel supplier, into-plane agent, airport IATA code, fuel type (Jet-A1, SAF), hedging contract reference, and cost centre allocation. Critical for CASK fuel component reporting and fuel hedge effectiveness measurement.'
}

Table finance.lease_contract {
  lease_contract_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate identifier for the IFRS 16 lease contract record within the finance data platform. Primary key for the lease_contract data product.']
  base_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to crew.crew_base. Business justification: Aircraft and facility leases associated with crew bases for base operating cost allocation, hub economics analysis, and IFRS 16 lease accounting. Essential for base profitability and cost attribution.']
  contract_manager_employee_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to workforce.employee. Business justification: Lease contracts (aircraft, facilities, equipment) managed by specific employees require relationship manager tracking for accountability, renewal negotiations, and contract administration per airline ']
  cost_centre_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.cost_centre. Business justification: Each lease contract is assigned to ONE cost center for cost allocation. The lease_contract table has cost_centre_code (STRING) which should be replaced with a FK to the cost_centre master.']
  vendor_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to procurement.vendor. Business justification: Lease contracts must link to vendor master for lessor payment terms, performance tracking, vendor consolidation reporting, and IFRS 16 disclosure requirements. Essential for lease accounting and vendo']
  asset_category varchar [note: 'High-level asset class of the underlying leased asset. Used for IFRS 16 disclosure note groupings (right-of-use asset classes) and fleet planning integration.']
  asset_registration varchar [note: 'ICAO/national civil aviation authority tail number or asset registration identifier for the leased aircraft or engine (e.g., N-number, G-prefix). Null for property leases and spare part pool agreements. Links to fleet planning and MRO systems.']
  commencement_date date [note: 'Date on which the lessor makes the underlying asset available for use by the lessee. This is the IFRS 16 commencement date from which the right-of-use (ROU) asset and lease liability are initially recognised on the balance sheet.']
  contract_currency varchar [note: 'ISO 4217 three-letter currency code in which lease payments are denominated (e.g., USD for US Dollar, EUR for Euro). Aircraft leases are predominantly USD-denominated. Drives FX translation to functional currency for IFRS reporting.']
  contract_number varchar [note: 'Externally-known, human-readable contract reference number assigned by the lessor or jointly agreed at execution. Used for correspondence, audit trails, and SAP S/4HANA FI-AA lease object identification.']
  contract_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the lease contract. Drives IFRS 16 accounting treatment and GL journal generation. \'modified\' indicates a lease modification event has been processed per IFRS 16 paragraph 44.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the lease contract record was first created in the finance data platform. Supports audit trail, data lineage, and IFRS 16 initial recognition date verification.']
  discount_rate_pct decimal(8,6) [note: 'Annual discount rate used to calculate the present value of future lease payments. Represents the rate implicit in the lease if readily determinable; otherwise the lessee\'s incremental borrowing rate (IBR) as determined by the treasury function. Stored as a decimal fraction (e.g., 0.045000 = 4.5%).']
  expiry_date date [note: 'Contractual end date of the non-cancellable lease term as stated in the executed agreement, before consideration of renewal or termination options. Used as the base for lease term calculation and amortisation schedule generation.']
  functional_currency varchar [note: 'ISO 4217 three-letter currency code of the reporting entity\'s functional currency. Used for translating the lease liability and ROU asset from contract currency to functional currency per IAS 21.']
  gl_lease_liability_account varchar [note: 'SAP S/4HANA GL account code for the lease liability balance sheet line. Supports automated posting of opening balance, interest accrual, and principal repayment journal entries.']
  gl_rou_asset_account varchar [note: 'SAP S/4HANA GL account code for the right-of-use asset balance sheet line. Used for automated journal entry generation for initial recognition, depreciation, and derecognition of the ROU asset.']
  governing_law varchar [note: 'Legal jurisdiction whose laws govern the lease contract (e.g., \'English Law\', \'New York Law\', \'Irish Law\'). Relevant for dispute resolution, enforcement, and cross-border lease structuring common in aviation.']
  ifrs16_exemption_type varchar [note: 'Indicates whether the lease qualifies for a recognition exemption under IFRS 16. \'short_term\' = lease term of 12 months or less at commencement; \'low_value\' = underlying asset value below the entity\'s low-value threshold (typically USD 5,000); \'none\' = full IFRS 16 recognition required.']
  last_modification_date date [note: 'Date of the most recent lease modification event (e.g., extension of lease term, change in lease payments, change in scope). Triggers remeasurement of the lease liability at the revised discount rate.']
  lease_liability_opening decimal(18,2) [note: 'Present value of the lease payments not yet made at the commencement date, discounted at the incremental borrowing rate (IBR) or the rate implicit in the lease. This is the initial recognition amount of the lease liability on the balance sheet.']
  lease_term_months varchar [note: 'Total lease term in whole months as determined under IFRS 16, including the non-cancellable period plus any optional renewal periods that the lessee is reasonably certain to exercise, and excluding optional termination periods the lessee is reasonably certain to exercise.']
  lease_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the lease by asset category and operational arrangement. Dry lease = aircraft without crew; wet lease / ACMI (Aircraft Crew Maintenance Insurance) = aircraft with crew, maintenance, and insurance; engine_lease = standalone engine; spare_part_pool = access agreement for pooled spare parts; property_lease = airport lounges, hangars, office space. Determines IFRS 16 scope and disclosure grouping.']
  lessor_country_code varchar [note: 'ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 country code of the lessor\'s registered jurisdiction. Relevant for withholding tax treatment, OECD transfer pricing, and cross-border lease disclosure.']
  maintenance_reserve_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the lease contract includes maintenance reserve payments (common in aircraft dry leases). Maintenance reserves are typically excluded from IFRS 16 lease liability measurement and treated as variable lease payments or prepaid maintenance.']
  modification_count varchar [note: 'Running count of the number of lease modifications processed against this contract since commencement. Each modification triggers remeasurement of the lease liability and ROU asset per IFRS 16 paragraphs 44–46. Supports audit trail and modification history tracking.']
  payment_frequency varchar [note: 'Frequency at which fixed lease payments are contractually due. Determines the period length used in the amortisation schedule and GL journal entry generation cadence.']
  payment_in_advance_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether lease payments are due at the beginning (True) or end (False) of each payment period. Affects the timing of interest expense accrual and principal repayment in the IFRS 16 amortisation schedule.']
  periodic_lease_payment decimal(18,2) [note: 'Fixed lease payment amount due per payment period (monthly, quarterly, or annually as defined by payment_frequency). Excludes variable lease payments not dependent on an index or rate. Used as the primary input to the amortisation schedule.']
  purchase_option_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the lease contract includes an option for the lessee to purchase the underlying asset. If the lessee is reasonably certain to exercise the purchase option, the option price is included in the lease liability measurement.']
  purchase_option_price decimal(18,2) [note: 'Contractual price at which the lessee may purchase the underlying asset if the purchase option is exercised. Included in the lease liability present value calculation when the lessee is reasonably certain to exercise. Null when purchase_option_flag is False.']
  renewal_option_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the lease contract contains one or more renewal options exercisable by the lessee. When True, renewal_option_reasonably_certain_flag must be assessed for IFRS 16 lease term determination.']
  renewal_option_reasonably_certain boolean [note: 'Management\'s assessment of whether the lessee is reasonably certain to exercise the renewal option. When True, the optional renewal period is included in the IFRS 16 lease term and the lease liability is remeasured accordingly.']
  residual_value_guarantee decimal(18,2) [note: 'Amount of any residual value guarantee provided by the lessee to the lessor, representing the lessee\'s exposure to the residual value of the leased asset at lease end. Included in the IFRS 16 lease liability measurement as a lease payment.']
  rou_asset_value decimal(18,2) [note: 'Initial carrying amount of the right-of-use (ROU) asset recognised on the balance sheet at the lease commencement date, comprising the initial lease liability measurement plus any initial direct costs, prepaid lease payments, and estimated dismantling costs, less any lease incentives received. Expressed in the contract currency.']
  security_deposit_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Cash security deposit paid to the lessor at lease commencement, refundable at lease expiry subject to asset return conditions. Classified as a financial asset (receivable) on the lessee\'s balance sheet, separate from the lease liability.']
  termination_option_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the lease contract contains a termination option exercisable by the lessee before the contractual expiry date. Affects IFRS 16 lease term and liability measurement.']
  termination_option_reasonably_certain boolean [note: 'Management\'s assessment of whether the lessee is reasonably certain to exercise the termination option. When True, the lease term is shortened to the termination date and the liability is remeasured.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the lease contract record in the finance data platform. Used for incremental data pipeline processing, change detection, and audit compliance.']
  variable_payment_terms varchar [note: 'Free-text description of any variable lease payment clauses, including index-linked escalation (e.g., CPI, LIBOR/SOFR), utilisation-based payments (e.g., per flight hour for ACMI), or maintenance reserve adjustments. Required for IFRS 16 disclosure notes.']

  Note: 'IFRS 16 lease contract master and full amortisation schedule for aircraft dry leases, wet leases (ACMI), engine leases, spare part pool access agreements, and property leases (airport lounges, hangars, office space). Stores lessor details, lease commencement and expiry dates, lease term, right-of-use asset value, lease liability opening balance, discount rate (incremental borrowing rate), variable lease payment terms, renewal/termination options, modification history, and complete period-by-period liability amortisation schedule (opening balance, interest expense, lease payment, principal repayment, closing balance). Supports automated GL journal entry generation, IFRS 16 disclosure notes, lease vs buy analysis, fleet planning integration, and auditor reconciliation.'
}

Table finance.lease_liability_schedule {
  lease_liability_schedule_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate identifier for each lease liability amortisation schedule record in the IFRS 16 ledger. Primary key for this table.']
  cost_centre_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.cost_centre. Business justification: Each lease liability schedule line is assigned to ONE cost center for cost allocation. The lease_liability_schedule table has cost_centre_code (STRING) which should be replaced with a FK to the cost_c']
  general_ledger_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.general_ledger. Business justification: Each lease liability schedule line posts to ONE GL account. The lease_liability_schedule table has gl_account_code (STRING) which should be replaced with a FK to the general_ledger master. This links ']
  lease_contract_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the parent lease contract master record from which this amortisation schedule is derived. Links each period row back to the originating lease agreement.']
  asset_registration varchar [note: 'ICAO/national civil aviation authority aircraft registration mark (e.g., N-number, G-prefix) or property/equipment asset identifier for the underlying leased asset. Links the financial schedule to the physical asset in the fleet or property register.']
  closing_balance decimal(18,2) [note: 'Carrying amount of the lease liability at the end of the period (opening balance + interest expense − principal repayment). Becomes the next period\'s opening balance. Key IFRS 16 balance sheet figure.']
  commencement_date date [note: 'Date on which the lessor makes the underlying asset available for use by the lessee. This is the date from which the right-of-use (ROU) asset and lease liability are initially recognised on the balance sheet.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this lease liability schedule record was first created in the data platform, in ISO 8601 format (yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX). Supports audit trail and data lineage requirements.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code in which the lease liability amounts are denominated (e.g., USD, EUR, GBP). Aircraft leases are frequently denominated in USD regardless of the airline\'s functional currency.']
  discount_rate decimal(10,6) [note: 'The interest rate used to discount future lease payments to present value, typically the lessee\'s incremental borrowing rate (IBR) or the implicit rate in the lease if determinable. Expressed as an annual decimal (e.g., 0.045 = 4.5%).']
  extension_option_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the lease term used in the liability calculation includes one or more extension option periods that management has assessed as reasonably certain to be exercised.']
  fiscal_period varchar [note: 'Numeric accounting period (1–12 for monthly, 1–4 for quarterly) within the fiscal year. Drives journal entry posting period in SAP S/4HANA FI.']
  fiscal_year varchar [note: 'Four-digit fiscal year to which this amortisation period belongs, as defined in the airline\'s SAP S/4HANA fiscal year variant. Used for annual IFRS 16 disclosure aggregation.']
  functional_currency_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 4217 code for the airline\'s functional currency used for consolidated financial reporting. Enables FX translation of foreign-currency lease liabilities for IFRS disclosure.']
  fx_rate decimal(18,6) [note: 'Exchange rate applied to translate lease liability amounts from the lease currency to the functional currency at the period end date. Sourced from the treasury system or SAP S/4HANA exchange rate table.']
  initial_recognition_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Present value of future lease payments at the commencement date, representing the lease liability recognised on the balance sheet at inception. Equals the initial ROU asset cost (before initial direct costs and lease incentives).']
  interest_expense decimal(18,2) [note: 'Finance charge accrued during the period calculated by applying the incremental borrowing rate (or implicit rate) to the opening lease liability balance. Posted to the P&L interest expense account.']
  journal_entry_document_number varchar [note: 'SAP S/4HANA FI accounting document number assigned when the IFRS 16 journal entry is posted. Provides a direct audit trail from the amortisation schedule row to the posted ledger document.']
  journal_entry_posted_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the automated IFRS 16 journal entries (interest accrual and principal repayment) for this period have been successfully posted to the SAP S/4HANA general ledger. Supports period-close control and reconciliation.']
  lease_end_date date [note: 'Contractual end date of the lease term, including any reasonably certain extension options. Used to determine the remaining lease term for present value calculations and maturity analysis disclosures.']
  lease_payment decimal(18,2) [note: 'Total cash payment made to the lessor during the period, comprising both the principal repayment and the interest component. Corresponds to the cash outflow in the statement of cash flows.']
  lease_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the lease contract as of the period. Drives whether amortisation rows continue to be generated and whether remeasurement events have occurred.']
  lease_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the lease by asset category and IFRS 16 lease classification (finance vs. operating). Aircraft leases dominate the airline balance sheet; property leases cover airport lounges, offices, and hangars. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: aircraft_operating|aircraft_finance|property_operating|property_finance|equipment_operating|equipment_finance — promote to reference product]']
  lessor_name varchar [note: 'Legal name of the lessor (aircraft leasing company, property owner, or equipment provider) from whom the asset is leased. Common aviation lessors include AerCap, Air Lease Corporation, SMBC Aviation Capital.']
  low_value_asset_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the lease qualifies for the IFRS 16 low-value asset exemption (underlying asset value when new is approximately USD 5,000 or below). When true, payments are expensed and no liability is recognised.']
  opening_balance decimal(18,2) [note: 'Carrying amount of the lease liability at the beginning of the period before any payments or interest accrual. Equals the prior period\'s closing balance. Core IFRS 16 disclosure figure.']
  period_end_date date [note: 'Last calendar date of the amortisation period covered by this schedule row. Typically the last day of the accounting month or quarter.']
  period_sequence varchar [note: 'Sequential integer identifying the ordinal position of this amortisation period within the full lease term (e.g., 1 = first period, 24 = twenty-fourth period). Enables ordered traversal of the schedule.']
  period_start_date date [note: 'First calendar date of the amortisation period covered by this schedule row. Aligns with the accounting period open date in the general ledger.']
  principal_repayment decimal(18,2) [note: 'Portion of the lease payment that reduces the lease liability principal (lease payment minus interest expense). Classified as a financing cash outflow under IAS 7.']
  profit_centre_code varchar [note: 'SAP S/4HANA CO profit centre associated with the leased asset, enabling segment-level IFRS 16 reporting (e.g., narrow-body fleet, wide-body fleet, cargo operations).']
  remeasurement_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Adjustment to the lease liability arising from a remeasurement event (e.g., lease modification, change in extension option assessment, change in index/rate). Positive values increase the liability; negative values decrease it.']
  remeasurement_date date [note: 'Date on which the lease liability was remeasured due to a triggering event such as a lease modification, reassessment of extension option, or change in variable lease payments linked to an index. Null if no remeasurement occurred in this period.']
  remeasurement_reason varchar [note: 'Reason code explaining why the lease liability was remeasured in this period. Required for IFRS 16 disclosure notes and auditor reconciliation. Null if no remeasurement occurred.']
  short_term_lease_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the lease qualifies for the IFRS 16 short-term lease exemption (lease term of 12 months or less at commencement). When true, payments are expensed on a straight-line basis and no liability is recognised.']
  termination_option_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the lease contains a termination option and whether management has assessed it as reasonably certain NOT to be exercised (i.e., the full term is included in the liability measurement).']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to this lease liability schedule record, in ISO 8601 format. Captures remeasurement postings, corrections, and period-close adjustments.']
  variable_lease_payment decimal(18,2) [note: 'Lease payments that depend on an index or rate (e.g., CPI-linked rent escalations) included in the lease liability measurement. Excludes purely variable payments not based on an index or rate, which are expensed as incurred.']

  Note: 'IFRS 16 lease liability amortisation schedule records for each lease contract, detailing period-by-period opening balance, interest expense, lease payment, principal repayment, and closing balance. Supports automated journal entry generation for right-of-use asset depreciation and interest accrual. Enables IFRS 16 disclosure notes and auditor reconciliation for aircraft and property leases.'
}

Table finance.interline_billing {
  interline_billing_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the interline billing record. Primary key.']
  cost_centre_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.cost_centre. Business justification: Each interline billing record is assigned to ONE cost center for cost allocation. The interline_billing table has cost_center_code (STRING) which should be replaced with a FK to the cost_centre master']
  general_ledger_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.general_ledger. Business justification: Each interline billing record posts to ONE GL account. The interline_billing table has gl_account_code (STRING) which should be replaced with a FK to the general_ledger master. This links interline se']
  accounting_period varchar [note: 'Accounting period (YYYY-MM format) to which this interline billing transaction is posted in the general ledger. Used for financial reporting and period close.']
  awb_number varchar [note: 'Eleven-digit Air Waybill number (format: 3-digit airline code + 8-digit serial number) for cargo interline billing. Links billing to the cargo shipment.']
  billing_document_number varchar [note: 'Unique externally-known billing document number or reference assigned to this interline billing transaction. Used for reconciliation and audit trail.']
  billing_period_end_date date [note: 'End date of the billing period covered by this interline billing record. Defines the close of the settlement window.']
  billing_period_start_date date [note: 'Start date of the billing period covered by this interline billing record. Defines the beginning of the settlement window.']
  billing_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the interline billing record. Tracks the billing through submission, acceptance, dispute resolution, and final settlement stages.']
  billing_type varchar [note: 'Type of interline billing transaction. Categorizes the nature of the settlement: passenger ticketing, cargo waybill, ancillary services, or billing adjustment.']
  cabin_class varchar [note: 'Cabin class of service for the interline segment. Used for proration calculation and revenue allocation between partner airlines.']
  commission_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Commission or handling fee amount deducted from the gross billing amount. May represent agent commission or interline handling charges.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this interline billing record was first created in the system. Audit trail for record origination.']
  destination_airport_iata_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter IATA airport code for the destination of the interline segment. Identifies the arrival point for billing allocation.']
  dispute_reason_code varchar [note: 'Standardized code indicating the reason for billing dispute if status is disputed. Used for tracking and resolving interline billing disagreements.']
  exchange_rate decimal(12,6) [note: 'Currency exchange rate applied to convert original transaction currency to settlement currency. Used for multi-currency interline billing reconciliation.']
  fare_basis_code varchar [note: 'Fare basis code associated with the ticket or segment being billed. Defines the fare rules and proration methodology for interline revenue sharing.']
  flight_date date [note: 'Scheduled departure date of the flight segment associated with this interline billing transaction. Key date for revenue recognition and settlement timing.']
  flight_number varchar [note: 'Flight number associated with the interline segment being billed. Combines airline code and numeric flight identifier.']
  gross_billing_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Total gross amount billed before any adjustments, fees, or commissions. Base amount for the interline transaction.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this interline billing record was last updated. Audit trail for tracking changes and dispute resolution activity.']
  net_settlement_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Final net amount to be settled between airlines after all adjustments, commissions, and taxes. This is the actual payable or receivable amount.']
  origin_airport_iata_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter IATA airport code for the origin of the interline segment. Identifies the departure point for billing allocation.']
  original_currency_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code of the original transaction before currency conversion for settlement. Used for reconciliation and audit purposes.']
  partner_airline_iata_code varchar [note: 'Two-character IATA airline designator code of the partner carrier involved in the interline agreement. Identifies the counterparty airline for billing settlement.']
  pnr_locator varchar [note: 'Six-character alphanumeric Passenger Name Record locator code associated with this interline billing transaction. Links billing to the original passenger reservation.']
  proration_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Revenue or cost amount allocated to the partner airline based on interline proration rules. Represents the partner\'s share of the total ticket or waybill value.']
  remarks varchar [note: 'Free-text remarks or notes associated with this interline billing record. Used for clarifications, special instructions, or dispute documentation.']
  settlement_currency_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code in which the interline settlement is denominated. Defines the currency for all monetary amounts in this billing record.']
  settlement_date date [note: 'Actual date when the interline billing was settled and payment was processed. Null if settlement is pending.']
  settlement_due_date date [note: 'Date by which the interline settlement payment is due. Defines the payment deadline per IATA settlement cycle rules.']
  settlement_method varchar [note: 'Method used for interline settlement. BSP (Billing and Settlement Plan) for passenger, ARC (Airlines Reporting Corporation) for US market, CASS (Cargo Accounts Settlement System) for cargo, direct bilateral billing, or ICH (IATA Clearing House).']
  tax_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Total tax amount included in the interline billing. Covers applicable aviation taxes, fees, and surcharges allocated to the partner carrier.']
  ticket_number varchar [note: 'Thirteen-digit electronic ticket number (e-ticket) associated with this interline billing record. Unique identifier for the passenger ticket in interline transactions.']
  transaction_type varchar [note: 'Specific transaction type within the billing record. Indicates whether this is a debit memo (amount owed to partner), credit memo (amount owed by partner), proration allocation, or final settlement.']

  Note: 'Interline billing and settlement records for revenue and cost flows between the airline and partner carriers under interline ticketing agreements. Captures IATA billing and settlement plan (BSP) transactions, interline proration amounts, debit memos, credit memos, billing period, partner airline IATA code, and settlement currency. Supports IATA CASS (Cargo Accounts Settlement System) and passenger interline reconciliation.'
}

Table finance.treasury_position {
  treasury_position_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate identifier for each treasury cash and liquidity position record in the silver layer lakehouse. Primary key for this data product.']
  cash_pool_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the notional or physical cash pool to which this bank account belongs. Supports zero-balance sweeping, notional pooling, and group-level liquidity optimisation across legal entities.']
  company_code_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.company_code. Business justification: Each treasury position belongs to ONE legal entity (company code). The treasury_position table has legal_entity_code (STRING) which should be replaced with a FK to the company_code master. Treasury po']
  cost_centre_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.cost_centre. Business justification: Each treasury position can be assigned to ONE cost center for cost allocation. The treasury_position table has cost_centre_code (STRING) which should be replaced with a FK to the cost_centre master.']
  general_ledger_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.general_ledger. Business justification: Each treasury position is linked to ONE GL account. The treasury_position table has gl_account_code (STRING) which should be replaced with a FK to the general_ledger master. This links treasury positi']
  treasury_officer_employee_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to workforce.employee. Business justification: Treasury positions managed by specific treasury officers require accountability tracking for cash management, investment decisions, and regulatory compliance (SOX, internal controls) per airline treas']
  balance_amount decimal(20,4) [note: 'The gross cash or investment balance in the transaction currency for this position record. Positive values represent assets/inflows; negative values represent liabilities/outflows or overdraft positions. Core monetary field for daily cash management.']
  balance_amount_usd decimal(20,4) [note: 'The balance amount translated to US Dollars (USD) using the applicable FX rate on the position date. Used for consolidated group-level cash reporting, CASK analysis, and DOT financial reporting in USD.']
  balance_type varchar [note: 'Classifies the nature of the balance recorded: \'actual\' (confirmed bank balance), \'forecast\' (projected inflow/outflow), \'committed\' (contractually obligated but not yet settled), \'projected\' (model-driven estimate), \'opening\' (start-of-day balance), \'closing\' (end-of-day balance). Supports daily cash management and IROP contingency liquidity planning.']
  bank_account_iban varchar [note: 'The International Bank Account Number (IBAN) for the bank account associated with this position, where applicable (primarily European and SEPA-zone accounts). Classified as confidential financial data.']
  bank_account_name varchar [note: 'The descriptive name of the bank account as registered with the counterparty bank (e.g., \'Airlines Operating Account USD - JPMorgan NYC\'). Used for human-readable identification in cash management dashboards.']
  bank_account_number varchar [note: 'The bank account number associated with this treasury position. Classified as confidential financial data. Used for bank account reconciliation and cash pooling identification.']
  bank_statement_reference varchar [note: 'The bank-assigned statement or transaction reference number from the SWIFT MT940 or ISO 20022 camt.053 bank statement. Used for bank account reconciliation matching in SAP S/4HANA FI-BL.']
  bsp_settlement_period varchar [note: 'The IATA BSP or ARC settlement period (YYYY-MM format) to which this interline or agency settlement cash flow belongs. Populated only when interline_settlement_flag is True. Used for BSP reconciliation and interline billing dispute resolution.']
  counterparty_bank_code varchar [note: 'The BIC (Bank Identifier Code) / SWIFT code of the counterparty bank holding this position. Used for FX exposure reporting, counterparty credit risk monitoring, and debt covenant compliance.']
  counterparty_bank_name varchar [note: 'Full legal name of the counterparty bank or financial institution holding this treasury position (e.g., \'JPMorgan Chase Bank N.A.\'). Used in FX exposure and counterparty risk reports.']
  country_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 country code of the jurisdiction where the bank account is domiciled (e.g., USA, GBR, DEU). Used for regulatory reporting, capital controls monitoring, and country-level FX exposure aggregation.']
  covenant_breach_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the current balance_amount is below the covenant_threshold_amount, triggering a potential debt covenant breach. True = covenant breach condition; False = compliant. Drives automated treasury alerts.']
  covenant_threshold_amount decimal(20,4) [note: 'The minimum cash or liquidity balance required under debt covenant agreements with lenders, in the transaction currency. Used for debt covenant monitoring and breach early-warning reporting. Null if no covenant applies to this account.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The timestamp when this treasury position record was first created in the data platform, in ISO 8601 format (yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX). Supports audit trail and SOX compliance.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for the denomination of this treasury position (e.g., USD, EUR, GBP). Drives FX exposure calculation and multi-currency cash reporting.']
  facility_limit_amount decimal(20,4) [note: 'The total approved credit limit of the bank facility (revolving credit line, overdraft facility) in the transaction currency. Used for bank facility utilisation monitoring and debt covenant compliance reporting.']
  facility_utilised_amount decimal(20,4) [note: 'The amount currently drawn down against the bank facility limit on the position date. The difference between facility_limit_amount and this field represents available headroom. Used for IROP contingency liquidity planning.']
  fx_rate decimal(18,8) [note: 'The exchange rate applied to translate the position currency to USD on the position date. Sourced from the treasury system\'s daily FX rate feed. Used for FX exposure reporting and IAS 21 translation.']
  fx_rate_type varchar [note: 'Identifies the type of FX rate used for translation: \'spot\' (real-time market rate), \'average\' (period average rate), \'closing\' (period-end rate), \'budget\' (planning rate), \'hedge\' (contractual hedge rate). Determines the accounting treatment under IAS 21.']
  hedge_designation varchar [note: 'Indicates whether this position is designated as a hedging instrument under IFRS 9 hedge accounting: \'fair_value_hedge\', \'cash_flow_hedge\', \'net_investment_hedge\', or \'not_designated\'. Drives hedge effectiveness testing and P&L reclassification.']
  ifrs16_lease_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this treasury position relates to an IFRS 16 lease liability payment obligation (e.g., aircraft dry lease or wet lease financing). True = IFRS 16 lease-related cash flow; False = non-lease treasury position.']
  instrument_type varchar [note: 'The specific financial instrument type underlying this treasury position. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: demand_deposit|term_deposit|money_market_fund|commercial_paper|treasury_bill|revolving_credit|overdraft|certificate_of_deposit|repo|fx_forward — promote to reference product]']
  interest_rate decimal(10,6) [note: 'The annual interest rate (as a decimal, e.g., 0.045 = 4.5%) applicable to this treasury position for time deposits, money market funds, or revolving credit facilities. Used for interest income/expense accrual and yield analysis.']
  interline_settlement_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this treasury position is associated with an interline billing settlement (IATA BSP or ARC settlement cycle). True = interline settlement cash flow; False = standard treasury position. Supports interline prorate reconciliation.']
  irop_reserve_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this treasury position is designated as an IROP (Irregular Operations) contingency liquidity reserve. True = reserved for IROP events (e.g., mass rebooking, hotel accommodation, compensation payouts); False = general operating liquidity.']
  liquidity_category varchar [note: 'Classifies the type of liquid asset or instrument: \'cash_and_equivalents\' (demand deposits), \'money_market_fund\', \'commercial_paper\', \'time_deposit\', \'bank_facility\' (revolving credit facility drawdown), \'restricted_cash\' (escrowed or pledged funds). Aligns with IAS 7 cash and cash equivalents classification.']
  maturity_date date [note: 'The date on which a short-term investment (time deposit, commercial paper, money market fund) matures and principal is returned. Null for demand deposits and revolving facilities. Critical for maturity profile and liquidity ladder reporting.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text notes or commentary entered by the treasury analyst regarding this position record (e.g., reason for manual adjustment, covenant waiver details, IROP reserve activation rationale). Supports audit trail and operational transparency.']
  position_date date [note: 'The business date for which this cash or liquidity position is recorded. Used as the primary time dimension for daily cash management, bank reconciliation, and FX exposure reporting.']
  position_reference varchar [note: 'Externally-known business identifier for the treasury position record, sourced from SAP S/4HANA FI-TR or the bank\'s statement reference. Used for reconciliation and audit trail across systems.']
  position_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the treasury position record: \'open\' (pending confirmation), \'confirmed\' (bank-confirmed balance), \'reconciled\' (matched to bank statement), \'disputed\' (discrepancy under investigation), \'closed\' (period-end finalised), \'cancelled\' (voided entry).']
  reconciliation_status varchar [note: 'Current reconciliation state of this position against the bank statement: \'unreconciled\' (not yet matched), \'matched\' (fully reconciled to bank statement), \'partially_matched\' (partial match with outstanding difference), \'exception\' (reconciliation exception requiring investigation).']
  source_system varchar [note: 'Identifies the operational system of record from which this treasury position record was sourced: \'SAP_S4HANA\' (FI-TR module), \'BANK_DIRECT\' (bank portal feed), \'SWIFT_MT940\' (bank statement message), \'KYRIBA\' (treasury management system), \'MANUAL\' (manually entered adjustment).']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The timestamp when this treasury position record was last modified in the data platform, in ISO 8601 format (yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX). Tracks balance revisions, reconciliation updates, and status changes for audit compliance.']
  value_date date [note: 'The date on which the funds are actually available or settled in the bank account, which may differ from the position date due to clearing cycles or float. Critical for accurate liquidity planning.']

  Note: 'Treasury cash and liquidity position records tracking the airlines cash balances, short-term investments (money market funds, commercial paper, time deposits), foreign currency exposures, and bank facility utilisation across all bank accounts, currencies, and legal entities. Stores position date, bank account, currency, balance type (actual/forecast/committed), liquidity category, counterparty bank, and maturity profile. Supports daily cash management, bank account reconciliation, FX exposure reporting, debt covenant monitoring, and IROP (Irregular Operations) contingency liquidity planning.'
}

Table finance.hedge_contract {
  hedge_contract_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the hedge contract record. Primary key.']
  fuel_contract_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to procurement.fuel_contract. Business justification: Fuel hedging programs must link hedge instruments to specific fuel supply contracts for hedge effectiveness testing, P&L attribution, and IFRS 9 hedge accounting documentation. Required for fuel risk ']
  collateral_currency varchar [note: 'ISO 4217 three-letter currency code in which collateral is posted. Null if no collateral posted.']
  collateral_posted_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Amount of cash or securities posted as collateral to the counterparty under Credit Support Annex (CSA) terms. Null if no collateral arrangement exists.']
  contract_number varchar [note: 'Externally-known unique business identifier for the hedge contract, used in treasury operations and external reporting.']
  contract_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the hedge contract, indicating whether it is in force, expired, or terminated early.']
  counterparty_identifier varchar [note: 'Unique identifier for the counterparty, typically LEI (Legal Entity Identifier) or internal counterparty code.']
  counterparty_name varchar [note: 'Legal name of the financial institution or counterparty with whom the hedge contract is executed.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this hedge contract record was first created in the system.']
