  cumulative_gain_loss decimal(18,2) [note: 'Cumulative realized and unrealized gain or loss on the hedge contract since inception, used for hedge accounting reserve calculations.']
  currency_pair varchar [note: 'ISO 4217 currency pair for foreign exchange hedges (e.g., USD/EUR, GBP/USD). Null for non-FX hedges.']
  documentation_complete_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether all required IFRS 9 hedge accounting documentation (hedge designation memo, effectiveness testing methodology) has been completed and approved.']
  effective_date date [note: 'Date from which the hedge contract becomes active and hedge accounting treatment begins.']
  effectiveness_test_date date [note: 'Date on which the most recent hedge effectiveness test was performed.']
  fair_value_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Current fair value (mark-to-market) of the hedge instrument as of the last valuation date, used for balance sheet reporting.']
  fair_value_currency varchar [note: 'ISO 4217 three-letter currency code in which the fair value is denominated.']
  fair_value_date date [note: 'Date as of which the fair value was calculated, typically month-end or quarter-end for financial reporting.']
  hedge_designation varchar [note: 'Formal hedge accounting designation under IFRS 9, determining how gains and losses are recognized in financial statements.']
  hedge_effectiveness_test_result varchar [note: 'Result of the most recent prospective or retrospective hedge effectiveness test under IFRS 9, determining continued eligibility for hedge accounting.']
  hedge_ratio decimal(5,4) [note: 'Ratio of the hedging instrument quantity to the hedged item quantity, used in hedge effectiveness calculations (e.g., 1.0000 for perfect hedge).']
  hedged_item_description varchar [note: 'Textual description of the specific forecasted transaction or recognized asset/liability being hedged (e.g., Q3 2024 jet fuel purchases for transatlantic routes).']
  instrument_type varchar [note: 'Type of financial derivative instrument used for hedging. Determines the contractual structure and settlement mechanism.']
  isda_master_agreement_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this hedge contract is governed by an ISDA Master Agreement with the counterparty.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this hedge contract record was last updated, capturing any changes to contract terms, valuations, or status.']
  maturity_date date [note: 'Date on which the hedge contract expires or settles, marking the end of the hedging relationship.']
  notional_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Principal amount or volume of the underlying exposure being hedged (e.g., barrels of fuel, currency units, interest principal).']
  notional_unit varchar [note: 'Unit of measure for the notional amount (barrels for fuel, currency code for FX, basis points for interest rate). [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: barrels|gallons|metric_tons|usd|eur|gbp|basis_points — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  premium_currency varchar [note: 'ISO 4217 three-letter currency code in which the premium was paid.']
  premium_paid decimal(18,2) [note: 'Upfront premium paid to acquire the hedge instrument (primarily for options and collars). Zero for forwards and swaps with no upfront cost.']
  settlement_date date [note: 'Actual date on which cash settlement or physical delivery occurred. Null if contract has not yet settled.']
  strike_price decimal(18,4) [note: 'Agreed price or rate at which the hedge instrument can be exercised (for options) or settled (for forwards/swaps). Null for instruments without a fixed strike.']
  termination_date date [note: 'Date on which the hedge contract was terminated early, if applicable. Null for contracts that matured naturally or are still active.']
  termination_reason varchar [note: 'Business reason for early termination of the hedge contract (e.g., hedged item no longer expected, counterparty credit event, portfolio rebalancing). Null if not terminated.']
  trade_date date [note: 'Date on which the hedge contract was executed and agreed between the airline and counterparty.']
  underlying_commodity_code varchar [note: 'Standardized code for the specific commodity being hedged (e.g., JET for jet fuel, BRENT for Brent crude). Null for non-commodity hedges.']
  underlying_type varchar [note: 'Category of the underlying asset or rate being hedged (fuel commodity, currency pair, interest rate benchmark, or carbon allowance).']

  Note: 'Financial hedging instrument master records for the airline\'s fuel price hedging, foreign currency hedging, and interest rate hedging programmes. Captures instrument type (swap, option, forward, collar), underlying commodity or currency, notional amount, strike price, premium paid, hedge designation (cash flow / fair value), effectiveness test results, and maturity date. Supports IFRS 9 hedge accounting documentation.'
}

Table finance.tax_transaction {
  tax_transaction_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate identifier for each tax posting record in the finance domain. Primary key for the tax_transaction data product.']
  company_code_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.company_code. Business justification: Each tax transaction belongs to ONE company code. The tax_transaction table has company_code (STRING) which should be replaced with a FK to the company_code master. Tax transactions are company-code s']
  cost_centre_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.cost_centre. Business justification: Each tax transaction can be assigned to ONE cost center for cost allocation. The tax_transaction table has cost_center (STRING) which should be replaced with a FK to the cost_centre master.']
  general_ledger_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the general ledger account to which this tax posting is recorded, aligning with SAP S/4HANA FI chart of accounts.']
  profile_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the customer or passenger party associated with this tax transaction, applicable for output VAT/GST on ancillary services, passenger facility charges, and airport taxes.']
  tax_employee_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to workforce.employee. Business justification: Tax transactions related to employee benefits, payroll taxes, expatriate tax equalization require employee linkage for compliance reporting, tax certificate generation, and employee tax statement prep']
  vendor_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the vendor or supplier party associated with this tax transaction, applicable for withholding tax on vendor payments and input VAT/GST recoveries.']
  airport_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter IATA airport code associated with this tax transaction, applicable for airport taxes, Passenger Facility Charges (PFC), and slot-related levies. Identifies the airport at which the tax obligation arises.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The timestamp when this tax transaction record was first created in the data platform, in ISO 8601 format (yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX). Supports audit trail and data lineage requirements.']
  document_date date [note: 'The date of the originating source document (invoice, receipt, or tax certificate) that triggered this tax posting. May differ from posting_date due to processing lag.']
  document_number varchar [note: 'Externally-known accounting document number assigned by SAP S/4HANA FI at the time of tax posting. Used as the primary business reference for audit and reconciliation.']
  exchange_rate decimal(18,6) [note: 'The foreign exchange rate applied to convert the transaction currency tax amounts to local currency at the posting date. Sourced from SAP FI exchange rate tables aligned with IATA rate publications.']
  filing_reference varchar [note: 'The reference number assigned by the tax authority or internal tax filing system when this tax transaction is included in a periodic tax return or remittance filing (e.g., VAT return reference, PFC quarterly report number, WHT certificate number).']
  fiscal_period varchar [note: 'The fiscal period number (1–16 in SAP, including special periods) within the fiscal year to which this tax transaction is assigned. Supports period-end close and tax accrual reporting.']
  fiscal_year varchar [note: 'The fiscal year to which this tax transaction is assigned in the airline\'s financial calendar. Used for annual tax provision, corporate income tax filing, and IFRS financial statement preparation.']
  is_recoverable boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this input tax amount is recoverable (reclaimable) from the tax authority as a credit against output tax liability. True for recoverable input VAT/GST; False for irrecoverable taxes such as non-deductible VAT on entertainment or blocked input tax.']
  local_currency_code varchar [note: 'ISO 4217 three-letter code for the company code\'s local (functional) currency (e.g., USD for a US entity, EUR for a German entity). Used for statutory reporting and local tax filings.']
  local_currency_tax_amount decimal(18,4) [note: 'The tax amount translated into the company code\'s local (functional) currency using the exchange rate at posting date. Required for statutory financial reporting and local tax authority filings.']
  net_amount decimal(18,4) [note: 'The net amount after tax, representing the total transaction value inclusive of tax (taxable_base_amount + tax_amount). Used for financial statement presentation and settlement reconciliation.']
  posting_date date [note: 'The date on which the tax transaction was posted to the general ledger. Determines the fiscal period assignment and is the principal business event date for this transaction.']
  profit_center varchar [note: 'SAP CO profit center associated with this tax transaction, enabling profitability analysis by route, fleet type, or business unit. Supports RASK (Revenue per Available Seat Kilometer) and segment reporting.']
  remittance_date date [note: 'The date on which the tax amount was remitted (paid) to the relevant tax authority. Null until payment is made. Used to confirm compliance with statutory payment deadlines and avoid late payment penalties.']
  reversal_document_number varchar [note: 'The SAP FI document number of the reversal posting if this tax transaction has been reversed or cancelled. Null for active postings. Supports audit trail and reconciliation of corrected tax entries.']
  reversal_reason_code varchar [note: 'SAP FI reversal reason code explaining why this tax posting was reversed (e.g., data entry error, duplicate posting, credit note issued). Null for non-reversed transactions.']
  source_document_reference varchar [note: 'The reference number of the originating source document (e.g., vendor invoice number, AWB number for cargo customs, PNR for passenger-related taxes, BSP billing reference). Enables traceability from tax posting back to the originating business transaction.']
  source_document_type varchar [note: 'Type of the originating business document that generated this tax posting. INVOICE=vendor or customer invoice; CREDIT_NOTE=credit memo; PAYMENT=payment document; JOURNAL=manual journal entry; CUSTOMS_DECL=customs declaration for cargo; PFC_REPORT=Passenger Facility Charge quarterly report; WHT_CERT=withholding tax certificate. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: INVOICE|CREDIT_NOTE|PAYMENT|JOURNAL|CUSTOMS_DECL|PFC_REPORT|WHT_CERT — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  tax_amount decimal(18,4) [note: 'The calculated tax amount in the transaction currency. Represents the actual tax liability or recoverable tax for this posting. Core monetary component of the MONETARY_TRIPLET.']
  tax_authority_name varchar [note: 'Name of the government or regulatory body to which this tax is remitted (e.g., \'IRS\', \'HMRC\', \'Bundeszentralamt für Steuern\', \'FAA - PFC\'). Required for tax filing and regulatory reporting.']
  tax_code varchar [note: 'SAP FI-TX tax code identifying the applicable tax type and rate combination (e.g., V1 for standard VAT, W1 for withholding tax, PF for Passenger Facility Charge). Drives automatic tax calculation and account determination.']
  tax_direction varchar [note: 'Indicates whether this is an input tax (recoverable tax paid on purchases/vendor invoices) or output tax (tax collected from customers on sales of ancillary services and tickets). Drives VAT/GST net settlement calculation.']
  tax_jurisdiction_code varchar [note: 'Code identifying the tax jurisdiction (country, state, or municipality) that levies this tax. Supports multi-jurisdiction tax compliance across the airline\'s global route network. Aligns with SAP FI-TX jurisdiction configuration.']
  tax_jurisdiction_name varchar [note: 'Human-readable name of the tax jurisdiction (e.g., \'United States Federal\', \'Germany VAT\', \'New York State\', \'London Heathrow Airport\'). Supports tax reporting and audit documentation.']
  tax_period varchar [note: 'The fiscal tax reporting period in YYYY-MM format to which this tax transaction belongs. Used for VAT/GST return filing, withholding tax remittance, and corporate income tax provision reporting.']
  tax_rate decimal(8,4) [note: 'The applicable tax rate as a percentage (e.g., 20.0000 for 20% VAT, 7.5000 for 7.5% WHT). Sourced from the tax code configuration in SAP FI-TX and validated against jurisdiction-specific statutory rates.']
  tax_return_period varchar [note: 'The period identifier for the tax return in which this transaction is reported to the tax authority. May be monthly (YYYY-MM) or quarterly (YYYY-Q1 through YYYY-Q4) depending on jurisdiction requirements.']
  tax_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the tax category applicable to this transaction. VAT=Value Added Tax, GST=Goods and Services Tax, WHT=Withholding Tax, PFC=Passenger Facility Charge, AIRPORT_TAX=Airport-levied tax, CORP_INCOME_TAX=Corporate Income Tax provision, EXCISE=Fuel/aviation excise duty, CUSTOMS=Import/export customs duty on cargo. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: VAT|GST|WHT|PFC|AIRPORT_TAX|CORP_INCOME_TAX|EXCISE|CUSTOMS — 8 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  taxable_base_amount decimal(18,4) [note: 'The gross monetary amount on which the tax is calculated, expressed in the transaction currency. For VAT/GST this is the net invoice value; for PFC it is the per-passenger charge base; for WHT it is the gross vendor payment.']
  transaction_currency varchar [note: 'ISO 4217 three-letter currency code of the transaction in which the tax amounts are denominated (e.g., USD, EUR, GBP). Supports multi-currency operations across the airline\'s global network.']
  transaction_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the tax posting in SAP FI. POSTED=active tax document posted to GL; REVERSED=document reversed/cancelled; CLEARED=tax settled/remitted to authority; PARKED=saved but not yet posted; BLOCKED=held for review.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The timestamp when this tax transaction record was last modified in the data platform, in ISO 8601 format (yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX). Tracks the most recent change for audit and reconciliation purposes.']

  Note: 'Tax posting and compliance records for all applicable taxes including VAT/GST on ancillary services, passenger facility charges (PFC), airport taxes, withholding tax on vendor payments, and corporate income tax provisions. Captures tax code, tax jurisdiction, taxable base amount, tax amount, tax period, filing reference, and tax authority. Supports multi-jurisdiction tax compliance and DOT/EASA regulatory financial reporting.'
}

Table finance.fixed_asset {
  fixed_asset_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate identifier for each fixed asset record in the SAP FI-AA module. Primary key for the fixed asset master data product.']
  aircraft_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to fleet.aircraft. Business justification: Aircraft are the largest fixed assets on the balance sheet. This FK links the financial asset record to the operational aircraft master for depreciation tracking, asset valuation, and financial report']
  company_code_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.company_code. Business justification: Each fixed asset belongs to ONE company code. The fixed_asset table has company_code (STRING) which should be replaced with a FK to the company_code master. Fixed assets are company-code specific in S']
  cost_centre_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.cost_centre. Business justification: Each fixed asset can be assigned to ONE cost center for depreciation allocation. The fixed_asset table has cost_center (STRING) which should be replaced with a FK to the cost_centre master.']
  custodian_employee_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to workforce.employee. Business justification: Assets assigned to employees (laptops, uniforms, tools, crew equipment) require custodian tracking for asset accountability, loss prevention, and disposal authorization per airline asset management po']
  general_ledger_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.general_ledger. Business justification: Each fixed asset is linked to ONE GL account for asset accounting. The fixed_asset table has gl_account (STRING) which should be replaced with a FK to the general_ledger master. This links fixed asset']
  lease_contract_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.lease_contract. Business justification: Each IFRS 16 right-of-use asset is created from ONE lease contract. The fixed_asset table has lease_contract_number (STRING) and is_ifrs16_rou_asset flag, which should be replaced with a FK to the lea']
  accumulated_depreciation decimal(18,2) [note: 'Total depreciation charged against the asset from acquisition date to the current reporting period. Corresponds to SAP ANLC.KNAFA (cumulative ordinary depreciation). Used to compute net book value and for balance sheet reporting.']
  acquisition_cost decimal(18,2) [note: 'Original acquisition cost (historical cost) of the asset in the document currency at the time of purchase. For aircraft, includes purchase price, import duties, and directly attributable costs to bring the asset to its intended use. Corresponds to SAP ANLC.KANSW.']
  acquisition_date date [note: 'Date on which the asset was acquired, purchased, or placed in service. Corresponds to SAP ANLA.AKTIV. Used as the start date for depreciation calculation and CAPEX reporting.']
  asset_class_code varchar [note: 'SAP asset class (ANLKL) categorising the asset for depreciation rules, GL account determination, and reporting. Examples: 1000=Aircraft, 1100=Aircraft Engines, 1200=Simulators, 2000=Ground Support Equipment, 3000=IT Infrastructure, 4000=Leasehold Improvements, 5000=Right-of-Use Assets (IFRS 16). [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: 1000|1100|1200|2000|3000|4000|5000|6000 — promote to reference product]']
  asset_class_name varchar [note: 'Human-readable name of the SAP asset class (e.g., \'Aircraft\', \'Aircraft Engines\', \'Flight Simulators\', \'Ground Support Equipment\'). Denormalised for reporting convenience.']
  asset_description varchar [note: 'Primary human-readable description of the fixed asset (e.g., \'Boeing 737-800 MSN 45123\', \'Full Flight Simulator B737\', \'Ground Power Unit GPU-400\'). Corresponds to SAP ANLA.TXT50 field.']
  asset_location_code varchar [note: 'IATA airport or facility code indicating the primary physical location of the asset (e.g., \'LHR\' for Heathrow maintenance base, \'JFK\' for ground support equipment). Corresponds to SAP ANLA.STORT. Supports insurance valuation and physical asset verification.']
  asset_number varchar [note: 'SAP FI-AA main asset number uniquely identifying the asset within the company code. Corresponds to the ANLN1 field in SAP Asset Master (ANLZ/ANLB tables). Used for CAPEX tracking and asset register reconciliation.']
  asset_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the fixed asset. \'active\' = in service and depreciating; \'under_construction\' = Asset Under Construction (AUC) not yet capitalised; \'disposed\' = sold, scrapped, or written off; \'transferred\' = transferred to another company code or cost centre; \'impaired\' = impairment loss recognised per IAS 36; \'deactivated\' = temporarily taken out of service.']
  asset_subnumber varchar [note: 'SAP FI-AA sub-number (ANLN2) used to track component-level assets under a main asset number, such as individual aircraft engines under a parent airframe asset. Supports component accounting per IAS 16.']
  asset_type varchar [note: 'Categorises the asset by ownership or lease arrangement. \'owned\' = outright purchase; \'finance_lease\' = IFRS 16 right-of-use finance lease; \'operating_lease_rou\' = IFRS 16 right-of-use operating lease; \'dry_lease\' = aircraft leased without crew; \'wet_lease\' = aircraft leased with crew; \'acmi\' = Aircraft Crew Maintenance Insurance lease. Drives IFRS 16 vs IAS 16 accounting treatment.']
  capitalisation_date date [note: 'Date on which the asset was formally capitalised and transferred from Asset Under Construction (AUC) to a productive asset class. May differ from acquisition_date for self-constructed assets or aircraft deliveries with pre-delivery payments.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the fixed asset master record was first created in SAP FI-AA. Corresponds to SAP ANLA.ERDAT and ANLA.ERZET. Used for audit trail and data lineage tracking.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'ISO 4217 three-letter currency code for the acquisition cost and valuation amounts (e.g., USD, EUR, GBP). Aligns with the transaction currency used at time of asset acquisition.']
  depreciation_area varchar [note: 'SAP FI-AA depreciation area code (AFABE) identifying the valuation basis (e.g., 01=Book Depreciation IFRS, 10=Tax Depreciation, 20=Group Reporting). Multiple depreciation areas allow parallel valuation for IFRS, local GAAP, and tax purposes.']
  depreciation_method_code varchar [note: 'Code identifying the depreciation method applied to the asset. SL=Straight-Line; DB=Declining Balance; UOP=Units of Production (e.g., flight cycles or block hours for engines); SOYD=Sum of Years Digits; NONE=Non-depreciable (e.g., land). Corresponds to SAP ANLB.AFASL.']
  disposal_date date [note: 'Date on which the asset was disposed of, sold, scrapped, or retired from service. Triggers cessation of depreciation and recognition of disposal gain or loss. Corresponds to SAP ANLA.DEAKT.']
  disposal_proceeds decimal(18,2) [note: 'Net proceeds received on disposal or sale of the asset. Used to calculate the gain or loss on disposal (disposal proceeds minus net book value at disposal date). Relevant for aircraft sales, part-out transactions, and GSE disposals.']
  impairment_loss decimal(18,2) [note: 'Cumulative impairment loss recognised on the asset where the carrying amount exceeds the recoverable amount. Applicable for aircraft fleets affected by route network changes, AOG events, or market value declines. Corresponds to SAP ANLC extraordinary depreciation.']
  insurance_value decimal(18,2) [note: 'Agreed hull insurance value of the asset as declared to the insurer, typically the agreed value or replacement cost for aircraft. Used for insurance premium calculation and claims settlement. May differ from net book value for older aircraft.']
  is_ifrs16_rou_asset boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this asset is a right-of-use (ROU) asset recognised under IFRS 16 Leases. True for aircraft, engines, and equipment under finance or operating leases. Drives separate balance sheet presentation and disclosure requirements.']
  last_changed_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent change to the fixed asset master record in SAP FI-AA. Corresponds to SAP ANLA.AEDAT and ANLA.AEZET. Supports change audit trail for SOX compliance and data reconciliation.']
  lease_commencement_date date [note: 'Date on which the lease term commences and the right-of-use (ROU) asset is recognised on the balance sheet under IFRS 16. Determines the start of ROU asset amortisation and lease liability unwinding.']
  lease_end_date date [note: 'Contractual end date of the lease term for right-of-use assets. Used to determine the remaining lease term for IFRS 16 ROU asset amortisation and lease liability discounting. Includes any reasonably certain renewal option periods.']
  local_currency_code varchar [note: 'ISO 4217 three-letter code for the company code local currency used for statutory financial reporting. Supports multi-currency consolidation in SAP FI-AA.']
  net_book_value decimal(18,2) [note: 'Current net book value of the asset calculated as acquisition cost minus accumulated depreciation and any impairment losses. Corresponds to SAP ANLC carrying amount. Key metric for CAPEX tracking, insurance valuation, and balance sheet reporting.']
  profit_center varchar [note: 'SAP Controlling profit centre (PRCTR) associated with the asset for segment reporting and profitability analysis. Enables allocation of depreciation to route or fleet profit centres for management accounting.']
  purchase_order_number varchar [note: 'SAP purchase order number (EBELN) associated with the asset acquisition. Links the fixed asset to the procurement process for CAPEX budget control and three-way matching (PO, goods receipt, invoice).']
  registration_number varchar [note: 'Official civil aviation authority registration number (tail number) assigned to the aircraft asset (e.g., G-BOAC, N12345). Applicable to aircraft and rotorcraft assets. Used for cross-referencing with flight operations, MRO, and insurance records.']
  residual_value decimal(18,2) [note: 'Estimated residual (salvage) value of the asset at the end of its useful life. For aircraft, this is typically the estimated scrap or resale value. Depreciation is calculated on the depreciable amount (acquisition cost minus residual value). Corresponds to SAP ANLB.SCHRW.']
  revaluation_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Cumulative revaluation surplus or deficit recognised where the airline applies the revaluation model for aircraft or property assets. Recorded in Other Comprehensive Income (OCI) under IAS 16 revaluation model. Corresponds to SAP ANLC.KZINS.']
  serial_number varchar [note: 'Manufacturer serial number (MSN) or equipment serial number uniquely identifying the physical asset. For aircraft, this is the Manufacturer Serial Number (MSN); for engines, the Engine Serial Number (ESN). Critical for cross-referencing with AMOS maintenance records and insurance policies.']
  useful_life_periods varchar [note: 'Remaining useful life expressed in fiscal periods (months) within the current fiscal year. Complements useful_life_years for mid-year acquisitions and partial-period depreciation calculations. Corresponds to SAP ANLB.NDPER.']
  useful_life_years varchar [note: 'Estimated useful economic life of the asset in years as defined in the depreciation key. For aircraft, typically 20-25 years; for engines, 15-20 years; for simulators, 10-15 years; for IT infrastructure, 3-5 years. Corresponds to SAP ANLB.NDJAR.']
  vendor_number varchar [note: 'SAP vendor account number (LIFNR) of the supplier or lessor from whom the asset was acquired or leased. Used for accounts payable reconciliation and CAPEX purchase order matching.']
  wbs_element varchar [note: 'SAP Project System WBS element (POSID) linked to the asset for CAPEX project tracking. Used during the Asset Under Construction (AUC) phase to accumulate project costs before capitalisation. Common for aircraft delivery projects and MRO facility upgrades.']

  Note: 'SAP FI-AA fixed asset master for the airline\'s capitalised assets including owned aircraft, engines, simulators, ground support equipment, IT infrastructure, and leasehold improvements. Stores asset class, acquisition date, acquisition cost, useful life, depreciation method, accumulated depreciation, net book value, asset location, and disposal date. Supports CAPEX tracking, insurance valuation, and IFRS 16 right-of-use asset management.'
}

Table finance.company_code {
  company_code_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate key for the company code record in the Silver Layer lakehouse. Primary key for this entity.']
  airline_accounting_code varchar [note: 'Three-digit IATA numeric airline accounting code (also known as the airline prefix) used in e-ticket stock numbering, interline billing, and BSP/ARC settlement transactions. Uniquely identifies the airline in financial settlement systems.']
  arc_participant_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this legal entity participates in the Airlines Reporting Corporation (ARC) settlement system for US domestic ticket sales. Relevant for North American entities and drives ARC settlement accounts receivable processing.']
  bsp_participant_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this legal entity is a registered participant in the IATA Billing and Settlement Plan (BSP) for settlement of ticket sales through travel agents. Drives interline billing and accounts receivable settlement workflows.']
  chart_of_accounts varchar [note: 'Four-character SAP code identifying the chart of accounts assigned to this company code. Defines the general ledger account structure, account numbering, and account descriptions applicable to all financial postings. Corresponds to SAP KTOPL field.']
  company_code_code varchar [note: 'Four-character alphanumeric SAP FI company code uniquely identifying the legal entity within the SAP S/4HANA system. Matches the SAP Bukrs field. Used as the primary business key across all FI/CO postings, journal entries, and financial statements.']
  company_code_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the company code within SAP FI. Controls whether new financial postings can be made against this entity. \'inactive\' blocks new postings; \'in_liquidation\' triggers specific closing procedures under IFRS.']
  consolidation_group varchar [note: 'Identifier of the financial consolidation group to which this company code belongs within the group reporting hierarchy. Used for intercompany elimination, minority interest calculations, and preparation of consolidated financial statements under IFRS 10.']
  consolidation_unit varchar [note: 'SAP Group Reporting consolidation unit identifier mapped to this company code. Represents the lowest-level reporting unit in the consolidation hierarchy for multi-entity financial roll-up and statutory consolidation.']
  controlling_area varchar [note: 'Four-character SAP CO controlling area code assigned to this company code. Defines the scope of cost accounting, profit center accounting, and internal order management. Enables CASK (Cost per Available Seat Kilometer) and RASK reporting by aligning cost objects to revenue data.']
  corsia_participant_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this legal entity is subject to CORSIA (Carbon Offsetting and Reduction Scheme for International Aviation) obligations. Drives carbon offset liability accounting and regulatory reporting to ICAO.']
  country_of_incorporation varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 country code of the jurisdiction in which the legal entity is incorporated. Drives statutory reporting requirements, applicable tax law, and national civil aviation authority oversight.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this company code record was first created in the Silver Layer data product. Supports audit trail, data lineage tracking, and compliance with data governance policies.']
  credit_control_area varchar [note: 'Four-character SAP code identifying the credit control area governing credit limit management and accounts receivable risk assessment for this company code. Relevant for interline billing settlements and BSP/ARC credit exposure.']
  effective_from_date date [note: 'Date from which this company code became active and eligible for financial postings in SAP FI. Marks the start of the entity\'s operational financial lifecycle within the group.']
  effective_to_date date [note: 'Date on which this company code was deactivated or ceased financial operations. Null for currently active entities. Used to enforce period-end close and prevent postings after entity dissolution or merger.']
  entity_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the legal entity by its corporate structure. Determines consolidation treatment, intercompany elimination rules, and applicable accounting standards. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: airline|subsidiary|holding|joint_venture|special_purpose_vehicle|branch — promote to reference product if additional types are required]']
  fiscal_year_variant varchar [note: 'Two-character SAP code defining the fiscal year structure for this company code, including the number of posting periods, special periods, and whether the fiscal year aligns with the calendar year. Corresponds to SAP PERIV field. Critical for period-end close and CASK reporting cycles.']
  group_currency_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code of the parent group\'s reporting currency used for financial consolidation. Transactions in local currency are translated to group currency for consolidated financial statements.']
  iata_airline_code varchar [note: 'Two-character IATA airline designator code assigned to this legal entity. Used in BSP (Billing and Settlement Plan) settlements, interline billing, ticket issuance, and ARC (Airlines Reporting Corporation) reporting. Links financial entity to operational airline identity.']
  icao_airline_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ICAO airline designator code for this legal entity. Used in flight plan filings, ATC communications, NOTAM references, and regulatory correspondence with national civil aviation authorities.']
  ifrs16_lessee_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this company code is subject to IFRS 16 Leases accounting, requiring recognition of right-of-use assets and lease liabilities on the balance sheet. Applicable to entities with aircraft dry leases, wet leases, or ACMI arrangements.']
  ifrs_reporting_standard varchar [note: 'Accounting standard under which this legal entity prepares its statutory financial statements. Determines recognition, measurement, and disclosure requirements including IFRS 16 lease liability treatment and CORSIA carbon offset accounting.']
  incorporation_date date [note: 'Date on which the legal entity was formally incorporated or registered with the relevant national company registry. Used for entity lifecycle tracking and statutory reporting.']
  intercompany_elimination_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether intercompany transactions posted against this company code are subject to elimination during group financial consolidation. True for entities within the consolidation perimeter; false for associates or joint ventures accounted for under equity method.']
  last_updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to this company code record in the Silver Layer. Used for incremental load detection, change data capture, and audit compliance.']
  legal_name varchar [note: 'Full registered legal name of the entity as recorded with the relevant national company registry or civil aviation authority. Used for statutory reporting, regulatory filings, and intercompany agreements.']
  local_currency_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code representing the functional/local currency of the company code as defined in SAP FI. All financial transactions are translated to this currency for statutory reporting. Corresponds to SAP WAERS field.']
  operating_certificate_number varchar [note: 'Air Operator Certificate (AOC) number issued by the national civil aviation authority authorising this legal entity to conduct commercial air transport operations. Links the financial entity to its operational regulatory authorisation.']
  ownership_percentage decimal(5,2) [note: 'Percentage of equity ownership held by the immediate parent entity in this legal entity. Determines consolidation method: full consolidation (>50%), equity method (20-50%), or cost method (<20%) under IFRS 10/IAS 28.']
  posting_period_variant varchar [note: 'Four-character SAP code controlling which fiscal periods are open or closed for posting in this company code. Governs period-end close procedures and prevents backdated postings outside approved windows. Corresponds to SAP BDATV field.']
  registered_address varchar [note: 'Full registered office address of the legal entity as recorded with the national company registry. Used for statutory correspondence, regulatory filings, and legal notices.']
  registered_address_country varchar [note: 'ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 country code of the registered office address of the legal entity. May differ from country of incorporation for branch entities. Required for statutory filings and regulatory correspondence.']
  short_name varchar [note: 'Abbreviated trading or operating name of the legal entity used in internal reports, SAP correspondence, and financial dashboards. Corresponds to SAP BUTXT field.']
  source_system varchar [note: 'Identifies the operational system of record from which this company code record was sourced. Supports data lineage, reconciliation, and Silver Layer provenance tracking.']
  tax_jurisdiction_code varchar [note: 'Tax jurisdiction code identifying the applicable tax authority and tax calculation procedure for this company code. Used for VAT/GST determination, withholding tax, and Passenger Facility Charge (PFC) reporting to DOT.']
  vat_registration_number varchar [note: 'Official VAT or GST registration number issued by the national tax authority for this legal entity. Required for VAT invoicing, reclaim submissions, and cross-border tax compliance. Sensitive business identifier.']

  Note: 'Legal entity and company code master representing the airline\'s individual legal entities, subsidiaries, and joint ventures registered in SAP FI. Stores company code, legal entity name, country of incorporation, local currency, chart of accounts, fiscal year variant, and consolidation group. Supports multi-entity financial consolidation, intercompany eliminations, and statutory reporting per jurisdiction.'
}

Table finance.cash_pool {
  cash_pool_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for cash_pool']
  bank_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the financial institution providing the cash pooling service and managing the pool accounts.']
  company_code_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the legal entity that acts as the pool leader or header account holder, responsible for overall pool management and bank relationship.']
  cost_centre_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the cost center responsible for treasury management costs and interest income/expense allocation related to this cash pool.']
  parent_cash_pool_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing FK on cash_pool (parent_cash_pool_id)']
  treasury_manager_employee_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the employee responsible for managing and monitoring this cash pool arrangement.']
  auto_sweep_enabled_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether automatic cash sweeping is enabled to transfer excess balances from participant accounts to the master account.']
  base_interest_rate decimal(8,5) [note: 'Reference interest rate (in decimal form, e.g., 0.0525 for 5.25%) used as the foundation for pool interest calculations, typically linked to a benchmark rate such as LIBOR, SOFR, or EURIBOR.']
  created_by_user_code varchar [note: 'User identifier of the person who created this cash pool record.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this cash pool record was first created in the system.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO currency code for the cash pool. Multi-currency pools may require separate pool records per currency.']
  effective_date date [note: 'Date when the cash pool arrangement becomes active and operational for treasury transactions.']
  expiry_date date [note: 'Date when the cash pool arrangement terminates or requires renewal. Nullable for open-ended arrangements.']
  ifrs_16_applicable_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether IFRS 16 lease accounting considerations apply to this cash pool, relevant when pool is linked to lease liability financing arrangements.']
  interest_calculation_method varchar [note: 'Day count convention used for calculating interest on pooled balances and compensating participating accounts.']
  interest_spread_bps varchar [note: 'Spread in basis points (1 bp = 0.01%) added to or subtracted from the base rate for credit or debit positions within the pool.']
  last_review_date date [note: 'Date when the cash pool arrangement was last reviewed for performance, compliance, and alignment with treasury policy.']
  master_account_number varchar [note: 'The primary bank account number serving as the header or concentration account for the cash pool structure.']
  maximum_pool_limit decimal(18,2) [note: 'Maximum aggregate exposure or balance limit for the cash pool arrangement, set by the bank or internal treasury policy.']
  minimum_balance_threshold decimal(18,2) [note: 'Minimum aggregate balance required to be maintained in the cash pool to avoid penalties or maintain favorable interest terms.']
  modified_by_user_code varchar [note: 'User identifier of the person who last modified this cash pool record.']
  modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this cash pool record was last modified.']
  netting_enabled_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether intercompany netting is enabled within the cash pool to offset payables and receivables among participating entities.']
  next_review_date date [note: 'Scheduled date for the next periodic review of the cash pool arrangement.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text field for additional comments, special terms, or operational notes related to the cash pool arrangement.']
  overdraft_facility_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the cash pool includes an overdraft facility allowing negative aggregate balances up to an approved limit.']
  overdraft_limit decimal(18,2) [note: 'Maximum overdraft amount permitted for the cash pool when overdraft facility is enabled. Nullable if no overdraft facility exists.']
  pool_code varchar [note: 'Externally-known unique business identifier for the cash pool arrangement, used in treasury operations and intercompany settlements.']
  pool_name varchar [note: 'Human-readable name of the cash pool arrangement, typically reflecting the participating entities or geographic scope.']
  pool_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the cash pool arrangement indicating operational state and availability for treasury operations.']
  pool_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the cash pooling structure: notional (interest compensation without physical transfer), physical (actual fund transfers), hybrid (combination), zero balancing (daily sweep to zero), target balancing (sweep to target level), or header structure (master account with sub-accounts).']
  pooling_frequency varchar [note: 'Frequency at which cash concentration or interest calculation occurs within the pool structure.']
  regulatory_approval_number varchar [note: 'Reference number for regulatory approval or notification required for cross-border or multi-jurisdictional cash pooling arrangements.']
  sweep_threshold_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Minimum balance threshold that triggers an automatic sweep transaction from a participant account to the master account.']
  target_balance_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Target balance to be maintained in participant accounts after sweep operations in target balancing pool structures.']
  tax_jurisdiction_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter country code representing the primary tax jurisdiction governing the cash pool for withholding tax and interest reporting purposes.']
  withholding_tax_rate decimal(5,4) [note: 'Applicable withholding tax rate (in decimal form, e.g., 0.15 for 15%) on interest income generated within the cash pool, based on tax jurisdiction.']

  Note: 'Master reference table for cash_pool. Referenced by cash_pool_id.'
}

Table finance.legal_entity {
  legal_entity_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for legal_entity']
  parent_entity_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the immediate parent legal entity in the corporate ownership hierarchy. Null if this is the ultimate parent.']
  parent_legal_entity_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing FK on legal_entity (parent_legal_entity_id)']
  ultimate_parent_entity_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the top-level parent legal entity in the corporate ownership hierarchy. Null if this is the ultimate parent.']
  accounting_standard varchar [note: 'Primary accounting framework used by the entity for financial reporting (IFRS, US GAAP, or local GAAP).']
  air_operator_certificate_number varchar [note: 'Certificate number issued by the civil aviation authority authorizing the entity to conduct commercial air transport operations.']
  aoc_effective_date date [note: 'Date on which the Air Operator Certificate became effective and the entity was authorized to commence operations.']
  aoc_expiry_date date [note: 'Date on which the Air Operator Certificate expires and must be renewed. Null if certificate has no expiry.']
  aoc_issuing_authority varchar [note: 'Name of the civil aviation authority that issued the Air Operator Certificate (e.g., FAA, EASA, CAAC).']
  consolidation_method varchar [note: 'Accounting method used to consolidate this entity\'s financial results into group reporting.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this legal entity record was first created in the system.']
  entity_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the entity\'s business purpose within the corporate structure (e.g., operating company, holding company, SPV).']
  fiscal_year_end_day varchar [note: 'Day of month (1-31) representing the end of the entity\'s fiscal year.']
  fiscal_year_end_month varchar [note: 'Month number (1-12) representing the end of the entity\'s fiscal year for financial reporting purposes.']
  functional_currency_code varchar [note: 'ISO 4217 three-letter currency code representing the primary currency of the economic environment in which the entity operates.']
  iata_airline_code varchar [note: 'Two-character IATA airline designator code assigned to the legal entity if it operates as an airline.']
  icao_airline_code varchar [note: 'Three-character ICAO airline designator code assigned to the legal entity if it operates as an airline.']
  incorporation_country_code varchar [note: 'ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 country code of the jurisdiction where the entity is incorporated.']
  incorporation_date date [note: 'Date on which the legal entity was officially incorporated or registered with the relevant authority.']
  incorporation_jurisdiction varchar [note: 'State, province, or sub-national jurisdiction within the incorporation country (e.g., Delaware, Ontario).']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this legal entity record was most recently updated.']
  legal_entity_code varchar [note: 'Business-assigned unique code for the legal entity used in financial reporting and consolidation.']
  legal_entity_name varchar [note: 'Full registered legal name of the entity as it appears in incorporation documents and regulatory filings.']
  legal_form varchar [note: 'Type of legal structure of the entity (e.g., corporation, LLC, partnership, branch).']
  lei_code varchar [note: 'ISO 17442 Legal Entity Identifier, a 20-character alphanumeric code for unique identification in financial transactions and regulatory reporting.']
  operating_country_code varchar [note: 'ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 country code of the primary country where the entity conducts business operations.']
  operational_status varchar [note: 'Current operational state indicating whether the entity is actively conducting business.']
  ownership_percentage decimal(5,2) [note: 'Percentage of equity ownership held by the parent entity (0.00 to 100.00). Used for consolidation calculations.']
  registered_address_line1 varchar [note: 'First line of the official registered address of the legal entity as filed with the corporate registry.']
  registered_address_line2 varchar [note: 'Second line of the official registered address (suite, floor, building name).']
  registered_city varchar [note: 'City or municipality of the registered address.']
  registered_country_code varchar [note: 'ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 country code of the registered address.']
  registered_postal_code varchar [note: 'Postal or ZIP code of the registered address.']
  registered_state_province varchar [note: 'State, province, or region of the registered address.']
  registration_number varchar [note: 'Official registration number assigned by the corporate registry or companies house in the jurisdiction of incorporation.']
  regulatory_status varchar [note: 'Current regulatory standing of the legal entity with the corporate registry or governing authority.']
  reporting_currency_code varchar [note: 'ISO 4217 three-letter currency code used for financial reporting and consolidation purposes.']
  short_name varchar [note: 'Abbreviated or trading name of the legal entity used in operational systems and reports.']
  tax_identification_number varchar [note: 'Primary tax identification number assigned by the tax authority in the entity\'s jurisdiction of incorporation.']

  Note: 'Master reference table for legal_entity. Referenced by lessee_entity_id.'
}

Table finance.bank {
  bank_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for bank']
  correspondent_bank_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing FK on bank (correspondent_bank_id)']
  account_opening_date date [note: 'Date when the airline first established a banking relationship with this bank.']
  address_line_1 varchar [note: 'Primary street address line of the bank\'s headquarters or main branch office.']
  address_line_2 varchar [note: 'Secondary address line for additional location details such as building name, floor, or suite number.']
  bank_code varchar [note: 'Unique bank identification code assigned by the national banking authority or central bank. Used for domestic payment routing and interbank settlements.']
  bank_name varchar [note: 'Full legal name of the bank as registered with the banking authority.']
  bank_status varchar [note: 'Current operational status of the bank in the airline\'s treasury and payment systems.']
  bank_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the bank based on its primary business function and regulatory category.']
  city varchar [note: 'City or municipality where the bank\'s headquarters or main branch is located.']
  country_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO country code representing the country where the bank is domiciled and regulated.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this bank record was first created in the system.']
  credit_rating varchar [note: 'Current credit rating assigned to the bank by major rating agencies, used for counterparty risk assessment in treasury operations.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO currency code representing the primary currency in which the bank operates and settles transactions.']
  email_address varchar [note: 'Primary email address for treasury operations, payment inquiries, and interline settlement communications with the bank.']
  iban_prefix varchar [note: 'Country-specific IBAN prefix used for international account number validation. Consists of 2-letter country code and 2-digit check digits.']
  is_preferred_vendor boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this bank is designated as a preferred banking partner for the airline\'s treasury operations, offering negotiated rates and priority service.']
  is_sanctions_compliant boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the bank has been verified as compliant with international sanctions regulations (OFAC, UN, EU) and is approved for airline treasury operations.']
  last_transaction_date date [note: 'Date of the most recent financial transaction processed through this bank.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text field for additional information, special instructions, or operational notes related to the bank relationship.']
  phone_number varchar [note: 'Primary contact telephone number for the bank\'s treasury or corporate banking department.']
  postal_code varchar [note: 'Postal or ZIP code for the bank\'s address.']
  regulatory_authority varchar [note: 'Name of the primary banking regulatory authority or central bank that supervises and licenses this bank.']
  relationship_manager_email varchar [note: 'Email address of the bank\'s relationship manager for treasury correspondence and payment instructions.']
  relationship_manager_name varchar [note: 'Name of the bank\'s relationship manager or account officer assigned to the airline\'s corporate banking account.']
  relationship_manager_phone varchar [note: 'Direct phone number of the bank\'s relationship manager for urgent treasury matters and payment escalations.']
  routing_number varchar [note: 'Nine-digit routing transit number (RTN) used for domestic electronic funds transfers and automated clearing house (ACH) transactions. Primarily used in United States banking.']
  sanctions_screening_date date [note: 'Date when the bank was last screened against international sanctions lists for compliance verification.']
  state_province varchar [note: 'State, province, or administrative region where the bank is located.']
  swift_code varchar [note: 'International bank identifier code (BIC) used for cross-border wire transfers and interline billing settlements. Format: 8 or 11 alphanumeric characters.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this bank record was last modified in the system.']
  website_url varchar [note: 'Official website URL of the bank for reference and online banking portal access.']

  Note: 'Master reference table for bank. Referenced by bank_id.'
}

Table workforce.employee {
  employee_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the employee. Primary key. System of record: SAP S/4HANA HR PA master (Infotype 0001).']
  job_classification_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to workforce.job_classification. Business justification: Employee\'s job classification should reference the master job_classification table for grade structure, pay bands, and job family. The STRING job_classification attribute should be replaced by FK to t']
  union_agreement_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to workforce.union_agreement. Business justification: Employee\'s union membership should reference the master union_agreement for CBA terms, pay scales, and labor relations. The union_code is an attribute of union_agreement and should be retrieved via JO']
  workforce_position_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to workforce.position. Business justification: Employee should reference their authorized position in the organizational structure. The job_title is an attribute of position and should be retrieved via JOIN. This links employees to the position ma']
  cost_center_code varchar [note: 'Cost center to which the employee\'s salary and benefits are charged. Used for financial reporting and budget allocation.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this employee record was first created in the HR system.']
  date_of_birth date [note: 'Date of birth of the employee. Used for age verification, retirement planning, and benefits eligibility.']
  department_code varchar [note: 'Code representing the organizational department or division to which the employee is assigned.']
  email_address varchar [note: 'Primary corporate email address for the employee. Used for official communication and system access.']
  emergency_contact_name varchar [note: 'Full name of the employee\'s designated emergency contact person.']
  emergency_contact_phone varchar [note: 'Phone number of the employee\'s designated emergency contact person.']
  emergency_contact_relationship varchar [note: 'Relationship of the emergency contact to the employee (e.g., spouse, parent, sibling, friend).']
  employee_number varchar [note: 'Externally-known unique employee number assigned by HR system. Business identifier used on badges, payroll, and HR documents.']
  employment_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the employee\'s employment relationship with the airline.']
  employment_type varchar [note: 'Type of employment contract. ACMI indicates crew leased under Aircraft Crew Maintenance Insurance agreements.']
  first_name varchar [note: 'Legal first name of the employee as recorded in HR master data.']
  hire_date date [note: 'Date the employee was hired or commenced employment with the airline. Used for seniority calculations, benefits eligibility, and tenure reporting.']
  home_base_airport_code varchar [note: 'IATA 3-letter code of the employee\'s home base airport. Critical for crew scheduling, commute planning, and operational assignments.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this employee record was last updated in the HR system.']
  last_name varchar [note: 'Legal last name (surname) of the employee as recorded in HR master data.']
  last_performance_review_date date [note: 'Date of the employee\'s most recent performance review or appraisal.']
  medical_certificate_expiry_date date [note: 'Expiration date of the employee\'s aviation medical certificate. Monitored to ensure crew remain medically qualified for flight duties.']
  medical_certificate_status varchar [note: 'Current status of the employee\'s aviation medical certificate. Required for pilots and cabin crew per FAA/EASA regulations.']
  middle_name varchar [note: 'Middle name or initial of the employee, if applicable.']
  nationality varchar [note: 'Nationality of the employee represented as ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 country code. Critical for visa, work permit, and crew scheduling compliance.']
  passport_expiry_date date [note: 'Expiration date of the employee\'s passport. Monitored to ensure crew remain travel-eligible for international operations.']
  passport_number varchar [note: 'Passport number of the employee. Required for international crew operations and travel authorization.']
  performance_rating varchar [note: 'Most recent performance appraisal rating for the employee. Used for promotion decisions, compensation adjustments, and talent management.']
  phone_number varchar [note: 'Primary contact phone number for the employee. May be mobile or landline.']
  salary_grade varchar [note: 'Salary grade or pay band assigned to the employee. Used for compensation planning and equity analysis.']
  seniority_date date [note: 'Date used to calculate employee seniority for bidding, scheduling, and union contract purposes. May differ from hire date due to transfers or breaks in service.']
  termination_date date [note: 'Date the employee\'s employment was terminated or ended. Null for active employees.']
  union_membership_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the employee is a member of a labor union. Critical for labor relations, collective bargaining, and contract compliance.']
  work_permit_expiry_date date [note: 'Expiration date of the employee\'s work permit or visa. Monitored to ensure continuous legal employment status.']
  work_permit_status varchar [note: 'Current status of the employee\'s work permit or visa authorization. Critical for legal employment compliance.']

  Note: 'Core master entity representing all airline staff — pilots, cabin crew, ground staff, maintenance technicians, and corporate employees. SSOT for employee identity, employment status, job classification, cost centre assignment, and HR master data. Captures employee number, hire date, employment type (permanent, contract, ACMI), home base airport (IATA code), union membership flag, nationality, visa/work permit status, and emergency contact. Aligns with SAP S/4HANA HR PA master (Infotype 0001/0002).'
}

Table workforce.org_unit {
  org_unit_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the organizational unit. Primary key.']
  parent_org_unit_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the parent organizational unit in the hierarchy, enabling multi-level organizational structure representation.']
  address_line1 varchar [note: 'Primary street address line for the organizational unit\'s physical location.']
  address_line2 varchar [note: 'Secondary street address line (suite, floor, building) for the organizational unit\'s physical location.']
  city varchar [note: 'City where the organizational unit is physically located.']
  contact_email varchar [note: 'Primary email address for organizational unit correspondence and communication.']
  contact_phone varchar [note: 'Primary phone number for organizational unit contact.']
  country_code varchar [note: 'ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 country code where the organizational unit is domiciled.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this organizational unit record was first created in the system.']
  effective_from_date date [note: 'Date when this organizational unit became or will become active, supporting organizational restructuring and time-dependent hierarchy.']
  effective_to_date date [note: 'Date when this organizational unit was or will be dissolved or restructured. Null for currently active units with no planned end date.']
  functional_area varchar [note: 'Primary business function or operational domain of the organizational unit, aligned with IATA functional area classifications. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: flight_operations|cabin_services|mro|ground_handling|cargo|revenue_management|network_planning|corporate|finance|hr|it|safety|compliance — 13 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  headcount_actual varchar [note: 'Current actual full-time equivalent headcount assigned to this organizational unit.']
  headcount_planned varchar [note: 'Planned or budgeted full-time equivalent headcount for this organizational unit.']
  iata_functional_code varchar [note: 'IATA standard functional area code for airline organizational classification and industry benchmarking.']
  is_hub boolean [note: 'Flag indicating whether this organizational unit is located at or supports a hub airport in the airline\'s network.']
  is_revenue_generating boolean [note: 'Flag indicating whether this organizational unit directly generates revenue (e.g., flight operations, cargo) versus support functions.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this organizational unit record was last updated.']
  location_code varchar [note: 'IATA three-letter airport or city code representing the primary geographic location of this organizational unit.']
  org_level varchar [note: 'Hierarchical level of the organizational unit within the overall structure (e.g., 1=corporate, 2=division, 3=department, 4=section).']
  org_unit_code varchar [note: 'Business identifier code for the organizational unit, used across systems and reporting. May align with IATA functional area codes or internal cost center codes.']
  org_unit_description varchar [note: 'Detailed description of the organizational unit\'s purpose, responsibilities, and scope of operations.']
  org_unit_name varchar [note: 'Full business name of the organizational unit (e.g., Flight Operations Division, Cabin Services Department, MRO Engineering).']
  org_unit_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the organizational unit.']
  org_unit_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the organizational unit by structural type within the airline hierarchy. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: division|department|cost_center|functional_unit|station|hub|regional_office|corporate — 8 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  postal_code varchar [note: 'Postal or ZIP code for the organizational unit\'s physical location.']
  profit_center_code varchar [note: 'Profit center code for revenue-generating organizational units, used in management accounting and profitability analysis.']
  region varchar [note: 'Geographic region or area classification for the organizational unit (e.g., North America, Europe, Asia Pacific).']
  regulatory_oversight_authority varchar [note: 'Primary regulatory authority with jurisdiction over this organizational unit\'s operations (e.g., FAA, EASA, national CAA).']
  safety_critical_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this organizational unit performs safety-critical functions requiring SMS (Safety Management System) oversight and regulatory compliance.']
  short_name varchar [note: 'Abbreviated name or acronym for the organizational unit, used in reports and displays.']
  state_province varchar [note: 'State or province where the organizational unit is physically located.']
  union_representation_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether employees in this organizational unit are covered by collective bargaining agreements or union representation.']

  Note: 'Organisational hierarchy master defining all departments, divisions, cost centres, and functional units within the airline. Supports the full SAP S/4HANA HR organisational management structure including parent-child relationships, org unit type (flight ops, cabin services, MRO, ground handling, corporate), effective dates for restructuring, and IATA functional area codes. Enables headcount planning and workforce analytics by organisational node.'
}

Table workforce.position {
  position_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for position']
  job_classification_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to workforce.job_classification. Business justification: Position\'s job code should reference the master job_classification table for job family, pay grade, and FLSA status. The job_code STRING attribute should be replaced by FK to the authoritative master.']
  org_unit_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to workforce.org_unit. Business justification: Position should reference its organizational unit for hierarchy and cost allocation. The organizational_unit_code and cost_center are attributes of org_unit and should be retrieved via JOIN. This link']
  reports_to_position_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key reference to the position that this position reports to in the organizational hierarchy. Null for top-level executive positions.']
  union_agreement_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to workforce.union_agreement. Business justification: Position should reference the union agreement that governs the bargaining unit for labor relations and CBA compliance. The union_bargaining_unit is an attribute of union_agreement and should be retrie']
  base_station_code varchar [note: 'IATA three-letter airport code for the crew base or work location assigned to this position. Determines domicile for crew scheduling and commuting eligibility.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the position record was first created in the system. Used for audit trail and data lineage.']
  crew_category varchar [note: 'Classification of the position within airline operational crew structure. Determines regulatory training requirements, duty time limitations, and operational authority. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: captain|first_officer|cabin_crew|flight_dispatcher|ground_staff|maintenance_engineer|corporate|not_applicable — 8 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  effective_end_date date [note: 'Date when the position was retired or made obsolete. Null for active positions. Used for historical analysis and organizational restructuring tracking.']
  effective_start_date date [note: 'Date when the position became active in the organizational structure. Used for historical tracking and organizational change management.']
  exempt_status boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the position is exempt from overtime pay regulations under labor law (True) or non-exempt and eligible for overtime (False).']
  fte_allocation decimal(5,2) [note: 'Full-time equivalent allocation for the position. 1.00 represents a full-time position; fractional values represent part-time or shared positions.']
  grade_band varchar [note: 'Compensation grade or pay band assigned to the position. Determines salary range, benefits eligibility, and career progression path.']
  icao_licence_category varchar [note: 'ICAO-defined licence category required for the position (e.g., ATPL, CPL, cabin crew attestation). Applicable to flight crew and safety-critical operational positions.']
  language_requirements varchar [note: 'Language proficiency requirements for the position (e.g., English ICAO Level 4, Mandarin fluent). Critical for flight crew and customer-facing positions.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the position record was last updated. Used for change tracking and audit purposes.']
  medical_class_required varchar [note: 'Aviation medical certificate class required for the position per regulatory standards. Class 1 for airline transport pilots, Class 2 for commercial pilots, Class 3 for air traffic controllers.']
  minimum_education_level varchar [note: 'Minimum educational qualification required for the position. Used for recruitment screening and succession planning. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: high_school|associate|bachelor|master|doctorate|professional_certification|not_specified — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  minimum_flight_hours varchar [note: 'Minimum total flight hours required for the position. Applicable to flight crew positions. Used for recruitment and internal promotion eligibility.']
  modified_by_user varchar [note: 'User identifier of the person who last modified the position record. Used for audit trail and accountability.']
  position_code varchar [note: 'Unique business identifier for the position within the organizational structure. Used for HR reporting and organizational planning.']
  position_description varchar [note: 'Detailed description of the positions responsibilities, duties, and scope of authority. Used for recruitment, performance management, and organizational planning.']
  position_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the position within the organizational structure. Active positions can be filled; frozen positions are temporarily unavailable; obsolete positions are retired.']
  required_certifications varchar [note: 'Professional certifications or qualifications required for the position (e.g., A&P licence, dispatch licence, safety auditor certification). Multiple certifications separated by semicolon.']
  safety_critical_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the position is designated as safety-critical per regulatory requirements. Safety-critical positions require enhanced background checks, medical certification, and recurrent training.']
  security_clearance_level varchar [note: 'Security clearance or background check level required for the position. Determines access to secure areas, sensitive information, and critical systems.']
  seniority_list_code varchar [note: 'Code identifying the seniority list or bidding group for the position. Used in crew scheduling, vacation bidding, and base assignment processes.']
  shift_pattern varchar [note: 'Standard shift or duty pattern for the position (e.g., day shift, rotating shifts, reserve duty, line holder). Applicable to operational and crew positions.']
  title varchar [note: 'Official job title for the position as it appears in organizational charts and HR systems.']
  type_rating_required varchar [note: 'Aircraft type rating(s) required for the position (e.g., B737, A320, B777). Applicable to pilot and flight engineer positions. Multiple ratings separated by semicolon.']

  Note: 'Master entity defining all authorised positions (posts) within the airline organisational structure. Each position is a slot in the org chart that can be filled by an employee. Captures position title, job code, grade band, FTE allocation, required qualifications, crew category (Captain, First Officer, Cabin Crew, Dispatcher, etc.), ICAO licence category requirement, union bargaining unit, and whether the position is safety-critical per regulatory requirements.'
}

Table workforce.job_classification {
  job_classification_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the job classification record. Primary key.']
  union_agreement_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to workforce.union_agreement. Business justification: Job classification should reference the union agreement that governs the bargaining unit. The bargaining_unit is an attribute of union_agreement and should be retrieved via JOIN. This links job classi']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this job classification record was first created in the HR system. Used for audit trail and data lineage tracking.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for the pay grade amounts. Typically the airline\'s reporting currency or local currency for regional operations.']
  effective_end_date date [note: 'Date when this job classification was or will be retired or superseded. Null for currently active classifications. Used for temporal validity and historical analysis of organizational structure evolution.']
  effective_start_date date [note: 'Date when this job classification became or will become effective and available for use in the HR system. Used for temporal validity and historical tracking of job structure changes.']
  eligible_for_bonus boolean [note: 'Indicates whether employees in this job classification are eligible for annual performance bonus or incentive compensation. Used for compensation planning and total rewards calculations.']
  eligible_for_stock boolean [note: 'Indicates whether employees in this job classification are eligible for equity compensation such as stock options, restricted stock units, or employee stock purchase plans. Typically limited to management and executive positions.']
  flsa_status varchar [note: 'Classification under the US Fair Labor Standards Act indicating whether the position is exempt from overtime pay requirements (exempt) or eligible for overtime (non-exempt). Critical for payroll processing and labor law compliance.']
  iata_job_category varchar [note: 'IATA standardized job category code used for industry benchmarking, salary surveys, and workforce analytics across airlines. Enables comparison with industry peers.']
  icao_occupational_category varchar [note: 'ICAO standardized occupational category code for aviation-specific roles. Used for international regulatory reporting and workforce statistics submitted to ICAO.']
  is_executive boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this job classification is an executive-level position (C-suite, senior vice president, or equivalent). Used for executive compensation programs, board reporting, and regulatory disclosures.']
  is_management boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this job classification is a management or supervisory position with direct reports. Used for leadership development programs, management training, and organizational structure analysis.']
  job_classification_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of this job classification. Active classifications are available for hiring and assignment. Inactive classifications are temporarily suspended. Obsolete classifications are retired and no longer used. Pending classifications are under review before activation.']
  job_code varchar [note: 'Unique alphanumeric code identifying the job classification within the airline\'s human resources system. Used for position identification, payroll processing, and workforce planning.']
  job_description varchar [note: 'Detailed narrative description of the job responsibilities, duties, and accountabilities. Used for job postings, performance management, and organizational documentation.']
  job_family varchar [note: 'High-level grouping of related jobs within the airline. Categories include Flight Deck (pilots), Cabin Crew (flight attendants), Technical/MRO (Maintenance Repair and Overhaul), Ground Operations, Commercial (sales, revenue management), Corporate (finance, HR, IT), Cargo, and Customer Service. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: flight_deck|cabin_crew|technical_mro|ground_operations|commercial|corporate|cargo|customer_service — 8 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  job_subfamily varchar [note: 'Secondary classification within the job family providing more granular segmentation. For example, within Flight Deck: Captain, First Officer, Second Officer; within Technical/MRO: Line Maintenance, Base Maintenance, Component Repair.']
  job_title varchar [note: 'Official job title as used in employment contracts, organizational charts, and external job postings. Human-readable name for the position.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this job classification record was last updated. Used for change tracking, audit trail, and data synchronization with downstream systems.']
  license_type_required varchar [note: 'Specific type of aviation license required for this position if applicable. Examples: ATPL (Airline Transport Pilot License), CPL (Commercial Pilot License), AME (Aircraft Maintenance Engineer), cabin crew attestation. Null if no license required.']
  medical_class_required varchar [note: 'Class of aviation medical certificate required for this position. Class 1 for airline pilots, Class 2 for commercial pilots, Class 3 for private pilots, Cabin Crew for flight attendants, Not Required for non-safety-sensitive roles.']
  minimum_education_level varchar [note: 'Minimum educational qualification required for this job classification. Used for recruitment, job posting, and candidate screening. Examples: high school diploma, bachelor\'s degree, vocational certification. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: high_school|associate|bachelor|master|doctorate|vocational|none — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  minimum_experience_years varchar [note: 'Minimum number of years of relevant work experience required for this job classification. Used for recruitment qualification screening and career progression planning.']
  organizational_level varchar [note: 'Numeric indicator of the position\'s level in the organizational hierarchy. Lower numbers represent higher levels (e.g., 1 = CEO, 2 = Executive VP, 3 = VP, etc.). Used for organizational charting and span-of-control analysis.']
  pay_grade varchar [note: 'Compensation grade or band assigned to this job classification. Used for salary benchmarking, compensation planning, and pay equity analysis. Links to the airline\'s compensation structure.']
  pay_grade_maximum decimal(15,2) [note: 'Maximum annual base salary for this pay grade. Represents the ceiling for compensation within this classification without promotion.']
  pay_grade_midpoint decimal(15,2) [note: 'Midpoint annual base salary for this pay grade representing the target compensation for fully competent performance. Used for market benchmarking and compa-ratio calculations.']
  pay_grade_minimum decimal(15,2) [note: 'Minimum annual base salary for this pay grade in the airline\'s base currency. Used for compensation planning and offer letter generation.']
  reports_to_job_code varchar [note: 'Job code of the supervisory position to which this job classification typically reports. Defines the standard reporting relationship in the organizational hierarchy. Null for executive positions.']
  requires_aviation_license boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this job classification requires a valid aviation license issued by a civil aviation authority (e.g., pilot license, aircraft maintenance engineer license). True for flight deck crew, licensed aircraft maintenance engineers, and certain technical roles.']
  requires_medical_certificate boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this job classification requires a valid aviation medical certificate. True for flight deck crew, cabin crew, and certain safety-sensitive positions. Medical certificate class and validity period vary by role.']
