  destination_url varchar [note: 'Target landing page or destination URL where users are directed when they click on the creative. Includes tracking parameters.']
  expiry_date date [note: 'Date when the creative is scheduled to expire and stop serving. End of active campaign period. Nullable for evergreen creatives.']
  format_dimensions varchar [note: 'Technical dimensions or specifications of the creative asset (e.g., 300x250, 728x90, 1920x1080, responsive). Captures size and aspect ratio.']
  go_live_date date [note: 'Date when the creative is scheduled to go live and begin serving to audiences. Start of active campaign period.']
  headline_copy varchar [note: 'Primary headline or title text displayed in the creative. Main attention-grabbing message for the audience.']
  is_active boolean [note: 'Boolean flag indicating whether the creative is currently active and serving. True if live, false if paused or inactive.']
  is_high_performer boolean [note: 'Boolean flag indicating whether this creative has been identified as a high-performing asset based on engagement or conversion metrics.']
  language_code varchar [note: 'Two-letter ISO 639-1 language code indicating the language of the creative content (e.g., en, es, fr, de).']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the creative record was last modified or updated. Supports change tracking and audit trail.']
  legal_compliance_flag boolean [note: 'Boolean flag indicating whether the creative has been reviewed and confirmed to comply with legal and regulatory requirements (e.g., advertising standards, consumer protection).']
  market_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 country or market code indicating the target geographic market for this creative (e.g., USA, GBR, DEU).']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text field for additional notes, comments, or context about the creative. Supports collaboration and documentation.']
  target_audience_segment varchar [note: 'Description or identifier of the target audience segment this creative is designed for (e.g., business travelers, leisure families, frequent flyers).']
  created_by varchar [note: 'Name or identifier of the person or team who created the creative asset. Supports asset ownership and accountability.']

  Note: 'Master record for individual digital creative executions (ad units, email templates, landing pages, social posts, push notification copy) associated with marketing campaigns. Captures creative name, creative type (banner ad, rich media, email HTML, landing page, video pre-roll, social image, push copy), associated campaign, channel, format dimensions, headline copy, body copy, call-to-action text, destination URL, A/B test variant identifier, approval status, go-live date, expiry date, and performance flags. Enables creative-level performance attribution and A/B test management distinct from the campaign-level record.'
}

Table marketing.spend {
  spend_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the marketing spend record. Primary key for this entity.']
  adjustment_spend_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing FK on spend (adjustment_spend_id)']
  approved_by_employee_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to workforce.employee. Business justification: Spend approval requires employee authorization for budget commitment, financial controls, and procurement compliance. Airlines track approver identity for audit trails, financial governance, and deleg']
  campaign_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the marketing campaign associated with this spend record.']
  channel_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the marketing channel through which the spend was executed (e.g., digital, social media, print, broadcast).']
  cost_centre_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the cost centre or business unit responsible for this marketing expenditure.']
  general_ledger_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.general_ledger. Business justification: Marketing spend transactions must post to the general ledger for financial consolidation, audit trail, and statutory reporting. Airlines track marketing expenses through GL accounts for IFRS complianc']
  purchase_order_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to procurement.purchase_order. Business justification: Large marketing expenditures (media buys, agency retainers, production) flow through formal POs. Finance requires linking spend records to POs for three-way matching (PO-invoice-receipt), audit trails']
  vendor_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the vendor, agency, or supplier providing the marketing service or media placement.']
  actual_spend_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Actual expenditure amount incurred for this marketing activity. Represents the realized cost for ROI calculation and financial reconciliation.']
  approval_status varchar [note: 'Current approval status of this marketing spend record. Reflects the governance workflow state for spend authorization.']
  approved_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this marketing spend was approved. Records the moment of authorization for compliance and audit purposes.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this marketing spend record was first created in the system. Provides audit trail for data lineage and record lifecycle tracking.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for the spend amounts (e.g., USD, EUR, GBP). Required for multi-currency spend consolidation.']
  fiscal_month varchar [note: 'Fiscal month (1-12) to which this marketing spend is attributed. Used for monthly budget tracking and trend analysis.']
  fiscal_quarter varchar [note: 'Fiscal quarter to which this marketing spend is attributed. Used for quarterly budget tracking and seasonal spend analysis.']
  fiscal_year varchar [note: 'Fiscal year to which this marketing spend is attributed. Used for annual budget tracking and year-over-year spend analysis.']
  invoice_date date [note: 'Date the vendor invoice was issued for this marketing spend. Used for accounts payable aging and payment scheduling.']
  invoice_number varchar [note: 'Vendor invoice number associated with this marketing spend. Used for payment reconciliation and audit trail.']
  market_region varchar [note: 'Geographic market or region targeted by this marketing spend (e.g., North America, Europe, Asia Pacific). Used for regional spend allocation and market-specific ROI analysis.']
  modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this marketing spend record was last modified. Tracks the most recent update for change management and audit purposes.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text notes or comments about this marketing spend record. Captures additional context, exceptions, or clarifications for audit and review purposes.']
  passenger_segment varchar [note: 'Target passenger segment for this marketing spend (e.g., business, leisure, premium, economy). Used for segment-specific marketing effectiveness analysis.']
  payment_due_date date [note: 'Date by which payment is due to the vendor for this marketing spend. Used for cash flow planning and vendor relationship management.']
  payment_status varchar [note: 'Current status of payment processing for this marketing spend. Tracks the spend through the approval and payment lifecycle.']
  period_end_date date [note: 'End date of the period to which this marketing spend applies. Defines the temporal boundary for spend attribution.']
  period_start_date date [note: 'Start date of the period to which this marketing spend applies. Used for monthly, quarterly, or campaign-specific spend allocation.']
  planned_spend_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Budgeted or planned expenditure amount for this marketing activity. Used for variance analysis against actual spend.']
  reference_number varchar [note: 'Business identifier or reference code for this marketing spend transaction, used for tracking and reconciliation.']
  route_group varchar [note: 'Route group or network segment targeted by this marketing spend (e.g., domestic, international, long-haul). Specific to airline marketing spend attribution.']
  spend_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the marketing expenditure by nature of cost. Determines how the spend is categorized for budget tracking and ROI analysis.']

  Note: 'Transactional record capturing actual and planned marketing expenditure at the campaign and channel level. Stores campaign reference, channel reference, spend period (month/quarter), spend type (media buy, agency fee, production cost, technology platform fee, sponsorship, event cost), planned spend amount, actual spend amount, currency, purchase order reference, vendor or agency name, cost centre reference, approval status, and invoice reference. Provides the marketing domain\'s view of spend actuals for campaign ROI calculation and budget tracking. Distinct from finance.general_ledger which holds the accounting entries; this entity provides the marketing-context spend attribution.'
}

Table marketing.sponsorship {
  sponsorship_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the sponsorship agreement record. Primary key.']
  lease_contract_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.lease_contract. Business justification: Sponsorships involving leased assets (branded aircraft livery, lounge spaces, venue rights) require IFRS 16 lease accounting. Airlines must link sponsorship contracts to lease contracts for right-of-u']
  partner_organization_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to marketing.partner_organization. Business justification: Sponsorship agreements are with partner organizations. Currently, sponsorship duplicates partner_organization_name and partner_organization_type as denormalized strings. Adding partner_organization_id']
  renewed_sponsorship_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing FK on sponsorship (renewed_sponsorship_id)']
  supply_contract_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to procurement.supply_contract. Business justification: Major sponsorships (sports teams, venues, events) are multi-year commercial agreements managed as supply contracts with payment schedules, rights packages, deliverables, and renewal terms. Procurement']
  activation_status varchar [note: 'Current operational state of the sponsorship: active (rights being exercised), inactive (signed but not yet started), pending (under negotiation), suspended (temporarily paused), expired (term ended), or terminated (cancelled before term end).']
  annual_value decimal(18,2) [note: 'Average annual monetary value of the sponsorship, calculated by dividing contracted value by term in years.']
  brand_alignment_score varchar [note: 'Internal assessment score (typically 1-10) measuring how well the sponsorship aligns with the airline\'s brand values, target demographics, and strategic positioning.']
  contract_signed_date date [note: 'The date when the sponsorship agreement was formally executed by both parties, in yyyy-MM-dd format.']
  contract_term_months varchar [note: 'Duration of the sponsorship agreement expressed in months, calculated from start to end date.']
  contracted_value decimal(18,2) [note: 'Total monetary value of the sponsorship agreement over its full term, representing the airline\'s financial commitment.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The timestamp when this sponsorship record was first created in the system, in yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX format.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for the contracted value (e.g., USD, EUR, GBP).']
  end_date date [note: 'The date when the sponsorship agreement expires and rights cease, in yyyy-MM-dd format. Nullable for open-ended or perpetual agreements.']
  exclusivity_category varchar [note: 'The product or service category in which the airline holds exclusive sponsorship rights (e.g., \'official airline\', \'exclusive aviation partner\'), preventing competitor sponsorships in the same category.']
  geographic_market varchar [note: 'Primary geographic market or country where the sponsorship has brand visibility and activation, using ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 country code (e.g., USA, GBR, AUS).']
  hospitality_entitlement varchar [note: 'Description of hospitality benefits included in the sponsorship, such as number of VIP tickets, suite access, meet-and-greet opportunities, and event passes.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The timestamp when this sponsorship record was most recently updated, in yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX format.']
  logo_placement_rights varchar [note: 'Specific locations and formats where the airline logo may be displayed (e.g., jersey front, stadium signage, broadcast overlays, digital assets).']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text field for additional context, special conditions, historical background, or operational notes relevant to the sponsorship management.']
  owning_commercial_team varchar [note: 'The internal marketing or commercial team responsible for managing the sponsorship relationship, activation planning, and performance tracking.']
  payment_structure varchar [note: 'The schedule and method by which sponsorship fees are paid to the partner organization.']
  performance_kpi_description varchar [note: 'Description of the key performance indicators used to measure sponsorship effectiveness, such as brand awareness lift, media impressions, social media engagement, or incremental bookings.']
  regional_scope varchar [note: 'Geographic reach of the sponsorship activation: local (city/metro), national (single country), regional (multi-country area), or global (worldwide visibility).']
  renewal_deadline_date date [note: 'The date by which the airline must notify the partner of intent to renew, in yyyy-MM-dd format. Nullable if no renewal option exists.']
  renewal_option_details varchar [note: 'Description of renewal terms, including notice period required, pricing adjustment mechanisms, and any conditions precedent for exercising the renewal option.']
  renewal_option_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the sponsorship agreement includes a contractual option for renewal or extension beyond the initial term.']
  rights_package_description varchar [note: 'Comprehensive description of the sponsorship benefits and rights granted, including logo placement locations, hospitality ticket allocations, co-branded advertising opportunities, route promotion rights, social media mentions, and other activation entitlements.']
  sponsorship_category varchar [note: 'High-level classification of the sponsorship by thematic area to support portfolio analysis and strategic alignment. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: sports|entertainment|culture|tourism|community|sustainability|other — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  sponsorship_code varchar [note: 'Internal alphanumeric code used to uniquely identify and track the sponsorship in financial and marketing systems.']
  sponsorship_name varchar [note: 'The official name or title of the sponsorship agreement as recognized in marketing materials and contracts.']
  sponsorship_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the sponsorship level and nature: title sponsor (highest tier, brand in event name), presenting sponsor (secondary tier), official airline partner (category exclusivity), jersey sponsor (logo on team uniform), stadium naming rights (venue branding), or event sponsor (single event support).']
  start_date date [note: 'The date when the sponsorship agreement becomes effective and rights activation begins, in yyyy-MM-dd format.']
  termination_clause_summary varchar [note: 'Summary of conditions under which either party may terminate the sponsorship agreement early, including notice periods and financial penalties.']

  Note: 'Master record for airline sponsorship agreements and partnerships with sports teams, events, cultural institutions, and destination tourism boards. Captures sponsorship name, sponsorship type (title sponsor, presenting sponsor, official airline partner, jersey sponsor, stadium naming rights, event sponsor), partner organisation name, geographic market, sponsorship start and end dates, contracted value, currency, rights package description (logo placement, hospitality tickets, co-branded advertising, route promotion), activation status, renewal option details, and owning commercial team. Provides the authoritative register of all sponsorship commitments and brand partnership investments.'
}

Table marketing.sponsorship_activation {
  sponsorship_activation_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the sponsorship activation event. Primary key for this transactional record capturing a specific deliverable execution under a sponsorship agreement.']

Business justification: Activation manager is an employee responsible for sponsorship execution, deliverable tracking, and partner relationship management. Airlines track activation ownership for accountability, performance ']
  aircraft_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to fleet.aircraft. Business justification: Sponsorship activations on specific aircraft (branded liveries, cabin wraps, in-flight branding) require tracking which tail numbers carry sponsor assets for contractual proof-of-delivery, rights mana']
  campaign_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to marketing.campaign. Business justification: sponsorship_activation currently has campaign_name (STRING) which is a denormalized reference. Normalizing to campaign_id FK to marketing.campaign.campaign_id. Sponsorship activations are often execut']
  contract_deliverable_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the specific deliverable line item in the sponsorship contract that this activation fulfills. Enables tracking of contract fulfillment and rights utilization.']
  parent_sponsorship_activation_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing FK on sponsorship_activation (parent_sponsorship_activation_id)']
  sponsorship_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the parent sponsorship agreement under which this activation was executed. Links this activation to the master contract defining rights, obligations, and deliverables.']
  station_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to airport.station. Business justification: Sponsorship activations occur at specific airport stations (lounge events, gate area branding, terminal displays). Real business process: sponsor deliverable fulfillment tracking at contracted airport']
  activation_date date [note: 'The date on which the sponsorship activation event occurred or is scheduled to occur. Primary business event timestamp for this transactional record.']
  activation_end_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Precise date and time when the activation event concluded. Enables calculation of activation duration and supports time-based performance analysis.']
  activation_reference_number varchar [note: 'Business-facing unique reference code or number assigned to this activation event for tracking and reporting purposes. Used in communications with sponsors and internal stakeholders.']
  activation_start_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Precise date and time when the activation event began. Used for time-bound activations such as hospitality events, press conferences, or limited-duration digital campaigns.']
  activation_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the activation event. Tracks whether the deliverable has been executed, is pending, or was cancelled. Critical for contract fulfillment tracking and sponsor reporting.']
  activation_type varchar [note: 'Category of sponsorship activation deliverable executed. Defines the nature of the marketing touchpoint or brand exposure delivered to the sponsor under the agreement. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: hospitality_event|co_branded_campaign|airport_activation|social_media_post|press_event|in_stadium_branding|lounge_branding|aircraft_livery|digital_display|experiential_activation — 10 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  actual_audience_reach varchar [note: 'Verified number of individuals who were exposed to the sponsorship activation based on post-event measurement. Critical for ROI analysis and sponsor value reporting.']
  actual_spend_usd decimal(15,2) [note: 'Total actual expenditure incurred to execute this activation event, expressed in US dollars. Includes production costs, venue fees, staffing, and all direct activation expenses.']
  aircraft_registration varchar [note: 'Tail number or registration identifier of the aircraft used for the activation. Applicable for aircraft livery, in-flight branding, or aircraft-specific sponsorship deliverables.']
  budgeted_spend_usd decimal(15,2) [note: 'Planned budget allocated for this activation event, expressed in US dollars. Used for budget variance analysis and financial planning of sponsorship programs.']
  city_name varchar [note: 'Name of the city where the activation event took place. Used for geographic segmentation and market-level performance analysis of sponsorship activities.']
  content_url varchar [note: 'Web address or link to the digital content created for this activation. Enables direct access to social media posts, landing pages, or digital assets produced.']
  country_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 country code identifying the country where the activation occurred. Enables country-level aggregation and regional performance reporting.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this activation record was first created in the system. Audit field for data lineage and record lifecycle tracking.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for the original transaction currency if different from USD. Supports multi-currency sponsorship programs and local market activations.']
  deliverable_description varchar [note: 'Detailed narrative description of what was delivered during this activation event. Documents the specific sponsorship rights exercised and the execution details for sponsor reporting.']
  digital_channel varchar [note: 'Specific digital platform or channel used for the activation (e.g., Facebook, Instagram, LinkedIn, airline website, mobile app). Applicable for digital and social media activations.']
  engagement_count varchar [note: 'Number of direct interactions or engagements with the activation (clicks, shares, likes, physical interactions). Measures active audience participation beyond passive exposure.']
  estimated_audience_reach varchar [note: 'Projected number of individuals exposed to the sponsorship activation. Used for pre-event planning and to estimate the marketing value delivered to the sponsor.']
  flight_number varchar [note: 'Flight number associated with the activation, applicable for in-flight activations, co-branded flights, or aircraft livery deployments. Links activation to specific flight operations.']
  impressions_count varchar [note: 'Total number of times the sponsorship branding or message was displayed or viewed during the activation. Used for digital and physical media performance measurement.']
  is_contractually_required boolean [note: 'Boolean flag indicating whether this activation was a mandatory deliverable under the sponsorship contract (true) or a value-added bonus activation (false). Critical for compliance tracking.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this activation record was most recently updated. Audit field for change tracking and data quality monitoring.']
  location_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the physical or digital location where the activation took place. Supports geographic and channel-based performance analysis of sponsorship activations. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: airport|aircraft|lounge|stadium|digital|event_venue|city|route|hub — 9 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  media_value_usd decimal(15,2) [note: 'Estimated equivalent advertising value of the exposure delivered through this activation, expressed in US dollars. Calculated based on reach, impressions, and industry media rate benchmarks.']
  outcome_notes varchar [note: 'Free-text field capturing qualitative outcomes, learnings, sponsor feedback, and operational notes from the activation. Supports continuous improvement and sponsor relationship management.']
  responsible_team varchar [note: 'Name of the internal marketing team or department responsible for executing this activation. Supports accountability tracking and resource allocation analysis.']
  sponsor_approval_status varchar [note: 'Indicates whether the sponsor reviewed and approved the activation execution or deliverable. Critical for contract compliance and sponsor satisfaction tracking.']
  sponsor_feedback varchar [note: 'Direct feedback or comments provided by the sponsor regarding the activation execution and quality. Used for relationship management and service improvement.']
  venue_name varchar [note: 'Name of the specific venue, facility, or location where the activation event was executed. Examples include stadium names, conference centers, or specific airport terminals.']

  Note: 'Transactional record capturing specific activation events and deliverables executed under a sponsorship agreement. Stores sponsorship reference, activation type (hospitality event, co-branded campaign flight, airport activation, social media post, press event, in-stadium branding deployment), activation date, location, estimated audience reach, actual spend on activation, activation status, and outcome notes. Enables tracking of whether contracted sponsorship rights are being fully utilised and measures the marketing value delivered per activation.'
}

Table marketing.passenger_touchpoint {
  passenger_touchpoint_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for each passenger touchpoint interaction record.']
  ancillary_order_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to ancillary.order. Business justification: Passenger touchpoints (web visits, app interactions) that result in ancillary purchases require direct attribution for customer journey analysis. Real business need to link touchpoints to ancillary re']
  campaign_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the marketing campaign that generated or influenced this touchpoint, if applicable.']
  digital_campaign_creative_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to marketing.digital_campaign_creative. Business justification: passenger_touchpoint should link to the specific creative asset viewed or interacted with during the touchpoint. When a passenger interacts with marketing content, tracking which creative they engaged']
  employee_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the call center agent, airport staff, or chatbot agent who handled the touchpoint, if applicable.']
  pax_profile_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the passenger who generated this touchpoint interaction.']
  previous_passenger_touchpoint_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing FK on passenger_touchpoint (previous_passenger_touchpoint_id)']
  pricing_record_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to revenue.pricing_record. Business justification: Journey analytics - connects customer touchpoints to pricing sessions for conversion path analysis. Airlines analyze which touchpoint sequences lead to quotes for customer journey optimization and dig']
  ticket_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to revenue.ticket. Business justification: Links customer journey touchpoints to final ticket purchase for multi-touch attribution modeling. Airlines analyze complete customer journeys from first touchpoint to purchase for marketing mix optimi']
  airport_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter IATA airport code if the touchpoint occurred at an airport location (kiosk, gate, lounge).']
  booking_reference varchar [note: 'Booking reference number if this touchpoint resulted in a flight or ancillary booking.']
  browser varchar [note: 'Web browser used during the touchpoint interaction, if applicable (e.g., Chrome, Safari, Firefox).']
  channel varchar [note: 'Marketing or commercial channel through which the touchpoint occurred. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: web|mobile_app|email|paid_search|display_advertising|social_media|call_center|airport|inflight|sms|push_notification|chatbot — promote to reference product]']
  content_viewed varchar [note: 'Description or identifier of the content, page, or screen viewed during the touchpoint (e.g., URL path, page title, IFE content ID).']
  conversion_flag boolean [note: 'Boolean indicator whether this touchpoint resulted in a conversion event (booking, purchase, registration).']
  conversion_type varchar [note: 'Type of conversion that occurred as a result of this touchpoint, if any.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this touchpoint record was first created in the system, in format yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX.']
  customer_segment varchar [note: 'Marketing or customer segment classification of the passenger at the time of the touchpoint (e.g., business traveler, leisure, VIP).']
  destination_airport_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter IATA code of the destination airport if the touchpoint is related to a specific route or booking search.']
  device_type varchar [note: 'Type of device used by the passenger during the touchpoint interaction.']
  engagement_duration_seconds varchar [note: 'Duration of the touchpoint interaction in seconds (e.g., time spent on page, call duration, IFE session length).']
  ffp_number varchar [note: 'Frequent Flyer Program membership number of the passenger associated with this touchpoint, if authenticated.']
  flight_number varchar [note: 'Flight number associated with the touchpoint if the interaction occurred in the context of a specific flight (e.g., IFE engagement, gate interaction).']
  geographic_city varchar [note: 'City name where the touchpoint interaction occurred, derived from IP geolocation or device location.']
  geographic_country_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO country code representing the geographic location of the touchpoint interaction.']
  interaction_depth varchar [note: 'Measure of interaction depth such as scroll depth percentage, pages per session, or clicks within the touchpoint.']
  ip_address varchar [note: 'IP address of the device from which the touchpoint originated, used for geographic and fraud analysis.']
  operating_system varchar [note: 'Operating system of the device used during the touchpoint (e.g., iOS, Android, Windows, macOS).']
  origin_airport_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter IATA code of the origin airport if the touchpoint is related to a specific route or booking search.']
  page_url varchar [note: 'Full URL of the web page or app screen viewed during the touchpoint, if applicable.']
  pnr varchar [note: 'Six-character alphanumeric Passenger Name Record identifier associated with this touchpoint, if applicable.']
  referrer_url varchar [note: 'URL of the referring page or source that led the passenger to this touchpoint.']
  revenue_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'Revenue amount directly attributed to this touchpoint interaction, if a purchase or booking occurred.']
  revenue_currency varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO currency code for the revenue amount attributed to this touchpoint.']
  session_reference varchar [note: 'Unique session identifier grouping multiple touchpoints within a single user session or visit.']
  touchpoint_status varchar [note: 'Status of the touchpoint interaction indicating whether it was completed successfully or abandoned.']
  touchpoint_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Precise date and time when the passenger touchpoint interaction occurred, in format yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX.']
  touchpoint_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the marketing or commercial touchpoint interaction type. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: website_visit|mobile_app_session|email_open|email_click|ad_impression|ad_click|call_center_contact|airport_kiosk|checkin_counter|gate_interaction|lounge_visit|ife_engagement|chatbot_session|sms_interaction|push_notification|social_media_interaction — promote to reference product]']
  utm_campaign varchar [note: 'UTM campaign parameter identifying the specific campaign name for attribution tracking.']
  utm_content varchar [note: 'UTM content parameter used to differentiate similar content or links within the same campaign.']
  utm_medium varchar [note: 'UTM medium parameter identifying the marketing medium (e.g., cpc, email, social) for campaign attribution.']
  utm_source varchar [note: 'UTM source parameter identifying the traffic source (e.g., google, newsletter, facebook) for campaign attribution.']
  utm_term varchar [note: 'UTM term parameter identifying paid search keywords for campaign attribution.']

  Note: 'Transactional record capturing every marketing and commercial touchpoint interaction between the airline and a passenger across digital and physical channels throughout the customer journey. Stores passenger profile reference, touchpoint type (website visit, app session, email open, ad impression, call centre contact, airport interaction, IFE engagement, chatbot session), channel, touchpoint timestamp, session identifier, device type, geographic location, content or page viewed, campaign attribution reference (if applicable), and whether the touchpoint resulted in a booking or ancillary purchase. Provides the raw interaction log for customer journey mapping and attribution modelling.'
}

Table marketing.ab_test {
  ab_test_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the A/B or multivariate test. Primary key.']
  campaign_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the marketing campaign associated with this test.']
  iterated_ab_test_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing FK on ab_test (iterated_ab_test_id)']
  test_owner_employee_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to workforce.employee. Business justification: Test owner is an employee responsible for test design, hypothesis validation, and results analysis. Airlines track test ownership for accountability, methodology validation, and performance optimizati']
  actual_end_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Actual date and time when the test was concluded and data collection stopped.']
  actual_start_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Actual date and time when the test was launched and began collecting data.']
  approval_status varchar [note: 'Approval status of the test design before launch: pending (awaiting review), approved (cleared to run), or rejected (not approved).']
  approval_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the test was approved for execution.']
  approved_by varchar [note: 'Name or identifier of the individual who approved the test for execution.']
  cabin_class_focus varchar [note: 'Cabin class targeted by the test, if the experiment is specific to a travel class.']
  control_variant_description varchar [note: 'Detailed description of the control (baseline) variant used in the test.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the test record was first created in the system.']
  digital_channel varchar [note: 'The digital marketing channel where the test is being conducted.']
  ffp_tier_focus varchar [note: 'Specific FFP loyalty tier targeted by the test (e.g., Silver, Gold, Platinum), if applicable.']
  hypothesis varchar [note: 'The business hypothesis being tested, describing the expected outcome and rationale for the experiment.']
  minimum_detectable_effect decimal(5,4) [note: 'The smallest effect size that the test is designed to detect with statistical confidence, expressed as a decimal (e.g., 0.0200 for 2% lift).']
  modified_by varchar [note: 'Identifier of the user or system that last modified the test record.']
  modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the test record was last modified.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Additional notes, observations, or context about the test design, execution, or results.']
  owning_team varchar [note: 'Marketing team or business unit responsible for the test (e.g., Digital Marketing, Email Marketing, Revenue Management).']
  p_value decimal(6,5) [note: 'The probability that the observed difference between variants occurred by chance, used to assess statistical significance.']
  planned_end_date date [note: 'Scheduled date for the test to conclude.']
  planned_start_date date [note: 'Scheduled date for the test to begin.']
  primary_success_metric varchar [note: 'The primary metric used to determine test success, such as conversion rate, click-through rate (CTR), revenue per visitor (RPV), or booking completion rate.']
  route_focus varchar [note: 'Specific route or origin-destination pair targeted by the test, if applicable (e.g., JFK-LHR, LAX-NRT).']
  sample_size_target varchar [note: 'Target number of observations (visitors, impressions, or conversions) required to achieve statistical power.']
  secondary_metrics varchar [note: 'Additional metrics tracked to understand broader impact, such as engagement rate, bounce rate, average order value, or ancillary attachment rate.']
  statistical_confidence_threshold decimal(4,3) [note: 'The confidence level required to declare statistical significance, typically 0.950 (95%) or 0.990 (99%).']
  statistical_significance_achieved boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the test results achieved the required statistical confidence threshold (True) or not (False).']
  target_audience_segment varchar [note: 'Customer segment or audience targeted by the test, such as Frequent Flyer Program (FFP) tier, geographic market, or behavioral cohort.']
  test_code varchar [note: 'Business-readable unique code for the test, used for operational reference and reporting.']
  test_name varchar [note: 'Descriptive name of the A/B or multivariate test for business identification.']
  test_platform varchar [note: 'Technology platform or tool used to execute the test (e.g., Optimizely, Adobe Target, Google Optimize, internal experimentation platform).']
  test_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the test: draft (being designed), approved (ready to launch), running (actively collecting data), paused (temporarily stopped), concluded (completed with results), or cancelled (terminated without conclusion).']
  test_type varchar [note: 'Type of experiment design: A/B (two variants), multivariate (multiple factors), split URL (different landing pages), sequential (phased rollout), or bandit (adaptive allocation).']
  traffic_split_percentage varchar [note: 'Distribution of traffic across variants, expressed as percentages (e.g., 50/50 for A/B, 33/33/34 for three-way split).']
  treatment_variant_description varchar [note: 'Detailed description of the treatment (experimental) variant(s) being tested against the control.']
  variant_count varchar [note: 'Total number of variants in the test, including control and all treatment variants.']
  winning_variant varchar [note: 'Identifier of the variant that achieved the best performance on the primary success metric, determined after test conclusion.']

  Note: 'Master record for A/B and multivariate tests conducted within marketing campaigns, email programmes, and digital experiences. Captures test name, test type (A/B, multivariate, split URL), hypothesis, associated campaign or digital channel, test variants (control and treatment descriptions), traffic split percentages, primary success metric (conversion rate, CTR, revenue per visitor), secondary metrics, test start and end dates, minimum detectable effect, statistical confidence threshold, test status (running, paused, concluded), winning variant, and statistical significance achieved. Provides the authoritative register of all controlled marketing experiments.'
}

Table marketing.competitor_fare_watch {
  competitor_fare_watch_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the competitor fare observation record.']
  previous_competitor_fare_watch_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing FK on competitor_fare_watch (previous_competitor_fare_watch_id)']
  advance_purchase_days varchar [note: 'Number of days between the observation date and the travel date, representing the booking window or advance purchase period.']
  alliance_affiliation varchar [note: 'The global airline alliance membership of the competitor carrier, if applicable.']
  availability_status varchar [note: 'The seat availability status observed at the time of fare capture, indicating inventory levels for the booking class.']
  booking_class_code varchar [note: 'Single-letter booking class or fare basis code observed for the competitor fare, indicating fare restrictions and inventory bucket.']
  cabin_class varchar [note: 'The cabin class or service level for which the fare was observed.']
  codeshare_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the observed fare is for a codeshare flight operated by a partner carrier (true) or a flight operated by the marketing carrier itself (false).']
  collection_method varchar [note: 'The technical method or process used to capture the competitor fare observation.']
  competitor_carrier_code varchar [note: 'Two-character IATA airline code identifying the competitor carrier whose fare was observed.']
  competitor_flight_number varchar [note: 'The specific flight number operated by the competitor carrier for which the fare was observed, combining carrier code and numeric flight identifier.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The timestamp when this competitor fare watch record was first inserted into the data platform.']
  data_quality_score decimal(3,2) [note: 'A numeric score (0.00 to 1.00) representing the completeness and reliability of the fare observation data, based on validation rules and source system confidence.']
  data_source varchar [note: 'The type of source system or channel from which the competitor fare data was collected.']
  destination_airport_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter IATA airport code representing the arrival airport for the fare observation.']
  fare_basis_code varchar [note: 'The full fare basis code string observed, providing detailed fare rule and inventory classification information.']
  fare_conditions_summary varchar [note: 'Textual summary of key fare rules and restrictions observed, including refundability, change fees, minimum stay, and other material conditions.']
  fare_currency_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code in which the observed fare is denominated.']
  fare_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the observed fare indicating whether it is a standard published fare, private negotiated rate, promotional offer, or opaque fare.']
  gds_system_code varchar [note: 'Two-character code identifying the specific GDS system used for fare collection, if applicable (e.g., 1A for Amadeus, 1B for Abacus, 1G for Galileo, 1P for Worldspan, 1S for Sabre, 1V for Apollo).']
  market_segment varchar [note: 'The target customer segment or market classification for the observed fare (leisure, business, visiting friends and relatives, group, corporate).']
  modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The timestamp when this competitor fare watch record was last updated in the data platform.']
  number_of_stops varchar [note: 'The number of intermediate stops or connections included in the observed fare itinerary (0 for nonstop, 1+ for connecting flights).']
  observation_status varchar [note: 'The current status of the fare observation record in the competitive intelligence system.']
  observation_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The exact date and time when the competitor fare was observed and captured from the data source.']
  observed_fare_amount decimal(12,2) [note: 'The base fare amount observed for the competitor offering, excluding taxes and fees.']
  operating_carrier_code varchar [note: 'Two-character IATA code of the airline actually operating the flight, if different from the marketing carrier in a codeshare arrangement.']
  origin_airport_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter IATA airport code representing the departure airport for the fare observation.']
  ota_platform_name varchar [note: 'Name of the online travel agency or metasearch platform from which the fare was observed, if applicable.']
  point_of_sale_country varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO country code representing the geographic market or point of sale where the fare was observed.']
  return_travel_date date [note: 'The return departure date for round-trip fares, null for one-way observations.']
  round_trip_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the observed fare is for a round-trip journey (true) or one-way travel (false).']
  scraping_session_reference varchar [note: 'Identifier for the automated data collection session or batch run during which this fare observation was captured, enabling traceability and quality audits.']
  seats_available_count varchar [note: 'The number of seats available in the observed booking class at the time of observation, if disclosed by the source system.']
  taxes_fees_amount decimal(12,2) [note: 'The total amount of taxes, government fees, and carrier-imposed surcharges included in the total fare.']
  total_fare_amount decimal(12,2) [note: 'The all-in fare amount including base fare, taxes, fees, and surcharges as displayed to the customer.']
  travel_date date [note: 'The departure date for which the competitor fare was quoted.']

  Note: 'Transactional record capturing competitive fare monitoring data collected from GDS, OTA, and direct channel scraping for key origin-destination markets. Stores observation timestamp, origin airport, destination airport, competitor carrier IATA code, observed fare amount, currency, cabin class, travel date, booking class, advance purchase days, fare conditions summary, data source, and collection method. Provides the commercial intelligence dataset used by revenue management and marketing teams to benchmark pricing competitiveness and inform promotional fare decisions. Distinct from route.market_assessment which is a higher-level strategic assessment; this entity captures granular point-in-time fare observations.'
}

Table marketing.promotional_fare {
  promotional_fare_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the promotional fare offer. Primary key for this entity.']
  campaign_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the parent marketing campaign under which this promotional fare is published.']
  fare_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to revenue.fare. Business justification: Links promotional fare campaigns to actual fare records in revenue system for pricing execution, inventory control, and yield management. Airlines must connect marketing promotions to operational fare']
  general_ledger_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.general_ledger. Business justification: Promotional fare revenue impacts must be tracked in GL for revenue recognition under IFRS 15, fare discount accounting, and financial reporting. Airlines need to reconcile promotional revenue to GL ac']
  origin_station_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to airport.station. Business justification: Promotional fares originate from specific stations for route-based pricing campaigns and load factor optimization. Real business process: origin-station fare promotions for demand stimulation, competi']
  primary_promotional_employee_id varchar [note: 'User identifier of the marketing or revenue management staff member who created this promotional fare record.']
  superseded_promotional_fare_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing FK on promotional_fare (superseded_promotional_fare_id)']
  advance_purchase_days varchar [note: 'Minimum number of days before departure that the booking must be made to qualify for this promotional fare. Null if no advance purchase requirement.']
  amount decimal(12,2) [note: 'Base promotional fare price offered to passengers, excluding taxes and fees.']
  approval_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this promotional fare was approved for publication. Null if not yet approved.']
  blackout_dates varchar [note: 'Comma-separated list of dates or date ranges within the travel window when the promotional fare is NOT valid (e.g., peak holiday periods).']
  booking_class varchar [note: 'Single-letter Revenue Management System (RMS) booking class code associated with this promotional fare (e.g., \'Q\', \'T\', \'L\').']
  booking_window_end_date date [note: 'Last date on which passengers can book this promotional fare.']
  booking_window_start_date date [note: 'First date on which passengers can book this promotional fare.']
  cabin_class varchar [note: 'Cabin class to which this promotional fare applies. \'all\' indicates the promotion is valid across all cabin classes.']
  change_fee_amount decimal(10,2) [note: 'Fee charged for making changes to tickets purchased under this promotional fare. Null if changes are not permitted or are free.']
  changeable_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether tickets purchased under this promotional fare allow date or flight changes. True = changeable, False = non-changeable.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this promotional fare record was first created in the system.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for the promotional fare amount.']
  destination_airport_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter IATA airport code for the destination. May be null if promotion applies to a region or globally.']
  discount_percentage decimal(5,2) [note: 'Percentage discount off the standard published fare, if applicable. Null if promotional fare is an absolute amount.']
  distribution_channels varchar [note: 'Comma-separated list of channels through which this promotional fare is available (e.g., \'website\', \'mobile_app\', \'GDS\', \'call_center\', \'travel_agent\').']
  ffp_tier_eligibility varchar [note: 'Comma-separated list of FFP tier codes eligible for this promotional fare (e.g., \'gold\', \'platinum\', \'all\'). Null if no tier restriction.']
  gds_distribution_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this promotional fare is distributed through Global Distribution Systems (Amadeus, Sabre, Travelport). True = available via GDS, False = direct channels only.']
  maximum_stay_days varchar [note: 'Maximum number of days the passenger may stay at the destination under this promotional fare. Null if no maximum stay restriction.']
  mileage_accrual_percentage decimal(5,2) [note: 'Percentage of flown miles that passengers earn toward their FFP account when booking this promotional fare. 100 = full mileage, 0 = no mileage.']
  minimum_stay_days varchar [note: 'Minimum number of days the passenger must stay at the destination to qualify for this promotional fare. Null if no minimum stay required.']
  modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this promotional fare record was last modified.']
  origin_airport_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter IATA airport code for the origin. May be null if promotion applies to a region or globally.']
  owning_team varchar [note: 'Name of the commercial or marketing team responsible for managing this promotional fare offer.']
  promotion_code varchar [note: 'Unique alphanumeric code passengers must enter at booking to access this promotional fare. May be null for automatic promotions.']
  promotion_name varchar [note: 'Marketing name of the promotional fare offer (e.g., \'Summer Flash Sale\', \'Route Launch Special\', \'Black Friday Fare\').']
  promotion_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the promotional fare offer by marketing strategy type. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: seat_sale|flash_sale|route_launch|seasonal|error_fare_managed|loyalty_exclusive|partner_promotion — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  promotional_fare_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the promotional fare offer.']
  refundable_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether tickets purchased under this promotional fare are refundable. True = refundable, False = non-refundable.']
  region_code varchar [note: 'Internal region code when route_scope is regional (e.g., \'APAC\', \'EMEA\', \'AMER\'). Null for specific routes.']
  route_scope varchar [note: 'Geographic scope of the promotional fare: specific origin-destination pair, regional coverage, country-level, or global network.']
  seat_allocation_total varchar [note: 'Total number of seats allocated across all flights for this promotional fare offer. Null if unlimited.']
  seat_cap_per_flight varchar [note: 'Maximum number of promotional fare seats available on any single flight. Null if no per-flight cap.']
  seats_sold varchar [note: 'Current count of seats sold under this promotional fare offer across all flights.']
  travel_window_end_date date [note: 'Last date of travel for which this promotional fare is valid.']
  travel_window_start_date date [note: 'First date of travel for which this promotional fare is valid.']

  Note: 'Master record for airline promotional fare offers published through marketing channels, including seat sales, flash sales, error fares (managed), route launch fares, and seasonal promotional pricing. Captures promotion name, associated campaign reference, origin-destination scope (specific route, region, or global), cabin class, promotional fare amount, currency, booking window (start and end dates), travel window (start and end dates), fare conditions (refundable, changeable, minimum stay), distribution channels, promo code (if applicable), seat cap per flight, total seat allocation, status (draft, active, expired, sold out), and owning commercial team. Distinct from revenue.fare which is the full fare basis master; this entity specifically tracks marketing-driven promotional offers.'
}

Table marketing.co_marketing_agreement {
  co_marketing_agreement_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the co-marketing agreement record. Primary key.']
  accounts_payable_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.accounts_payable. Business justification: Co-marketing agreements with partners generate shared cost invoices and partner contribution settlements that flow through AP. Airlines track these for intercompany reconciliation and financial report']
  approved_by_employee_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to workforce.employee. Business justification: Agreement approval requires employee authorization for legal commitment, budget allocation, and strategic alignment. Airlines track approver identity for audit trails, contract governance, and delegat']
  campaign_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to marketing.campaign. Business justification: co_marketing_agreement should link to a primary campaign that represents the agreement\'s main marketing initiative. While agreements may spawn multiple campaigns over time, linking to a primary/flagsh']
  partner_organization_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the commercial partner organization (tourism board, hotel chain, car rental company, credit card issuer, destination marketing organization, or OTA).']
  renewed_co_marketing_agreement_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing FK on co_marketing_agreement (renewed_co_marketing_agreement_id)']
  agreement_manager varchar [note: 'Name or identifier of the airline employee serving as the primary point of contact and manager for this co-marketing agreement.']
  agreement_reference_number varchar [note: 'External business identifier for the co-marketing agreement, used in contracts and partner communications.']
  agreement_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the co-marketing agreement indicating its operational state and validity. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: draft|pending approval|active|suspended|expired|terminated|renewed — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  agreement_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the co-marketing agreement defining the nature of the partnership and marketing collaboration model.']
  airline_contribution_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Financial contribution committed by the airline to the co-marketing agreement budget.']
  approval_date date [note: 'Date when the co-marketing agreement received final internal approval from the airline.']
  approval_status varchar [note: 'Internal approval workflow status indicating whether the agreement has been reviewed and authorized by the airline\'s commercial leadership.']
  campaign_deliverables varchar [note: 'Detailed description of the marketing deliverables, activities, and outputs committed under the co-marketing agreement (e.g., digital campaigns, print advertising, event sponsorships, content creation).']
  contract_document_reference varchar [note: 'Reference identifier or file path to the signed legal contract document for the co-marketing agreement.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the co-marketing agreement record was first created in the system.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for all monetary amounts in the agreement.']
  end_date date [note: 'Scheduled expiration date of the co-marketing agreement. Nullable for evergreen agreements subject to termination notice.']
  exclusivity_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the agreement grants exclusive co-marketing rights within a specific category or geography, preventing similar agreements with competing partners.']
  geographic_scope varchar [note: 'Geographic markets or regions covered by the co-marketing agreement, defining where joint marketing activities will be executed. May include multiple countries or regions.']
  joint_budget_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Total combined marketing budget committed by both the airline and partner for the duration of the agreement.']
  legal_entity varchar [note: 'Name of the airline legal entity that is the contracting party for this co-marketing agreement.']
  modified_by varchar [note: 'Identifier of the user or system that last modified the co-marketing agreement record.']
  modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the co-marketing agreement record was last modified or updated.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Additional free-text notes, comments, or special conditions related to the co-marketing agreement.']
  owning_commercial_team varchar [note: 'Name of the airline commercial or marketing team responsible for managing the co-marketing agreement and partner relationship.']
  partner_contact_email varchar [note: 'Email address of the primary partner contact for agreement coordination and communication.']
  partner_contact_name varchar [note: 'Name of the primary contact person at the partner organization responsible for the co-marketing agreement.']
  partner_contact_phone varchar [note: 'Phone number of the primary partner contact for agreement coordination.']
  partner_contribution_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Financial contribution committed by the partner organization to the co-marketing agreement budget.']
  performance_metrics varchar [note: 'Key performance indicators and success metrics defined to measure the effectiveness of the co-marketing agreement (e.g., incremental bookings, brand awareness lift, revenue targets).']
  renewal_terms varchar [note: 'Contractual terms and conditions governing the renewal or extension of the co-marketing agreement beyond the initial term.']
  route_focus varchar [note: 'Specific routes, origin-destination pairs, or route networks that are the focus of the co-marketing activities.']
  start_date date [note: 'Effective start date when the co-marketing agreement becomes active and joint marketing activities may commence.']
  target_passenger_segment varchar [note: 'Description of the passenger segments or customer profiles targeted by the co-marketing agreement (e.g., leisure travelers, business travelers, FFP elite members).']
  termination_notice_days varchar [note: 'Number of days advance notice required by either party to terminate the co-marketing agreement.']

  Note: 'Master record for co-marketing and joint marketing agreements between the airline and commercial partners including tourism boards, hotel chains, car rental companies, credit card issuers, and destination marketing organisations. Captures partner organisation name, agreement type (co-branded campaign, joint advertising, destination marketing, credit card co-brand, OTA partnership), geographic scope, agreement start and end dates, joint budget contribution, airline contribution amount, partner contribution amount, currency, campaign deliverables, approval status, and owning commercial team. Provides the authoritative register of all co-marketing commitments and partner marketing investments.'
}

Table marketing.social_media_post {
  social_media_post_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the social media post record.']
  aircraft_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to fleet.aircraft. Business justification: Social media posts featuring specific aircraft (delivery announcements, special liveries, milestone flights, behind-the-scenes content) reference tail numbers for content planning, asset tracking, and']
  campaign_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the marketing campaign this post is associated with, if applicable.']
  digital_campaign_creative_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to marketing.digital_campaign_creative. Business justification: social_media_post should link to the creative asset used in the post. Social posts use designed creative assets (images, videos, copy). Adding digital_campaign_creative_id FK to marketing.digital_camp']
  employee_id varchar [note: 'The internal user identifier of the marketing team member who created or authored the post.']
  reply_to_social_media_post_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing FK on social_media_post (reply_to_social_media_post_id)']
  account_handle varchar [note: 'The airline\'s social media account handle or username on the platform (e.g., @AirlineName).']
  actual_publish_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The actual date and time when the post was successfully published to the platform.']
  approval_status varchar [note: 'The approval workflow status for the post: pending (awaiting review), approved (cleared for publishing), rejected (not approved), not_required (no approval needed).']
  approval_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when the post was approved for publishing.']
  approved_by varchar [note: 'The user identifier or name of the person who approved the post for publishing.']
  boosted_spend_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'The amount spent to boost or promote this post, if applicable.']
  boosted_spend_currency varchar [note: 'The three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for the boosted spend amount.']
  comments varchar [note: 'Total number of comments posted by users on this post.']
  content_text varchar [note: 'The text content or caption of the social media post, including hashtags and mentions.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when this social media post record was first created in the system.']
  engagement_rate decimal(5,2) [note: 'Calculated engagement rate as a percentage, representing total engagements (likes, comments, shares, saves) divided by reach or impressions.']
  hashtags varchar [note: 'Comma-separated list of hashtags used in the post for discoverability and campaign tracking.']
  impressions varchar [note: 'Total number of times the post was displayed to users, regardless of whether it was clicked or engaged with.']
  language_code varchar [note: 'The two-letter ISO 639-1 language code representing the language of the post content.']
  likes varchar [note: 'Total number of likes or reactions the post received.']
  link_clicks varchar [note: 'Total number of clicks on links embedded in the post content.']
  market_region varchar [note: 'The geographic market or region this post is targeted toward (e.g., North America, Europe, Asia-Pacific).']
  media_attachment_reference varchar [note: 'Reference identifier or URI to the media assets (images, videos, graphics) attached to the post, stored in the brand asset management system.']
  mentions varchar [note: 'Comma-separated list of user handles or accounts mentioned in the post.']
  modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when this social media post record was last modified or updated.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text notes or comments from the marketing team regarding the post, including context, learnings, or special instructions.']
  platform varchar [note: 'The social media platform where the post is published (Instagram, Facebook, X/Twitter, LinkedIn, TikTok, YouTube). [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: Instagram|Facebook|X|LinkedIn|TikTok|YouTube|WeChat|LINE — promote to reference product]']
  post_reference_number varchar [note: 'Business-facing unique reference code for the social media post, used for tracking and governance.']
  post_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the post: draft (being created), scheduled (queued for publishing), published (live), deleted (removed), failed (publishing error).']
  post_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the post type: organic (unpaid), boosted (promoted organic), paid dark post (paid without organic presence), story, reel, carousel.']
  post_url varchar [note: 'The direct URL link to the published post on the social media platform.']
  reach varchar [note: 'Total number of unique users who saw the post at least once.']
  saves varchar [note: 'Total number of times users saved or bookmarked the post for later viewing.']
  scheduled_publish_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The planned date and time when the post is scheduled to be published to the platform.']
  sentiment_score decimal(5,2) [note: 'Aggregated sentiment score derived from user comments and reactions, typically ranging from -1.0 (negative) to +1.0 (positive).']
  shares varchar [note: 'Total number of times the post was shared or reposted by users.']
  target_audience_segment varchar [note: 'The audience segment or demographic targeting criteria applied to the post for paid or boosted distribution.']
  video_views varchar [note: 'Total number of video views if the post contains video content, measured according to platform-specific view thresholds.']

  Note: 'Transactional record capturing published and scheduled social media posts across the airline\'s owned social media accounts. Stores platform (Instagram, Facebook, X/Twitter, LinkedIn, TikTok, YouTube, WeChat, LINE), account handle, post type (organic, boosted, paid dark post), post content text, media attachments reference, associated campaign reference, scheduled publish timestamp, actual publish timestamp, post status (draft, scheduled, published, deleted), impressions, reach, engagements (likes, comments, shares, saves), link clicks, video views, and boosted spend. Provides the operational record of all social media publishing activity for performance tracking and content governance.'
}

Table marketing.email_send {
  email_send_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the email send event. Primary key.']
  ancillary_order_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to ancillary.order. Business justification: Email campaigns promoting ancillary services (seat selection reminders, baggage pre-purchase offers) need conversion tracking to ancillary orders. Real business need for email campaign ROI measurement']
  campaign_execution_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the campaign execution batch that triggered this email send.']
  digital_campaign_creative_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the email template used for this send event.']
  passenger_profile_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the passenger profile record for known customers. Null for anonymous recipients.']
  resend_email_send_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing FK on email_send (resend_email_send_id)']
  ab_test_variant varchar [note: 'Identifier for the A/B test variant or split test group assigned to this email send. Null if not part of a test.']
  bounce_reason varchar [note: 'Detailed reason or SMTP error message explaining why the email bounced. Null if delivered successfully.']
  bounce_type varchar [note: 'Classification of bounce reason when delivery_status indicates a bounce. Null if delivered successfully.']
  click_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the recipient clicked any link within the email. True if clicked, False otherwise.']
  clicked_url varchar [note: 'The first URL clicked by the recipient within the email. Null if no clicks recorded.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this email send record was created in the system. Format: yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX']
  delivery_status varchar [note: 'Final delivery status of the email as reported by the email service provider or receiving mail server.']
  delivery_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the email was successfully delivered to the recipient mail server. Null if not delivered. Format: yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX']
  device_type varchar [note: 'Type of device used by the recipient to open or interact with the email.']
  email_client varchar [note: 'Email client or application used by the recipient to open the email (e.g., Outlook, Gmail, Apple Mail). Derived from user agent string.']
  esp_provider varchar [note: 'Name of the email service provider used to send this email (e.g., SendGrid, Mailchimp, Adobe Campaign).']
  first_click_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time of the first recorded click event for any link in this email. Null if never clicked. Format: yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX']
  first_open_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time of the first recorded open event for this email. Null if never opened. Format: yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX']
  from_address varchar [note: 'Email address used in the From field of the email header.']
  ip_address varchar [note: 'IP address from which the email was opened or clicked. Used for geographic and device tracking. May be considered PII in some jurisdictions.']
  message_reference varchar [note: 'Unique message identifier assigned by the email service provider or SMTP server for tracking and troubleshooting.']
  modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this email send record was last updated (e.g., delivery status change, engagement event). Format: yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX']
  open_city varchar [note: 'City name where the email was opened, derived from IP geolocation. Null if not opened or location unavailable.']
  open_country_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO country code representing the geographic location where the email was opened, derived from IP address. Null if not opened.']
  open_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the recipient opened the email (tracked via pixel or image load). True if opened, False otherwise.']
  operating_system varchar [note: 'Operating system of the device used to open the email (e.g., iOS, Android, Windows, macOS). Derived from user agent string.']
  personalization_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the email content was personalized for the recipient (e.g., name, FFP tier, route preferences). True if personalized, False otherwise.']
  recipient_email_address varchar [note: 'Email address to which the message was sent. May be anonymous or linked to passenger profile.']
  reply_to_address varchar [note: 'Email address specified in the Reply-To field for recipient responses.']
  send_priority varchar [note: 'Priority level assigned to this email send for queue processing and delivery scheduling.']
  send_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the email was dispatched from the email service provider. Format: yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX']
  spam_complaint_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the recipient marked this email as spam or junk. True if complaint filed, False otherwise.']
  spam_complaint_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the spam complaint was registered. Null if no complaint. Format: yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX']
  subject_line varchar [note: 'Subject line text of the email as sent to the recipient. May be personalized or A/B tested.']
  suppression_list_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this email address is on a suppression list (bounce, unsubscribe, complaint) at the time of send. True if suppressed, False otherwise.']
  total_click_count varchar [note: 'Total number of link clicks recorded for this email (includes multiple clicks by same user).']
  total_open_count varchar [note: 'Total number of times the email was opened by the recipient (includes multiple opens by same user).']
  unsubscribe_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the recipient unsubscribed from the mailing list as a result of this email. True if unsubscribed, False otherwise.']
  unsubscribe_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the recipient unsubscribed. Null if no unsubscribe event. Format: yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX']

  Note: 'Transactional record capturing individual email send events within a campaign execution, representing the dispatch of a marketing or transactional email to a specific passenger or prospect. Stores campaign execution reference, recipient passenger profile reference or anonymous email address, email template reference, send timestamp, delivery status (delivered, bounced hard, bounced soft, filtered), open timestamp, click timestamp, clicked URL, unsubscribe event flag, spam complaint flag, email client, device type, and geographic location of open. Provides the granular email delivery and engagement log used for deliverability management, suppression list maintenance, and individual-level attribution.'
}

Table marketing.destination_content {
  destination_content_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the destination content asset. Primary key.']
  approved_by_employee_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to workforce.employee. Business justification: Content approval requires employee authorization for brand compliance, legal clearance, and publication readiness. Airlines track approver identity for audit trails, content governance, and quality co']
  brand_asset_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to marketing.brand_asset. Business justification: destination_content should link to the brand assets used in the content (images, videos, logos). Destination content is built using brand-approved assets. Adding brand_asset_id FK to marketing.brand_a']
  campaign_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to marketing.campaign. Business justification: destination_content currently has no direct link to campaign, but destination content is often created as part of marketing campaigns to promote specific routes and destinations. Adding campaign_id FK']
  destination_station_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to airport.station. Business justification: Destination content promotes travel to specific stations/cities for route development and destination awareness. Real business process: content marketing effectiveness measurement by destination stati']
  parent_destination_content_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing FK on destination_content (parent_destination_content_id)']
  approval_status varchar [note: 'Current approval state of the content in the governance workflow before publication.']
  approval_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the content was approved for publication.']
  associated_routes varchar [note: 'Comma-separated list of route codes or origin-destination pairs that this content supports, enabling route-specific marketing.']
  author_name varchar [note: 'Name of the content creator or author responsible for producing this destination content.']
  average_time_on_page_seconds varchar [note: 'Average duration in seconds that users spend viewing this content, indicating engagement level.']
  content_code varchar [note: 'Unique business identifier code for the content asset, used for external reference and content management system integration.']
  content_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the destination content asset in the content management workflow.']
  content_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the content format and purpose for destination marketing.']
  copyright_holder varchar [note: 'Legal entity or individual holding copyright ownership of this content asset.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this content record was first created in the system.']
  creative_theme varchar [note: 'Overarching creative or brand theme applied to this content, such as adventure, luxury, family, or cultural exploration.']
  destination_content_description varchar [note: 'Detailed narrative description of the destination content, including key highlights and intended messaging.']
  destination_iata_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter IATA airport or city code representing the destination featured in this content.']
  expiry_date date [note: 'Date when the content is scheduled to expire and be removed from active channels. Null indicates no expiration.']
  ffp_tier_focus varchar [note: 'Frequent flyer program tier or membership level this content is targeted toward, such as Gold, Platinum, or general members.']
  file_format varchar [note: 'Technical file format of the content asset, such as HTML, PDF, MP4, JPG, or PNG.']
  file_reference varchar [note: 'Storage path, URL, or digital asset management system reference to the primary content file (video, image, document).']
  file_size_mb decimal(10,2) [note: 'Size of the content file in megabytes, used for storage and bandwidth planning.']
  gds_distribution_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this content is distributed through Global Distribution Systems for travel agent access.']
  iata_season varchar [note: 'IATA-defined travel season (summer or winter schedule period) that this content is aligned with for seasonal marketing campaigns.']
  language_code varchar [note: 'ISO 639-1 two-letter language code indicating the language of the content.']
  modified_by varchar [note: 'User identifier or name of the person who last modified this content record.']
  modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this content record was last modified or updated.']
  owning_team varchar [note: 'Marketing team or department responsible for managing and maintaining this content asset.']
  page_views varchar [note: 'Total number of times this content has been viewed across all digital channels.']
  partner_airline_code varchar [note: 'IATA two-letter or three-letter airline code of a partner carrier featured or co-promoted in this content.']
  promotional_offer_reference varchar [note: 'Reference code or identifier linking this content to a specific promotional offer or campaign incentive.']
  publication_date date [note: 'Date when the content was first published and made available to customers.']
  seo_keywords varchar [note: 'Comma-separated list of search engine optimization keywords and phrases to improve content discoverability.']
  social_shares varchar [note: 'Total count of times this content has been shared on social media platforms.']
  target_audience_segment varchar [note: 'Primary customer segment this content is designed to engage, such as business travelers, leisure families, or premium customers. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: business_travelers|leisure_families|premium_customers|millennials|adventure_seekers|luxury_travelers|budget_conscious — promote to reference product]']
  title varchar [note: 'The display title of the destination content asset as shown to customers across digital channels.']
  usage_restrictions varchar [note: 'Any restrictions or limitations on how and where this content may be used, such as geographic or channel restrictions.']
  usage_rights varchar [note: 'Description of the permitted usage rights and licensing terms for this content asset.']

  Note: 'Master record for destination and travel inspiration content assets managed by the airline\'s marketing team for use across digital channels, in-flight entertainment, and printed collateral.
