Captures destination IATA airport or city code, content title, content type (destination guide, travel blog article, video, photo gallery, route spotlight, seasonal travel inspiration), language, author, publication date, expiry date, associated routes served, target audience segment, SEO keywords, content status (draft, published, archived), and performance metrics (page views, time on page, social shares). Provides the content management register for destination marketing and travel inspiration programmes.'
}

Table marketing.event {
  event_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the marketing event record. Primary key.']
  accounts_payable_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.accounts_payable. Business justification: Marketing events generate vendor invoices (venue, catering, logistics) that create AP entries. Airlines need to reconcile event spend commitments to AP for payment processing and financial close.']
  aircraft_type_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to fleet.aircraft_type. Business justification: Marketing events showcasing aircraft types (delivery ceremonies, trade shows, media tours, customer experience events) link event records to aircraft types for logistics planning, stakeholder coordina']
  approved_by_employee_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to workforce.employee. Business justification: Event approval requires employee authorization for budget commitment, resource allocation, and strategic alignment. Airlines track approver identity for audit trails, financial controls, and governanc']
  campaign_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key reference to the parent marketing campaign under which this event is organized.']
  parent_event_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing FK on event (parent_event_id)']
  station_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to airport.station. Business justification: Marketing events (trade shows, roadshows, customer appreciation events) are hosted at or target specific station markets for lead generation and brand awareness. Real business process: event-based mar']
  actual_attendance varchar [note: 'Confirmed number of attendees who actually participated in the marketing event, recorded post-event for performance measurement.']
  actual_spend_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'Total actual expenditure incurred for the marketing event, recorded post-event for financial reconciliation and ROI analysis.']
  approval_date date [note: 'Date when the marketing event received official approval to proceed with planning and execution.']
  approval_status varchar [note: 'Current approval state of the marketing event within the internal governance and budget approval workflow.']
  booth_number varchar [note: 'Assigned booth, stand, or exhibition space number at the event venue where the airline is represented.']
  booth_size_sqm decimal(8,2) [note: 'Total floor area in square meters of the airline\'s exhibition booth or display space at the event.']
  budget_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'Total planned budget allocated for the marketing event covering all anticipated costs including venue, materials, staffing, and promotional activities.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the marketing event record was first created in the system.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO currency code for the budget and spend amounts associated with the marketing event.']
  end_date date [note: 'The date when the marketing event officially concludes, marking the last day of event activities.']
  estimated_attendance varchar [note: 'Projected number of attendees expected to participate in the marketing event, used for planning and resource allocation.']
  event_code varchar [note: 'Unique business identifier code for the marketing event, used for external reference and reporting.']
  event_name varchar [note: 'Full name of the marketing event as it appears in promotional materials and internal documentation.']
  event_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the marketing event indicating its stage from planning through completion.']
  event_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the marketing event by its primary purpose and format. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: trade_show|consumer_travel_fair|press_launch|route_launch_event|corporate_roadshow|travel_agent_fam_trip|sponsorship_activation|airline_awards_ceremony|industry_conference|customer_appreciation_event — promote to reference product]']
  host_city varchar [note: 'Name of the city where the marketing event is taking place.']
  host_country_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO country code for the country hosting the marketing event.']
  lead_generation_target varchar [note: 'Target number of qualified leads or prospect contacts the airline aims to capture during the marketing event.']
  leads_captured varchar [note: 'Actual number of qualified leads or prospect contacts captured during the marketing event, recorded post-event.']
  manager_email varchar [note: 'Email address of the event manager for coordination and communication purposes.']
  manager_name varchar [note: 'Full name of the individual responsible for managing and coordinating all aspects of the marketing event.']
  market_region varchar [note: 'Geographic market or region that the event is targeting for passenger acquisition or brand presence.']
  modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the marketing event record was last updated or modified.']
  owning_team varchar [note: 'Name of the marketing team, department, or business unit responsible for organizing and executing the event.']
  participation_type varchar [note: 'The airline\'s role and level of involvement in the marketing event.']
  partner_organizations varchar [note: 'Names of external organizations, tourism boards, or partner airlines collaborating on the marketing event.']
  post_event_outcome_notes varchar [note: 'Free-text summary of key outcomes, learnings, successes, and areas for improvement captured after the marketing event concludes.']
  roi_score decimal(5,2) [note: 'Calculated return on investment score for the marketing event, expressed as a ratio or percentage, measuring financial effectiveness.']
  route_focus varchar [note: 'Specific route, origin-destination pair, or route network that is the primary focus of the marketing event promotion.']
  start_date date [note: 'The date when the marketing event officially begins, marking the first day of event activities.']
  strategic_objective varchar [note: 'High-level business goal or strategic priority that the marketing event is designed to support, such as brand awareness, route launch, or customer acquisition.']
  target_audience varchar [note: 'Primary audience segment that the marketing event is designed to reach and engage.']
  venue_address varchar [note: 'Full street address of the event venue including building number, street name, and any suite or floor information.']
  venue_name varchar [note: 'Name of the specific venue, convention center, hotel, or facility where the event is held.']

  Note: 'Master record for marketing events, trade shows, roadshows, press launches, and consumer travel fairs organised or attended by the airline. Captures event name, event type (trade show, consumer travel fair, press launch, route launch event, corporate roadshow, travel agent familiarisation trip, sponsorship activation event, airline awards ceremony), host city, venue, event start and end dates, target audience (trade, consumer, media, corporate), airline participation type (exhibitor, sponsor, attendee, host), estimated attendance, actual attendance, budget, actual spend, owning team, and post-event outcome notes. Provides the authoritative register of all marketing event commitments and activations.'
}

Table marketing.campaign_exposure {
  campaign_exposure_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for this campaign exposure record. Primary key.']
  campaign_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to the marketing campaign to which the member was exposed']
  ffp_member_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to the FFP member who was exposed to the campaign']
  attributed_revenue decimal(18,2) [note: 'Revenue amount attributed to this specific campaign exposure, used for ROI calculation and campaign performance measurement. Explicitly identified in detection phase as relationship data.']
  attributed_revenue_currency varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for the attributed revenue amount.']
  attribution_model varchar [note: 'Attribution model used to calculate attributed revenue for this exposure (first-touch, last-touch, linear, time-decay, position-based).']
  conversion_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the member converted (completed desired action such as booking, purchase, enrollment) as a result of this campaign exposure. Explicitly identified in detection phase as relationship data.']
  conversion_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the member converted as a result of the campaign, if conversion_flag is true.']
  creative_variant varchar [note: 'Specific creative variant or A/B test version shown to this member during this exposure.']
  exposure_channel varchar [note: 'Channel through which the member was exposed to the campaign (email, SMS, digital ad, social media, etc.). Explicitly identified in detection phase as relationship data.']
  exposure_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the member was exposed to the campaign. Explicitly identified in detection phase as relationship data.']
  impression_count varchar [note: 'Number of times this member was exposed to this campaign (for frequency capping and saturation analysis).']
  member_segment_at_exposure varchar [note: 'Member segment classification at the time of exposure, captured for campaign effectiveness analysis by segment.']
  response_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the member responded to the campaign exposure (clicked, opened, engaged). Explicitly identified in detection phase as relationship data.']
  response_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the member responded to the campaign, if response_flag is true.']
  tier_level_at_exposure varchar [note: 'FFP tier level of the member at the time of exposure, captured for tier-specific campaign performance analysis.']

  Note: 'This association product represents the exposure event between FFP members and marketing campaigns. It captures when and how a member was exposed to a campaign, their response behavior, and any attributed business value. Each record links one FFP member to one campaign with attributes that exist only in the context of this specific exposure interaction. This is the foundation for campaign attribution, member journey analysis, and marketing ROI measurement.. Existence Justification: In airline marketing operations, FFP members are exposed to multiple campaigns over time (email promotions, route launches, seasonal offers, tier upgrade campaigns), and each campaign reaches thousands or millions of members across multiple channels. The business actively manages and measures these exposures as discrete events with specific timestamps, channels, response behaviors, and attributed revenue. Marketing teams query \'show me all exposures for campaign X\' and \'show me all campaigns member Y was exposed to\' as core attribution and journey analysis workflows.'
}

Table marketing.partner_organization {
  partner_organization_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for partner_organization']
  parent_partner_organization_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing FK on partner_organization (parent_partner_organization_id)']
  alliance_membership varchar [note: 'Global airline alliance to which the partner organization belongs, if applicable.']
  annual_passenger_volume varchar [note: 'Estimated or actual annual passenger volume attributed to this partnership relationship.']
  annual_revenue_contribution decimal(18,2) [note: 'Estimated or actual annual revenue contribution from this partnership in the airline\'s reporting currency.']
  brand_visibility_level varchar [note: 'Level of brand visibility and co-branding prominence granted to the partner in marketing materials.']
  codeshare_enabled boolean [note: 'Indicates whether codeshare flight arrangements are enabled with this airline partner.']
  commission_rate_percentage decimal(5,2) [note: 'Standard commission rate percentage paid to or received from the partner organization.']
  contract_renewal_date date [note: 'Next scheduled date for partnership contract renewal or review.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this partner organization record was first created in the system.']
  data_sharing_agreement boolean [note: 'Indicates whether a formal data sharing agreement exists with the partner organization.']
  gdpr_compliant boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the partner organization has been verified as GDPR compliant for data processing activities.']
  headquarters_city varchar [note: 'City where the partner organization\'s headquarters is located.']
  headquarters_country_code varchar [note: 'ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 country code for the partner organization\'s headquarters location.']
  iata_code varchar [note: 'Two or three-character IATA airline designator code for airline partners.']
  icao_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ICAO airline designator code for airline partners.']
  interline_agreement boolean [note: 'Indicates whether an interline ticketing agreement exists with this airline partner.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this partner organization record was last modified.']
  legal_entity_identifier varchar [note: 'ISO 17442 compliant Legal Entity Identifier for the partner organization.']
  loyalty_program_integration boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the partner organization is integrated with the airline\'s loyalty program for points earning and redemption.']
  marketing_campaign_participation boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the partner organization actively participates in co-branded marketing campaigns.']
  net_promoter_score varchar [note: 'Net Promoter Score measuring the partner organization\'s likelihood to recommend the airline to other potential partners, ranging from -100 to +100.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text notes and comments regarding the partnership relationship, special arrangements, or historical context.']
  organization_code varchar [note: 'Short alphanumeric code used to identify the partner organization in operational systems and marketing materials.']
  organization_name varchar [note: 'Official legal or trading name of the partner organization.']
  partner_satisfaction_score decimal(3,1) [note: 'Satisfaction score from partner relationship surveys, typically on a scale of 0 to 10.']
  partner_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the partner organization by industry or service category.']
  partnership_end_date date [note: 'Date when the partnership relationship ended or is scheduled to end. Null for active ongoing partnerships.']
  partnership_start_date date [note: 'Date when the partnership relationship officially commenced.']
  partnership_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the partnership relationship.']
  partnership_tier varchar [note: 'Strategic classification indicating the level of partnership engagement and benefits.']
  preferred_communication_language varchar [note: 'ISO 639-1 two-letter language code for the partner organization\'s preferred communication language.']
  primary_contact_email varchar [note: 'Primary email address for partnership communications and coordination.']
  primary_contact_phone varchar [note: 'Primary telephone number for partnership communications.']
  revenue_share_percentage decimal(5,2) [note: 'Percentage of revenue shared with the partner organization under the partnership agreement.']
  tax_identification_number varchar [note: 'Tax identification number or equivalent for the partner organization in their primary jurisdiction.']
  website_url varchar [note: 'Official website URL of the partner organization.']

  Note: 'Master reference table for partner_organization. Referenced by partner_organization_id.'
}

Table marketing.ab_test_variant {
  ab_test_variant_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for ab_test_variant']
  ab_test_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the parent A/B test experiment to which this variant belongs.']
  control_ab_test_variant_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing FK on ab_test_variant (control_ab_test_variant_id)']
  call_to_action_text varchar [note: 'Text displayed on the primary call-to-action button or link in this variant (e.g., Book Now, Learn More, Claim Offer).']
  call_to_action_url varchar [note: 'Destination URL for the call-to-action link, including tracking parameters for attribution.']
  channel varchar [note: 'Digital or physical channel through which this variant is delivered to customers.']
  conversion_count varchar [note: 'Total number of conversions (desired actions) achieved by this variant during the test.']
  cost decimal(12,2) [note: 'Total cost incurred to execute this variant, including creative production, offer fulfillment, and channel costs.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this variant record was first created in the system.']
  creative_asset_url varchar [note: 'URL or file path to the creative asset (image, video, HTML template) used in this variant.']
  description varchar [note: 'Detailed description of the variant configuration, including creative elements, messaging, offers, and user experience changes being tested.']
  effective_end_date date [note: 'Date when this variant was deactivated or the test concluded. Null for ongoing variants.']
  effective_start_date date [note: 'Date when this variant became active and began receiving traffic in the A/B test.']
  expected_lift_percentage decimal(5,2) [note: 'Anticipated percentage improvement in the primary success metric compared to control variant.']
  hypothesis varchar [note: 'The business hypothesis being tested with this variant (e.g., Offering 20% discount will increase conversion by 15%).']
  is_winner boolean [note: 'Boolean flag indicating whether this variant was declared the winner of the A/B test based on statistical significance and business criteria.']
  messaging_content varchar [note: 'Text content or copy used in the variant, including headlines, body text, and call-to-action messaging.']
  modified_by varchar [note: 'Username or identifier of the user who last modified this variant configuration.']
  modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this variant record was last updated.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text notes or comments about this variant, including learnings, observations, or special considerations.']
  offer_currency_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for monetary offers (e.g., USD, EUR, GBP).']
  offer_type varchar [note: 'Type of promotional offer included in the variant: discount (price reduction), upgrade (cabin/service upgrade), bonus_miles (loyalty points), free_service (complimentary amenity), none (no offer).']
  offer_value decimal(12,2) [note: 'Monetary value or quantity of the offer (e.g., discount amount in currency, number of bonus miles, upgrade value).']
  personalization_rules varchar [note: 'JSON or text description of dynamic personalization rules applied to this variant based on customer attributes or behavior.']
  priority_rank varchar [note: 'Numeric ranking indicating the priority or sequence of this variant within the test (1 = highest priority).']
  revenue_generated decimal(15,2) [note: 'Total revenue attributed to this variant during the test period, measured in the test\'s base currency.']
  sample_size varchar [note: 'Total number of customers or impressions exposed to this variant during the test period.']
  statistical_significance_level decimal(5,4) [note: 'P-value or confidence level achieved by this variant in the test results (e.g., 0.9500 for 95% confidence).']
  status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the variant: draft (being configured), active (live in test), paused (temporarily stopped), completed (test finished), archived (historical record).']
  target_segment varchar [note: 'Customer segment or audience criteria targeted by this variant (e.g., frequent flyers, leisure travelers, business class passengers).']
  traffic_allocation_percentage decimal(5,2) [note: 'Percentage of total test traffic allocated to this variant (0.00 to 100.00). Sum across all variants in a test should equal 100.']
  variant_code varchar [note: 'Short alphanumeric code uniquely identifying the variant within the test (e.g., CONTROL, VAR_A, VAR_B).']
  variant_name varchar [note: 'Human-readable name describing the variant (e.g., Control Group, Discount Offer 20%, Premium Messaging).']
  variant_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the variant role in the experiment: control (baseline), treatment (experimental), or holdout (excluded from test).']
  created_by varchar [note: 'Username or identifier of the marketing user who created this variant configuration.']

  Note: 'Master reference table for ab_test_variant. Referenced by ab_test_variant_id.'
}

Table marketing.contract_deliverable {
  contract_deliverable_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for contract_deliverable']
  co_marketing_agreement_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the parent marketing contract or agreement under which this deliverable is defined.']
  employee_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the internal team, department, or employee responsible for executing this deliverable.']
  parent_contract_deliverable_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing FK on contract_deliverable (parent_contract_deliverable_id)']
  vendor_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the external vendor or agency contracted to deliver this item, if applicable.']
  acceptance_criteria varchar [note: 'Defined criteria or conditions that must be met for this deliverable to be considered complete and accepted.']
  acceptance_date date [note: 'Date when the deliverable was formally accepted by the stakeholder or client.']
  accepted_by varchar [note: 'Name or identifier of the individual who formally accepted the deliverable.']
  actual_end_date date [note: 'Actual date when this deliverable was completed or closed.']
  actual_metric_value decimal(18,2) [note: 'Actual achieved value for the performance metric after deliverable execution.']
  actual_start_date date [note: 'Actual date when work on this deliverable commenced.']
  channel varchar [note: 'Primary marketing channel or medium through which this deliverable will be distributed or executed.']
  completion_percentage decimal(5,2) [note: 'Percentage of work completed for this deliverable, ranging from 0.00 to 100.00.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this contract deliverable record was first created in the system.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for the deliverable value amount.']
  deliverable_category varchar [note: 'Broader classification or grouping of the deliverable for organizational and reporting purposes.']
  deliverable_code varchar [note: 'Unique business identifier or code assigned to this contract deliverable for external reference and tracking.']
  deliverable_name varchar [note: 'Human-readable name or title of the contract deliverable.']
  deliverable_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the contract deliverable indicating its progress and state.']
  deliverable_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the deliverable by its nature or category within the marketing contract.']
  deliverable_value_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Monetary value or contract price allocated to this specific deliverable.']
  dependencies varchar [note: 'Description of other deliverables or tasks that this deliverable depends on or is blocked by.']
  description varchar [note: 'Detailed textual description of the contract deliverable, including scope, objectives, and key requirements.']
  geographic_scope varchar [note: 'Geographic region or market where this deliverable will be deployed or is applicable.']
  is_milestone boolean [note: 'Flag indicating whether this deliverable represents a major milestone within the contract.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this contract deliverable record was last updated or modified.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Additional notes, comments, or observations related to this contract deliverable.']
  performance_metric varchar [note: 'Key performance indicator or metric used to measure the success of this deliverable.']
  priority_level varchar [note: 'Business priority assigned to this deliverable indicating its relative importance within the contract.']
  risk_level varchar [note: 'Assessment of the risk level associated with successful completion of this deliverable.']
  scheduled_end_date date [note: 'Planned date by which this deliverable is scheduled to be completed.']
  scheduled_start_date date [note: 'Planned date when work on this deliverable is scheduled to begin.']
  target_audience varchar [note: 'Intended audience or customer segment that this marketing deliverable is designed to reach.']
  target_metric_value decimal(18,2) [note: 'Target or goal value for the performance metric associated with this deliverable.']

  Note: 'Master reference table for contract_deliverable. Referenced by contract_deliverable_id.'
}
